gmw_00003321.name: PSO J009.7355--10.4316
gmw_00003321.overview: PSO J009.7355--10.4316 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1801.02641] that exhibits weak emission lines [arXiv:2010.06902]. Its ionized carbon emission shows an elongated morphology along the northwest-southeast axis and displays a small velocity gradient [arXiv:1801.02641].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PJ009--10 as a well-resolved high-redshift quasar host whose [CII] emission is elongated northwest-southeast and shows a small velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: Velocity maps and dynamical masses Four sources in our sample are well--resolved in (i.e., the observed size of the continuum--subtracted emission from the 2D gaussian modeling is >1.4 the beam): PJ009--10, J0142--3327, PJ167--13, and J1306+0356 (see Figure fig_beam_ima_spc ).
PJ009--10 shows an elongated morphology along the North-West -- South-East axis. Its velocity map shows a small velocity gradient.

sources.summary: The appendix describes this quasar as having weak lines and a non-power-law continuum, requiring simplified continuum and line fitting near telluric absorption.

quotes.quote: PSO J009.7355--10.4316 This quasar has especially weak lines and the continuum does not resemble a power law shape. Hence, we approximated the continuum around the line with a simple power-law and fit the line with one Gaussian profile. The line lies very close to one telluric absorption band and model fits were not able to constrain the line properties.

gmw_00003322.name: VIK J0046--2837
gmw_00003322.overview: VIK J0046--2837 is a quasar characterized by especially weak emission lines [arXiv:2010.06902]. Its continuum does not resemble a power-law shape, and its low J-band signal-to-noise ratio limits the ability to constrain its line properties [arXiv:2010.06902].

sources.summary: The appendix describes this quasar as having weak lines, a non-power-law continuum, simplified line fitting, and low J-band signal-to-noise that limited constraints.

quotes.quote: VIK J0046--2837 This quasar has especially weak lines and the continuum does not resemble a power law shape. We approximated the continuum around the line with a simple power-law and fit for the line. The low signal-to-noise ratio in the J band did not allow us to constrain the properties of or line.

gmw_00003323.name: VIK J0305--3150
gmw_00003323.overview: The quasar host galaxy VIK J0305--3150 is a high-redshift system at z ~ 6.6 [arXiv:1707.05238, arXiv:1809.10916] surrounded by three companion galaxies [arXiv:2204.00793]. It contains a massive molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:1707.05238] with complex gas kinematics and cavities that suggest the central quasar influences its surrounding interstellar medium [arXiv:2204.00793].

sources.summary: The paper confirms C1 as a companion source around the quasar, detects multiple ISM lines, measures its line flux in a small aperture, and reports upper limits for other lines.

quotes.quote: Companion galaxies Our re-analysis of the data confirms the three companion sources originally reported in venemans19 . In addition, we detect the or/and lines in some of the companion galaxies (namely C1, C2, and C3). The , , , and intensity maps of C1, C2, and C3 are shown in Figure companionfig . The spectral line fluxes for companion galaxies are measured in intensity maps through adopting line widths determined from super-high resolution data venemans19 . We detect the and lines in C1.

Considering its close distance to the quasar, we measure the line flux within a 0 3 radius aperture centered on the peak. The resulting line fluxes are 0.06 0.01 and 0.48 0.03 for and . The 3 upper limits for and are both 0.04 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J0305--3150 as the brightest line-emitting VIKING quasar host, with detected CO(6--5) and CO(7--6), a 2.7e10 solar-mass molecular reservoir, and gas-dominated dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: J0305--3150 ( z_ [ CII ] =6.6145 ) The host galaxy of quasar J0305--3150 shows the brightest \ line of the three VIKING quasars presented in ven16 . The \ line was detected at a redshift of z_ [ CII ] =6.6145 0.0001 with a strength of F_ [ CII ] =3.44 0.15 \ and a width of FWHM = 255 12 . Fitting a Gaussian with a FWHM of 255 \ centered on the (redshifted) frequency of the CO(6--5) emission line ( _ CO(6-5),obs = _ CO(6-5),rest /(1+z_ [ CII ] ) ) to the spectrum (Figure fig:spectra ) resulted in 8 detection (Figure fig:maps ) of the CO(6--5) line with a strength of 0.65 0.07 \ and a luminosity of =(2.6 0.3) 10^8 . Allowing the width and center of the Gaussian to vary gives very similar parameters: z_ CO(6-5) =6.6139 0.0005 , FWHM _ CO(6-5) =314 48 , and F_ CO(6-5) =0.74 0.10 . The CO(7--6) emission line was detected at 6 with z_ CO(7-6) =6.6155 0.0004 , FWHM _ CO(7-6) =225 38 , and F_ CO(7-6) =0.68 0.10 .

Based on the derived CO(1--0) luminosities, we estimate that the quasar host galaxies contain a molecular gas mass of (2.7 0.2) 10^ 10 \ (J0305--3150), (1.0 0.2) 10^ 10 \ (J0109--3047), and (1.2 0.2) 10^ 10 \ (J2348--3054, see Table tab:obsdesc ).
On the other hand, in J0305--3150 as much as 54--81 of the dynamical mass of M_ dyn =(4.1 0.5) 10^ 10 \ comprises of molecular gas, which implies a smaller stellar mass of M_* (0.7-2.1) 10^ 10 .

sources.summary: The paper confirms C2 as a companion galaxy, detects it in one line with a measured flux, and gives line and continuum surface-brightness upper limits from non-detections.

quotes.quote: Companion galaxies Our re-analysis of the data confirms the three companion sources originally reported in venemans19 . In addition, we detect the or/and lines in some of the companion galaxies (namely C1, C2, and C3). The , , , and intensity maps of C1, C2, and C3 are shown in Figure companionfig . The spectral line fluxes for companion galaxies are measured in intensity maps through adopting line widths determined from super-high resolution data venemans19 .
C2 is only detected in the line with a line flux of 0.27 0.07 , leaving 3 upper limits of 0.05, 0.04, and 0.04 for , , and .

The non-detection of the C2 continuum at 258.1GHz thus suggests that the 3 limit of the emission surface brightness is 16.0 Jy \ kpc^ 2 . The 3 upper limits for the surface brightness for C2 and C3 at 98.7GHz are both 1.5 Jy \ kpc^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper studies the quasar host's molecular ISM, noting high FIR and line luminosity, complex high-resolution gas structure, and cavities suggesting quasar influence on host-galaxy ISM kinematics.

quotes.quote: The quasar VIKING J030516.92–315056.0 (hereafter ) is among the FIR brightest quasars at z> 6 with a FIR luminosity of (1.60 0.06) 10^ 13 venemans19 . It was also detected in the line with a luminosity of (5.9 0.4) 10^ 9 venemans19 . In ALMA Cycle 2, venemans17a detected the and lines. ALMA observations of the line with extremely high spatial resolution ( 0.076'' , 410pc) was reported in venemans19 , which reveal complex gas spatial distribution and kinematics. Two cavities found in the zero velocity channel map as well as the intensity map suggest that the quasar is likely to affect the spatial distribution and kinematics of its surrounding ISM in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses J0305 3150 as the other detected z>6 QSO halo, describing its halo as smaller, fainter, and lower-surface-brightness than the CFHQ target.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, farina17 have found a small and faint halo with 9 pkpc extent around the QSO J0305 3150 at z=6.61 even using a high sensitivity of Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE: bacon15 ).

A comparison with J0305 3150 shows that 's halo has an about 10 times brighter SB profile than J0305 3150's, albeit with similar profile slopes. As a result, J0305 3150's halo has a factor 50 fainter total luminosity and a factor of 4.2 smaller size than 's, despite a deeper SB limit of 1.1 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 at 3 level farina17 .

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar could not include one line because the spectrum deviated from a power law, while another line used a single Gaussian model.

quotes.quote: VIK J0305--3150 While the redshift of this quasar would allow to include the line in the fit, the shape of the spectrum deviates from a power law blue-ward of the line. Hence, the line was not included in our fit. The line was modeled with a single Gaussian component.

gmw_00003324.name: PSO J065.4085--26.9543
gmw_00003324.overview: PSO J065.4085--26.9543 is a quasar with a continuum that strongly deviates from a power law shape [arXiv:2010.06902]. Its spectrum features a broad emission line and a broad red-ward absorption feature that may be due to a poor telluric correction [arXiv:2010.06902].

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar has a continuum far from a power law, separately fitted continuum regions, a broad line, and a possible telluric absorption feature.

quotes.quote: PSO J065.4085--26.9543 The continuum strongly deviates from a power law shape. We approximated the continuum around the and lines and fit them separately with individual continuum models. The line is rather broad in this spectrum and shows a broad red-ward absorption feature that might well be the result of a poor telluric correction in the 11000-11600 region. We fit the line using a single Gaussian component and caution against over-interpreting the fit results.

gmw_00003325.name: PSO J065.5041--19.4579
gmw_00003325.overview: PSO J065.5041--19.4579 is a quasar with a continuum that strongly deviates from a power law shape [arXiv:2010.06902]. Its Lyman-alpha emission line is partially absorbed by a strong blue-ward absorption trough [arXiv:2010.06902].

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar has a non-power-law continuum and a line partly absorbed by a strong blue-ward trough, requiring restricted single-Gaussian fitting.

quotes.quote: PSO J065.5041--19.4579 In the spectrum of this quasar the continuum strongly deviates from a power law shape. We approximated the continuum around the and lines and fit them separately with individual continuum models. The line is partially absorbed by a strong blue-ward absorption trough. Thus, we restricted the line fit to the red-ward half of the line and approximated the line using only one Gaussian component.

gmw_00003326.name: SDSS J0842+1218
gmw_00003326.overview: SDSS J0842+1218 is a quasar [arXiv:2010.06902]. Its continuum modeling requires disabling fit weights over the entire wavelength range, while telluric absorption introduces high uncertainties to its line fitting [arXiv:2010.06902].

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar needed unweighted continuum fitting, while telluric absorption made line fitting highly uncertain and led the authors to omit that model.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0842+1218 In order to properly fit the continuum over the entire wavelength range, it was necessary to disable the fit weights for the continuum model. The blue-ward wing of the complex is outside of the telluric absorption band, but its peak is not. Therefore, any line fit would be associated with high uncertainties and we decided against modeling of the in this quasar.

gmw_00003327.name: PSO J183.1124+05.0926
gmw_00003327.overview: PSO J183.1124+05.0926 is a highly luminous quasar host galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.44 [arXiv:2302.04312, arXiv:2401.04211]. The system features a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2401.04211], a star formation rate of 1330 solar masses per year [arXiv:2401.04211], and a neutral and molecular medium that is not dominated by X-ray dominated region conditions [arXiv:2302.04312].

sources.summary: PJ183+05 is a very luminous z=6.4386 quasar host targeted with multiple ISM lines; the paper uses those diagnostics to argue its neutral/molecular medium is not XDR-dominated.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present a multi-line study of the star--forming medium in the host galaxy of the quasar PSO J183.1124+05.0926 (hereafter, PJ183+05; R.A.: 12:12:26.97, Dec.: +05:05:33.4) at z=6.4386 . This quasar was discovered by banados16 using color-color selections from the Pan-STARRS1 database chambers16 , and follow-up photometric and spectroscopic observations. The luminosity in this source is the highest among 27 quasars at z>6 surveyed in decarli18 , and one of the highest from non-lensed sources currently known at any redshift e.g., pavesi16,tadaki19,andika20,mitsuhashi21 . The quasar resides at a redshift that is convenient for observations of various far-infrared emission lines that fall in transparent windows of the atmosphere at z 6.4 . We targeted a suite of lines sampling the molecular, neutral and ionized components of the ISM in PJ183+05.

In summary, all these lines of evidence disfavor a scenario in which the neutral and molecular medium in PJ183+05 is dominated by XDR conditions, but leave room for a combination of PDR and XDR, or a PDR--dominated regime. A more extensive census of both lower- and higher-J CO transitions is required to further address the relative importance of PDR and XDR conditions in the host of PJ183+05.

sources.summary: The paper describes J183+05 as a z=6.439 QSO with exceptionally luminous [CII], a velocity gradient, a massive black hole, and detailed cold-dust and line detections.

quotes.quote: PSO J183.1124+05.0926 (hereafter J183+05) at z = 6.439 was discovered by banados2018 using color-color selections from the Pan-STARRS1 database chambers2016 and follow-up photometric and spectroscopic observations. The [ CII ] luminosity in this source is the highest among 27 quasars at z>6 surveyed in decarli2018 . J183+05 showed a clear velocity gradient however, given the resolution of the observation, only an upper limit to dynamical mass has been derived M_ dyn >1.3 10^ 11 \ M_ from the study of its rotation curve neeleman2021 . It has a BH mass of M_ BH = 3.0 10^9 \ M_ derived from MgII emission line. Recently, decarli2023 analysed the cold dust SED of this QSO in detail, deriving T_ dust =47 2 K, M_ dust = (8.7 1.1) 10^ 8 \ M_ and SFR =1330 \ M_ yr^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports follow-up spectroscopy for PSO J183.1124+05.0926, confirms it as a high-redshift quasar, and notes evidence for a proximate damped Lyman absorber.

quotes.quote: PSO J183.1124+05.0926 @ z=6.4386 PSO183+05 was first followed up with the SofI and EFOSC2 instruments at the NTT, in February 2015. The discovery spectrum was taken with the Red Channel spectrograph at the MMT; higher quality spectra were later acquired with Magellan/FIRE and VLT/FORS2, in April and May 2015, respectively. Evidence was found for the presence of a very proximate Damped Lyman Absorber (DLA; z 6.404) along the same line-of-sight (see also Chen16 ). An in-depth study of this source will be presented in .

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar has telluric or intrinsic absorption near one line, additional absorption in another profile, and residuals excluded from line fits.

quotes.quote: PSO J183.1124+05.0926 In this spectrum the line falls into the wavelength range of 11000-11600 , where either telluric absorption features could not be fully corrected or intrinsic absorption is present. We further see absorption in the profile of the line. We exclude the worst residuals from the both, and , line fits and approximate the line using only one Gaussian component.

gmw_00003328.name: PSO J323.1382+12.2986
gmw_00003328.overview: PSO J323.1382+12.2986 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z = 6.5881 [arXiv:1710.01251]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1710.01251], and its high redshift has allowed researchers to model its emission lines using a single Gaussian component [arXiv:2010.06902].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PSO J323.1382+12.2986 as the highest-redshift newly discovered quasar in the sample, with optical and NIR follow-up observations.

quotes.quote: PSO J323.1382+12.2986 @ z=6.5881 Imaging follow-up of PSO323+12 was acquired with CAHA 3.5m/Omega2000 and NTT/SofI in August 2014 and February 2015, respectively. Spectroscopic observations with FORS2 at the VLT in December 2015 confirmed that the source is a high redshift quasar. The NIR spectrum was later obtained with Magellan/FIRE, in August 2016. This quasar is the one at the highest redshift among the newly discovered objects ( z= 6.5881; see Section subsecRedTempl ).

sources.summary: The appendix says this higher-redshift quasar allowed one line to be modeled, but telluric absorption affected the complex and limited interpretation except its peak redshift.

quotes.quote: PSO J323.1382+12.2986 The higher redshift of this quasar allows us to successfully model the line with a single Gaussian component. The complex falls partially in one of the bands of strong telluric absorption. As a consequence we set the contribution of the line to the complex to zero and caution against over-interpreting the complex properties with the exception of the peak redshift.

gmw_00003329.name: VIK J2348--3054
gmw_00003329.overview: VIK J2348--3054 is a luminous broad absorption line quasar at redshift z = 6.9018 [arXiv:1707.05238, arXiv:2201.06396]. The host galaxy features a compact, rotation-supported disk with extreme central gas and dust densities [arXiv:2201.06396], and it resides within a large-scale overdensity of Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies [arXiv:2402.06870].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2348--3054 as the sample highest-redshift quasar host, with faint line emission among the three, detected CO(6--5)/CO(7--6), tentative carbon emission, and detected 3 mm continuum.

quotes.quote: J2348--3054 ( z_ [ CII ] =6.9018 ) J2348--3054 is the highest redshift quasar in our sample with z_ [ CII ] =6.9018 0.0007 . It has the faintest \ line of our three observed quasars with F_ [ CII ] =1.57 0.26 Jy , and FWHM _ [ CII ] =405 69 \ ven16 . Both the CO(6--5) and CO(7--6) lines are detected at S/N 5 in the ALMA band 3 data (Figures fig:spectra and fig:maps ).

Intriguingly, the \ line in J2348--3054 was also detected albeit with a low significance (S/N 3). Both in frequency (Figure fig:spectra ) and spatially (Figure fig:maps ) the \ emission coincides with the expectations from the \ emission line. We derive a line flux of F_ [ CI ] =0.16 0.06 \ and a luminosity of L_ [ CI ] =(8.0 2.8) 10^7 \ from the spectrum. We will discuss the implications of the detection of this line in Section sec:cimass . The continuum of J2348--3054 was also detected (Figure fig:maps ) with S_ 94.9 GHz =118 13 Jy (S/N 9).

sources.summary: The paper presents 200 pc ALMA imaging of the z=6.9 quasar host J2348--3054, finding a compact rotation-supported disk, extreme central gas and dust densities, and no resolved black-hole sphere of influence.

quotes.quote: The quasar J234833.34--305410.0 at z = 6.9 hereafter J2348--3054, venemans13 is a luminous broad absorption line (BAL) quasar powered by a black hole with a mass of 2.1 10^9 M _ derosa14, mazzucchelli17 , and is one of the dozen z 7 quasars currently known. Early spatially--unresolved ALMA observations (resolution: 0 74 0 54 ) targeting the emission line revealed a highly significant detection with a line width FWHM = 405 69 km s ^ 1 and a luminosity of L_ CII =(1.9 0.3) 10^9 L _ , as well as an underlying continuum flux density of f_c = 1.92 0.14 mJy venemans16 . These observations also revised J2348--3054's original redshift of z = 6.889 0.007 from the Mg 2 measurement derosa14 to a more accurate value of z = 6.9018 0.0007 using the line venemans16 .

We present ALMA 200 pc imaging of the line and the underlying dust continuum of the z = 6.9 quasar J2348--3054, the highest--angular resolution observations yet obtained for a distant quasar host galaxy. The observations reveal very compact dust continuum and emission, reaching extreme densities in the very central region. We derive a minimum dust temperature of 132 K for the central resolution element, which leads to a very high TIR luminosity in that region.

The total gas mass in the central 200 pc beam is M_ H2 = (4 2) 10^ 9 M _ , or about a factor of two higher than that of the central supermassive black hole. Therefore, the gas kinematics in the center are not dominated by the influence of the supermassive black hole.

sources.summary: The paper searches around quasar VIK J2348--3054, finding a large-scale LAE overdensity but a central deficit interpreted as possible quasar suppression of nearby star formation.

quotes.quote: Here we present a three square degree search ( 1000 pMpc) for LAEs around the z=6.9 quasar VIK J2348--3054 using the Dark Energy CAMera (DECam), housed on the 4m Blanco telescope, finding 38 LAEs. The systemic redshift of VIK J2348--3054 is known from ALMA [ CII ] observations and place the Ly- emission line of companions within the NB964 narrowband of DECam. This is the largest field of view LAE search around a z>6 quasar conducted to date.

We find that this field is 10 times more overdense when compared to the Chandra Deep-Field South, observed previously with the same instrumental setup as well as several combined blank fields. This is strong evidence that VIK J2348--3054 resides in an overdensity of LAEs over several Mpc. Surprisingly, we find a lack of LAEs within 5 physical Mpc of the quasar and take this to most likely be evidence of the quasar suppressing star formation in its immediate vicinity.

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar has a line partly absorbed by a blue-ward trough and a strong absorption doublet masked to fit the complex.

quotes.quote: VIK J2348--3054 The line is partially absorbed by a strong blue-ward absorption trough. Thus, we restricted the line fit to the red-ward half of the line and approximated the line using only a single Gaussian component. To properly fit the complex we mask a strong absorption doublet in its center.

gmw_00003330.name: 0151+045A
gmw_00003330.overview: The galaxy 0151+045A is a bright emission-line galaxy with an R-band magnitude of 19 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401053]. It is identified as an Mg II absorber [arXiv:astro-ph/0401053, arXiv:astro-ph/0411767] and exhibits an approximately solar metallicity [arXiv:astro-ph/0411767].

sources.summary: The Q0151+045 absorbing galaxy is a bright R=19 Mg II absorber at 6 arcsec impact parameter observed with PMAS; its spectrum shows H, [O III], and [S II] emission.

quotes.quote: The galaxy responsible for the Mg II absoption is readily detected; it is a bright R=19 galaxy at an impact parameter of 6 \ (Bergeron et al. 1988). Given the brightness of the galaxy it posed as a good test case for the analysis of the low redshift absorbers. The observations were done with PMAS in August 2002, for a total of 2 hours integrations, with the QSO placed at the edge of the field of view. This was done in order to observe the absorbing galaxy simultaneously, and secondly allowing tracing of the differential atmospheric refraction in the 3D data-cubes, which is done more accurately for point sources. We corrected the data cubes for a substantial differential atmospheric refraction as the airmass during the 4 integrations varied between 1.3 and 1.6.

An image of the galaxy is shown in Fig. fig:q0151_im . Summing the spatial pixels (spaxels) belonging to the absorbing galaxy, after background subtraction, yields the spectrum shown in Fig. fig:q0151_spec . Emission lines from H , [ O III ] , H , and [ S II ] are clearly seen.

sources.summary: The paper treats 0151+045A as an Mg II absorption-selected galaxy, applying extinction corrections and citing independent emission-line spectroscopy that supports an upper-branch, approximately solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: In the absence of accurate H fluxes, we use the local E(B-V) to M _B relation (Jansen et al. \ 2001) converted to our cosmology (R. Jansen, private communication, 2003) to determine the extinction corrections for 0151+045A,B and 0229+131: E(B-V) = -(0.0507 0.0068) M_B - (0.818 0.127)

We note that for one of the Mg II galaxies presented here (0151+045A), independent emission-line spectroscopy which includes a nitrogen detection indicates that, at least for this one galaxy, the upper R_ 23 calibration is appropriate (Christensen et al. 2004).
In one case (0151+045A) spectroscopy by Christensen et al. (2004) agree with our upper branch metallicity and support an approximately solar metallicity for this galaxy.

gmw_00003331.name: VCC 1426
gmw_00003331.overview: VCC 1426 is a small galaxy located southeast of M87 in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1611.04435]. It features a low surface brightness southern tail, which likely originated from slow tidal interactions within a tight physical group of three galaxies [arXiv:1611.04435].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 1426 as a small Virgo galaxy with a faint southern tail, likely produced by slow interactions within a compact group near M87.

quotes.quote: Lying 38 \ (182 kpc) southeast of M87, VCC 1426 (833 ) shows a low surface brightness tail ( =28.2, =0.6) extending 3.9 \ (19 kpc) to the south. VCC 1426 is part of a trio of small galaxies including IC 3461 (993 ) and IC 3466 (883 ) which all lie within 6.5 \ of each other. The tight grouping in velocity of these galaxies suggests that they may be a physical group, in which case VCC 1426's tidal tail may come from slow interactions between the group members rather than by tidal stripping from the cluster potential or M87 itself.

gmw_00003332.name: NGC 4425
gmw_00003332.overview: NGC 4425 is a highly inclined disk galaxy featuring a boxy bulge, cylindrical stellar rotation, and little dust [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839]. Its stellar populations are primarily old [arXiv:0704.2839], and a bright southern plume suggests a past out-of-plane interaction [arXiv:1611.04435].

sources.summary: The paper reports a bright plume south of edge-on disk galaxy NGC 4425, interpreting its edge-brightened morphology and tilted position angle as evidence for an out-of-plane interaction.

quotes.quote: The edge on disk galaxy NGC 4425 (1899 ) shows a bright plume extending 3.5 \ to its south, originally identified in photographic imaging by Malin (1994). The plume is brightest at its southernmost tip, where it has a peak surface brightness of =26.0 and color of =0.76. While tidal tails often show condensations of stars and gas ( Hibbard 1994, Duc 2000, Weilbacher 2000, Lelli 2015), in this case the tip of the plume has a fairly constant surface brightness with no sign of any central condensation.

This morphology is somewhat suggestive of edge-brightening, where an optically thin and curved tidal tail is observed edge on. At the point where the tail curves back in projection along the line of sight the observational path length through the tail is maximized and the projected surface brightness thus increases. The position angle of the tail is offset by 50 \ from the plane of the galaxy's disk, arguing that the tail formed during an interaction that occurred out of the galaxy's disk plane.

sources.summary: NGC 4425 is described as a highly inclined, relatively dust-poor galaxy with a boxy bulge, cylindrical stellar rotation, and generally low stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4425 is a highly inclined galaxy without much dust. The \ reconstructed intensity image reveals a boxy bulge component ldp00 . The stellar velocity field exhibits cylindrical rotation. The stellar velocity dispersion is generally low ( 100 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4425 as highly inclined with little dust and a boxy bulge, only patchy ionized-gas emission, and mainly old stellar populations in absorption-line maps.

quotes.quote: NGC 4425 is a highly inclined galaxy without much dust with a boxy bulge component (Paper VII). The ionised-gas maps show only patchy traces of emission. The absorption line maps show that the stellar populations are mainly old.

gmw_00003333.name: NGC 4413
gmw_00003333.overview: NGC 4413 is a dwarf spiral galaxy located in the direction of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1103.5735]. It features a short bar [arXiv:1103.5735], weak flocculence [arXiv:1103.5735], and red, bridge-like stellar streams that suggest a fast interaction or stripping of its outer disk [arXiv:1611.04435].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC0040705 as a rare dwarf spiral toward Virgo, noting its small diameter, aliases, bar, flocculence, intermediate arm curvature, Sc classification, and faint nucleated structure.

quotes.quote: We found two such objects using the criteria 2 , and g absolute magnitude fainter than -17: PGC0039483 and PGC0040705, with magnitudes 16.9 and 14.8 , and intrinsic D_ 25 values of 5.7 kpc and 1.9 kpc. Both galaxies are located in the direction of the Virgo cluster, with J2000 RA . and DEC . coordinates (184.499 ^ ,6.654 ^ ) and (186.635 ^ ,12.610 ^ ), but with significantly smaller recession velocities of 791 km/s and 193 km/s (corrected for virgocentric infall). Other names for PGC0039483 are NGC 4241 and IC 3115, whereas PGC0040705 is also named NGC 4413 (NED also provides NGC 4407, but it is not recognised in HyperLeda).

Both galaxies have a short bar ( \ = 2) and weak flocculence ( \ = 1). PGC0039483 has open arms ( \ = 3), whereas those of PGC0040705 have an intermediate curvature ( \ = 2). The well designed spiral arms and small B/T ratio of PGC0039483 result in its assigned Scd type in both the RC3 and the EFIGI catalogue. In contrast, PGC0040705 was classified as Sab in the RC3, probably because of its tightly wound spiral arms, whereas in the EFIGI classification, it was assigned an Sc type that reflects its low B/T ratio and flocculence. By its small size, very faint absolute magnitude, and central nucleus, PGC0040705 recalls the spiral structure seen in some of the EFIGI dE galaxies, which also have a nucleus and a bar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4413 as an inclined spiral with red bridge/tail-like streams, likely stripped from its disk outskirts during a fast or M86-subgroup interaction.

quotes.quote: The outer isophotes of the inclined spiral NGC 4413 (88 , Rines Geller; the galaxy is also cataloged as NGC 4407) show more complex structure. There is a diffuse stream of starlight arcing west towards NGC 4388, and another extending southeast towards IC 3363. Both streams are quite red ( =0.9--1.0), and are morphologically similar to the two armed ``bridge/tail'' morphology of spiral galaxy encounters ( Toomre Toomre 1972). While the colors of the streams are significantly redder than the interior of NGC 4413 ( =0.62), the galaxy's disk shows a redward color gradient --- at larger radius the colors are =0.7--0.8, and the outermost parts of many spiral disks show even redder colors (Bakos 2008, Zheng 2015, Watkins 2016).

Thus the colors are consistent with stripping of the disk outskirts, although the identity of the perturbing companion remains ambiguous. The mismatch in velocity with both NGC 4388 and IC 3363 means that even if these galaxies are in close proximity to NGC 4413, any interaction would be fast and less able to trigger a strong tidal response. On the other hand, NGC 4413's velocity suggests membership in the M86 subgroup, where an interaction with the massive elliptical might be responsible for the observed tidal debris.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC4407 through its resolved halo stars in the SW2 field, noting its SB spiral classification, dense halo stars at 8.7 kpc, overlap with SW2 lsb31, and velocity distinct from VCC871.

quotes.quote: That dwarf lies between two larger galaxies, one an SB spiral (NGC4407) and the other a much brighter, nucleated dE (VCC871). While the centers of those two do not fall on the small ACS field, their extended stellar halos do show up in the images.

Stars in the halo of the spiral galaxy NGC 4407 are quite dense in one corner of the frame, where the distance to the center of the galaxy is 120 , or 8.7 kpc in projection. The stars extend well towards the center. There is some overlap with SW2 lsb31, thus a local background subtraction will be necessary. In the other corner are found stars from the dE VCC871, 70 from its center, or 5 kpc in projection. It's interesting to note that the velocity of NGC4407 is 102 , while that of VCC871 is 1427.

gmw_00003334.name: VCC 755
gmw_00003334.overview: VCC 755 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy that is a companion to NGC 4365 [arXiv:1611.04435]. It is associated with a curved stream of diffuse starlight of similar color, indicating that the galaxy has undergone tidal stripping [arXiv:1611.04435].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 755 as a low-surface-brightness dwarf companion whose similarly colored stream contains more starlight than the galaxy, indicating significant tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: Also visible in Figure  N4365 is a curved stream of diffuse starlight to the northeast of NGC 4365, coincident with the companion galaxy VCC 755 (1241 ). The stream is relatively thin (2 ) and most clearly delineated over a 12.5 \ segment where it emerges from the northeast of VCC 755 and is then lost in the halo of a nearby bright star. Here the stream has a surface brightness of =28.0, \ color of 0.8, and total magnitude m_V =15.6. To the southwest of VCC 755 we see more diffuse light apparently connecting the galaxy to NGC 4365, but this component is broader and higher in surface brightness ( =27.0) making it unclear whether this is a continuation of the northeast stream or an independent structure embedded in the halo of NGC 4365. VCC 755 itself is a low surface brightness dwarf ( =22.8, m_V =16.2, =0.85); the similarity in color between the galaxy and the stream supports the idea that the stream originates from tidal stripping of VCC 755, which must be significant given that the stream contains significantly more starlight than the galaxy itself.

gmw_00003335.name: VLSB-D
gmw_00003335.overview: VLSB-D is a nucleated ultradiffuse dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1611.04435, arXiv:2405.09104]. It exhibits an elongated structure [arXiv:1909.01347, arXiv:2405.09104], extended tidal debris [arXiv:1611.04435], and a velocity gradient [arXiv:2405.09104], suggesting it is undergoing tidal disruption [arXiv:1611.04435, arXiv:1909.01347].

sources.summary: The survey adds VLSB-D as an extreme nucleated ultradiffuse galaxy north of M84, with tidal debris and structural properties suggesting ongoing disruption toward a compact remnant.

quotes.quote: Three of these objects were reported in an earlier paper (VLSB-A, -B, and -C, Mihos 2015), and to that list we add here a fourth: VLSB-D, a nucleated UDG located 180 kpc north of the bright elliptical M84. The galaxy is well-fit by a Sers ic profile with index n =0.97, r_e =166 \ (13.3 kpc), B = 28.2, and total magnitude m_B =15.5, making it one of the most extreme UDGs yet detected in any galaxy cluster. The galaxy is quite elongated and shows evidence of extended tidal debris, and may be in the process of being disrupted by the cluster environment. Like its cousin VLSB-A (Mihos 2015), in VLSB-D we may be witnessing undergoing a tranformation from a nucleated LSB galaxy to a ultracompact dwarf, once the galaxy has been completely disrupted and only its compact nucleus remains intact.

Excluding the central source, the object has a central surface brightness of =27.1 and a total magnitude of m_B = 16.3 within a 150 \ (12 kpc) extent. However, there is additional light beyond this radius, suggestive of a tidal stream to the north and perhaps also to the south, although the presence of bright stars and other sources in this direction makes it difficult to measure the profile. The surface brightness profile shows an exponential form; a Sersic fit (shown in Figure  UDG ) is virtually indistinguishable from an exponential fit, and yields a Sersic index n=0.97 0.14 , effective radius of r_e = 166 25 \ (13.3 2 kpc), mean surface brightness _ e,B = 28.2 0.2 , and total magnitude m_ B,tot = 15.5 0.4 .

sources.summary: VLSB-D is discussed as a Virgo dwarf with low velocity dispersion, elongated morphology and globular clusters, making it a candidate extreme tidally transformed UDG with little dark matter.

quotes.quote: Our results suggest that systems like DF-44 are consistent with having been born as UDGs, while others such as DF2, DF4 and VLSB-D are possibly extreme T-UDG examples. galaxies: clusters: general -- galaxies: haloes -- galaxies: dwarfs Low surface brightness (LSB) galaxies have long been known to constitute an important segment of the galaxy population, but challenges in identifying them in large panoramic surveys have resulted in incomplete counts and insufficiently accurate characterization of their intrinsic properties Sandage1984,Dalcanton1997,Impey1988,Galaz2011,Greco2018 .

On the other hand, the comparatively low velocity dispersion of the Virgo VLSB-D dwarf is in better agreement with T-UDGs, which have undergone substantial tidal disruption. Encouragingly, the morphology of VLSB-D is very elongated, as is its globular cluster system, strongly suggestive of ongoing tidal disruption Toloba2018 .

A few candidates have been identified, such as VLSB-D Toloba2018 , DF2 vanDokkum2018a and DF4 vanDokkum2019 , with velocity dispersions as suggested by their globular cluster system and stars for the case of DF2, see Danieli2019 that are too low to contain significant amounts of dark matter. Our model identifies these systems with ``extreme tidal remnants'' that have lost the majority of their mass (dark and luminous) to tides. If this is true, objects like VLSB-D, DF2, and DF4 should be rare, but they may yet provide the best evidence for the substantial transformation of galaxies through tides that our models predict.

sources.summary: VLSB-D is a Virgo UDG with elongated structure, a velocity gradient, suspected tidal stripping, and many spectroscopically confirmed globular clusters.

quotes.quote: VLSB-D VLSB-D is a Virgo cluster galaxy. It has an elongated structure and velocity gradient Toloba2018 that suggests it is undergoing tidal stripping. Any dynamical mass derived with the properties listed must be treated with caution. Note that many of the properties presented in the catalogue were updated in from those listed in . It is worth noting that while this galaxy has an estimated GC number of 13  6.9, 14 GCs have been confirmed spectroscopically.

sources.summary: NGVSUDG-04 may host an offset nucleus-like object, shows hints of coherent GC velocity disturbance, and has extremely low velocity dispersion for its luminosity.

quotes.quote: However, NGVSUDG-04 and NGVSUDG-A04 both have an overly bright GC-like object that is offset from the center of the stellar light distribution, this object could be considered a nucleus as previously mentioned in Toloba2018 and Mihos2022 .
In that paper, we find hints of some coherent disturbance in the velocities of the GCs of NGVSUDG-04 along the semimajor axis.
NGVSUDG-04 has an extremely low velocity dispersion, consistent with dSphs of the same luminosity.

gmw_00003336.name: SDSS J141918.50+000327.8
gmw_00003336.overview: SDSS J141918.50+000327.8 is a star-forming galaxy located at a distance of approximately 460 Mpc [arXiv:2103.09937]. The galaxy has a star formation rate of 0.6 solar masses per year and shows no active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2103.09937].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J141918.50+000327.8 as AT2020caa's candidate host, rejects nuclear variability, and treats it as a star-forming galaxy hosting likely sibling supernovae.

quotes.quote: It is located at (J2000) RA 14h19m18.61s, Dec +00d03m27.86s, 1 58 offset from the bright nucleus of a candidate host galaxy SDSS J141918.50+000327.8 (hereafter, SDSSJ1419) at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.097 ( sdss ; see Fig.  fig:offset Panel a) and a distance of around 460 Mpc ( H_ 0 =67.8, _ M =0.307 ; planck-13 ). In addition to the offset of 20caa from the nucleus, the decade-long light curve of this galaxy from the Catalina Sky Survey Drake-2009 does not show any variability, thus ruling out activity from an active galactic nucleus.
Assuming the 2020 event of 20caa is a sibling SN, both of which occurred in the galaxy SDSSJ1419, we assess the likelihood of their spatial coincidence.

In this case, the spatial and temporal proximity of the siblings coupled with the fact that SDSSJ1419 is a star forming galaxy ( SFR  (M_   yr ^ 1 ) = 0.6 ; Brinchmann-2004 ) provide an opportunity to investigate their influence on the host galaxy local environment.

gmw_00003337.name: NGC 1278
gmw_00003337.overview: NGC 1278 is a regular massive early-type galaxy located in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2303.11360]. It hosts a rich globular cluster system with both red and blue populations [arXiv:1803.04893], and its central region contains a detectable nuclear X-ray point source [arXiv:0709.1336].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1278 as NGC 1277's projected companion, contrasting its similar mass but red-and-blue cluster system and prominent blue cluster peak.

quotes.quote: 1) indicate that both NGC 1277 and its companion galaxy in projection, NGC 1278, have relatively rich cluster systems. Separating the clusters in the ACS field by colour by cutting at ( g_ 475W z_ 850LP )_0 = 1.15 , the approximate separation between the red and blue peaks for a galaxy of this mass in the ACS Virgo Cluster Survey , it is apparent that while NGC 1277 has a rich red cluster population, it has few blue clusters. This is in strong contrast to NGC 1278, which has a similar stellar mass to NGC 1277 ( 2.4 10^ 11 ), but has both red and blue clusters.
NGC 1278 shows both red and blue cluster populations, whereas NGC 1277 only has a significant population of red clusters.
NGC 1278 shows a prominent blue peak, and an evident though less prominent red peak. gmm (heteroscedastic case) locates peaks at ( g_ 475W z_ 850LP )_0 = 0.95 0.24 and 1.40 0.10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1278 to show that Perseus observing conditions leave only its nuclear point source and a possible southwest LMXB detectable, with most halo point sources lost in the background.

quotes.quote: We show an image of NGC 1278, NGC 720 before processing, and the final simulated NGC 720 image in Fig. fig:fake . It is clear that most of the halo of point sources (LMXB) spread several arcmin around NGC 720 become undetectable under the observing conditions of NGC 1278. The situation would become worse after the higher absorption to the Perseus cluster is included. We only detect in NGC 1278 a point source coincident with the nucleus and a possible LMXB to the SW. It would be interesting to see whether this last source coincides with a globular cluster there but we have found no published information of globular clusters in our 13 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1278 as a regular massive early-type comparison galaxy, modeling its kinematics and showing its dark-matter fraction follows expectations unlike NGC 1277.

quotes.quote: An additional advantage of our dataset is that the field of view simultaneously covers NGC 1278, a regular massive ETG in the Perseus cluster with a mass similar to that of NGC 1277. Thus, we can study a relic galaxy alongside an object that has undergone the complete two-phase evolution with exactly the same instrumental setup, enabling a straightforward comparison of their properties. In this paper, we examine the stellar kinematics of NGC 1277 and NGC 1278 and produce detailed dynamical Jeans models with JAM We used v6.3.2 of the Python package JamPy , available from https://pypi.org/project/jampy/ . Cappellari2008, Cappellari2020 .

In Fig. fdm6kpc we compare f_ DM (6 kpc ) for NGC 1277 and NGC 1278 with that of a prediction calculated as for Figs. fdm and fdm5Re . We again show that, whereas NGC 1278 falls within the expected trend, NGC 1277 is below it.

gmw_00003338.name: S12b
gmw_00003338.overview: S12b is a massive companion galaxy that is interacting with the post-starburst system S12 [arXiv:2106.14883]. The galaxy hosts a typical, old globular cluster system with faint luminosities and red colors [arXiv:2106.14883].

sources.summary: The paper treats S12b as S12's interacting companion, using its fainter, red old-cluster population as a control against the unusual cluster population in S12.

quotes.quote: In this work, we use archival HST observations of the post-starburst system S12 (SDSS 623-52051-207), and its more massive companion (SDSS 6316-56483-722), shown in Figure fig:s12fov , to detect and study its star cluster population.

The handful of clusters detected in S12b, the interacting companion galaxy (shown as the open circles), all have similar colors to those of the reddest clusters in S12, but significantly fainter luminosities. For comparison, we also show the dereddened B-R colors and R band luminosities of ancient globular clusters in the Milky Way (red squares). The colors of these ancient stellar systems overlap with the red clusters in S12 and all clusters detected in S12. However, the brightest Galactic globular clusters are several orders of magnitude fainter than the red clusters in the post-starburst galaxy S12 (but quite similar to the brightest clusters in the companion S12b). This suggests that the clusters in the companion galaxy S12b are a typical, old globular cluster system, but that the red clusters in S12 are not; we will return to this point in Section sec:oldsfh . t Young cluster populations in nearby galaxies are known to have luminosity (and mass) functions that can be represented by a power law, dN/dL L^ , with 2 e.g., Harris&Pudritz94 .
The post-starburst S12 has a jellyfish-like structure, and a nearby companion (S12b).

gmw_00003339.name: ID 19753
gmw_00003339.overview: ID 19753 is a complex, clumpy system that is likely a galaxy merger [arXiv:2201.04270, arXiv:2204.06937]. It features a red eastern clump and a blue western clump, with its dust and infrared emissions spatially offset from the UV-bright component [arXiv:2201.04270, arXiv:2204.06937].

sources.summary: The paper removes ID 19753 from analysis because its line emission and rest-optical lines likely arise from different clumps in a red-blue, clumpy morphology.

quotes.quote: One source, ID 19753, is robustly detected in \ but is not included in the analysis because of ambiguity in whether the rest-optical line emission is associated with the region dominating the \ emission. ID 19753 displays a clumpy morphology with two dominant components displaying a large color difference, with a red clump to the east and a blue western clump that dominates the UV. It is probable that the rest-optical line emission originates from the UV-bright blue component since the H /H ratio implies relatively low nebular reddening ( =0.16).

Dust continuum emission from ALMA Band 6 observations are offset from the blue component and are more spatially coincident with the red component shi22 . Due to the large beam size of the Band 3 observations, we cannot determine whether the \ emission is co-spatial with the blue or red component, though it is likely that the CO emission aligns with the dust emission. Given the likelihood of the \ and rest-optical lines of ID 19753 arising from distinct spatial components, we remove this source and proceed with a final sample of 13 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper combines MOSFIRE and MOSDEF spectra for COSMOS-19753, predicts an auroral-line flux, and reports a significant 6302 detection.

quotes.quote: For COSMOS-19439 and COSMOS-19753, there were existing observations from the MOSDEF survey with 2 hours in each band. We therefore combined the deep MOSFIRE spectra with existing MOSDEF observations for these two targets, thereby increasing the total exposure time by 2 hours. For COSMOS-19439 and COSMOS-19753, we predicted auroral \ line fluxes in the ranges of 0.8-1.9 10^ 18 and 2.1-5.0 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 respectively.

Since the 2 upper limits are above our lowest line flux predictions, this suggests that the observations did not reach the required depth in the 10 combined hours of integration. Including the filler targets, a total of nine galaxies yielded significant ( > 2 ) 6302 detections, with two of the auroral \ targets (COSMOS-19753 and GOODS-N-6699) yielding detections.

sources.summary: ID 19753 is excluded from later ALMA-based stacks because visual inspection suggests a merger or complex multi-component system with non-co-spatial UV and 1.2 mm emission.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: 19753 & 150.0757599 & 2.38064408 & 228 & 43 & 53 & 10; Columns left to right: 3D-HST v4 catalog ID, RA (deg, J2000.0), Dec (deg, J2000.0), ALMA 1.2 mm flux density, its error, Spitzer 24 flux density, and its error.

quotes.quote: From a visual inspection, we exclude one of the 27 targets (ID 19753), which is likely a merger or a complex system with multiple star-forming components, from this part of the analysis. The 1.2 mm and UV peak emission of this object are not co-spatial, and while the MOSFIRE slit is centered on the component with the peak UV emission, the IR emission (traced by ALMA) is mainly from another component. Excluding this source does not change the results of the best-fit SED models in the previous sections.

gmw_00003340.name: Object 8280
gmw_00003340.overview: Object 8280 is a solar-metallicity galaxy with an estimated SED age of 50 to 100 Myr [arXiv:2201.04270]. It exhibits warm-dust infrared emission and an elevated UV-based star formation rate, indicating it has recently undergone a strong starburst [arXiv:2201.04270].

sources.summary: Object 8280 is a solar-metallicity sample outlier with warm-dust IR emission, young SED age, elevated UV-based SFR, and likely recent starburst; the paper removes it from stacking.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: 8280 & 150.0865021 & 2.2643168 & 149 & 64 & 293 & 9; Columns left to right: 3D-HST v4 catalog ID, RA (deg, J2000.0), Dec (deg, J2000.0), ALMA 1.2 mm flux density, its error, Spitzer 24 flux density, and its error.

quotes.quote: The exception is Object 8280 (Table tab:almafluxes ), whose IR emission behavior is significantly different from the rest of the solar-metallicity sample. This galaxy shows an elevated 24-160 emission compared to its 250-1200 , indicating the presence of warm dust, which can be due to a recent starburst or buried AGN. Object 8280 has a metallicity of =8.66^ +0.05 _ 0.08 and its estimated age from SED fitting is 50-100 Myr depending on the star-formation history assumptions.

Additionally, its SED inferred SFR (determined through the rest-frame UV continuum emission) is more than a factor of 2 larger than its estimated SFR. The young age and the elevated UV to SFR both strengthen the argument that this galaxy has recently undergone a strong starburst. As this peculiar galaxy can bias the results of the stacks, we remove it from the rest of the stacking analysis.

gmw_00003341.name: AM 1158-345
gmw_00003341.overview: AM 1158-345 is an interacting galaxy disk in a transient phase of a minor merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080]. The system is accreting a low-mass satellite galaxy, leaving its small companion and tidal debris clearly visible [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080].

sources.summary: The paper presents AM 1158-345 as a transient minor-merger disk with visible debris, short-scale scale-height perturbations, and an outward scale-height increase producing a large gradient.

quotes.quote: Figure 3 shows two galaxies in a transient phase of the merging process, accreting a low-mass satellite. While the small companion and its tidal debris are still clearly visible (left part of the disk in both images) the measured radial behaviour of scale height shows significant variations on short scales. The amplitude of these variations is 1.5 times the mean scale height of the corresponding object.

In the case of AM 1158-345 the lower panel clearly shows that the scale height increases substantially towards the outer parts of the disk while showing significant perturbations on small scales at the same time. The first of these two effects is responsible for the large scale height gradient of the order of ( )_ grad 0.05 (depending on the vertical disk model used for the fit; for comparison see Fig. 1). It also causes the large deviation of 25 30 of ( )_ mean that was found for the scale height of this disk.

gmw_00003342.name: AM 0256-321
gmw_00003342.overview: AM 0256-321 is an interacting disk galaxy in a transient phase of a minor merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080]. The system is accreting a low-mass satellite galaxy, whose companion debris remains clearly visible alongside short-scale perturbations in the disk's scale height [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080].

sources.summary: The paper describes AM 0256-321 as a transient minor-merger disk with visible companion debris, strong short-scale scale-height perturbations, and no significant scale-height gradient.

quotes.quote: Figure 3 shows two galaxies in a transient phase of the merging process, accreting a low-mass satellite. While the small companion and its tidal debris are still clearly visible (left part of the disk in both images) the measured radial behaviour of scale height shows significant variations on short scales. The amplitude of these variations is 1.5 times the mean scale height of the corresponding object.

The disk of AM 0256-321 shows perturbations on short scales with large amplitudes of the order of 10 . There is no evidence of a significant gradient of the scale height. This behaviour was found to be typical for those galaxy disks observed in a transition phase of a still ongoing interacting/merging process.

gmw_00003343.name: NGC 3187
gmw_00003343.overview: NGC 3187 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080, arXiv:1705.06210] located in a compact group [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080, arXiv:1705.06210]. It features an unusually thick disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080], tidally elongated arms undergoing active star formation [arXiv:1302.5011], and an H I tail likely created by past tidal interactions [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080, arXiv:1705.06210].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3187 as a late-type edge-on spiral in a compact group, with past tidal disturbance, large scale-height gradients, fringed isophotes, and an unusually thick disk.

quotes.quote: Figure 4 shows two examples of late-type (T=5,6) edge-on spirals that are, suggested by their location in compact groups of galaxies (Verdes-Montenegro et al. ; Zepf ) and by the typical features detected in their isophotes, in a more progressed phase of the interaction/merging process. Although there is evidence that these galaxies were strongly affected by such events in the recent past it is not clear whether their disk properties do result from accreting a small companion or from tidal interactions without a merger. However, the behaviour of the scale height of these disks is characteristic for many galaxies in the interacting/merging sample: they show substantial scale height gradients between 0.1 and 0.25, caused by a 1.5 - 2.5 times larger scale height in a region between 0.5 and 4 disk scale lengths (exceeding slightly the upper limit of the fit region used in the right part of the NGC 3187-panel).

Furthermore, the outer isophotes of NGC 3187 and NGC 4634 both show significantly ``fringed'' disks. The deviations ( )_ std from their mean scale heights were found to be between 22 and 26 . Considering their morphological types ( T= 5-6 ) and the derived ratio between radial and vertical scale parameters ( 1 h/ 2 ) their disks are 4 times thicker than those of typical non-interacting galaxies of the same type (Figs. 6 and 7 in SD II).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3187 as a galaxy with UV-detected tidally elongated arms, no archival IRAS fluxes, and an IRAC/sSFR signature associated with active star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3187: This galaxy has a pair of tidally elongated arms, which are best detected in the UV. This galaxy did not have archival IRAS fluxes. NGC 3187 has a positive IRAC slope and its sSFR places on the edge of the distribution of galaxies with positive slopes, argued by Tzanavaris (2010) to indicate active star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3187 as a barred spiral in HCG 44, notes a 4.85 GHz bridge toward it, and interprets an H I tail as possible encounter debris.

quotes.quote: A bridge of radio emission that reaches 5 .m.s. contour near NGC 3190, extends northwestward pointing at another member object, i.e. the barred spiral galaxy NGC 3187; surprisingly, this bridge is visible only at 4.85 GHz. The disk of NGC 3187 is not visible at 4.85 GHz.
This tail is possibly a remnant of the encounter between NGC 3187 and other group members.

gmw_00003344.name: NGC 4634
gmw_00003344.overview: NGC 4634 is an actively star-forming, edge-on spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0804.3819, arXiv:2204.06596]. It forms an interacting pair with NGC 4633 [arXiv:2204.06596, arXiv:2306.04513], displaying a tidally disturbed, thickened disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0107080] and extra-planar radio emission [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4634 as a late-type edge-on spiral example whose compact-group environment, fringed isophotes, large scale-height deviations, and thick disk indicate past tidal disturbance.

quotes.quote: Figure 4 shows two examples of late-type (T=5,6) edge-on spirals that are, suggested by their location in compact groups of galaxies (Verdes-Montenegro et al. ; Zepf ) and by the typical features detected in their isophotes, in a more progressed phase of the interaction/merging process. Although there is evidence that these galaxies were strongly affected by such events in the recent past it is not clear whether their disk properties do result from accreting a small companion or from tidal interactions without a merger. However, the behaviour of the scale height of these disks is characteristic for many galaxies in the interacting/merging sample: they show substantial scale height gradients between 0.1 and 0.25, caused by a 1.5 - 2.5 times larger scale height in a region between 0.5 and 4 disk scale lengths (exceeding slightly the upper limit of the fit region used in the right part of the NGC 3187-panel).

Furthermore, the outer isophotes of NGC 3187 and NGC 4634 both show significantly ``fringed'' disks. The deviations ( )_ std from their mean scale heights were found to be between 22 and 26 . Considering their morphological types ( T= 5-6 ) and the derived ratio between radial and vertical scale parameters ( 1 h/ 2 ) their disks are 4 times thicker than those of typical non-interacting galaxies of the same type (Figs. 6 and 7 in SD II).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4634 as an actively star-forming edge-on spiral whose halo H emission remains smooth and unresolved, lacking prominent bubbles, loops, or filamentary structures.

quotes.quote: Our ACS H image of NGC 4634 (see Figure f:n4634sixpanel ) shows widely distributed star formation in its disk, as already suggested by the broadband image introduced above. Individual H 2 regions are seen through gaps in the dust lane. But there are no prominent H bubbles visible. In the halo extended H emission is found that is, despite the superb spatial resolution of HST /ACS, unresolved (see Figure f:n4634sixpanel ). No filaments, loops or similar structures are discernible, apart from a few minor exceptions at large galactocentric radii.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4634 as an interacting Virgo spiral whose field is strongly affected by structured cirrus, limiting tidal-feature searches.

quotes.quote: NGC 4634 NGC 4634 is interacting with another spiral galaxy, NGC 4633. Both are members of the Virgo cluster. Structured cirrus is ubiquitous across the field, with an unfortunate filament passing directly across the interacting pair. Without a high resolution map of cirrus and (ideally) a method to subtract it from Dragonfly imaging, it is not realistic to search for tidal features associated with these galaxies. It is certainly an interesting but complicated field.

sources.summary: VCC 1932 is an edge-on spiral close pair with extra-planar westward radio emission, a nearby stripped or ejected star-forming object, and inconclusive tail evidence.

quotes.quote: VCC 1932 (NGC 4634) The low-resolution LOFAR map of this edge-on spiral galaxy which forms a close pair with NGC 4633 and shows extra-planar radio emission to the west, which could be interpreted as a radio tail. In the same direction, a star forming object that was likely created from material stripped (or tidally ejected) from VCC 1932 is located Stein2018 . However, the galaxy is not known to show a tail at any other wavelength, thus, interpretation of the radio morphology remains somewhat inconclusive.

gmw_00003345.name: COS-207144
gmw_00003345.overview: COS-207144 is identified as a single object in the UltraVISTA catalog [arXiv:1702.04352]. However, high-resolution imaging resolves the system into two distinct components consistent with an interacting galaxy pair [arXiv:1702.04352].

sources.summary: The paper resolves COS-207144 into two HST components consistent with an interacting pair and finds the spectrum-dominated stellar mass agrees with the brighter component.

quotes.quote: COS-207144, though identified as a single object in the UltraVISTA catalog, is clearly resolved in two components in the HST image (see cutouts ). COS-207144 is the only object in our sample that falls within the 3D-HST survey skelton2014 . In 3D-HST, COS-207144 is resolved as two objects, consistent with being an interacting pair at z=2.02^ +0.09 _ 0.14 and z=2.07 0.05 .

In the case of COS-207144 (the blended object), stellar mass estimates obtained here may be compared against those obtained for the objects resolved in 3DHST by . The best-fit stellar mass of the brighter galaxy of the pair is in good agreement (within 68 confidence interval) with that of COS-207144. The sum of the best-fit stellar masses of the resolved objects is significantly larger ( ( M_*/M_ )=11.62 ) than our best-fit mass for COS-207144. This is likely a result of the brighter galaxy dominating the light of the spectrum -- the best-fit stellar mass of COS-207144 ( (M_*/M_ )=11.46 ^ +0.00 _ 0.26 ) is in strong agreement with the best-fit stellar mass of this source as derived by of (M_*/M_ )=11.40 .

gmw_00003346.name: COS-37207
gmw_00003346.overview: COS-37207 is a high-redshift galaxy securely identified as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1702.04352]. It exhibits high levels of star formation alongside the highest estimated dust extinction in its sample [arXiv:1702.04352].

sources.summary: The paper classifies COS-37207 as the sample’s dusty star-forming galaxy, with high SED-derived star formation, high dust extinction, and no secure emission lines.

quotes.quote: COS-37207 is the only galaxy in the sample to show high levels of star formation, with a best-fit SED star formation rate (SFR) of 339^ +108 _ 221 M_ yr ^ 1 . The error bars quoted here correspond to the 68 confidence interval. The 99 confidence interval bounds are [ 31,788 ] M_ yr ^ 1 , the lower bound of which is greater than the best-fit SFR for any of the other galaxies in the sample. We therefore conclude that this galaxy is securely identified as star forming.

Additionally, the estimated dust extinction for COS-37207 is the highest in the sample, with A_V=1.70^ +0.10 _ 0.40 mag. COS-71929 displays the second-highest level of dust extinction at A_V = 1.30^ +0.10 _ 0.40 mag. We therefore characterize COS-37207 as a dusty star-forming galaxy, COS-71929 as a galaxy with mostly suppressed star-formation intermediate between star-froming and quiescent galaxies, and the rest of the galaxies in our sample as quiescent.

gmw_00003347.name: COS-71929
gmw_00003347.overview: COS-71929 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately z=2.000 [arXiv:1702.04352]. It is characterized as a dusty galaxy with mostly suppressed star formation, placing it intermediate between star-forming and quiescent galaxies [arXiv:1702.04352].

sources.summary: The paper measures COS-71929 at z≈2 from absorption features and characterizes it as dusty, mostly quenched, and intermediate between star-forming and quiescent galaxies.

quotes.quote: Secondly, the spectrum of COS-71929 shows absorption features consistent with , allowing us to measure a spectroscopic redshift at z_ spec =2.000 0.006 . finds a spectroscopic redshift for COS-75355 using the absorption features, in conjunction with H _ in absorption. The spectrum in our sample does not have sufficient signal-to-noise to detect most of the features used to identify the spectroscopic redshift -- there is a possible detection of Na in absorption at z=1.822 , but the detection is not secure. The features used to determine a spectroscopic redshift for COS-71929 are shown in cahk , as labeled.

Though COS-71929 also shows evidence of some star formation (SFR =8.7^ +0.4 _ 6.9 M_ yr ^ 1 ), a measurement of the quenching factor of this galaxy indicates that the SFR was significantly higher in the past ( q_ sf 0.95 ). Additionally, the estimated dust extinction for COS-37207 is the highest in the sample, with A_V=1.70^ +0.10 _ 0.40 mag. COS-71929 displays the second-highest level of dust extinction at A_V = 1.30^ +0.10 _ 0.40 mag. We therefore characterize COS-37207 as a dusty star-forming galaxy, COS-71929 as a galaxy with mostly suppressed star-formation intermediate between star-froming and quiescent galaxies, and the rest of the galaxies in our sample as quiescent.

gmw_00003348.name: COS-75355
gmw_00003348.overview: COS-75355 is a quiescent, high-mass galaxy [arXiv:1702.04352] located at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 1.82 [arXiv:1204.3099, arXiv:1702.04352]. Its morphology is consistent with a system undergoing one or two minor mergers [arXiv:1702.04352], displaying a smoother and more centrally concentrated structure in the rest-frame optical than in the ultraviolet [arXiv:1204.3099].

sources.summary: COSMOS-254025 is included as a z=1.82 quiescent galaxy with an upper-limit velocity dispersion, revised rest-frame optical size, smoother optical morphology, and lower dynamical mass limit.

quotes.quote: COSMOS-254025 reports _* < 326 km s ^ 1 derived from a Subaru/MOIRCS spectrum of a z=1.82 galaxy selected from the COSMOS survey. This is an upper limit due to the limited resolution of the spectrograph. Using the results of surface brightness fits by ( r_e=5.79 0.61 kpc and n=4.14 ) from HST/ACS F814W band observations, they derive a dynamical mass of M< 7 10^ 11 M_ .

Indeed, comparison of the ACS and Ultravista images shows that the galaxy is extended and clumpy in the restframe UV, but smoother and more centrally concentrated in the restframe optical. Using an identical approach as described in Section sec.surfacebrightnessfits we run galfit on the deep J, H, and K band UltraVISTA survey observations (McCracken et al, in prep.), masking out neighboring galaxies, and using a bright nearby star as PSF model, we find a mean effective radius of r_e=2.4 0.4 kpc, and n=2.5 0.4 . The derived r_e is 60 smaller than the value derived by in the F814W band.

sources.summary: The paper describes COS-75355 as an ultramassive high-redshift galaxy with minor-merger morphology, a literature spectroscopic redshift, and quiescent high-mass stellar population properties.

quotes.quote: HST Imaging The galaxies at hand span a significant variety of morphologies, as shown in cutouts : COS-75355 appears to be consistent with a galaxy undergoing one to two minor mergers and COS-37207 is consistent with being a massive face-on red spiral, while COS-71929 and COS-90676 are relatively isolated with some diffuse emission. For a quantitative discussion of these morphologies, see Marsan et al. (2017; in prep.).

First, COS-75355 has a literature z_ spec =1.822 from . Secondly, the spectrum of COS-71929 shows absorption features consistent with , allowing us to measure a spectroscopic redshift at z_ spec =2.000 0.006 . finds a spectroscopic redshift for COS-75355 using the absorption features, in conjunction with H _ in absorption. The spectrum in our sample does not have sufficient signal-to-noise to detect most of the features used to identify the spectroscopic redshift -- there is a possible detection of Na in absorption at z=1.822 , but the detection is not secure.
The FAST-derived stellar masses and SFRs for our sample place the three quiescent galaxies (COS-75355, COS-207144, and COS-90676) on the high-mass end of the quiescent population.

gmw_00003349.name: COS-90676
gmw_00003349.overview: COS-90676 is an ultramassive, quiescent galaxy that is relatively isolated [arXiv:1702.04352]. It exhibits some diffuse emission and has a best-fit stellar age that is a factor of two to three younger than the age of the universe at its redshift [arXiv:1702.04352].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes COS-90676 as a relatively isolated ultramassive galaxy with diffuse emission, low-dust quiescent classification, and a younger best-fit age than the universe at its redshift.

quotes.quote: HST Imaging The galaxies at hand span a significant variety of morphologies, as shown in cutouts : COS-75355 appears to be consistent with a galaxy undergoing one to two minor mergers and COS-37207 is consistent with being a massive face-on red spiral, while COS-71929 and COS-90676 are relatively isolated with some diffuse emission. For a quantitative discussion of these morphologies, see Marsan et al. (2017; in prep.).
The FAST-derived stellar masses and SFRs for our sample place the three quiescent galaxies (COS-75355, COS-207144, and COS-90676) on the high-mass end of the quiescent population.

COS-207144 and COS-71929 have best-fit ages comparable to the age the universe at their redshift, whereas COS-75355 and COS-90676 have best-fit stellar ages a factor of 2-3 younger than the age of the universe at their redshift.

gmw_00003350.name: GALEX1240+6233
gmw_00003350.overview: GALEX1240+6233 is a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:0909.0031]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus based on its placement on the BPT diagram and its strong HeII emission [arXiv:0909.0031].

sources.summary: The paper classifies GALEX1240+6233 as an AGN-contaminating Ly galaxy, citing its BPT placement and strong HeII emission, and excludes it from most later figures.

quotes.quote: Although most of our sources lie along the star-forming galaxy track, the diagram suggests that one of the optically-confirmed Ly Galaxies (GALEX1240+6233) is in fact an AGN. We have eliminated this object from all other figures, except the BPT diagrams of Figure bpt_morph . Finkelstein et al. \ (2009) similarly see a small number of BPT-selected AGNs in their analysis of the Deharvang et al. \ (2008) Ly sample. Finkelstein et al. \ (2009) also classified GALEX1417+5228 as an AGN based on the presence of HeII 4686 in the spectrum. Three of the present optical spectra show detectable HeII: GALEX1001+0233 ( f (HeII)/ f (H )=0.019 0.005 ), GALEX1240+6233 ( f (HeII)/ f (H )=0.072 0.015 ), and GALEX1417+5228 ( f (HeII)/ f (H )=0.018 0.005 ). GALEX1240+6233 is classified as an AGN based on the BPT diagram, and the strong HeII confirms this interpretation.
However, one of these (GALEX1240+6233) is classified as an AGN based on the BPT diagram.

gmw_00003351.name: GALEX1001+0233
gmw_00003351.overview: GALEX1001+0233 is a Lyman-alpha selected star-forming galaxy [arXiv:0909.0031]. It is characterized by a direct oxygen abundance of approximately 7.94, weak helium II emission, and very weak or undetected nitrogen II emission [arXiv:0909.0031].

sources.summary: The paper treats GALEX1001+0233 as a Ly-selected star-forming galaxy with weak HeII, weak or undetected NII, no NeV, and a direct oxygen abundance near 7.94.

quotes.quote: Three of the present optical spectra show detectable HeII: GALEX1001+0233 ( f (HeII)/ f (H )=0.019 0.005 ), GALEX1240+6233 ( f (HeII)/ f (H )=0.072 0.015 ), and GALEX1417+5228 ( f (HeII)/ f (H )=0.018 0.005 ). GALEX1240+6233 is classified as an AGN based on the BPT diagram, and the strong HeII confirms this interpretation. However, we do not think that the presence of the weaker HeII lines necessarily implies that the other two objects are AGNs rather than metal-poor star-forming galaxies. Roughly 10 of blue compact galaxies have detectable HeII at the level seen in these galaxies, probably produced by Wolf-Rayet stars or shocks in the galaxies, and the presence of the line becomes more common as one moves to more metal-poor galaxies (e.g., Thuan Izotov 2005). GALEX1001+0233 and GALEX1417+5228 also have very weak or undetected NII 6584 which would require them to be metal-poor AGNs. While a very small number of such objects have now been found (Izotov Thuan 2008), they would not be expected to lie on the low-metallicity star track in the BPT diagram as the present objects do (Groves et al. \ 2006). In the case of GALEX1001+0233, where the spectrum covers NeV 3426 , there is no sign of this high-excitation line. We therefore classify GALEX1001+0233 and GALEX1417+5228 as star formers.

The remaining 4 galaxies (GALEX0332-2811, GALEX0959+0151, GALEX1000+0201, and GALEX1001+0233) have direct abundances 12+ (O/H)=( 8.14 0.03, 8.27 0.03, 7.96 0.12 and 7.94 0.03 ) compared to the N2 determinations of 12+ (O/H)=( 7.96 0.02,8.17 0.02, 8.20 0.02 and 8.01 0.03 ).

gmw_00003352.name: GALEX1417+5228
gmw_00003352.overview: GALEX1417+5228 is a Lyman-alpha emission-line galaxy characterized by an extremely low metallicity [arXiv:0909.0031]. It is classified as a very high-excitation star-forming galaxy rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0909.0031].

sources.summary: The paper argues GALEX1417+5228 is a very high-excitation, extremely low-metallicity star-forming Ly galaxy rather than an AGN.

quotes.quote: They also classified GALEX1417+5228 as an AGN, but we believe this source to be a very high-excitation star former. We discuss this very interesting source in Section la_metal .

The lowest metallicity source is GALEX1417+5228. This extremely high EW(H ) ( 1400 \ in the rest frame) source is shown in Figure whole_spectrum . It is the interesting source we mentioned in Section lae_prop . The [ NII ] 6584 /H ratio in Figure met_lum is only at the 1 level, and the 1 upper limit from the Pettini Pagel (2004) calibration gives 12+ (O/H) <7.4 . The [ OIII ] 4363 line is exceptionally strong, from which we derive 12+ (O/H) =7.65 0.03 . Thus, GALEX 1417+5228 is near the extremely low-metallicity class and below all of the strong emission-line sources in the KISS sample of Salzer et al. \ (2009), as can be seen from Figure met_lum .

gmw_00003353.name: AM 1054A
gmw_00003353.overview: AM 1054A is an optically bright spiral galaxy currently in the early stages of a merger with a companion galaxy [arXiv:1701.07054]. The light along its disk is dominated by a young stellar population, and it contains a region that may be a starburst or a second nucleus [arXiv:1701.07054].

sources.summary: The paper describes AM 1054A as an optically bright spiral in an early merger, with disk light dominated by young stars and a slit crossing a possible second nucleus or starburst region.

quotes.quote: This system is composed by a main spiral galaxy (hereafter AM 1054A) and a companion galaxy (hereafter AM 1054B). AM 1054A is an optically bright spiral in the early stages of merging 85 Myr; mullan11 . As can be seen in Fig. sin_1054AB (top panel) the population synthesis results indicate that the light along the disk of AM 1054A, is mainly dominated by a young stellar population component ( t 4 10^ 7 yr), with a not negligible contribution of an intermediate population. This spatial profile corresponds to a slit position crossing the supposed 'second' nucleus (or starburst region?) of AM1054 A (see Paper I).

gmw_00003354.name: AM 1054B
gmw_00003354.overview: AM 1054B is a companion galaxy in the AM 1054 system that exhibits an elliptical morphology [arXiv:1701.07054]. The galaxy is dominated by an old stellar population in both light and mass, with an intermediate-age stellar population contributing approximately 30% to its light and 16% to its mass [arXiv:1701.07054].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AM 1054B as the companion in the AM 1054 system, dominated by old stellar light and mass while retaining a significant intermediate-age population.

quotes.quote: This system is composed by a main spiral galaxy (hereafter AM 1054A) and a companion galaxy (hereafter AM 1054B).

In the bottom panel of Fig. sin_1054AB , we can see that AM 1054B is mainly dominated by the old stellar population component, both in the contribution of light and mass, with a mean value of 62 for light and 84 for mass. The Intermediate stellar population contributes significantly for this galaxy, with a mean value of about 30 for a light-weighted context and 16 for a mass-weighted one.

Moreover, only AM 1054B has an old population ( t>2 10^ 9 years) as being the main source of the flux at 5870 . In Fig. \ 1 of Paper I we can see that this object has an elliptical form, being the old population predominant on it.

gmw_00003355.name: AM 1219A
gmw_00003355.overview: AM 1219A is a spiral galaxy that acts as the primary component of an interacting galaxy pair, featuring a tidal tail, a connecting bridge of material, and asymmetric ionized-gas rotation [arXiv:1309.5989, arXiv:1701.07054]. The galaxy exhibits starburst activity and bright H II regions dominated by a young stellar population [arXiv:1309.5989, arXiv:1701.07054].

sources.summary: AM 1219A is the brighter primary galaxy, with a tidal arm, star-forming light excess, asymmetric ionized-gas rotation, and a modeled dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: We detected a tidal tail in the main galaxy (AM 1219A) and a bridge of material connecting the galaxies. In luminosity, AM 1219A is about 3.8 times brighter than the secondary (AM 1219B). The surface brightness profile of AM 1219A was decomposed into bulge and disc components. The profile shows a light excess of 53 due to the contribution of star-forming regions, which is typical of starburst galaxies.

The rotation curve of AM 1219A derived from the emission line of ionized gas is quite asymmetric, suggesting a gas perturbed by interaction. We explore all possible values of stellar and dark matter masses. The overall best-fitting solution for the mass distribution of AM 1219A was found with M/L for bulge and disc of _ b =2.8_ 0.4 ^ +0.4 and _ d =2.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 , respectively, and a navarro95 profile of M_ 200 =2.0_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 10^ 12 M_ and =16.0_ 1.1 ^ +1.2 .

sources.summary: The paper presents AM 1219A as the primary spiral with a tidal arm and HII regions, with slit observations showing young stellar components around bright HII complexes.

quotes.quote: This system is composed by two galaxies, a main spiral galaxy (hereafter, AM 1219A) and a secondary one (hereafter, AM 1219B). The primary galaxy presents a normal arm to the south-west and a strong tidal arm, with several H ii regions along it.

Three different slit positions were observed in AM 1219A: PA = 162 \ and PA = 341 \ along the North-South direction crossing the nuclei and the disturbed arm, respectively; and PA = 70 \ in the Northeast-Southwest direction crossing two very bright H ii regions. For AM 1219B, we only have observations with a PA = 25 crossing its nucleus: PA = 25 . For the slits with PA = 162 \ and PA = 70 \ the light emitted by AM 1219A is dominated by the contribution of the young stellar component ( 54 ), with a significant contribution ( 35 ) of the old component.
