J0651+2734 appears to show a faint extension of \ gas towards B2 0648+27 (visible in Fig. fig:hi ), but additional data are necessary to confirm this.

gmw_00003396.name: HE 1249-0648
gmw_00003396.overview: HE 1249-0648 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0606309]. It exhibits discrepant line widths and spectral features, including blueshifted broad absorption, suggesting it may be a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0606309].

sources.summary: The paper flags HE 1249-0648 as an unusual quasar with discrepant line widths and spectral features suggesting it may be a BAL QSO with blueshifted broad absorption.

quotes.quote: HE 1249-0648 and HE 1258 0823 show FWHM( ) FWHM( ). While FWHM( ) is subject to large uncertainty, the difference is confirmed by careful reinspection of these spectra. This condition is seen in only 2/215 sources in the marzianietal03a sample.

The absence of any detectable \ emission, along with a possible \ feature at 5016 \ support the possibility that HE 1249-0648 and HE 1258 0823 could be BAL QSOs similar to IRAS 07598+6508 and Mkn 235. Further support comes from an inspection of the HE survey spectra of both objects which indeed show blueshifted broad absorption in the \ line.

gmw_00003397.name: HE 1258 0823
gmw_00003397.overview: HE 1258 0823 is a quasar that exhibits discrepant spectral line widths [arXiv:astro-ph/0606309]. It may be a broad-absorption line quasar, as supported by blueshifted broad absorption in its hydrogen line and a possible helium feature [arXiv:astro-ph/0606309].

sources.summary: The paper flags HE 1258 0823 as an unusual quasar with discrepant line widths and spectral features suggesting it may be a BAL QSO with blueshifted broad absorption.

quotes.quote: HE 1249-0648 and HE 1258 0823 show FWHM( ) FWHM( ). While FWHM( ) is subject to large uncertainty, the difference is confirmed by careful reinspection of these spectra. This condition is seen in only 2/215 sources in the marzianietal03a sample.

The absence of any detectable \ emission, along with a possible \ feature at 5016 \ support the possibility that HE 1249-0648 and HE 1258 0823 could be BAL QSOs similar to IRAS 07598+6508 and Mkn 235. Further support comes from an inspection of the HE survey spectra of both objects which indeed show blueshifted broad absorption in the \ line.

gmw_00003398.name: TXS 1720+102
gmw_00003398.overview: TXS 1720+102 is a radio-quasar type blazar located at a redshift of 0.732 [arXiv:1403.0161]. Although it was associated with a signal excess in Milagro observations, it has not yet been confirmed as a TeV-emitting source [arXiv:1403.0161].

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 1720+102 as a z=0.732 radio-quasar blazar with a Milagro excess that appears only under a loose FDR cut and may be background.

quotes.quote: Milagro also observed a signal excess at the sky locations of Mkn 501, TXS 1720+102 and 1ES 0502+675 . Their significances are 2.93, 2.84 and 2.53 respectively, which is insufficient to pass our standard FDR cut of =0.01 .
However TXS 1720+102 has not yet been identified as a source with TeV emission. This is a radio quasar type blazar identified at a redshift of 0.732 TXSRedshift .

The lowest FDR cut that TXS 1720+102 passes is =0.32 . With this loose FDR cut, three candidates become TeV associations: Mkn 421, Mkn 501 and TXS 1720+102. However, the expected contamination of the resulting candidates list is 32 so it is likely that TXS 1720+102 is a background fluctuation. While it is hard to advocate a dedicated IACT observation of TXS 1720+102, better observations will be performed by the High Altitude Water Cherenkov (HAWC) survey instrument HAWCRef , which is already started to operate at a sensitivity better than Milagro.

gmw_00003399.name: La Flaca
gmw_00003399.overview: La Flaca is a high-redshift, dusty star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1411.7998] that is triply imaged through gravitational lensing [arXiv:2210.08163]. It has a short gas depletion timescale of approximately 70 Myr and may be interacting with a nearby source [arXiv:2210.08163].

sources.summary: The paper identifies La Flaca as an exceptionally bright high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy projected near El Gordo, deriving photometric, radio, redshift, luminosity, and possible lensing-related properties.

quotes.quote: El Gordo (ACT-CL J0102-4915) has two bright point sources projected at small clustercentric radii; the brighter of the two ( S_ =36 2 mJy ) we refer to as ``La Flaca.'' We briefy describe La Flaca's properties here because it is exceptionally bright (The next brightest SMG within our sample has flux density 27 mJy and is located in ACT--CL J0438-5419), second in brightness only to the lensed SMG e.g. wilson2008,joha10 behind the Bullet cluster (ACT-CL J0658-5557). La Flaca is located at =15.732207^ =-49.252415^ and has an extremely faint radio counterpart with S_ 2.1 = 24 7 Jy . To test the possibility that La Flaca is a local SZE enhancement, we compare the spectral fits of the source's LABOCA+SPIRE photometry using a modified black body spectrum and thermal SZE spectrum.

We find that the SZE spectral shape is incompatible with the data; thus, La Flaca is more likely to be a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy than a compact SZE enhancement. The best-fit modified Planck function parameters are z=4.1 and L_ IR =3.2 10^ 12 L_ , assuming the median observed T_ D =37.4 K and =1.5 from the LABOCA Extended-CDFS SMG survey LESS; ward11 . Using the 610 MHz GMRT data lindner2014 , we derive a radio spectral index of 1.3 , giving a redshift estimate of z=4.0 based on the radio-to-far IR spectral index cari99 , in agreement with that of the spectral fit and further supporting the conclusion that La Flaca is a high-redshift SMG.

sources.summary: The paper identifies EG-ALMA 6c as the highly magnified image of a dusty z=4.324 ALMA source with CO emission, short gas depletion time, and possible interaction with ID 4a.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains one such source, the triply imaged system EG-ALMA 6a/6b/6c. EG-ALMA 6c is the most highly magnified of the three images. It is adjacent to the source ``ID 4a'' In the naming scheme of 2021ApJ...908..146C , ID 4 refers to the lensed source, and suffixes a, b, and refer to the three lensed images. of 2021ApJ...908..146C , which is part of their galaxy group at z 4.32 .

If EG-ALMA 6c is at a similar redshift to ID 4a, this line is CO J=4 -3, and the redshift corresponds to z=4.324 , showing that ID 4a and EG-ALMA 6c are distinct sources. The two peaks of the 86.6 GHz line are separated by 380 km s ^ 1 , which suggests that the source is likely massive but similar to other submm galaxies 2021MNRAS.501.3926B The integrated CO (4--3) flux is 0.79 0.08 mJy km s ^ 1 , which corresponds to M_ H_2 = 10^ 9.8 1.0 \ (magnification-factor corrected), adopting L'_ CO (4-3) /L'_ CO (1-0) = 0.46 , _ CO = 0.8 (K km s^ 1 pc^2)^ 1 for SMGs 2013ARA&A..51..105C . The depletion timescale M_ H_2 / SFR is about 70 Myr, which is close to the typical depletion timescales for local ULIRGs. The velocity difference between EG-ALMA 6c and ID 4a is 250 km s ^ 1 , suggesting that the two might be interacting.

gmw_00003400.name: G 27kpc
gmw_00003400.overview: G 27kpc is a low-mass dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.39863 [arXiv:2011.06071]. It exhibits a low star-formation rate and is associated with low-ionization gas that may be accreting onto the galaxy or transferring toward the nearby, more massive galaxy G 163kpc [arXiv:2011.06071].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G 27kpc as a newly discovered low-mass dwarf near Q0122 003, with low star formation and low-ionization gas interpreted as accreting onto it or transferring toward G 163kpc.

quotes.quote: We have discovered a dwarf galaxy, G 27kpc ( M_ =10^ 8.7 M _ ), at z=0.39863 that is 27 kpc from the quasar sightline. G 27kpc is only +21 from a more massive ( M_ =10^ 10.5 M _ ) star-forming galaxy, G 163kpc, at an impact parameter of 163 kpc. While G 163kpc is actively forming stars ( SFR =6.9 M _ yr ^ 1 ), G 27kpc has a low star-formation rate ( SFR =0.08 0.03 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and star formation surface density ( _ SFR =0.006 M _ kpc ^ 2 yr ^ 1 ), implying no active outflows.

(1) Part of the low-ionization phase has a metallicity and kinematics consistent with being accreted onto G 27kpc. (2) The remainder of the low ionization phase has metallicities and kinematics consistent with being intragroup gas being transferred from G 27kpc to G 163kpc.

gmw_00003401.name: G 163kpc
gmw_00003401.overview: G 163kpc is a massive star-forming galaxy with a stellar mass of 10^10.5 solar masses [arXiv:2011.06071]. It actively forms stars at a rate of 6.9 solar masses per year, driving high-ionization outflows from its massive halo and receiving transferred intragroup gas from its neighbor, G 27kpc [arXiv:2011.06071].

sources.summary: The paper treats G 163kpc as the more massive star-forming galaxy in the Q0122 003 absorber pair, linking its halo to high-ionization outflows and intragroup gas transfer from G 27kpc.

quotes.quote: G 27kpc is only +21 from a more massive ( M_ =10^ 10.5 M _ ) star-forming galaxy, G 163kpc, at an impact parameter of 163 kpc. While G 163kpc is actively forming stars ( SFR =6.9 M _ yr ^ 1 ), G 27kpc has a low star-formation rate ( SFR =0.08 0.03 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and star formation surface density ( _ SFR =0.006 M _ kpc ^ 2 yr ^ 1 ), implying no active outflows.

(2) The remainder of the low ionization phase has metallicities and kinematics consistent with being intragroup gas being transferred from G 27kpc to G 163kpc. (3) The high ionization phase is consistent with being produced solely by outflows originating from the massive halo of G 163kpc.

gmw_00003402.name: NRCJ1631+3008-z6.1
gmw_00003402.overview: NRCJ1631+3008-z6.1 is an emission-line galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 6.112 [arXiv:2207.11259]. It is resolved into two components undergoing a major merger, which has likely triggered its ongoing starburst and strong optical emission lines [arXiv:2207.11259].

sources.summary: The paper reports NRCJ1631+3008-z6.1 as a z=6.112 JWST-discovered emission-line galaxy, resolving two components whose mass ratio and timescale suggest a major merger triggering a recent starburst.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present the first serendipitous spectroscopic discovery of an 4959/5007 and \ line emitter at z > 6 with the JWST/NIRCam WFSS mode. The galaxy, named NRCJ1631+3008-z6.1, was discovered in the field around the flux calibrator P330-E (GSC 02581-02323), which was observed during the commissioning phase of this observing mode. The detection of 4959/5007 and \ emission lines at 3.528, 3.562 and 4.668 \ indicates a spectroscopic redshift of z = 6.112 0.001 .

The two spatial and velocity components of NRCJ1631+3008-z6.1 resolved with JWST/NIRCam imaging and slitless spectroscopy suggest that this source is a galaxy pair. Assuming a constant mass-to-light ratio for the two components, the mass ratio is 2.6 0.6 , satisfying the classification criterion of a major merger event. The dynamic timescale computed from the spatial and velocity offset of the two components is 2--3 Myr, consistent with the timescale of the late starburst (1--2 Myr) indicated by the strong nebular lines as modeled in Section ss:03c_sed . Therefore, it is possible that the strong optical emission lines and ongoing star formation seen in this source is triggered by the merger of the two galaxies undergoing the final coalescence phase.

gmw_00003403.name: UGC 12914
gmw_00003403.overview: UGC 12914 is a massive spiral galaxy that recently underwent a head-on collision with UGC 12915 [arXiv:2101.07092]. This interaction created a gas-rich bridge between the galaxies [arXiv:1903.12323, arXiv:1107.3280] and triggered star formation associated with UGC 12914's warped stellar disk [arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper models the Taffy system as two gas-rich colliding discs whose bridge contains radio continuum, molecular gas, X-rays, asymmetric ISM, and splash-bridge structures.

quotes.quote: An exception to this rule is the so-called Taffy galaxy system, consisting of two colliding gas-rich discs, UGC 12914 and UGC 12915. This system has been well observed in many different bands over the last 25 years. The nick-name derives from the polarized radio continuum bridge discovered by condon93 . There is a large amount of gas in the bridge between the two discs, and gao03 observed CO emission with the BIMA telescope.

Both the HI and CO observations show a clear asymmetry near the disc of UGC 12915. A decade later warm emission was observed in the bridge by peterson12 with a cooling time scale of only about 5000 years. It was suggested that the source of the emission is turbulence still occurring in the molecular gas.
The 7 kpc offset run shown in fig:taffy7kpc30myrsubfigures is the best candidate for a collision like the Taffy galaxy system.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12914 as one member of the Taffy collision, with lens and ring structure, CO maxima, a tidal molecular-disk bend, and bridge-directed gas filaments.

quotes.quote: The Taffy system (UGC 12914/15) consists of two massive spiral galaxies which had a head-on collision about 20 Myr ago. It represents an ideal laboratory to study the reaction of the interstellar medium to a high-speed ( 1000 km s ^ 1 ) gas-gas collision.

The optical image of UGC 12914 (upper panel of Fig. fig:cohistars ) shows an inner lens structure with dust lanes and a much fainter outer double-ring structure. In addition, a stellar arm starts from the northern tip of the stellar lens structure joining the eastern faint outer stellar ring. The CO emission distribution of UGC 12914 has three maxima along the major axis: the galaxy center (D1) and the two elongated structures at a distance of 20'' or 5.8 kpc from the center (D1 and D3). The latter structures correspond to the tips of the optical lens and are reminiscent of a limb-brightened molecular ring.

The northernmost part of UGC 12914's molecular disk is curved towards the bridge as is the stellar arm (upper panel of Fig. fig:cohistars and upper left panel of Fig. fig:taffy_sm2_mom_final_stars ), suggesting this is a tidal effect. Whereas the western border of the CO distribution, which corresponds to the western dust lane within the optical lens structure of UGC 12914, is sharp, the eastern border is disrupted showing east-west filaments elongated into the bridge direction.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Taffy pair as a recent head-on encounter with bridge radio emission, bridge gas and dust, and magnetic fields aligned between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: The unusual morphology of this pair of galaxies (UGC 12914 and UGC 12915), known as the Taffy system, comes from a recent ( 10^7 yr), nearly head-on encounter (Condon et al. ). Almost half of their radio emission at 1.49 GHz have been detected just from the bridge between the galaxies, which may be due to fresh relativistic CR electrons radiating in magnetic fields pulled out of the disks. In this system the H i gas is mostly distributed along the bridge (Condon et al. ), where a large amount of molecular gas and dust were also detected (Gao et al. ; Zhu et al. ). Our re-reduced radio polarization data at 4.86 GHz (Fig. f:taffytp ) show more details of the magnetic field structure than are presented in Condon et al. ( ). We have confirmed that the general orientation of magnetic field is along the line joining the galaxies, which is also a possible direction for passing the intruder galaxy UGC 12914.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 12914 has central star formation, star formation associated with a warped stellar disk, and higher SFE in the warped northern disk than in the non-warped part.

quotes.quote: High star formation activities are seen in the centre of UGC 12914 (bottom-right) and UGC 12915 (top-left), although UGC 12915 is a more active star-forming galaxy.
Star formation in UGC 12914 is associated with the warped stellar disc.
In UGC 12914, SFE _ mol and SFE _ gas in the warped part of the disc (the northern disc) are higher than in a non-warped part.

gmw_00003404.name: UGC 12915
gmw_00003404.overview: UGC 12915 is a massive spiral galaxy that underwent a head-on collision with UGC 12914 approximately 20 million years ago [arXiv:2101.07092]. It features an asymmetric dust ridge, a twisted molecular disk [arXiv:2101.07092], and active star formation that extends toward the overlap region [arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12915 as the other Taffy collision galaxy, with an asymmetric dust ridge, twisted molecular disk, bent northwest disk, and nearby high-dispersion bridge region.

quotes.quote: The Taffy system (UGC 12914/15) consists of two massive spiral galaxies which had a head-on collision about 20 Myr ago. It represents an ideal laboratory to study the reaction of the interstellar medium to a high-speed ( 1000 km s ^ 1 ) gas-gas collision.

The optical image of UGC 12915 (upper panel of Fig. fig:cohistars ) shows an asymmetric dust ridge or tilted ring visible in absorption and two symmetric stellar arms, the northern arm being brighter than the southern arm. The moment 0 map (Fig. fig:taffy_obs_mom_final_mom0 ) shows a bright, asymmetric, and twisted thin molecular disk rather than a tilted ring in UGC 12915 which corresponds to the asymmetric dust ridge. The surface brightness distribution along the major axis is asymmetric. The second brightest maximum in this disk corresponds to the galaxy center.

The brightest maximum is located in the southeastern half of the disk. The northwestern half has a much lower surface brightness and is approximately twice as extended as the southeastern half of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12915 as the more actively star-forming VV 254 component, with SFR extending toward the overlap region and the highest SFE south of its center.

quotes.quote: High star formation activities are seen in the centre of UGC 12914 (bottom-right) and UGC 12915 (top-left), although UGC 12915 is a more active star-forming galaxy.
Additionally, a region showing high SFR in UGC 12915 is slightly extended toward the overlap region, corresponding to the star-forming region previously reported by Komugi12 .
Unlike SFR, the highest SFE _ mol and SFE _ gas locate at < 10'' to the south from the centre of UGC 12915.

gmw_00003405.name: z=0.77 galaxy
gmw_00003405.overview: The z=0.77 galaxy is an isolated, star-forming system [arXiv:2105.01673, arXiv:2006.00006]. It features an extended, co-rotating disc [arXiv:2105.01673] and a turbulent circumgalactic medium with a galactic-scale outflow [arXiv:2105.01673, arXiv:2006.00006].

sources.summary: The paper studies the isolated z~0.77 galaxy G1 with gravitational-arc tomography, finding Mg II absorption across its CGM, an extended co-rotating disc out to tens of kiloparsecs, turbulent gas, and a likely galactic-scale outflow.

quotes.quote: We present gravitational-arc tomography of the cool-warm enriched circumgalactic medium (CGM) of an isolated galaxy (``G1'') at z 0.77 . Combining VLT/MUSE adaptive-optics and Magellan/MagE echelle spectroscopy we obtain partially-resolved kinematics of Mg ii in absorption and \ in emission. The unique arc configuration allows us to probe 42 spatially independent arc positions transverse to G1, plus 4 positions in front of it. The transverse positions cover G1's minor and major axes at impact parameters of 10-30 kpc and 60 kpc, respectively.

We observe a direct kinematic connection between the cool-warm enriched CGM (traced by Mg ii ) and the interstellar medium (traced by [ O ii ] ). This provides strong evidence for the existence of an extended disc that co-rotates with the galaxy out to tens of kiloparsecs. The Mg ii velocity dispersion ( 30-100 , depending on position) is of the same order as the modeled galaxy rotational velocity ( v_ rot 80 ), providing evidence for the presence of a turbulent and pressure-supported CGM component. We regard the absorption to be modulated by a galactic-scale outflow, as it offers a natural scenario for the observed line-of-sight dispersion and asymmetric profiles observed against both the arcs and the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses CSWA 38 lensed arcs to map the CGM of a z=0.77 star-forming absorber galaxy, finding anisotropic optically thick absorption, weak large-radius outflow signatures, and dispersion-supported metal-enriched gas recycling.

quotes.quote: Using background lensed arcs from the CSWA 38 lens system, we continuously probed, at a resolution element of about 15 kpc ^2 , the spatial and kinematic distribution of absorption in a star-forming galaxy at z=0.77 (stellar mass 10^ 9.7 , star formation rate 10 ) at impact parameters black D 5 -30 kpc . Our results present an anisotropic, optically thick medium whose absorption strength decreases with increasing impact parameter, in agreement with the statistics towards quasars and other gravitational arcs. black Furthermore, we find generally low line-of-sight velocities in comparison to the relatively high velocity dispersion in the gas (with typical 50 ). While the galaxy itself exhibits a clear outflow (with velocities up to 500 ) in the down-the-barrel spectrum, the outflow component is sub-dominant and only weakly detected at larger impact parameters probed by the background arcs. Our results provide evidence of mainly black dispersion-supported , metal-enriched gas recycling through the CGM.

gmw_00003406.name: Coma Berenices
gmw_00003406.overview: Coma Berenices is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 44 kpc from the Sun [arXiv:2209.15590, arXiv:2404.04881]. It is characterized by a high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:2209.15590] and experiences negligible tidal disruption from the Milky Way [arXiv:2209.15590, arXiv:2404.04881].

sources.summary: The paper targets the Coma Berenices dwarf spheroidal with FAST, finds no significant radio continuum emission, and uses its dark halo properties to constrain dark matter annihilation.

quotes.quote: We point the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope towards Coma Berenices, a dwarf Spheroidal galaxy, for 2-hours of observation, and we find no significant continuum radio emission, which could be attributed to dark matter annihilation, from our target. We set very stringent annihilation cross-section constraints, with roughly an order of magnitude improvement over a large range of masses compared with previous radio searches.

Coma Berenices was discovered in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey  , located at a distance of about 44 kpc from the Sun, centered at RA = 12h26m59s , DEC = +23^ 54'00''   in equatorial coordinates (J2000). Coma Berenices has a high mass-to-light ratio  , making it one of the darkest galaxies known so far, which can be anticipated to have potentially strong DM signals  . In addition, Coma Berenices is almost unaffected by the tidal disruption from the Milky Way  . More importantly, the position of the Coma Berenices falls within the proper zenith angle suitable for the FAST observation  .
In this paper, we use the FAST observation of Coma Berenices to search for the DM annihilation signals.

sources.summary: The paper selects Coma Berenices as a nearby dark dwarf spheroidal target for FAST ALP searches, gives its distance and coordinates, models its dark halo, and reports radio constraints on ALP coupling.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA = 12h26m59s , DEC = +23^ 54'00'' Kalashev:2020hqc in equatorial coordinates (J2000)

quotes.quote: In this paper, we selected the Coma Berenices dwarf galaxy as our target to constrain the properties of ALP, considering its observability with FAST and its proximity to the Earth.

Coma Berenices, which was discovered in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS:2006fpg , located at a distance of about 44 kpc from the Sun, centered at RA = 12h26m59s , DEC = +23^ 54'00'' Kalashev:2020hqc in equatorial coordinates (J2000), is one of the darkest galaxies known so far Simon:2007dq, Battaglia:2022dii and is expected to have a strong DM signal Bonnivard:2015xpq . Coma Berenices also experiences negligible tidal disruption from the Milky Way Munoz:2009hj , implying that its stellar kinematic characteristics are primarily dominated by the gravitational potential of its dark matter halo. In addition, the location of Coma Berenices falls within the proper zenith angles suitable for FAST. Thus, we use the FAST observation of Coma Berenices to search for the ALP signals in this work.

Following the work analyzing stellar kinematic data Bonnivard:2015xpq , we can model the dark halo profile of Coma Berenices as the Einasto profile ()= _ 0 2 r_ s ^ 1 , where = 0.847 , r_ s = 5.14 kpc , and _ 0 = 1.962 GeV cm^ 3 represent the best-fitting values of logarithmic slope, scale radius and normalization, respectively.

gmw_00003407.name: NGC 6000
gmw_00003407.overview: NGC 6000 is a nearby barred spiral starburst galaxy [arXiv:1003.5634] with a large-scale bar [arXiv:1003.5634, arXiv:1501.07178] and circumnuclear star-forming rings [arXiv:1003.5634]. Its bulge displays peculiar chemical properties [arXiv:1501.07178], and its radio emission is best fit by a single star-forming region [arXiv:2207.08929].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6000 as a nearby barred spiral starburst; its first high-resolution molecular study finds a large-scale bar and centrally concentrated gas.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We accurately determine the dynamical center of NGC 6000 at _ J2000.0 =15^h49^m49 5 and _ J2000.0 =-29 23 13'' which agrees with the peak of molecular emission position.

quotes.quote: NGC 6000 is a nearby D 31.6 Mpc, pizzella05 barred spiral starburst galaxy. Despite its proximity and brightness, this galaxy has not been studied in as great detail as others of similar distance and luminosity, due to its southern declination. Hubble Space Telescope observations reveal several bright sources in circumnuclear star-forming rings and a large-scale ( 1 kpc) bar carollo97, carollo02, fathi03 .

In this paper we present observations of the carbon monoxide emission in the J=2-1 transition of the three brighter isotopologues ( ^ 12 CO , ^ 13 CO , C^ 18 O ) towards NGC 6000 using the Submillimeter Array SMA; ho04 . This is the first high resolution morphological study of the molecular component towards NGC 6000.

This is the case of NGC 6000 where most of the gas is located in the inner half kiloparsec (Sect. sec.COmass ). The large-scale bar revealed in the NIR may also have a fingerprint in the molecular gas kinematics described in this work.

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO-LV 4500200 has a modeled bar and peculiar bulge chemistry, probably tied to a central decoupled component and difficult to explain without recent acquisition or merging.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, the bulge of ESO-LV 4500200 shows peculiar chemical properties possibly due to the presence of a central kinematically-decoupled component. The negative metallicity gradient found in our bulges sample indicates a relevant role for the dissipative collapse in bulge formation. However, the shallow gradients found for the age and / Fe ratio suggests that merging can not be completely ruled out for the sample bulges. This is confirmed by the properties of ESO-LV 4500200 which can hardly be explained without invoking the capture of external material.

A bar was detected and modelled in ESO-LV 4460170 and ESO-LV 4500200, whereas no bar was needed to improve the surface-brightness models of ESO-LV 1890070 and ESO-LV 5140100. The bump in the surface-brightness profile associated to both a significant peak in the ellipticity and a roughly constant position angle is the photometric signature of the bar in ESO-LV 4460170 and ESO-LV 4500200 (see aguetal09 for a detailed discussion).

On the contrary, the values of \ and \ of ESO-LV 4500200 in the correspond to 0.25 , which is remarkably lower than the average / Fe ratio found for similar galaxies (Figure fig:hist_ama ). Furthermore, the kinematical and chemical properties of this galaxy place it in a region of the \ diagram which is not covered by stellar population models. This does prevented us to derive the age and metallicity of the bulge of ESO-LV 4500200 and we did not considered it in the analysis of the gradients of the stellar population properties.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6000 as a starburst galaxy whose radio SED is best fit by a single star-forming region without low-frequency turnover, with derived synchrotron slope and low AGN fraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 6000 : classified as a starburst galaxy Carollo02 , its radio SED covering 1.6 decades in frequency (70 MHz-3.0 GHz) is best-fitted with emission from a single star-forming region without a low frequency turnover. The synchrotron spectral index is 0.66. The TF is 0.3 . The CIGALE-estimated SFR, L_ dust , and AGN fraction are 2.04 M_ yr ^ 1 , 10 ^ 10.79 L_ , 1.06 , respectively.

gmw_00003408.name: J0119-935
gmw_00003408.overview: The galaxy J0119-935 exhibits a single-peaked, boomerang-shaped neutral hydrogen disk structure that is roughly aligned with its optical cometary emission [arXiv:1501.06709]. However, the center of this gas emission is offset from both the optical disk center and a bright star-forming knot [arXiv:1501.06709].

sources.summary: J0119-935 is described with boomerang-shaped H I, offset gas and optical centers, a central velocity gradient following the morphology, and distorted outskirts.

quotes.quote: J0119-935 From Ekta Chengalur (2010; their Fig. 3 and 4), with the GMRT. The lower resolution (46 x 32 ) H 1 map shows a single-peaked "boomerang-type" Northeast-Southwest disk structure (PA _ HI 120 ^ ), with an extension to the South and to the North, the latter which connects to a clump of emission to the Northwest. The orientation of the H 1 disk is roughly coincident with the position angle of the optical cometary emission (PA _ opt 115 ^ ). However, the center of the H 1 emission does not coincide with either the center of the optical disk, nor the bright star formation knot that leads the cometary structure in the Northeast ( D 10 ). In the central regions, the H 1 velocity gradient (PA _ VG 120 ^ ) follows the H 1 and optical morphology. However, in the outskirts, the H 1 velocity field displays distortions, with the mimicking of the "boomerang" shape observed in the H 1 morphology.

gmw_00003409.name: HS 0822+03542
gmw_00003409.overview: HS 0822+03542 is an extremely metal-poor galaxy [arXiv:2111.11508] that features a single-peaked, Northwest-Southeast disk [arXiv:1501.06709]. It is paired with a nearby low-surface-brightness companion galaxy and exhibits a distorted neutral hydrogen velocity field [arXiv:1501.06709].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J082555 as the lowest gas-metallicity emitter, with deep G160M follow-up and low-metallicity stellar constraints in the nebular C IV sample.

quotes.quote: In particular, we selected our sample to include the two highest equivalent width nebular emitters in this doublet J104457 at 11 and HS 1442+4250 at 4 ; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 ; two of the systems with the highest gas-phase metallicities still powering nebular emission in C 4 SB 2 and 82, at 12+ O/H 7.8 - 7.9 ; senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 ; and two metal-poor systems with extremely high equivalent width optical line emission yet relatively modest C 4 equivalent widths <4 J082555 and J120202; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,bergChemicalEvolutionCarbon2019 . These systems were targeted with G160M/1533 spectroscopy with allocations of 5--10 orbits each over the course of two programs (GO:15646 and GO:15881, PI: Stark) which we describe in Section sec:uvobs . The basic properties of this sample of emitters are summarized in Table tab:basicprop , and described in the remainder of this section.

The resulting direct- T_e oxygen abundances (Table tab:basicprop ) confirm that these emitters harbor very metal-poor gas. The values range from 12+ O/H =7.44 in the lowest-metallicity case (J082555) up to 7.93 in the highest (SB 82). This corresponds to a range of 5--15 the solar photospheric oxygen value, with all but SB 2 and SB 82 falling below 10 and thus in the realm of designated extremely metal-poor galaxies (XMPs).

In particular, J082555 and J120202 were previously only observed with the low-resolution COS/G140L grating in the FUV which affords nearly an order of magnitude lower spectral resolution than G160M, precluding clear disentanglement of the nebular emission profile from interstellar absorption. In addition, the broad ( >1000 km/s) and metallicity-dependent stellar wind contribution to this complex was generally too weak to be confidently detected in the single-orbit spectra obtained previously for this metal-poor galaxy sample though see senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 , leaving us with minimal information about the massive stars underlying this emission.

sources.summary: HS 0822+03542 is shown with a nearby low-surface-brightness companion, single-peaked extended H I, a distorted velocity field, and a higher-resolution disk plus clump.

quotes.quote: HS 0822+03542 From Chengalur et al. (2006; their Fig. 1, 4 and 5), with the GMRT. The low resolution (42 x 37 ) map shows the XMP target along with its pair to the Northeast, the low surface brightness galaxy SAO 0822+545, at a distance of D 11 kpc and a position angle of PA 125 ^ . At this resolution, the H 1 gas is single-peaked, with an extension to the East-Northeast. The velocity field (27 x 23 ) displays a distortion, with the velocity gradient Northwest-Southeast (PA _ VG 70 ^ ) near the center, aligned with the H 1 morphology (PA _ HI 70 ^ ), and approximately East-West (PA _ VG 170 ^ ), roughly perpendicular to the H 1 , towards the Southeast. Intermediate and high resolution (17 x 15 and 13 x 11 ) images show a single-peaked, Northwest-Southeast disk (PA _ HI 70 ^ ), with an extension to the Northwest-West, and a clump of emission to the Northeast, the latter tracing the extension seen at lower resolution. The extension to the Northwest-West appears to mimic the position angle of the optical cometary tail (PA _ opt 45 ^ ).

gmw_00003410.name: SBS 1129+576
gmw_00003410.overview: SBS 1129+576 is an extremely metal-poor, tadpole-like galaxy containing a star-forming region at the end of an extended light distribution [arXiv:1904.01615, arXiv:1909.10574]. It is interacting with its companion SBS 1129+577, as evidenced by a connecting neutral hydrogen bridge and a common envelope [arXiv:1501.06709].

sources.summary: SBS 1129+576 is shown with nearby companions and an H I bridge/common envelope, small tails, aligned H I and velocity-gradient position angles, and ordered central rotation.

quotes.quote: SBS 1129+576 From Ekta, Pustilnik Chengalur (2003; their Fig. 1, 3 and 6), with the GMRT. The low resolution (42 x 40 ) H 1 map shows the XMP target, along with its companion galaxy, SBS 1129+577, D 27 kpc to the North, and the dwarf galaxy SDSS J113227.68+572142 to the Southeast. The image signals the interaction of the SBS 1129+567/577 pair, by way of a H 1 bridge connecting both galaxies, and a common H 1 envelope. There is also a small tail of emission to the Northeast and Southwest of the XMP. At this resolution, the position angle of the H 1 emission associated with the XMP target is PA _ 75 ^ , which is roughly coincident with the direction of the velocity gradient (PA _ VG 70 ^ ). The velocity field is fairly ordered (solid-body rotation) in the central region and also in the bridge, consistent with a prograde merger.

sources.summary: The paper places SBSG1129+576 in an extended light distribution, measuring a large stellar mass and H equivalent width, narrow He emission, weak C constraints, and no P-Cygni absorption.

quotes.quote: SBSG1129+576: The targeted star-forming region in this galaxy is situated at the end of an extended light distribution, and contains a relatively large stellar mass of 10^ 6.1 0.2 M_ with H equivalent width 69 2 . The data reveal narrow 1640 emission at 1.14 0.09 and ] 1666 at 0.95 0.05 , but a nondetection of ] ( <3.5 ) and no detection of P-Cygni absorption.

All have dust-corrected 1640 fluxes comparable to or smaller than ] 1666 , with only one system (SBSG1129+576) presenting slightly stronger flux. While the ] / ratios are only consistent with star-forming models, the / upper limits for SBSG1129+576 and UM133 place them at the bottom limit of the star-forming models presented.

In contrast, the 1640 emission in SBSG1129+576 is fit by a single component with a substantially larger FWHM of 253 21 km/s, which indicates it likely arises in a stellar wind rather than ionized interstellar gas.

sources.summary: The paper presents SBSG1129+576 as a tadpole-like extremely metal-poor galaxy with no accepted nebular detection but a coincident X-ray source.

quotes.quote: SBSG1129+576 is a tadpole-like XMP at 25 Mpc dominated by a star-forming clump targeted with SDSS and Keck/ESI spectroscopy senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 . These spectra reveal a very low oxygen abundance 12+ O/H =7.47 0.06 and an SFR of 10^ 2.5 M_ /year , but a nondetection of nebular , /H <0.004 low signal-to-noise positive flux is visible at in the Keck/ESI spectrum, but with FWHM significantly larger than the strong nebular lines, so we conclude it is stellar in origin: senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 . An X-ray source is also detected in this galaxy with (ObsID: 11283, PI: Prestwich), at a luminosity of =11 2 38 erg/s and at 0.8'' separation from the center of the clump observed with ESI (within our adopted offset tolerance). This source was also identified by brorbyXrayBinaryFormation2014 at a luminosity in good agreement with our measurement. This measurement yields an X-ray production efficiency of 3.0 0.7 41 erg/s per M_ /year .

gmw_00003411.name: J2104-0035
gmw_00003411.overview: J2104-0035 is a cometary blue compact dwarf galaxy dominated by two bright star-forming knots [arXiv:0809.1772]. Its neutral hydrogen distribution is disturbed, displaying an inverted-triangular morphology with several extensions, a southeastern tail, and asymmetric outer rotation [arXiv:0809.1772, arXiv:1501.06709].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J2104--0035 as a cometary blue compact dwarf with extended disturbed H I, asymmetric velocity structure, and possible interaction or advanced merger origin.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2104--0035 The g band SDSS image of J2104--0035 is shown in Fig.  fig:27arcj2104hi . The optical emission is dominated by two bright star-forming knots, one at the northern edge of the galaxy and the other near the centre. note that its appearance is similar to that of `cometary' blue compact dwarf galaxies (e.g., Mkn 59, Mkn 71 ( )), which have a bright star-forming region at one end of an elongated stellar body.

The \ distribution instead looks disturbed with extensions to the south and east. From the synthetic \ spectrum (Fig.  fig:specj2104 ), we measure an \ flux of 1.5   0.15 Jy  . Overlaid on this spectrum is the spectrum obtained at Parkes, for which the integrated flux is 2.0   0.2 Jy  .

sources.summary: J2104-0035 has inverted-triangular H I with extensions and a tail, an H I peak offset from the bright knot, aligned gas/velocity gradients, and asymmetric outer rotation.

quotes.quote: J2104-0035 From Ekta, Chengalur Pustilnik (2008; their Fig. 5 and 7), with the GMRT. The low resolution (29 x 25 ) H 1 map shows a single-peaked inverted "triangular" morphology, with several small extensions to the North and a tail to the Southeast. The H 1 peak is not coincident with the bright star formation knot at the Northern edge of the cometary structure, but instead is coincident with the Southern edge of the optical disk. The overall H 1 morphology (PA _ HI 90 ^ ) is slightly misaligned from the optical structure (PA _ opt 110 ^ ), but aligned with the velocity gradient (PA _ VG 90 ^ ). The velocity field displays ordered (rotation) in the central part, but a North-South asymmetry is present in the outskirts. The intermediate and high resolution (17 x 11 ) map reveals "Y-shaped" emission, with extensions to the South, North and Northwest.

gmw_00003412.name: FCC 47
gmw_00003412.overview: FCC 47 is an early-type galaxy located in the outskirts of the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1908.00774, arXiv:1904.01041]. It features a complex structure shaped by a major merger, including two kinematically decoupled components [arXiv:1907.01007, arXiv:2005.12085], a very massive nuclear star cluster [arXiv:2304.13310, arXiv:2104.06412], and a rich globular cluster system [arXiv:1908.00774, arXiv:2201.06704].

sources.summary: FCC 47-UCD1 is a close, old, metal-poor UCD whose size, mass, velocity, and stellar population are consistent with either a massive star cluster or a stripped dwarf nucleus.

quotes.quote: We use archival Hubble Space Telescope data to study the photometric and structural properties of FCC 47-UCD1. In the MUSE data, the UCD is unresolved, but we use its spectrum to determine the radial velocity and metallicity. FCC 47-UCD1's surface brightness is best described by a single King profile with low concentration C = R_ t /R_ 10 and large effective radius ( r_ eff = 24 pc). Its integrated magnitude and a blue colour ( M_g = 10.55 mag, (g - z) = 1.46 mag) combined with with a metallicity of [ M/H ] = 1.12 0.10 dex and an age > 8 Gyr obtained from the full fitting of the MUSE spectrum suggests a stellar population mass of M_ = 4.87 10^6 M_ .

The UCD has a relative velocity of (65.3 4.9) km s ^ 1 compared to the systemic velocity of FCC 47 (1444.4 2.0 km s ^ 1 , Fahrion et. al. in prep). The relative radial velocity of the UCD reveals that it is indeed related to FCC 47 because the velocity dispersion of the stars in FCC 47 is 80 km s ^ 1 at the position of the UCD (Fahrion et. al. in prep).

The origin of the UCD cannot be constrained with certainty. The low metallicity, old age and magnitude are consistent with a star cluster origin, whereas the extended size and high mass are consistent with an origin as the stripped nucleus of a dwarf galaxy with a initial stellar mass of a few 10^8 M_ .

sources.summary: FCC 47 is presented as an early-type Fornax galaxy with a large NSC and rich globular-cluster system; MUSE data reveal decoupled kinematics and inform NSC formation scenarios.

quotes.quote: The early-type galaxy FCC 47 (NGC 1336) in the outskirts of the Fornax galaxy cluster at a distance of 18.3 Mpc ( ) is an excellent laboratory to study NSC formation with MUSE because of its rich GC system with 250 members ( , ) and its known large NSC with R_ eff = 0.7^ = 66 pc that allows to resolve the NSC spatially. We presented a detailed study of FCC 47, its NSCs, and the GC system with MUSE in . Here we want to highlight the key aspects of our analysis and their implications. We used science verification (SV) adaptive optics (AO) optimised MUSE data of FCC 47 taken in the wide field mode (WFM) to gain insights into the kinematics and stellar population properties of FCC 47's stellar body, the NSC, and the GCs.

The LOS velocity field of FCC 47 (left panel in Fig. fig:map_with_gcs ) shows two kinematically decoupled components (KDCs): the NSC and a rotating disk with a maximum rotation amplitude of 20 km s ^ 1 . The metallicity map shows a confined peak at the location of the NSC and then a steep gradient to lower metallicities. The NSC reaches the oldest ages ( 13 Gyr) while the outskirts of the galaxy are younger ( 8 Gyr, see Fig.

We interpret the results from our analysis of the MUSE + AO WFM SV data of FCC 47 in the context of NSC formation, focusing on the two most discussed scenarios: the (dry) GC accretion scenario and the in-situ formation. GC accretion scenario . N body simulations have shown that the angular moment of FCC 47's NSC can not be reached with the random infall of GCs, but requires a preferred orbital infall direction (see and for details).

sources.summary: The paper models FCC 47 as a nucleated early-type Fornax galaxy, measuring its SMBH while accounting for NSC kinematics, stellar mass-to-light gradients, and IMF variation.

quotes.quote: In this publication, we add a new galaxy to the SMBH-NSC compilations. FCC 47 (NGC 1336) is an early-type galaxy (ETG) located in the Fornax cluster at 20 Mpc. We list the basic properties of FCC 47 in Table properties . FCC 47 was included in the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) Fornax Cluster Survey ACSFCS; Jordan2007 and its NSC was evident from photometric decomposition by . derived the effective radius of the NSC to be r_ eff,NSC = 0.75 0.13 in the F475W filter, which corresponds to 66.5 pc at a distance of 18.3 Mpc. Using integral-field spectroscopy data from the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE), found that the NSC has a mass of about 7 10^ 8 M _ .

Compared to the mass of the host galaxy ( M_ *,gal 10^ 10 M _ ), this NSC is very over-massive. The NSC also appears very pronounced in kinematic data due to its strong rotation Lyubenova2019, Fahrion2019 and a comparison to the rotation of the main galaxy body reveals that the NSC is a kinematically decoupled component with a rotation axis almost orthogonal to the short axis of the host galaxy. From its star formation history Fahrion2019 , we know that the NSC in FCC 47 is very old ( > 13 Gyr) compared to the host galaxy (about 8 Gyr).

sources.summary: The paper uses FCC 47 as an example GC system with bimodal colors and negative red and blue globular-cluster color gradients toward larger radii.

quotes.quote: The right panels of Figure fig:cp show the color distributions for GCs in FCC 47 (NGC 1336, panel ) and FCC 153 (IC 335) panel d ). The GC color distribution of FCC 47 displays two peaks, while the GCs in FCC 153 has just one peak in color. The blue and red curves in panel are Gaussian fits to the blue and red GCs determined by KMM.

The left panels of Figure fig:cp show color profiles of GC systems in FCC 47 (panel a ) and FCC 153 (panel b ). We can see from the figure that both red and blue GC systems in FCC 47 show negative color gradients with color becoming gradually bluer from the center to the outskirts of the galaxy. The gradients of the whole GC system is steeper than that of each subpopulation due to the dominance of blue GCs at large radii.

sources.summary: FCC 47 is the low-mass early-type host of a newly found nearby UCD; the paper places it in Fornax, describes its MUSE/GC context, and contrasts its metallicity and dynamics with the UCD.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report the serendipitous discovery of a UCD in close projected distance ( _ proj = 1.1 kpc) to the low-mass early-type galaxy FCC 47 (NGC 1336) in Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) adaptive optics (AO) science verification (SV) data. For the photometric analysis, we use archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) data taken with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS).

With a projected distance of 780 kpc to NGC 1399, FCC 47 itself lies in the outskirts of the Fornax cluster, but was covered by photometric surveys of the cluster such as the HST ACS Fornax Cluster survey (ACSFCS, Jordan2007 ) and the Fornax Deep Survey (FDS, Iodice2016 ). However, the UCD in FCC 47 (hereafter FCC 47-UCD1) has not been discovered in these surveys, probably because their method of selecting classical GCs missed this UCD due to its large size and extended nature. Table tab:pointing lists some basic information about FCC 47.

The observing program was designed to study the NSC of FCC 47 in combination with FCC 47's rich GC system Jordan2015 , which will be presented in a forthcoming paper (Fahrion et. al. in prep). The simultaneous observation of the UCD in the MUSE FOV was purely coincidental. The MUSE data of FCC 47 consist of ten exposures of 360 seconds each.

sources.summary: The paper studies FCC 47 as an early-type Fornax galaxy with a massive nuclear star cluster, counter-rotating components, metallicity structure, and evidence for merger-driven kinematics.

quotes.quote: The large NSC ( _ eff = 66 pc) and rich GC system of the early-type Fornax cluster galaxy FCC 47 (NGC 1336) render this galaxy an ideal laboratory to constrain NSC formation. Using MUSE science verification data assisted by adaptive optics, we obtained maps for the stellar kinematics and for stellar-population properties of FCC 47. FCC 47 shows two decoupled components (KDCs): a rotating disk and the NSC. The main body consists of two counter-rotating populations and is dominated by a more metal-poor population.

The presence of two KDCs most probably are evidence of a major merger that has altered the structure of FCC 47 significantly, indicating the important role of galaxy mergers in forming the complex kinematics in the galaxy-NSC system. galaxies: individual: NGC 1336 -- galaxies: nuclei -- galaxies: kinematics and dynamics -- galaxies: star clusters: general The nuclei of galaxies are extreme environments located at the bottom of the galactic potential well, where numerous complex astrophysical processes take place and various phenomena occur in such as active galactic nuclei caused by accretion onto supermassive black holes (SMBHs), central star bursts and extreme stellar densities. We summarize our results as follows: We found that FCC 47 shows a complex velocity structure with two KDCs on different scales.

sources.summary: The paper places FCC 47 among the most massive sample galaxies, with a very massive, old, metal-rich nuclear star cluster interpreted through rapid early central enrichment and efficient formation.

quotes.quote: Most massive galaxies The most massive galaxies in our sample ( M_ gal > 10^ 10 M_ ), FCC 47, FCC 170, and FCC 193, also host the most massive NSCs ( > 10^8 M_ ). Their NSCs are significantly more metal-rich than the hosts and any GCs, and are older than the host galaxies. This could be explained by an inside-out formation scenario, where the central regions of the galaxies assembled first and were rapidly enriched due to efficient star formation, while enrichment in the outskirts was slower and less efficient.

In , we argued that the old age and high metallicity of the NSC in FCC 47 indicates an efficient formation at early times. Similar arguments were made in the detailed analysis of FCC 170 by using the same F3D MUSE data which showed that FCC 170 assembled very rapidly and efficiently at early times (see also Poci2021 ). Their SFH of the central region of FCC 170 is similar to the one presented here.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC1336 as a high-specific-frequency Fornax galaxy with merger evidence, dominated by two large GC structures including a complex hook-shaped J3 feature.

quotes.quote: Recent studies have found that NGC1336 possesses two kinematically decoupled components that indicate that this galaxy has undergone a major merger fahrion2019 . Moreover, the GCS of NGC1336 has a very high specific frequency liu2019 , second only to NGC1399 in Fornax, that can be explained by lack of significant GCs disruption favored by the isolation from the other members of the Fornax clusters, possibly suggesting that NGC1336 is infalling in the cluster and has not interacted with other members of cluster yet. The spatial distribution of GCs in NGC1336 (Figure fig:ngc1336 ) is dominated by two large structures (J1 and J3) that, together, occupy a significant fraction of the area of the host galaxy within the D _ 25 elliptical isophote. J1 is located at the S intersection of the major axis with the D _ 25 and extends towards large galactocentric distances. J3 is a geometrically complex, hook-shaped structure containing multiple overdensity peaks that spans a large range of radii and is likely the result of the chance projection of multiple structures.

sources.summary: NGC 1336 has a nearly flat velocity-dispersion profile, symmetric wave-like rotation, strong inner isophotal twist, and bar-indicative kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1336 The VDP dips 5 km s ^ 1 over the central 2 and contains additional oscillations at larger radii, having amplitudes of 10 km s ^ 1 . Smoothing these out, the VDP shows negligible signs of decreasing until a radius of 18 , remaining roughly flat at 100 km s ^ 1 . The RC is symmetric, being approximately flat over the inner 5 , where a strong isophotal twist is known to take place (up to 60 ^ ; Caon et al. \ 1994). Beyond this radius, the RC displays a wave-like appearance about a zero mean rotational velocity, with an amplitude of 20 km s ^ 1 . Such kinematics is indicative of the presence of a bar (Sparke Sellwood 1987; Bettoni 1989, and references therein).

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 47 as the nearest sample galaxy with the most extended nuclear star cluster, strong internal rotation, a central dispersion peak, and kinematics matching one GC-merger simulation.

quotes.quote: FCC 47 is the nearest galaxy in our sample and its NSC is most extended on the sky. The velocity map reveals a strong rotation (up to 70 ) inside the NSC's R_ eff and a pronounced peak reaching 150 in the centre, indicative of the presence of a supermassive black hole.

The nucleus of FCC 47, which is the most spatially extended and with the best resolved stellar kinematics available, overlaps with the contaminated Simulation B (blue symbols, GCs have fallen from a similar orbital direction). The detailed formation history of this NSC, including its stellar populations, are a subject of a forthcoming paper (Fahrion et al., in preparation ).

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 1336 as relatively isolated from the Fornax core, with high globular-cluster specific frequency and evidence for a merger-altered structure.

quotes.quote: The distinctiveness of NGC 1336 is also discussed by liu19 , who find that it has the second highest GC specific frequency, after NGC 1399, and the largest 3D clustercentric distance in the ACSFCS sample. The relative isolation of the galaxy from the Fornax core, at 2 times of the cluster virial radius, also supported by the lack of GC streams toward the core, might strengthen the hypothesis by liu19 that it ''is an infalling central galaxy with a higher total mass-to-light ratio, resembling the behavior of the most massive ETGs. Its GC system has possibly experienced fewer external disruption processes, and the GCs may have a higher survival efficiency.'' The presence of two kinematically decoupled cores fahrion19b , most probably evidencing a major merger that has altered the structure of NGC 1336 significantly, might further support such hypothesis.

gmw_00003413.name: Mayall II = G1
gmw_00003413.overview: Mayall II = G1 is one of the most luminous globular clusters known in the M31 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702012]. Because of its structural parameters and intrinsic metallicity dispersion, it is hypothesized to be the stripped core of a former dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702012].

sources.summary: The paper remeasures Mayall II = G1 structural parameters from HST/ACS imaging and argues its updated half-light radius and metallicity spread strengthen the case that it is a stripped core of a former dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: Mayall II = G1 is one of the most luminous globular clusters (GCs) known in M31. New deep, high-resolution observations with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on the Hubble Space Telescope are used to provide accurate photometric data to the smallest radii yet. In particular, we present the precise variation of ellipticity and position angle, and of surface brightness for the core of the object. Based on these accurate photometric data, we redetermine the structural parameters of G1 by fitting a single-mass isotropic King model.

With the updated value of R_h for G1 from this paper, we present a plot of M_V vs. R_h in Fig. 4, in which M_V was taken from meylan01 . It is evident that, with the updated R_h , G1 is still seen to lie above and brightward of the line defined by Eq. (1) mackey05 . Combined with the results of meylan01 that there exists an intrinsic metallicity dispersion amongst the stars of G1, this strengthens the conclusion that G1 may be the stripped core of a former dwarf galaxy see details from meylan01,mackey05 .

gmw_00003414.name: Hydra I
gmw_00003414.overview: Hydra I is a low-luminosity system with an intermediate-age, metal-rich stellar population [arXiv:1509.06391]. Its chemical abundance spreads and stellar properties suggest it is a tidally disrupting dwarf galaxy that has lost over 99.99 percent of its stellar mass [arXiv:1509.06391].

sources.summary: The paper argues Hydra I is likely a tidally disrupting dwarf galaxy: its intermediate-age, metal-rich population, abundance spreads, low luminosity, and inferred extreme stellar mass loss favor a Fornax-like dwarf progenitor over a star cluster.

quotes.quote: We combine new wide-field DECam imaging and MMT/Hectochelle spectroscopic observations of Hydra I with SDSS archival spectroscopic observations to quantify Hydra I's present-day chemodynamical properties, and to infer whether it originated as a star cluster or dwarf galaxy. While previous work using shallow SDSS imaging assumed a standard old, metal-poor stellar population, our deeper DECam imaging reveals that Hydra I has a thin, well-defined main sequence turnoff of intermediate age ( 5-6 Gyr) and metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] = 0.9 dex). We measure statistically significant spreads in both the iron and alpha-element abundances of _ [ Fe/H ] = 0.13 0.02 dex and _ [ / Fe ] = 0.09 0.03 dex, respectively, and place upper limits on both the rotation and its proper motion. Hydra I's intermediate age and [ Fe/H ] - as well as its low [ /Fe ] , apparent [ Fe/H ] spread, and present-day low luminosity -- suggest that its progenitor was a dwarf galaxy, which has subsequently lost more than 99.99 of its stellar mass.

Conversely, Hydra I's stellar populations, along with its spread in [ Fe/H ] , is consistent with those of a Fornax-like dwarf galaxy progenitor. If Hydra I's progenitor was a Fornax-like dwarf galaxy, then it must have lost >99.99 of its stars to bear the low luminosity it displays today. Hydra I may thus be the first example of a system truly in its final throes of disruption, making it an excellent benchmark of tidal disruption models. Although evidence strongly points towards a dwarf galaxy interpretation of Hydra I, we cannot rule out the hypothesis that Hydra I originated as a star cluster or that it is physically associated with the Monoceros Ring.

gmw_00003415.name: PSZ1-ARC G311.6602--18.4624
gmw_00003415.overview: PSZ1-ARC G311.6602--18.4624, also known as the Sunburst Arc, is a strongly lensed, star-forming galaxy at redshift z=2.37 [arXiv:1904.08186, arXiv:2106.10286]. It hosts a young, massive starburst cluster that leaks ionizing Lyman-continuum radiation through a narrow channel in its gas [arXiv:1904.07941, arXiv:1904.08186].

sources.summary: The Sunburst Arc shows strong young-population wind features, broad emission consistent with a 3 Myr Wolf-Rayet model, and one of the stronger inferred ionizing continua.

quotes.quote: In contrast, the normalized flux of the \ profile from the Sunburst Arc varies by a factor of 2.5 from the absorption depth to the emission peak, illustrating the strong \ P-Cygni profiles in populations with very young light-weighted ages. This trough-to-peak ratio decreases with increasing inferred stellar population age in fig:nv : it is 2.2 for S0033+0242 with an age of 5 Myr and 1.4 for S1429-1202 with an age of 15 Myr. The Si 4 P-Cygni feature P-Cygni is generally observed in supergiants (evolved stars) or in metal-rich stars because the dominant ionization state in main-sequence stellar winds is the Si ^ 4+ state.

The \ emission equivalent width from the Sunburst Arc spectrum is 1.2 \ and it has a FWHM = 379 , consistent with a 3 Myr, 0.5 Z _ Wolf-Rayet model schaerer98 . The presence of a broad \ emission profile strongly suggests that the spectrum is dominated by a <5 Myr stellar population.

In fig:ion , we show the inferred ionizing continua of four galaxies that span the full age range of the sample. Focusing on only the \ fits (blue lines), the general trend found in fig:ionizing_mods is reproduced: the inferred ionizing continua at 900 \ decreases with fitted stellar age from RCS Knot E (top panel; 2 Myr), to the Sunburst Arc (top-middle panel; 3 Myr), to S0108+0624 (bottom-middle panel; 7 Myr), and finally S1226+2152 (bottom panel; 26 Myr). The galaxies with the strongest inferred ionizing continua are the youngest galaxies with the strongest stellar wind profiles ( features ).

sources.summary: The Sunburst arc is a strongly lensed z=2.37 LyC emitter whose compact LyC-knot is inferred to have high stellar mass density and may be a gravitationally bound young massive cluster.

quotes.quote: We present a VLT/X-Shooter spectroscopy of the Lyman continuum (LyC) emitting galaxy Ion2 at z=3.2121 and compare it to that of the recently discovered strongly lensed LyC emitter at z=2.37, known as the Sunburst arc. Similarly, the Sunburst LyC emitter shows a triple peaked \ profile and from VLT/MUSE spectroscopy the presence of spectral features arising from young hot and massive stars. The strong lensing magnification, ( > 20 ), suggests that this exceptional object is a gravitationally bound star cluster observed at a cosmological distance, with a stellar mass M 10^7 \ and an effective radius smaller than 20 pc.

As shown in Figure mass , an effective radius ( R_e ) smaller than 20 pc is found if _ TOT > 25 , and it decreases below 9 pc if _ TOT > 50 . The stellar mass ranges between 10^ 6 10^ 7 \ depending on the IMF and magnification, with a ^ that enters the regime of the densest objects known (e.g., the globular clusters, e.g., hopkins10 ) or similar to the values of young massive star clusters bastian06,ostlin07,bastian13 . Combining R_e with the age of 3.0 Myr, and the stellar mass, we can infer the dynamical age as = Age/ T _ cr where T _ cr is the crossing time: 10( R_e^ 3 /GM) ^ 0.5 , gieles11 . Interestingly, as highlighted in Figure mass , the system enters the regime of a gravitationally bound object ( > 1 ) if _ TOT >25(50) in the case of Salpeter(top-heavy) IMF.

sources.summary: The paper describes PSZ1-ARC G311.6602--18.4624 as the lensed Sunburst Arc, a young star-forming z=2.37 galaxy where HST reveals compact, multiply imaged Lyman-continuum escape through a narrow channel.

quotes.quote: We present Hubble Space Telescope observations of the gravitationally lensed galaxy PSZ1-ARC G311.6602--18.4624, revealing bright, multiply-imaged ionizing photon escape from a compact star-forming region through a narrow channel in an optically thick gas. The gravitational lensing magnification shows how ionizing photons escape this galaxy, contributing to the re-ionisation of the Universe. The multiple sight lines to the source probe absorption by intergalactic neutral hydrogen on scales of no more than a few hundred, perhaps even less than ten, parsec.

Previous work has observed such a triple-peaked Lyman- profile in PSZ1-ARC G311.6602--18.4624, hereafter nicknamed the Sunburst Arc , a gravitationally lensed galaxy at redshift z=2.37 . The Sunburst is a single galaxy, lensed into at least 12 images by a massive foreground galaxy cluster at z=0.44 . The galaxy is young, strongly star forming, and shows no sign of an active nucleus (see fig. S1). The Lyman- profile indicated that it was a prime candidate for strong LyC escape through a narrow channel oriented towards Earth .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the Sunburst Arc as a z=2.37 lensed LyC leaker whose resolved spectra reveal strong ionized outflows and young stellar feedback.

quotes.quote: We analyze spectra of a gravitationally lensed galaxy, known as the Sunburst Arc, that is leaking ionizing photons, also known as the Lyman continuum (LyC). Magnification from gravitational lensing permits the galaxy to be spatially resolved into one region that leaks ionizing photons, and several that do not. Rest-frame ultraviolet and optical spectra from Magellan target ten different regions along the lensed Arc, including six multiple images of the LyC leaking region, as well as four regions that do not show LyC emission. The rest-frame optical spectra of the ionizing photon emitting regions reveal a blue-shifted ( V =27 km s ^ 1 ) broad emission component (FWHM=327 km s ^ 1 ) comprising 55 of the total [ OIII ] line flux, in addition to a narrow component (FWHM = 112 km s ^ 1 ), suggesting the presence of strong highly ionized gas outflows. This is consistent with the high-velocity ionized outflow inferred from the rest-frame UV spectra.

Analyses of stellar wind features reveal that the stellar population of the LyC leaking regions is considerably younger ( 3 Myr) than the non-leaking regions ( 12 Myr), highlighting that stellar feedback from young stars may play an important role in ionizing photon escape.

This point is powerfully demonstrated by the Sunburst Arc at z = 2.37 Dahle2016, Rivera-Thorsen2017, Rivera-Thorsen2019, Chisholm2019 , also known as PSZ1-ARC G311.6602–18.4624, one of the brightest gravitationally lensed galaxies known.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Sunburst Arc as a z=2.369 lensed starburst galaxy hosting a young LyC-leaking super star cluster and multiple lensed images of a compact star-forming clump.

quotes.quote: The Sunburst Arc, a lensed Cosmic Noon galaxy, hosts a young super star cluster with escaping Lyman continuum radiation. Analyzing archival HST images and emission line data from VLT/MUSE and X-shooter, we construct a physical model for the cluster and its surrounding photoionized nebula. We confirm that the cluster is 4 Myr old, is extremely massive M_ 10^7 M_ and yet has a central component as compact as several parsecs, and we find a gas-phase metallicity Z=(0.22 0.03) Z_ .

In this work, we scrutinize a young massive star cluster found in the Sunburst Arc at z_s=2.369 . The lensed galaxy was first discovered by behind the galaxy cluster PSZ1 G311.65-18.48 Planck2014SZClusterCatalog . It is the brightest known gravitationally lensed starburst galaxy at Cosmic Noon. A lensed compact star-forming clump was found on the arc, which shows twelve different lensed avatars arising from an Einstein ring configuration disrupted by secondary lensing caustics cast by multiple cluster member galaxies RiveraThorsen2019Sci, Pignataro2021SunburstLensModel, Diego2022godzilla, Sharon2022SunburstLensModel .

sources.summary: The paper measures direct chemical abundances in the Sunburst Arc, a gravitationally lensed z=2.37 galaxy, finding low metallicity and enhanced nitrogen consistent with Wolf-Rayet enrichment.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the analysis of direct T_e abundances in the Sunburst Arc Dahle16 , a highly magnified gravitationally lensed galaxy at redshift z=2.37 Sharon22sunburst,Diego22,Pignataro21 . We focus on a single physical region of the galaxy that is leaking ionizing photons RivThor19 , which we refer to as the Lyman-continuum emitter (LCE). This region is a compact star cluster Vanzella22 hosting a young stellar population with a light-weighted age of 3.6 Myr Chisholm19,RivThor24 and a steep UV slope 3 Kim23 .

We measure a gas-phase metallicity for the LCE of 12+ ( O / H ) = 7.97 0.05 , and find an enhanced nitrogen abundance ( N / O ) = -0.65^ +0.16 _ 0.25 . This nitrogen abundance is consistent with enrichment from a population of Wolf-Rayet stars, additional signatures of which are reported in a companion paper. Abundances of sulfur, argon, neon, and iron are consistent with local low-metallicity HII regions and low-redshift galaxies. This study represents the most complete chemical abundance analysis of a galaxy at Cosmic Noon to date, which enables direct comparisons between local HII regions and those in the distant universe.

sources.summary: The paper presents UV/optical spectra of the lensed z=2.4 Sunburst Arc and argues its triple-peaked Lyman profile reveals direct Lyman-photon escape through a perforated neutral medium.

quotes.quote: We present rest-frame ultraviolet and optical spectroscopy of the brightest lensed galaxy yet discovered, at redshift z=2.4 . The source reveals a characteristic, triple-peaked Lyman profile which has been predicted in various theoretical works but to our knowledge has not been unambiguously observed previously. The feature is well fit by a superposition of two components: a double-peak profile emerging from substantial radiative transfer, and a narrow, central component resulting from directly escaping photons; but is poorly fit by either component alone.

In this letter, we present rest-frame UV and optical spectroscopy of the lensed galaxy , which we nickname the Sunburst Arc A sunburst being a ``direct view at the sun through rifted clouds''. . We find that it contains strong evidence for direct escape of Lyman photons through a perforated, optically thick medium. The galaxy was serendipitously discovered in follow-up imaging of its lensing cluster, which was discovered through its Sunyaev-Zel'dovich effect in the Planck data PlanckSZ2014 and was first reported and described by .

sources.summary: The paper models the Sunburst Arc source as a lensed z=2.3702 star-forming galaxy whose LyC-leaking arc and compact knots are multiply imaged by cluster caustics.

quotes.quote: Subsequent studies revealed that the source of these arcs at z=2.3702 , dubbed ``Sunburst arc'' system, is very peculiar. presented rest-frame ultraviolet and optical spectrometry of the arc, which shows evidence that the source is a young, star-forming galaxy that leaks Lyman-continuum (Ly-C hereafter) radiation through a perforated neutral medium. Observations with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) show that such radiation originates from a bright, compact object that is multiply imaged twelve times in the Sunburst arc system RiveraT_2019 .

The majority of them are multiple images of compact stellar knots belonging to a single star-forming galaxy at z=2.3702 . This source is strongly lensed by the cluster to form the Sunburst Arc system. To accurately reproduce all the multiple images, we build a parametric mass model, which includes both cluster-scale and galaxy-scale components. The resulting model has a .m.s. separation between the model-predicted and the observed positions of the multiple images of only 0.14 . We conclude that PSZ1 G311.65-18.48 has a relatively round projected shape and a large Einstein radius (29 \ for z_s = 2.3702 ), which could indicate that the cluster is elongated along the line of sight. The Sunburst Arc source is located at the intersection of a complex network of caustics, which explains why parts of the arc are imaged with unprecedented multiplicity (up to 12 times).

sources.summary: The paper models the Sunburst arc as a strongly lensed z=2.37 galaxy with multiple ionizing knots, using lensing constraints to interpret the transient candidate Tr.

quotes.quote: Optical follow up of this cluster from the ESO revealed a strongly lensed bright galaxy at redshift z_s=2.3702 Dahle2016 . The lensed galaxy formed a giant arc with nearly circular symmetry, suggesting a roundish morphology (in projection) for the cluster mass. Space HST images and detailed spectroscopy from the ground have allowed for the identification of a powerful ionizing source at the giant arc.

Because of this fact, the giant arc was dubbed the Sunburst arc. The hypothesis of escaping ionizing photons was later confirmed in and . Ionizing emission was identified in an unprecedentedly large number of multiple images (12) of the same unresolved feature, or knot.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Sunburst Arc as a nitrogen-emitting lensed source whose He II emission and nitrogen yield may be produced by very massive stars.

quotes.quote: Recent studies of high-redshift galaxies with James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), such as GN-z11 at z=10.6, show unexpectedly significant amounts of nitrogen (N) in their spectra. As this phenomenology appears to extend to gravitionally lensed galaxies at Cosmic noon such as the Sunburst Arc at z=2.37 , as well as globular clusters overall, we suggest that the common ingredient among them are very massive stars (VMSs) with zero-age main sequence (ZAMS) masses in the range of 100-1000 M_ . The He ii in the Sunburst Arc might also be the result of the disproportionally large contribution of VMS to the total stellar contribution.

Mestric et al. (2023) recently performed population synthesis of the Sunburst Arc, a gravitationally lensed source at Cosmic Noon (z=2.37). Considering the He ii emission line to be due to VMSs (Vink et al. 2011), these authors combined new stellar atmosphere and evolution models of Martis Palacios (2022), arriving at a total number of VMSs of 400. Pascale et al. (2023) derived 500 M_ of nitrogen for the Sunburst Arc.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Sunburst Arc as a z=2.37 strongly lensed galaxy whose star-forming regions, LyC leakage, and local microlensing allow JWST study of Godzilla.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study a lensed star candidate in the Sunburst Arc galaxy at z=2.37 . The Sunburst Arc is the brightest known strongly lensed galaxy at optical wavelengths, with an integrated magnitude m _ AB 18 dahle2016 and dozens of highly magnified and multiply imaged star-forming regions sharon2022 . The Sunburst Arc earns its name from the strong ``direct escape'' Lyman- ( ) emission rivera-thorsen2017 and high fraction of Lyman Continuum (LyC) radiation escaping along the line of sight (LOS) from a single star-forming region rivera-thorsen2019 . As a result of strong gravitational lensing, unresolved features in the Sunburst Arc (clumps or knots) are replicated multiple times along the arc.

Godzilla is believed to be an object residing in the Sunburst Arc galaxy, as its spectroscopic redshift is the same as the Sunburst Arc ( z 2.37 ). The absence of any counterimages of Godzilla can be explained by an additional microlensing effect weisenbach2024 caused by a small object in front of Godzilla, extremely magnifying it only at that position diego2022,sharon2022 .

gmw_00003416.name: CEERS-93316
gmw_00003416.overview: Initially identified as a candidate galaxy at a redshift of approximately 16 [arXiv:2208.01612, arXiv:2304.13755], CEERS-93316 was later spectroscopically confirmed to be at a much lower redshift of 4.912 [arXiv:2303.15431, arXiv:2304.06658]. It is a dusty, extreme emission-line galaxy whose strong lines and red continuum mimicked the photometric signature of a high-redshift Lyman break [arXiv:2304.13721, arXiv:2312.07799].

sources.summary: The paper presents CEERS-1749 as an extremely luminous candidate at z 17, while arguing a z 5 dusty or quenched interloper solution is also plausible.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: CEERS-1749 at & z 17 & z 5 & z 5 & & (quiescent) & (starburst) R.A. & 3 14:19:39.48 Dec. & 3 +52:56:34.95

quotes.quote: Here we present the most extreme of these systems: CEERS-1749. Based on 0.6-5 m photometry, this strikingly luminous ( 26 mag) galaxy appears to lie at z 17 . This would make it an M_ UV 22 , M_ 5 10^ 9 M_ system that formed a mere 220 Myrs after the Big Bang. The implied number density of this galaxy and its analogues challenges virtually every early galaxy evolution model that assumes CDM cosmology.

However, there is strong environmental evidence supporting a secondary redshift solution of z 5 : all three of the galaxy's nearest neighbors at <2.5 have photometric redshifts of z 5 . Further, we show that CEERS-1749 may lie in a z 5 protocluster that is 5 overdense compared to the field. Intense line emission at z 5 from a quiescent galaxy harboring ionized gas, or from a dusty starburst, may provide satisfactory explanations for CEERS-1749's photometry.

sources.summary: The paper revises CEERS-93316 from an extreme high-redshift candidate to a z = 4.912 emission-line galaxy whose strong lines, reddening, and continuum shape mimic a Lyman break.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of CEERS-93316 (Figure fig:donnanspectrum ) shows emission lines from the hydrogen Balmer series, , , and \ ( _ rest = 6718,6733 ) at a redshift z = 4.912 0.001 . This is the same redshift as the galaxy CEERS-DSFG-1 (MSA ID 2), which lies 26 arcsec away and was previously discussed by as a moderate-redshift galaxy with colors that could mimic a high-redshift Ly break. This redshift is also consistent with the alternative lower-redshift solution proposed for CEERS-93316 Donnan2023, zavala23, Naidu2022b, perezgonzalez23 , and the same redshift as (at least) two other neighboring galaxies (see Methods).

The continuum emission of CEERS-93316 fades at wavelengths shorter than 2.5 m (rest frame 0.4 m), although flux is significantly detected in the spectrum down to 1.2 m. The strong H ( _ rest = 6564 ) and \ lines have large rest-frame equivalent widths ( 790 140 and 1020 180 respectively), and hence contribute significantly to the flux measured by NIRCam through the F277W, F356W, F410M, and F444W filters (specifically, at this precise redshift, H emission actually contributes to the flux in the last three of those filters; Figure fig:donnanspectrum ). The significant reddening implied by the relative lack of observed UV continuum flux is corroborated by the large Balmer decrement measured from the spectrum (H /H =5.6 0.6 ), which implies - despite the large equivalent widths of the lines - a substantial nebular color excess of E(B-V)_ nebular =0.71 0.12 assuming a Cardelli extinction law (see Methods).

sources.summary: The paper rejects 93316 as a z=16 candidate, instead finding a z=4.912 strong-line galaxy whose emission mimics a high-redshift Lyman break.

quotes.quote: 93316 was firstly photometrically reported in 2023MNRAS.518.6011D as a z 16 galaxy candidate (CR2-z16-1 in 2023ApJS..265....5H ), and many studies discuss the properties of this galaxy including the possible low-redshift solution based on the available photometric data 2023ApJS..265....5H,2023ApJ...943L...9Z,2022arXiv220802794N,2023ApJ...946L..13F,2022arXiv221206683B . As shown in Figure fig_spec_lowz , the spectrum of 93316 obtained in 2023arXiv230315431A shows two prominent emission lines at 2.95 and 3.89 m. This indicates that 93316 is a galaxy at z_ spec =4.91 whose strong emission lines mimic the photometric Lyman break at z 16 , as discussed in 2023ApJ...943L...9Z and 2022arXiv220802794N .

The previous z 16 galaxy candidate, 93316, is found to be a galaxy at z_ spec =4.912 . The strong Oiii 4959,5007 and H emission lines in 93316 boost the fluxes in the F277W band and the F356W, F410M, and F444W bands, respectively, mimicking the Lyman break at z 16 (Figure fig_SED_93316 ). Such a strong line emitter can be significant contamination in a galaxy selection at z 16 .

sources.summary: The paper uses CEERS-93316 as a dramatic EELG example whose blended extreme emission lines and red continuum can mimic an ultra-high-redshift galaxy in NIRCam colors.

quotes.quote: A dramatic example of this is the galaxy CEERS-93316, which has a relatively constant flux density in the F277W, F356W, F410M, and F444W filters and is undetected blueward of F200W. Such an SED can be mistaken for a high-redshift \ break and this galaxy was initially identified as a z=16.5 candidate by Donnan2023 .
