NIRSpec observations in Haro2023a instead demonstrated that this object has a combination of a red continuum, making the blue NIRCam filters undetected, and extreme emission-line contribution to several NIRCam filters: \ + \ in F277W, and \ blended across all three of F356W, F410M, and F444W.

The particular source CEERS-93316 is not included in our EELG sample because it was not detected in the minimum of 5 photometric filters required in this selection, but it spectroscopically confirms the characteristic shape of a photometrically blended extreme \ + \ lines across the reddest filters mimicking an ultra-high redshift galaxy. The spectroscopic confirmation of CEERS-93316 as an EELG with emission features blended across multiple filters suggests that the galaxies with similar unusual SEDs shown in Figure fig:falsecont3 are also EELGs with similar emission-line blending in their photometry. It is notable that EELGs like CEERS-93316 and the sources shown in Figure fig:falsecont3 would be missed by simple color-color selection due to the emission features blending nearly equally across different filters.

sources.summary: The paper includes CR2-z16-1 as one of its highest-redshift CEERS2 candidates, with colors consistent with a z=16.3 galaxy and spectroscopy still needed.

quotes.quote: One of the highest redshift source candidates in our catalogs is CR2-z16-1 at z=16.25^ +0.24 _ 0.46 in the CEERS2 field. As discussed later in Section ss_D22 , CR2-z16-1 is firstly identified as a z=16.4 source (ID 93116) in 2022arXiv220712356D . As shown in the middle panel of Figure fig_sed_z17 , our measured fluxes are almost consistent with those presented in 2022arXiv220802794N and 2022arXiv221105792F , while fluxes in 2022arXiv220712356D are systematically fainter than our measurements, probably because 2022arXiv220712356D assume the PSF for the aperture correction.

The colors measured in these three studies (this study, 2022arXiv220712356D , 2022arXiv220802794N , and 2022arXiv221105792F ) consistently show a clear break around F200W band, consistent with a z=16.3 galaxy, although there are some discussions about a possible solution of a dusty line emitter at z 5 2022arXiv220801816Z,2022arXiv220802794N . Although the NIRSpec spectroscopy is required to conclude the redshift of CR2-z16-1, we include this source as a F200W dropout galaxy candidate.

sources.summary: The paper treats CEERS-1749 as the earliest selected JWST galaxy candidate and finds that cosmic-string loops with low tension could generate such a z~16 object.

quotes.quote: The earliest galaxy candidate: CEERS-1749 with stellar mass _ 10 (M_*/M_ )= 9.6 0.2 and redshift z = 16 0.6 There are two galaxy candidates at z 16 . They have similar inferred redshift but this one is heavier so we consider CEERS-1749 here. .
Besides, loop-seeded halos have more advantages for higher redshift cases and we only need G 10^ 10 to generate CEERS-1749 at redshift z 16 .
An extreme value statistic analysis shows that we can explain the heaviest and earliest high-redshift galaxy candidates detected by JWST given a string tension as low as G 10^ 9 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes CEERS-1749 as a very high-redshift galaxy candidate, noting its possible secondary redshift and a large stellar-mass excess over CDM simulations.

quotes.quote: In a subsequent publication, Naidu_2022b_dust presented the galaxy candidate CEERS-1749 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.6_ 0.2 ^ +0.2 most likely located at z_ phot = 16.0_ 0.6 ^ +0.6 but a secondary redshift solution of z 5 cannot be excluded (see their figure 1 and table 3).

GL-z11 at 10.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 , GL-z13 at 13.1_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 , and CEERS-1749 at 16.0_ 0.6 ^ +0.6 are compared with the simulations at z = 10.98 , 11.98 , and 14.99 , respectively. The comparison with GL-z13 and CEERS-1749 is therewith more conservative because snapshots corresponding to lower redshifts than observed are addressed allowing the galaxies to grow in stars for a longer time span than in the observed cases.
In TNG100-1, the GSMF at z = 14.99 reaches a maximum value of _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 7.32 being therewith 191 times lower than the stellar mass of CEERS-1749 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.6_ 0.2 ^ +0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper treats CEERS-1749 as an extreme JWST galaxy with stellar mass log10 9.6 at redshift about 16, finding CDM expectations far too low unless star-formation efficiency and small-scale power are enhanced.

quotes.quote: Our analysis uses extreme value statistics (EVS) and considers two of the most extremal galaxies observed so far by JWST: CEERS-1749 of stellar mass _ 10 (M_*/ ) = 9.6 0.2 at redshift z = 16 0.6 and Galaxy 14924 of stellar mass _ 10 (M_*/ ) = 10.9 0.3 at redshift z = 9.9 0.5 .

For example, taking the CDM halo mass function and a typical optimistic efficiency according to lower redshift observations f_*=0.1 , we find that the expected number of halos similar to CEERS-1749 whose stellar mass is M_* > 10^ 9.6 at redshift z > 15 within f_ sky = 40 arcmin ^2 is N 7 10^ 8 .

The green shaded region indicates the stellar mass of CEERS-1749, the gray shaded region is excluded by the Lyman- constraints on the matter power spectrum and the blue line indicates the conservative constraint from the UV luminosity function. The dominant UV luminosity function constraint a_s = 0.66^ +0.43 _ 0.17 in Ref. is given for the relative enhancement over the CDM in the bin 0.5 ^ 1 k < 2.25 ^ 1 . As the constraint depends non-trivially on the shape of the power spectrum and has not been computed for the parametrization eq:Pk , we estimate the corresponding constraint on k_c by comparing the amplitude relative to the CDM at the lower edge of the bin, k=0.5 ^ 1 . We see that with the commonly used efficiency f_*=0.1 the CEERS-1749 observation is in more than 2 tension with the model even at the smallest allowed value of k_c .

sources.summary: The paper uses CEERS-93316 as a cautionary contaminant: initially selected as a bright z 15-17 candidate, NIRSpec later placed it at z=4.9 with strong lines mimicking a high-redshift UV continuum.

quotes.quote: The poster child for the dangers of such selection is the galaxy CEERS-93316 that was determined by several independent teams to be a plausible bright galaxy at a redshift between 15 and 17. An alternative low-redshift solution for this galaxy had been suggested based on a tentative sub-millimetre detection and a z 5 overdensity in the field Zavala2023, Naidu2022arXiv . Subsequent NIRSpec spectroscopy showed it to be a z=4.9 galaxy with a red continuum and strong emission lines ArrabalHaro2023a . At this particular redshift, all four NIRCam Long-Wave filters (F277W, F356W, F410M and F444W) contained a strong emission line that mimicked a z>15 blue ultraviolet (UV) continuum.

sources.summary: The paper discusses the CEERS z=16.4 source as a repeatedly selected candidate that spectroscopy later indicated is a dusty extreme emission-line galaxy at z~4.9.

quotes.quote: These are the luminous z=10 and z=12 sources in the GLASS fields as well as the z=16.4 and z=11.5 (`Maisie's Galaxy') sources in the CEERS P2 pointing. While reassuring that it is the brightest candidates that overlap the most between samples, the regular selection of the now disproven z=16.4 source in the CEERS fields arrabal2023 means that emission line treatments and template sets in various SED fitting tools will require revisiting.
Initial analysis shows that the z=16.4 candidate is in fact a dusty, extreme line emitter at z 4.9 arrabal2023 , as predicted independently by Zavala2022 and Naidu2022 .

sources.summary: CEERS-93316 is discussed as a candidate initially assigned photometric redshift 16.45, but spectroscopic follow-up lowered it to z=4.912, so the paper excludes it from the analysis.

quotes.quote: Initial photometric measurements of Maisie's galaxy and of CEERS-93316 placed them at photometric redshifts of z_ phot = 11.08 Maisie_initial and z_ phot = 16.45 CEERS-93316-initial , respectively. Spectroscopic follow-up measurements of these galaxies confirmed their redshifts to be z_ spec = 11.44 and z_ spec = 4.912 Maisie , respectively. Due to the decrease in redshift of CEERS-93316 to a much lower redshift, we do not include it in the analysis in this paper and instead only include the high redshift Maisie's galaxy.

gmw_00003417.name: J1306+0356
gmw_00003417.overview: J1306+0356 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:0707.1662, arXiv:astro-ph/0108063] located at a redshift of approximately 5.99 [arXiv:0707.1662, arXiv:astro-ph/0108063]. The resolved host galaxy exhibits an east-west velocity gradient of about 200 km/s [arXiv:1801.02641] and resides in an unusually underdense environment [arXiv:2110.13917].

sources.summary: The paper compares J1306+0356 redshift measurements and reports absorption systems in its continuum, including confirmed Mg II absorption and two very high-redshift absorbers.

quotes.quote: J1306+0356 Measurements to the same data presented here were published by pen02 , who find an uncorrected C 4 redshift of z = 6.00 0.01 , consistent with our result and with the value derived fom N 5 by bec01 .

Within the continuum emission of the QSO J1306+0356, we find two systems of double absorption lines: at 9797.7, 9827.4 \ and 9880.7, 9906.3 \ with observed EWs of 6.2, 4.0, and 11.6, 10.3 , respectively. In addition, a third system of double absorption may be present at the red edge of the J -band spectrum: at 11340.6, 11401.2 \ with observed EWs of 13, 11 , respectively (see Fig. fig:Z1306 ). These absorption features would be consistent with Mg 2 absorption at z = 2.504 , 2.533 and 3.060 (although the latter only marginally), but a systematic search with larger wavelength coverage by Jiang et al. \ (2007, AJ, in press) shows that the correct identifications for the first double absorption feature is Al 2 1670 at z = 4.864 and 4.882, while the third double absorption feature may be Fe 3 1926 from the same intervening systems.

We note, however, that the latter lines are at the edge of the observed spectrum where the noise is higher and the wavelength calibration is not very accurate. The second double absorption feature is indeed confirmed to be Mg 2 at z = 2.533 from other absorption lines identified. The two absorbers at z = 4.9 are the highest redshift Mg 2 absorbers known see, e.g., kob05 .

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSSp J130608.26+035626.3 as a newly discovered z=5.99 quasar in a complete z>5.8 SDSS sample, showing broad emission lines and strong Ly absorption.

quotes.quote: We describe the discovery of three new quasars, SDSSp J083643.85+005453.3 ( z=5.82 ), SDSSp J130608.26+035626.3 ( z=5.99 ) and SDSSp J103027.10+052455.0 ( z=6.28 ). The quasar SDSSp J083643.85+005453.3 is a radio source with flux of 1.1 mJy at 20 cm. The spectra of all three quasars show strong and broad Ly +NV emission lines, and very strong Ly forest absorption, with a mean continuum decrement D_A > 0.90 .

The three new quasars, with redshifts of 5.82, 5.99 and 6.28, are the highest-redshift objects with spectral confirmation known to date. The spectra of the new quasars show very strong Ly absorption blueward of the Ly emission, with more than 90 of the flux absorbed. The amount of absorption increases rapidly toward z_ abs 6 .
The black hole masses of these quasars are probably several times 10^9 M_ . The quasars are likely to reside in very massive systems, with the minimum mass of host dark halos 10^ 13 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1306+0356 as a resolved quasar host with an east-west velocity gradient of about 200 km/s, while leaving rotation, complex kinematics, or satellite emission unresolved.

quotes.quote: Velocity maps and dynamical masses Four sources in our sample are well--resolved in (i.e., the observed size of the continuum--subtracted emission from the 2D gaussian modeling is >1.4 the beam): PJ009--10, J0142--3327, PJ167--13, and J1306+0356 (see Figure fig_beam_ima_spc ).
J1306+0356 shows a velocity gradient along the East--West direction, although in this case the peak--to--peak velocity difference is lower ( 200 ).

The resolution of the available data is insufficient to assess whether the kinematics of the -emitting gas in the host galaxies of these two quasars are dominated by ordered rotation or if the underlying velocity structure is more complex (see, for instance, the high-resolution studies of z>6 quasars presented in venemans17a and shao17 ). In particular, given the present data quality it is impossible to rule out whether part of the spatially--resolved emission is associated with a close satellite galaxy of the quasar host galaxy, similar to the cases discussed in decarli17 .

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ 1306+0356 as an unusually underdense quasar sightline, linking its low-density CDF to large voids about 2-3 pMpc from the quasar.

quotes.quote: There are also large voids of size 1 pMpc, like the regions at 1-2 pMpc in CFHQSJ1509-1749 and at 2-3 pMpc in SDSSJ1306+0356.
The only exception is SDSSJ 1306+0356 , which is significantly more underdense than others. This is mainly due to the voids at 2-3 pMpc from this quasar (Figure fig:conti_den ).

gmw_00003418.name: RXS J11401+4115
gmw_00003418.overview: RXS J11401+4115 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy that exhibits strong Fe II emission and a potentially strong host galaxy continuum [arXiv:0808.0398]. The galaxy is classified as radio-quiet and has been observed to be fainter than its cataloged magnitude [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper describes RXS J11401+4115 as an S1 galaxy with strong FeII emission, possible host-galaxy continuum, fainter observed continuum than cataloged, and radio-quiet emission.

quotes.quote: RXS J11401+4115. This object again has a rather noisy spectrum, so the FeII measurements are rather insecure. The SDSS spectrum of this object clearly indicates an S1 galaxy with strong FeII emission, but also potentially strong host galaxy continuum. The Strasbourg CDS Simbad database gives V=13.8 for this object, but our observations suggest the object should be at least a magnitude fainter. Even fainter, the continuum appears to be on the SDSS spectrum. The level of detected radio emission (2 mJy) from this object places it well within the radio-quiet category.

gmw_00003419.name: RXS J17233+3630
gmw_00003419.overview: RXS J17233+3630 is a radio-quiet active galaxy [arXiv:0808.0398]. The galaxy is part of an interacting pair and has a narrow-line region electron density of approximately 1000 per cubic centimeter [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper describes RXS J17233+3630 as a radio-quiet active galaxy in an interacting pair, with archival spectra, measured [SII] density, and uncertain continuum and flux estimates.

quotes.quote: RXS J17233+3630. This active galaxy is a part of an interacting pair. De Grijp et al. 1992, published a spectrum of the objects of a slightly worse quality. The detected radio-emission of 3 mJy (Wadadekar, 2004) classifies the object as radio-quiet. The ratio of [ SII ] 6716 and [ SII ] 6731 , a doublet separated in this spectrum, is a good density indicator and implies N_ e 1000 cm ^ 3 for the NLR (e.g. Ostebrock, 1974). Due to the variable atmospheric conditions when the spectrum was taken, all the continuum and flux measurements (Tables 2 and 3) should only be considered as a rough estimate.

gmw_00003420.name: IRAS 18423+2201
gmw_00003420.overview: IRAS 18423+2201 is a radio-quiet active galaxy that exhibits broad emission lines [arXiv:0808.0398]. Its diagnostic line ratios place the object on the border between Seyfert and LINER classifications [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 18423+2201 as a radio-quiet broad-line object whose diagnostic ratios place it between Seyfert and LINER classifications.

quotes.quote: IRAS 18423+2201. Moran et al. (1996) published a spectrum of this object, where the broad component of H appears much stronger and probably broader. The low level of radio emission (2 mJy; Condon et al. 1998) classifies the object as radio quiet. Although the broad lines are clearly seen in the spectrum, the place of this object on the diagnostic diagrams (e.g. [ OIII ] /H vs. [ OI ] /H and [ OIII ] /H vs. [ SII ] /H ) appears to be on the border between the Seyferts and the LINERs.

gmw_00003421.name: NPM1G-05.0589
gmw_00003421.overview: NPM1G-05.0589 is a spatially extended Seyfert 1.9 active galaxy [arXiv:0808.0398]. Its optical continuum is entirely dominated by the host galaxy, and its narrow-line region has an estimated density of about 700 cm^-3 [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NPM1G-05.0589 as a spatially extended Seyfert S1.9 galaxy whose host dominates the continuum and whose [SII] ratio implies an NLR density near 700 cm^-3.

quotes.quote: NPM1G-05.0589. This spacially extended object (galaxy) does not show broad lines with the exception of a very broad wing of H . The host galaxy determines the optical continuum entirely. All ratios -- [ OIII ] /H , [ OI ] /H , [ SII ] /H , and [ NII ] /H indicate clearly Seyfert galaxy, so we classified the object as S1.9. The ratio or the [ SII ] doublet lines implies an NLR density of about 700 cm ^ 3 . A low-dispersion spectrum of the object is also available in Grazian et al. (2002).

gmw_00003422.name: RXS J21240-0021
gmw_00003422.overview: RXS J21240-0021 is a low-luminosity Seyfert 1 galaxy where the host galaxy continuum significantly dominates the active galactic nucleus emission [arXiv:0808.0398]. Its spectrum exhibits broad emission lines and composite Seyfert-HII-LINER characteristics, suggesting a possible composite nature [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper describes RXS J21240-0021 as host-dominated but with broad wings, composite Seyfert-HII-LINER diagnostics, low-luminosity S1 classification, and an [SII]-based NLR density estimate.

quotes.quote: RXS J21240-0021. It is another object, where the host galaxy continuum appears to significantly dominate the AGN continuum; however, broad wings (H , H , HeII 4686 ) are clearly seen. The place of the object on the diagnostic diagrams is on the border between Seyfert-HII-LINER galaxies, suggesting the possible composite nature of its spectrum. Most likely the object should be classified as a low luminosity S1 galaxy. The [ SII ] ratio of 1.15 implies an NLR density of about 600 cm ^ 3 . The SDSS spectrum and a low dispersion one in Grazian et al. (2002) are available as well.

gmw_00003423.name: NPM1G+24.0470
gmw_00003423.overview: NPM1G+24.0470 is a spatially extended galaxy that lies on the border between Seyfert, HII, and LINER regions on diagnostic diagrams [arXiv:0808.0398]. It is classified as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy due to the probable presence of weak broad lines, and its continuum is dominated by the host galaxy [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper describes NPM1G+24.0470 as a spatially extended composite object near Seyfert-HII-LINER boundaries, with weak broad lines, host-dominated continuum, and dense [SII]-emitting gas.

quotes.quote: NPM1G+24.0470. This spacially extended object probably also has a composite nature, being on the border between Seyfert-HII-LINER regions on the diagnostic diagrams. Weak broad lines are probably present in the H profile, which technically means the object should be classified as S1.9. The host galaxy is mostly contributing to the continuum. The [ SII ] ratio of 0.88 implies a density of about 2000 cm ^ 3 .

gmw_00003424.name: RXS J21592+0952
gmw_00003424.overview: RXS J21592+0952 is a radio-quiet active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0808.0398]. The object is characterized by broad emission lines that exhibit a double-peaked profile [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper describes RXS J21592+0952 as a broad-line, radio-quiet source with a double-peaked profile and stronger broad emission in another published spectrum.

quotes.quote: RXS J21592+0952. This is a broad-line object that appears to have a double-peaked profile (e.g. Eracleous Halpern, 1994; Strateva et al. 2003), clearly seen in H . The object was also observed by Wei et al. (1999), whose spectrum reveals stronger broad emission. It is a weak radio source, well within the radio-quiet range.

gmw_00003425.name: RXS J22027-1304
gmw_00003425.overview: RXS J22027-1304 is a spatially extended Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:0808.0398]. It is classified as either a Seyfert 1.5 or Seyfert 1.8 galaxy and shows no signatures of Fe II emission [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper classifies RXS J22027-1304 as a spatially extended Seyfert galaxy, probably S1.5 or S1.8, with no FeII signatures.

quotes.quote: RXS J22027-1304. This object is a spatially extended galaxy. On the diagnostic diagrams, it stays well inside the Seyfert region and should be classified as either S1.5 or S1.8. No signatures of FeII are seen.

gmw_00003426.name: NPM1G-04.063
gmw_00003426.overview: NPM1G-04.063 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy whose continuum is clearly dominated by the host galaxy itself [arXiv:0808.0398]. The intrinsic active galactic nucleus continuum appears to diminish toward shorter wavelengths, which suggests absorption between the continuum source and the broad-line region [arXiv:0808.0398].

sources.summary: The paper presents NPM1G-04.063 as host-continuum dominated, with broad lines and an AGN continuum that appears absorbed toward shorter wavelengths, possibly by BLR-associated material.

quotes.quote: NPM1G-04.063. This is a very interesting object with lines of significant width but continuum -- clearly dominated by the host galaxy. If the starlight correction is correct, the intrinsic AGN continuum appears to diminish towards the shorter wavelengths, down to zero around H , which suggests significant absorption between the continuum source (e.g. the accretion disk) and the BLR. Some of this absorption may also be associated with the BLR itself, due to the quite large Balmer decrement for the broad H components ( 6).

gmw_00003427.name: ESO 137-001
gmw_00003427.overview: ESO 137-001 is a blue, late-type barred spiral galaxy undergoing violent ram-pressure stripping as it falls into the Norma cluster [arXiv:2202.06712, arXiv:astro-ph/0511516]. This interaction has truncated its gas disk [arXiv:2202.03443] and created a spectacular, multiphase double tail extending up to 80 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2303.15500, arXiv:2202.03443].

sources.summary: ESO 137-001 is presented as a barred spiral undergoing ram-pressure stripping in Abell 3267, with long X-ray and H tails and gas removal transforming the galaxy.

quotes.quote: A textbook example of ram pressure stripping is ESO 137-001 as shown in Figure fig:eso137 left. It is a barred spiral galaxy falling through the very massive cluster Abell 3267 . Chandra and XMM-Newton imaging revealed a long, narrow, and bifurcated X-ray tail extending out to 70 kpc . The bright X-ray tail may form from mixing of cold stripped ISM with the surrounding hot ICM .

Optical images of ESO 137-001 reveal a 40 kpc long H tail comprised of diffuse gas and a large amount of warm H _2 emission . The MUSE observations show that the outskirts of ESO 137-001 are already completely devoid of cold gas . ESO 137-001 presents an ongoing transformation from a gas-rich and star forming galaxy to a red and dead galaxy due to ram pressure stripping. Multi-phase gaseous tails covering from 10 K to 10^7 K are typically associated with late-type galaxies such as ESO 137-001.

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 137-001 as a blue late-type cluster galaxy undergoing stripping, with a 70 kpc luminous X-ray tail, active star formation, and stripped interstellar gas.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of a 70 kpc X-ray tail behind the small late-type galaxy \ in the nearby, hot (T=6.5 keV) merging cluster A3627, from both \ and \ observations. The tail has a length-to-width ratio of 10. It is luminous (L _ 0.5-2 keV 10 ^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 ), with a temperature of 0.7 keV and an X-ray M _ gas of 10^ 9 M _ ( 10 of the galaxy's stellar mass). We interpret this tail as the stripped interstellar medium of \ mixed with the hot cluster medium, when this blue galaxy is being converted into a gas-poor galaxy.

The B-Ks color of \ is 1.0-1.6, much smaller than the B-Ks color of a typical early-type or late-type galaxy (3.7-4.0 or 3, Jarrett 2000). Thus, \ is a very blue galaxy. Spectroscopic studies (Woudt, Kraan-Korteweg Fairall 1999; Woudt et al. 2004) revealed emission lines of H , H , [ OIII ] 5007 and [ NII ] 6584 in the spectrum of . Thus, we conclude that \ is a late-type galaxy with active star formation.

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO137-001's disc and stripped tail with MUSE, finding shock-suggestive line ratios, photoionized stripped gas, and ionized regions embedded in its ram-pressure tail.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the physical properties of the disc and tail of , a galaxy suffering from extreme ram-pressure stripping during its infall into the Norma cluster. With sensitive and spatially-resolved MUSE spectroscopy, we analyse the emission line diagnostics in the tail of , finding high values of [ NII ] / \ and [ OI ] / \ that are suggestive of the presence of shocks in turbulent gas. However, the observed line ratios are not as strong as commonly seen in pure shock heating models, suggesting that other emission mechanisms may contribute to the observed emission. Indeed, part of the observed emission, particularly at close separations from the galaxy disc, may originate from recombination of photoionized gas stripped from the main body of .

In this paper, we have presented the analysis of the physical properties of the disc and stripped tail of , a galaxy that is infalling into the nearby Norma cluster at high velocity. Leveraging on the sensitivity and spatial resolution of MUSE observations, we have studied for the first time 2D maps of emission line ratios within a posterchild of violent ram-pressure stripping. Due to the complex interaction between different gas phases, several physical processes are likely involved in the ionisation that powers the observed emission of the tail and the embedded HII regions.

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 137-001 as a Norma-cluster jellyfish galaxy, analyzing ionized and molecular-gas turbulence in its long multiphase ram-pressure-stripped tail.

quotes.quote: ESO 137-001 is a quintessential jellyfish galaxy located in the nearest rich cluster, the Norma cluster. Its spectacular multiphase tail has complex morphology and kinematics both from the imprinted galaxy's interstellar medium (ISM) and as a result of the interactions between the stripped gas and the surrounding hot plasma, mediated by radiative cooling and magnetic fields. We study the kinematics of the multiphase tail using high-resolution observations of the ionized and the molecular gas in the entire structure.

ESO 137-001 is one of the nearest jellyfish galaxies with the richest amount of multi-wavelength data. It is located in the Norma cluster (Abell 3627), the closest rich cluster, with a projected distance of 200 kpc from the cluster center Sun2006, Pavel2014 . ESO 137-001 has a very small line-of-sight velocity difference ( 200 km/s) from the cluster mean, suggesting that its motion is mainly in the plane of the sky. The tail of ESO 137-001 extends beyond 80 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper finds abundant cold molecular gas in ESO 137-001 and its ram-pressure-stripped tail, with low star-formation efficiency and evidence that stripped molecular gas survives far from the disk.

quotes.quote: With the APEX telescope we have detected ^ 12 CO(2-1) emission corresponding to more than 10^9 M_ of H _2 in three H bright regions along the tail of the Norma cluster galaxy ESO 137-001, out to a projected distance of 40 kpc from the disk. ESO 137-001 has an 80 kpc long and bright X-ray tail associated with a shorter (40 kpc) and broader tail of numerous star-forming H 2 regions. The amount of 1.5 10^8 M_ of H _2 found in the most distant region is similar to molecular masses of tidal dwarf galaxies, though the standard Galactic CO-to-H _2 factor could overestimate the H _2 content. Along the tail, we find the amount of molecular gas to drop, while masses of the X-ray emitting and diffuse ionized components stay roughly constant.

We find a very low star formation efficiency ( _ dep > 10^ 10 yr) in the stripped gas in ESO 137-001 and suggest that this is due to a low average gas density in the tail, or turbulent heating of the interstellar medium that is induced by a ram pressure shock. The unprecedented bulk of observed H _2 in the ESO 137-001 tail suggests that some stripped gas may survive ram pressure stripping in the molecular phase.

sources.summary: The paper presents MUSE observations of ESO137-001, finding ram pressure stripped its outer disk, feeds a long gaseous tail from inner gas, and leaves disk rotation imprinted downstream.

quotes.quote: We present MUSE observations of , a spiral galaxy infalling towards the center of the massive Norma cluster at z 0.0162 . During the high-velocity encounter of \ with the intracluster medium, a dramatic ram-pressure stripping event gives rise to an extended gaseous tail, traced by our MUSE observations to >30 kpc from the galaxy center. By studying the \ surface brightness and kinematics in tandem with the stellar velocity field, we conclude that ram pressure has completely removed the interstellar medium from the outer disk, while the primary tail is still fed by gas from the inner regions. Gravitational interactions do not appear to be a primary mechanism for gas removal. The stripped gas retains the imprint of the disk rotational velocity to 20 kpc downstream, without a significant gradient along the tail, which suggests that \ is fast moving along a radial orbit in the plane of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO mapping of ESO 137-001, describing a nearby Norma jellyfish galaxy with ram-pressure-stripped multiphase tails rich in compact molecular clumps and star-forming fireballs.

quotes.quote: The Norma cluster galaxy ESO 137-001 is undergoing a strong interaction with the surrounding intra-cluster medium and is one of the nearest jellyfish galaxies with a long multi-phase tail. We have mapped the full extent of the tail at 1 (350 pc) angular resolution and found a rich distribution of mostly compact CO regions extending to nearly 60 kpc in length and 25 kpc in width. In total about 10^9 M_ of molecular gas was detected. The CO features are found predominantly at the heads of numerous small-scale ( 1.5  kpc) fireballs (i.e., star-forming clouds with linear streams of young stars extending toward the galaxy) but also of large-scale ( 8  kpc) super-fireballs, and double-sided fireballs that have additional diffuse ionized gas tails extending in the direction opposite to the stellar tails. The new data help to shed light on the origin of the molecular tail -- CO filaments oriented in the direction of the tail with only diffuse associated H emission are likely young molecular features formed in situ, whereas other large CO features tilted with respect to the tail may have originated from the densest gas complexes that were pushed gradually away from the disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO137-001 as a Norma-cluster ram-pressure case with an extreme double tail, compact HII regions, truncated gas disk, and shock-dominated later-stage stripping.

quotes.quote: Specifically, in this sample of galaxies suffering RPS we include ESO137-001, which is falling in the Norma cluster presenting a double tail of diffuse gas, and CGCG097-087 in A1367 which is travelling edge-on through the ICM of the cluster with its smaller companion CGCG097-087N.

We also include in the following sections data from ESO137-001 Fumagalli14, Fossati16 which has similar high-quality MUSE data and is falling in the Norma cluster which has a similar halo mass ( M_ halo,Norma 2-3 M_ halo,A1367 , Cortese04, Woudt08, Boselli21REW ). ESO137-001 is suffering extreme RPS showing a double tail which extends for at least 80 kpc and with a large number of bright compact HII regions, located where the gas is dynamically cool.

Conversely, CGCG097-120 and ESO137-001 show a significant truncation of the disk, with values of R_ H /R_ band 0.36 and 0.27 , respectively. We thus conclude that the two galaxies with high [ OIII ] / H appear to be undergoing a starburst at an early phase of the stripping process when they are still rich in cold/warm gas, while CGCG097-120 and ESO137-001 are already at a later stripping stage -- as shown by the significant truncation -- and their activity in the interface region is characterized by low levels of star formation and a more prominent contribution from shocks.

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 137-001 as an observed ram-pressure-stripped galaxy with H and X-ray tail emission and double tails, using it as a face-on benchmark for simulated magnetized bifurcated tails.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy with multiwavelength emission is ESO 137-001 (Sun et al. 2006, 2007), with a tail detected in both H and X-ray. Both ESO 137-001 and ESO 137-002 show double tails zhang13 .

Such a double tail behind a galaxy has indeed been observed in ESO 137-001 (Sun et al. 2006, 2007) and in ESO 137-002 zhang13 . It is intriguing that double tails have been observed in both nearly face-on (Sun et al. 2007) and in significantly tilted disks zhang13 , and our results are broadly consistent with these observations.

sources.summary: ESO 137-001 is cited as a Norma Cluster spiral with outside-in stripping, narrow X-ray/H-emission tails, CO in a secondary tail, and orphan extraplanar HII regions.

quotes.quote: There is similar evidence for outside-in stripping in the Norma Cluster spiral ESO 137-001 Sun07, sun10 . The galaxy has two narrow tails with X-ray and H emission, with CO emission also present in the secondary tail, as well as a number of orphan extraplanar HII regions that form a broad tail but are not associated with X-ray or diffuse H emission. The origin of the broad tail is not well-understood, but we think they may be similar to structures in NGC 4522 and IC 3418. Jachym14 propose that stripping progresses from the outside of the galaxy inwards, forming a broader tail at early stages, which narrows as the radial extent of the remaining gas disk shrinks.

sources.summary: The paper cites EOS137-001 as a Norma-cluster ram-pressure-stripped galaxy with an H2-rich, HI-deficient molecular tail; dust in its disk and inner tail may enhance H2 formation.

quotes.quote: It is also worth noting that the tails of the two ram pressure stripped galaxies, D100 in Coma cluster (Abell 1656) and EOS137-001 in Norma cluster (Abell 3627), are found to be H _2 rich but HI deficient jachym17,jachym19 . These molecular tails are estimated to be massive ( 10^9 M_ ) and extend to 60 kpc from the galaxy, but the level of star formation appears to be rather insignificant ( 10^ 3 10^ 2 M_ yr^ 1 ).

More interestingly, jachym17,jachym19 claim that the molecular tails are likely formed in-situ , rather than directly stripped from the galaxies because the age of the tails is estimated to be higher ( 200 Myr ) than the typical lifetime of dense molecular clouds ( 1-10 Myr).

Given that dust features are clearly seen in the disk of both galaxies and the inner tail of EOS137-001, the formation of H _2 may be enhanced due to dust, but our simulations fail to reproduce such H _2 rich but HI-deficient tails.

gmw_00003428.name: ESO 137-002
gmw_00003428.overview: ESO 137-002 is an edge-on disk galaxy undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2111.01821, arXiv:1203.1343]. The galaxy features double tails of stripped material [arXiv:1203.1343] and retains a large amount of cold molecular gas, though it exhibits a low star-formation efficiency [arXiv:2111.01821].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 137-002 as an edge-on ram-pressure-stripped galaxy retaining much molecular gas, with detected inner-tail gas and unusually low star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: Stripping history of ESO 137-002 We can compare the amount of gas at different phases (cold molecular, warm ionized and X-ray emitting) detected in the galaxy and its tail. Cold molecular gas We detected 5.5 10^ 9 M _ of the cold molecular gas from the galaxy and at least 2 10^ 8 M _ from the tail. Deeper observations plus wider coverage of the tail may reveal more cold molecular gas there. For the total stellar mass and the sSFR of , the cold gas fraction of the galaxy is 20 from the scaling relations by and , although the big scatter can change the result by a factor of several. This fraction translates to 7 10^ 9 M _ of the cold gas initially in the galaxy.

Despite a large scatter in the correlation see Fig. 3 in 2020ApJ...889..103L , it is clear that the values of the line ratios measured in the two regions in ESO 137-002 would in a normal star-forming galaxy correspond to a larger SFE. In other words, despite its large amounts of molecular gas and the relatively large fraction of the denser molecular gas traced by the CO(3-2) emission, ESO 137-002 has a rather low SFE: SFE = SFR /M_ H_2 0.89 M _ yr ^ 1 / 5.5 10^9 M _ 1.6 10^ 10 yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 137-002 as an observed ram-pressure-stripped disk galaxy with double tails, possibly linked to early cluster infall and disk tilt, matching its bifurcated-tail simulations.

quotes.quote: Both ESO 137-001 and ESO 137-002 show double tails zhang13 .

The recently detected double tails in ESO 137-002 have been suggested by zhang13 to be associated with the early stages of the infall of a disk galaxy into the cluster atmosphere (though the authors also suggested that the disk tilt is large).

Such a double tail behind a galaxy has indeed been observed in ESO 137-001 (Sun et al. 2006, 2007) and in ESO 137-002 zhang13 . It is intriguing that double tails have been observed in both nearly face-on (Sun et al. 2007) and in significantly tilted disks zhang13 , and our results are broadly consistent with these observations.

gmw_00003429.overview: IRAS 12112+0305 is an interacting, pre-merger ultraluminous infrared galaxy containing two nuclei classified as LINERs [arXiv:0803.1832, arXiv:1805.03667]. The system features a high star formation rate [arXiv:2306.14843], starburst-dominated infrared emission [arXiv:1609.01291, arXiv:2303.08178], and a blueshifted molecular outflow extending from its northeastern nucleus [arXiv:1708.09503].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F12112 + 0305 as an interacting LINER-type ULIRG, gives its OH megamaser measurements, and reports no detectable linear polarization.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12112 + 0305 : This ULIRG is classified as a LINER-type AGN and is an interacting pair of galaxies at z = 0.0730 . The only information concerning the OHM emission in this ULIRG is listed in tabular form in staveley-smithncawr92 ; no spectrum has been published. The 1667 MHz line was measured at a velocity of 5540 km \ s ^ 1 and no information about the 1665 MHz transition is published. Its 1667 MHz flux density is listed in darlingg02 as 45 mJy. There are no VLBI observations of the 1667 MHz OHM emission for this source.

IRAS F12112 + 0305 Figure fig:12112qandu shows the linear polarization results for 12112. The lower frequencies are plagued by RFI, which remarkably disappears at the low-frequency boundary of the 1667 MHz line (centered at 1554.5 MHz). There is no trace of any detectable linear polarization for this source.

sources.summary: The paper finds a blueshifted hot molecular outflow in the NE galaxy of IRAS 12112+0305, extending 1.6 kpc from the nucleus with a wing reaching -700 km/s.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12112+0305 The broad-component emission is seen in the NE galaxy of this galaxy-pair. The broad-component emission is blueshifted and found in the region where the narrow-component disk is redshifted or at the systemic velocity. The broad-component emission appears to originate from the nuclear region, is only seen on one side of the nucleus, and stretches out to 1.6 kpc.

We argue that the latter is the most likely PA of the minor axis of the galaxy, given that it is perpendicular to the elongated morphology of the galaxy in the HST/NICMOS imaging (Fig. fig:outflows ). This means that the outflow spans a cone that is oriented about 15 55 degrees off the minor axis. The outflow peaks in intensity and velocity roughly 0.5 kpc from the core. Here the outflow has a bulk velocity of 200 \ with respect to the systemic velocity, which it maintains out to a distance of 1.6 kpc. The wing of the broad component stretches out to -700 \ at zero-intensity.

sources.summary: The paper studies 12112+0305 as a low-redshift close-binary, pre-merger ULIRG with LINER nuclei, placing its weak AGN at the NE radio/submillimeter continuum peak with hot-dust HCN emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study three low- z ( d 350 Mpc) ULIRGs (six individual nuclei) with L_ IR L_ =12.0-12.3 (see Table tbl_sample ) based on their mid- and far-IR spectral energy distribution modeling (Section ss:ir_lum ) . These three ULIRG systems seem to be in a similar dynamical state. They were classified as type III by Veilleux2002 , which corresponds to a pre-merger stage characterized by two identifiable nuclei with well defined tidal tails and bridges. They also belong to the subclass of ``close binary'' (i.e., ``b'') as the projected separation of their nuclei is smaller than 10 kpc. Their nuclei are classified as low ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINER; 12112+0305 and 14348 1447; e.g., Colina2000, Evans2002 ) or H ii ( 22491 1808; e.g., Veilleux1999 ) and in all systems a weak AGN contribution ( 10-15 ) is detected in their mid-IR \ spectra Veilleux2009 .

For 12112+0305 and 22491 1808, we assume that the AGN is at the brightest nucleus in the radio/sub-mm continuum, i.e., 12112+0305 NE and 22491 1808 E (see below). In addition, vibrationally excited HCN J=4-3 emission is detected in 12112+0305 NE and 22491 1808 E which can be a signature of hot dust heated by an AGN Imanishi2016, Imanishi2018 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS12112+0305 as a deep-diving (U)LIRG with depressed O/H relative to its mass, high SFR, late merger stage, and solar-like N/O.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, four galaxies namely Haro11, IRAS12112+0305, IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 strongly deviate from the MZR showing metallicities lower by a factor of 2 than expected for these deep-diving (U)LIRGs stellar masses according to the MZR. While Haro11 is not a ULIRG galaxy like the others, this star-forming galaxy is reported to be a well known merger dominated galaxy that shared many of properties observed in ULIRGs. Notably, IRAS12112+0305 was previously reported to have a super-solar abundance ( 2Z _ ) , while IRAS20551-4250 and IRAS23128-5919 where reported to have abundances close to the values expected from the MZR ar their masses . These high estimations of O/H are driven by the nearly solar N/O values obtained here. However, our O/H estimation -- independent of N/O-- is significantly lower, thus suggesting a more complex mixing process of the gas affecting their chemical composition.

We show in Figure fig:MZR that these deep-diving (U)LIRGs have the highest SFR values in our sample ( >100 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and they are in the final stages of their merges. Additionally, we find that the N/O ratios for these four galaxies are similar to the solar ratio, which suggests that they have reached a relatively mature stage of their chemical enrichment history .

sources.summary: The survey describes IRAS 12112+0305 as an extended double ULIRG observed on its NE nucleus, with S(0)/S(7) detections and multi-component gas excitation.

quotes.quote: Only IRAS 09022-3615, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348-1447 show evidence of significant source extension i.e. FWHM 1.5 FWHM _ star . Their FWHM are 3.0, 1.7 and 1.7 times that of the calibration star, respectively. Both IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348-1447 are known doubles with nuclei separated by 2.9 and 3.4 , respectively and the 5 Gaussian FWHM corresponds to a linear diameter of 8 and 9 kpc, respectively. Our observations were centered on the NE nucleus in IRAS 12112+0305 and the SW nucleus in IRAS 14348-1447, as these are the dominant nuclei in the near-infrared and in CO (Evans et al. 2002 and Evans, Surace Mazzarella 2000).
NGC 6240 and IRAS 12112+0305 are not included in this selection, as their excitation diagrams are best described by multi-component gas, as we describe more fully in the next section.

Besides NGC 6240, we detect the S(0) line in only two other sources, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 13342+3932. Likewise, the S(7) line is detected in only six sources, NGC 6240, Arp 220, IRAS 06301-7954, IRAS 12032+1707, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 17068+4027 with S/N > 3.

sources.summary: The paper reports weak variability in IRAS F12112+0305, including dimming of the brightest maser feature and a changed Stokes V dip, but no magnetic-field evidence.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12112+0305 : In comparing the old and new spectra for this OHM, shown in Figure fig:12112 , the most obvious difference is the appearance of weak interference spikes in the older spectra that were not a problem in the more recent observations. There is also astrophysical change, as the brightest feature in the spectrum for IRAS F12112+0305 has apparently dimmed by 10 mJy, relative to a flux density of 100 mJy. At the same frequency as the change in the Stokes I spectrum, the Stokes V spectrum has also changed, as there is now a broad dip associated with the peak of the maser emission.

Despite this new dip, combined fits to the Stokes I and V profiles do not produce any evidence of magnetic fields. At the frequencies corresponding to 1667 MHz emission, the more recent spectra have comparable noise, and errors in derived Gaussian fits to the structure, as the original observations. We find a comparable upper limit of 3 mG for magnetic fields in the masing regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes the northeast component of IRAS F12112+0305 as one part of a double radio merger source and classifies it as a radio starburst despite unusual inverted-to-flat spatial behavior.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12112+0305 (NE) This is a double radio source in a galaxy merger, where the NE component is associated with the NE merging galaxy. The distribution of values goes from inverted to flat, but the spatial distribution is particular in the sense that from SW to NE gets from inverted to flat values. Also, the centre of the NE radio component has highest values of than the surroundings.

We see that the NE radio component falls mainly in category 2 of histograms. The inverted values of are located at the edges of the radio source, and due to the uncertainty we do not take them under consideration. We classify it as a radio-SB.

sources.summary: IRAS 12112 + 0305 is treated as a double-nucleus ULIRG dominated by PAH/starburst spectra but possibly hiding a deeply buried infrared-elusive AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12112 + 0305 ( z = 0.0730) is a ULIRG with L _ IR 10 ^ 12.3 L _ (Table 1), classified optically as a LINER vei99 or a Seyfert 2 yua10 . It consists of two merging nuclei (northeastern [ NE ] and southwestern [ SW ] ), with a separation of 3 arcsec kim02 . Infrared spectra at 3--35 m from nuclear emission, both combined and separately at individual nuclei, are dominated by strong PAH emission features ima06a,ima07b,nar09,vei09,ima10 . Such infrared spectral shapes are usually seen when the observed infrared spectra are dominated by starburst emission moo86,gen98,imd00 .

No clear observational AGN signatures are recognizable in infrared energy diagnostic methods. If a luminous AGN is present in either of the nuclei, the AGN must be deeply buried in gas and dust so as to be infrared elusive.

sources.summary: The paper distinguishes IRAS 12112 + 0305's NE and SW nuclei: NE has double-peaked dense-gas profiles, SW has weak lines and small HCN/HCO+ ratios, and ALMA indicates warmer nuclear gas.

quotes.quote: Single Gaussian fits are adopted, except for the HCN and HCO ^ + lines of IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE, which clearly exhibit a double-peaked profile with a strong central dip (Figure fig:profile j,k) wherein the results of a double Gaussian fit are adopted.

Two sources with small HCN-to-HCO ^ + flux ratios, located at the bottom-left part of Figure fig:3Dplot (NGC 1614 and IRAS 12112 + 0305 SW) may be better reproduced with [ HCN ] / [ HCO ^ + ] = 1 (i.e., comparable HCN and HCO ^ + abundances). Overall Trend of Molecular Gas Properties at (U)LIRGs' Nuclei In Figure fig:ULIRGfit , the uncertainty in the derived gas density and temperature is large for IRAS 12112 + 0305 SW and IRAS 12127 1412 simply because molecular line emission is fainter than other (U)LIRGs' nuclei (Figure fig:profile ).

NGC 1614 and IRAS 13509 0442, together with IRAS 12112 + 0305 SW, are classified as starburst-dominated with no luminous AGN signature at any wavelength, whereas the remaining ULIRGs are regarded as containing luminous AGNs based on previous observations ( 2). For the three ULIRGs, IRAS 08572 + 3915, IRAS 12112 + 0305, and IRAS 22491 1808, molecular gas temperature was estimated to be 60--150 K, based on large beam-sized CO multiple J-transition line observations and RADEX modeling pap12a . Our ALMA HCN and HCO ^ + line study provides significantly higher temperature for nuclear molecular gas in all the three ULIRGs (see Figure fig:ULIRGfit and Table tab:bestfit ).

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE as a ULIRG nucleus with double-peaked molecular lines, strong HCN/CS behavior, and evidence for an extremely deeply buried AGN.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE displays double-peaked emission line profiles for HCN J=4--3, HCO ^ + J=4--3, H ^ 13 CN J=3--2, CS J=5--4, and possibly CS J=7--6 (Figure 4c,d,g,h), as previously observed for HCN J=3--2 and HCO ^ + J=3--2 ima16c . The central dips of HCN and HCO ^ + are considerably stronger at J=4--3 (Figure 4c) than at J=3--2 ima16c . Such double-peaked emission line profiles are not clear for HNC at J=3--2 and J=4--3 (Figure 4a,e).

The observed HCN J=4--3 to CS J=7--6 flux ratio for IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE is estimated to be 5 (Tables 5 and 16), which is in the range occupied by AGNs izu16 , supporting the AGN-important scenario for IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE. We thus regard IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE and IRAS 22491 1808 as ULIRGs containing infrared-elusive, but (sub)millimeter detectable, extremely deeply buried AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 12112+0305 as a post-merger LINER/Seyfert system with two nuclei, PAH-dominated infrared nuclear emission, star-forming radio emission, and free-free absorption turnover.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12112+0305: classified as a LINER-type Kim98 or a Seyfert 2 galaxy Yuan10 , this system in a post-merger state with two merging nuclei Kim02 . This system shows an Infrared spectrum from the nuclear emission dominated by PAH emission features Veilleux09 , hence showing no AGN signature from the infrared energy diagnostics. Its radio SED is best-modeled by a single-component model (C) with a turnover at 0.26 GHz. Although our turnover frequency matches within uncertainty with that from the SYNAGE modeling 290 MHz Nandi21 ; the radio emission is due to starforming processes with the turnover due to FFA.

sources.summary: IRAS 12112+0305 is one of the interacting-pair ULIRGs; the paper finds H ii-like extranuclear ionization and a clear correlation across three line-ratio diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The sample of galaxies consists of six low- z ULIRGs (see general properties in table misulirgs ) covering a relative wide range of dynamical states of the merging process. Three of the galaxies (IRAS 08572+3915, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 14348 1447) are interacting pairs separated by projected distances of up to 6 kpc, while the rest of the galaxies (IRAS 15206+3342, IRAS 15250+3609, and IRAS 17208 0014) are more evolved, single nucleus ULIRGs, some with a light profile and overall kinematics close to that of intermediate mass ellipticals (e.g. IRAS 17208 0014, Genzel et al. 2001). The two-dimensional kinematic properties (velocity field and velocity dispersion) have been studied in detail before using integral field optical spectroscopy (see Colina et al. 2005 and references therein).

According to this ratio, LINER-like emission is found in the extended extranuclear regions in three systems: IRAS 14348 1447 , IRAS 15250+3609 , and IRAS 17208 0014 . Contrary, for IRAS 08572+3915 , IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 12112+0305 this line ratio suggests a dominant H ii like ionization.

Individually, two systems, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 14348 1447, show a clear correlation in the three line ratios. For three of the remaining systems, IRAS 08572+3915,IRAS 15206+3342 and IRAS 15250+3609, the range in velocity dispersion is relatively small to show the correlation.

sources.summary: The paper describes both nuclei of IRAS 12112+0305 as having L-band spectra whose PAH strength, flat continuum, lack of 3.4 micron absorption, and multiwavelength diagnostics favor starburst-powered infrared emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12112+0305 . The two nuclei in this source have similar L-band spectra. In both cases the high equivalent width of the 3.3 m line, the absence of 3.4 m absorption and the flat spectrum suggest a starburst origin for their infrared luminosity. This is in agreement with the classification at other wavelengths: in the optical, the [ OIII ] /H ratio, and the low flux of [ NII ] 6583 , [ SII ] 6716 and [ OI ] 6300 with respect to H , suggest a LINER classification ( veill99opt ). in the mid-IR the high equivalent width of the 7.7 m PAH emission feature suggests a pure starburst classification. in the hard X-rays no indication of AGN activity is found.

gmw_00003430.name: J0246
gmw_00003430.overview: J0246 is a candidate periodically variable quasar [arXiv:2008.12329]. Combined SDSS and DES light curves span more than four cycles of the candidate periodicity, with statistical evidence strongly favoring periodic models over purely stochastic variability [arXiv:2008.12329].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0246 as one of five candidate periodic quasars, with DES/SDSS light curves spanning more than four cycles and strong BIC evidence favoring periodic models over stochastic variability.

quotes.quote: Candidate Periodically Variable Quasars Using the method described in sec:method , we select five candidate periodically variable quasars from a parent sample of 625. They are: J024613.89 004028.2, J024703.24 010032.0, J024944.66 000036.8, J025214.67 002813.7, and J025406.26 + 002753.7 (hereafter J0246, J0247, J0249, J0252, and J0254 for short).

We focus on the other four candidates for the rest of the present paper. fig:lc_and_periodogram shows the SDSS and DES light curves and the GLS periodograms of the four candidate periodic quasars. By combining SDSS and DES the total time baseline spans more than four cycles of the candidate periodicity. tab:parameters lists the periodicity measurements for all four candidates in the griz bands, as well as the ACF periods and multi-band GLS periodogram. fig:lc_and_periodogram also shows archival light curves from publicly available data.

Among the four candidates, J0246 has consistently negative BIC differences for both periodic models compared to the null hypothesis in all bands. In particular, the BIC differences in the iz bands are <-10 , suggesting strong evidence that the periodic models are preferred over a pure stochastic model.

gmw_00003431.name: J0247
gmw_00003431.overview: J0247 is a candidate periodically variable quasar identified using multi-band SDSS and DES data [arXiv:2008.12329]. However, statistical testing shows that periodic models are not strongly preferred over pure stochastic variability for this source [arXiv:2008.12329].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0247 as a candidate periodic quasar with multi-band DES/SDSS periodicity evidence, but its BIC differences do not strongly favor the tested periodic models over stochastic variability.

quotes.quote: Candidate Periodically Variable Quasars Using the method described in sec:method , we select five candidate periodically variable quasars from a parent sample of 625. They are: J024613.89 004028.2, J024703.24 010032.0, J024944.66 000036.8, J025214.67 002813.7, and J025406.26 + 002753.7 (hereafter J0246, J0247, J0249, J0252, and J0254 for short).

We focus on the other four candidates for the rest of the present paper. fig:lc_and_periodogram shows the SDSS and DES light curves and the GLS periodograms of the four candidate periodic quasars. By combining SDSS and DES the total time baseline spans more than four cycles of the candidate periodicity. tab:parameters lists the periodicity measurements for all four candidates in the griz bands, as well as the ACF periods and multi-band GLS periodogram. fig:lc_and_periodogram also shows archival light curves from publicly available data.

For the other three candidates (i.e., J0247, J0249, and J0254), the BIC differences are small, which shows that the two periodic models are not preferred over the pure stochastic model. While this may seem at odds with our conclusion based on the GLS periodograms, it may be a result of the limitation of the specific periodic models we have considered.

gmw_00003432.name: J0249
gmw_00003432.overview: J0249 is a candidate periodically variable quasar identified from its light curve [arXiv:2008.12329]. This light curve displays an observed periodicity of three to five years, along with an additional periodogram peak at 1.5 years [arXiv:2008.12329].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0249 as a candidate periodic quasar; its light curve shows 3-5 year periodicity plus a significant 1.5-year peak, though tested periodic models are not BIC-preferred.

quotes.quote: Candidate Periodically Variable Quasars Using the method described in sec:method , we select five candidate periodically variable quasars from a parent sample of 625. They are: J024613.89 004028.2, J024703.24 010032.0, J024944.66 000036.8, J025214.67 002813.7, and J025406.26 + 002753.7 (hereafter J0246, J0247, J0249, J0252, and J0254 for short).

The GLS periodograms show that the four candidate periodic quasars have observed periodicity of 3--5 years (corresponding to rest-frame 1--2 years). In addition to the most significant periodogram peaks, two of the four candidates (i.e., J0249 and J0254) also show significant periodogram peaks at observed 1.5 years which are just above our 500 days threshold.

For the other three candidates (i.e., J0247, J0249, and J0254), the BIC differences are small, which shows that the two periodic models are not preferred over the pure stochastic model. While this may seem at odds with our conclusion based on the GLS periodograms, it may be a result of the limitation of the specific periodic models we have considered.

gmw_00003433.name: J0254
gmw_00003433.overview: J0254 is identified as a candidate periodically variable quasar [arXiv:2008.12329]. Its light curve displays an observed periodicity of three to five years, with an additional periodogram peak at an observed 1.5 years [arXiv:2008.12329].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0254 as a candidate periodic quasar; its light curve shows 3-5 year periodicity plus a significant 1.5-year peak, though tested periodic models are not BIC-preferred.

quotes.quote: Candidate Periodically Variable Quasars Using the method described in sec:method , we select five candidate periodically variable quasars from a parent sample of 625. They are: J024613.89 004028.2, J024703.24 010032.0, J024944.66 000036.8, J025214.67 002813.7, and J025406.26 + 002753.7 (hereafter J0246, J0247, J0249, J0252, and J0254 for short).

The GLS periodograms show that the four candidate periodic quasars have observed periodicity of 3--5 years (corresponding to rest-frame 1--2 years). In addition to the most significant periodogram peaks, two of the four candidates (i.e., J0249 and J0254) also show significant periodogram peaks at observed 1.5 years which are just above our 500 days threshold.

For the other three candidates (i.e., J0247, J0249, and J0254), the BIC differences are small, which shows that the two periodic models are not preferred over the pure stochastic model. While this may seem at odds with our conclusion based on the GLS periodograms, it may be a result of the limitation of the specific periodic models we have considered.

gmw_00003434.name: Lacerta I
gmw_00003434.overview: Lacerta I is a luminous, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:1305.5301]. Spectroscopic observations confirm that it is a satellite of the Andromeda Galaxy [arXiv:1408.5130], orbiting at an M31-centric distance of approximately 275 kpc [arXiv:1305.5301].

sources.summary: The paper confirms Lacerta I as an M31 dwarf satellite, measuring its systemic velocity, velocity dispersion, dynamical mass, mass-to-light ratio, and mean metallicity from Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: We present Keck II/DEIMOS spectroscopy of the three distant dwarf galaxies of M31 Lacerta I, Cassiopeia III, and Perseus I, recently discovered within the Pan-STARRS1 3 imaging survey. The systemic velocities of the three systems ( v_ ,helio = -198.4 1.1 , 371.6 0.7 , and 326 3 , respectively) confirm that they are satellites of M31. In the case of Lacerta I and Cassiopeia III, the high quality of the data obtained for 126 and 212 member stars, respectively, yields reliable constraints on their global velocity dispersions ( _ vr = 10.3 0.9 and 8.4 0.6 , respectively), leading to dynamical-mass estimates for both of 4 10^7 within their half-light radius. These translate to V band mass-to-light ratios of 15^ +12 _ 9 and 8^ +9 _ 5 in solar units.

We also use our spectroscopic data to determine the average metallicity of the 3 dwarf galaxies ( = -2.0 0.1 , 1.7 0.1 , and 2.0 0.2 , respectively). All these properties are typical of dwarf galaxy satellites of Andromeda with their luminosity and size.

sources.summary: The paper reports Lacerta I/Andromeda XXXI as a newly discovered, luminous, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy likely orbiting M31 at projected separation 20.3 degrees and M31-centric distance 275 kpc.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of two new dwarf galaxies, Lacerta I/Andromeda XXXI (Lac I/And XXXI) and Cassiopeia III/Andromeda XXXII (Cas III/And XXXII), in stacked Pan-STARRS1 r_ P1 and i_ P1 band imaging data. Both are luminous systems ( M_V 12 ) located at projected distances of 20.3 and 10.5 from M31. Lac I and Cas III are likely satellites of the Andromeda galaxy with heliocentric distances of 756^ +44 _ 28 and 772^ +61 _ 56 , respectively, and corresponding M31-centric distances of 275 7 and 144^ +6 _ 4 . The brightest of recent Local Group member discoveries, these two new dwarf galaxies owe their late discovery to their large sizes ( r_h = 4.2^ +0.4 _ 0.5 arcmin or 912^ +124 _ 93 for Lac I; r_h = 6.5^ +1.2 _ 1.0 arcmin or 1456 267 for Cas III), and consequently low surface brightness ( _0 26.0 mag/arcsec ^2 ), as well as to the lack of a systematic survey of regions at large radii from M31, close to the Galactic plane.

gmw_00003435.name: Cassiopeia III
gmw_00003435.overview: Cassiopeia III is a luminous, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:1305.5301]. Spectroscopic observations confirm that it is a satellite of the Andromeda Galaxy [arXiv:1408.5130], orbiting at an M31-centric distance of approximately 144 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1305.5301].

sources.summary: The paper confirms Cassiopeia III as an M31 dwarf satellite and derives its systemic velocity, dispersion, dynamical mass, mass-to-light ratio, and mean metallicity from Keck/DEIMOS member-star spectra.

quotes.quote: We present Keck II/DEIMOS spectroscopy of the three distant dwarf galaxies of M31 Lacerta I, Cassiopeia III, and Perseus I, recently discovered within the Pan-STARRS1 3 imaging survey. The systemic velocities of the three systems ( v_ ,helio = -198.4 1.1 , 371.6 0.7 , and 326 3 , respectively) confirm that they are satellites of M31. In the case of Lacerta I and Cassiopeia III, the high quality of the data obtained for 126 and 212 member stars, respectively, yields reliable constraints on their global velocity dispersions ( _ vr = 10.3 0.9 and 8.4 0.6 , respectively), leading to dynamical-mass estimates for both of 4 10^7 within their half-light radius. These translate to V band mass-to-light ratios of 15^ +12 _ 9 and 8^ +9 _ 5 in solar units.

We also use our spectroscopic data to determine the average metallicity of the 3 dwarf galaxies ( = -2.0 0.1 , 1.7 0.1 , and 2.0 0.2 , respectively). All these properties are typical of dwarf galaxy satellites of Andromeda with their luminosity and size.

sources.summary: The paper reports Cassiopeia III/Andromeda XXXII as a newly discovered, luminous, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy likely orbiting M31 at projected separation 10.5 degrees and M31-centric distance 144 kpc.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of two new dwarf galaxies, Lacerta I/Andromeda XXXI (Lac I/And XXXI) and Cassiopeia III/Andromeda XXXII (Cas III/And XXXII), in stacked Pan-STARRS1 r_ P1 and i_ P1 band imaging data. Both are luminous systems ( M_V 12 ) located at projected distances of 20.3 and 10.5 from M31. Lac I and Cas III are likely satellites of the Andromeda galaxy with heliocentric distances of 756^ +44 _ 28 and 772^ +61 _ 56 , respectively, and corresponding M31-centric distances of 275 7 and 144^ +6 _ 4 . The brightest of recent Local Group member discoveries, these two new dwarf galaxies owe their late discovery to their large sizes ( r_h = 4.2^ +0.4 _ 0.5 arcmin or 912^ +124 _ 93 for Lac I; r_h = 6.5^ +1.2 _ 1.0 arcmin or 1456 267 for Cas III), and consequently low surface brightness ( _0 26.0 mag/arcsec ^2 ), as well as to the lack of a systematic survey of regions at large radii from M31, close to the Galactic plane.

gmw_00003436.name: J173052.85-035247.2
gmw_00003436.overview: J173052.85-035247.2 is classified as a BL Lacertae object and serves as a candidate counterpart to the source 2FGL J1730.6-0353 [arXiv:1503.05196]. It has a redshift lower limit of 0.776 and lacks an associated NVSS radio counterpart [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J173052.85-035247.2 as a BL Lac counterpart candidate to 2FGL J1730.6-0353, measuring a redshift lower limit of 0.776 and emphasizing its lack of an NVSS radio counterpart.

quotes.quote: The blazar counterpart for one of these sources, \ J173052.85-035247.2 (candidate counterpart of 2FGL J1730.6-0353) appears intriguing. On the basis of our optical spectra, we classify the source as a BL Lac object and we put a lower limit on its redshift of 0.776 based on the detection of an intervening doublet system of Mg II ( _ obs =4965 4977 with EW_ obs =3.4 2.1 ; see also Figure fig:ugs6 ). However, this source is not associated with any NVSS sources, as expected for BL Lac objects. We note that, even if deeper radio observations detect emission from the source, blazars are traditionally defined as radio-loud sources on the basis of current radio data.

We classify \ J173052.85-035247.2 as a BL Lac although this source is not associated with any NVSS counterpart, as expected for BL Lac objects. The detection of an intervening doublet system of Mg II enabled us to set a lower limit on its redshift of 0.776.

gmw_00003437.name: J174507.82+015442.4
gmw_00003437.overview: J174507.82+015442.4 is an active galactic nucleus with a detected radio counterpart [arXiv:1503.05196]. It is a potential counterpart to the source 2FGL J1745.6+0203, and its redshift has been determined using broad emission lines [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper treats J174507.82+015442.4 as a possible 2FGL J1745.6+0203 counterpart with a radio detection and broad-line redshift, but says its blazar nature cannot be firmly established.

quotes.quote: There are two sources, \ J174507.82+015442.4 and \ J174526.95+020532.6, potential counterparts to 2FGL J1745.6+0203 found on the basis of the IR color selection method. According to our multifrequency investigation we note that only \ J174507.82+015442.4 has a radio counterpart, thus we conclude that \ J174526.95+020532.6 is a normal radio-quiet quasar contaminating our selection. However we cannot firmly establish the real blazar nature of \ J174507.82+015442.4 since the lack of multi-wavelength radio observations did not allow us to verify the flatness of its radio spectrum. For both objects we determined their redshifts based on broad emission lines (H and H are both present, see Figures fig:ugs7 fig:ugs8 ).

gmw_00003438.name: J174526.95+020532.6
gmw_00003438.overview: J174526.95+020532.6 is a broad-line radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1503.05196]. Its redshift has been determined based on the presence of broad hydrogen emission lines in its spectrum [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J174526.95+020532.6 as a broad-line radio-quiet quasar and likely contaminant in the infrared-color selection for the 2FGL J1745.6+0203 counterpart.

quotes.quote: There are two sources, \ J174507.82+015442.4 and \ J174526.95+020532.6, potential counterparts to 2FGL J1745.6+0203 found on the basis of the IR color selection method. According to our multifrequency investigation we note that only \ J174507.82+015442.4 has a radio counterpart, thus we conclude that \ J174526.95+020532.6 is a normal radio-quiet quasar contaminating our selection.
For both objects we determined their redshifts based on broad emission lines (H and H are both present, see Figures fig:ugs7 fig:ugs8 ).
One potential counterparts for 2FGL J1745.6+0203 found on the basis of the IR color selection method, namely \ J174526.95+020532.6, is a QSO that probably contaminates our selection method.

gmw_00003439.name: J162638.15-763855.5
gmw_00003439.overview: J162638.15-763855.5 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1503.05196] that serves as the counterpart to the gamma-ray source 2FGL J1626.0-7636 [arXiv:1503.05196]. Its optical spectrum exhibits both weak emission and absorption lines, which place the active galaxy at a redshift of 0.1050 [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J162638.15-763855.5 as a BL Lac counterpart to 2FGL J1626.0-7636, using weak emission lines plus emission and absorption features to measure z = 0.1050.

quotes.quote: We classify the \ J162638.15-763855.5 counterpart to 2FGL J1626.0-7636 as a BL Lac since its emission lines have EW < 5 . The detection of emission ( [ O I ] with EW_ obs =2.3 , the [ S II ] doublet _ obs =7421 7438 with EW_ obs =2.5 2.4 ) and absorption lines (G band, Mg I with EW_ obs =4.9 and Na I with EW_ obs =3.3 ), enable us to measure a redshift of 0.1050 (see Figure fig:agu8 ).

Two of them are QSOs, while for one of them, namely \ J162638.15-763855.5, we have been able to detect some features in the optical spectrum, both in emission and in absorption, leading to a redshift measurement of 0.1050.

gmw_00003440.name: J184931.74+274800.8
gmw_00003440.overview: J184931.74+274800.8 is an active galactic nucleus associated with the gamma-ray source 2FGL J1849.5+2744 [arXiv:1503.05196]. Optical observations reveal a nearly featureless spectrum, confirming its classification as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper says J184931.74+274800.8 had been classified as a BZB; its new nearly featureless spectrum confirms BL Lac nature but not the previously proposed redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: The optical spectra of the AGUs associated with the ray source 2FGL J1849.5+2744 are also published in Shaw et al. (2013a). The source is listed as a BZB in the 2LAC catalog, although the optical spectrum was not yet available. The optical spectrum collected by us for this object ( \ J184931.74+274800.8) is nearly featureless (see Figure fig:agu9 ), so we are not able to confirm the lower limit z estimate (i.e., 1.466) of this source proposed by Shaw et al. (2013a) on the basis of Mg II absorption doublet.
The AGU associated with the \ J184931.74+274800.8 has been classified as a BZB in the 2LAC paper but at that time there was no optical spectra. Our observations confirm its BL Lac nature.

gmw_00003441.name: BZB J0244-5819
gmw_00003441.overview: BZB J0244-5819 is a BL Lacertae object candidate [arXiv:1503.05196]. Its optical spectrum exhibits absorption features, including Ca H+K, G band, Mg I, and Na I, which suggest a redshift lower limit of approximately 0.265 [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper confirms BZB J0244-5819 as a BL Lac candidate and derives a redshift lower limit of about 0.265 from absorption features possibly arising in the host or intervening systems.

quotes.quote: For one BZB, BZB J0244-5819, we have been able to estimate a lower limit on its redshift, z 0.265, on the basis of absorption features in its optical spectrum. Our observation of \ J024440.30-581954.5 show the doublet Ca H+K ( _ obs =4975 5017 with EW_ obs =0.7 1 ), G band, Mg I ( EW_ obs =1.5 ) and Na I ( EW_ obs =1.1 ) absorption features that could be due to the host galaxy and/or to intervening systems (see Figure fig:bzb1 ).

For one of them, BZB J0244-5819, we have been able to set a lower limit on their redshifts on the basis of absorption systems (Ca H+K, G band, Mg I and Na I) that could be due to the host galaxy and/or to intervening systems.

gmw_00003442.name: BZB J1359-3746
gmw_00003442.overview: BZB J1359-3746 is classified as a BL Lacertae object and a blazar [arXiv:1503.05196]. Although a previous study reported a redshift of 0.334 based on a calcium break, subsequent optical spectroscopy shows a featureless continuum [arXiv:1503.05196].

sources.summary: The paper notes prior spectra for BZB J1359-3746 with a calcium-break redshift, but its own optical spectrum is featureless and does not confirm that result.

quotes.quote: We note that BZB J1359-3746, BZB J1649+5235 and BZB J1809+2910 also have published optical spectra for their candidate counterparts released in Shaw et al. (2013b). In addition, Shaw et al. (2013b) reported the spectrum of BZB J1359-3746, with the detection of the Calcium break that allowed them to determine a redshift of 0.334. We cannot confirm this result since our optical spectrum of \ J135949.71-374600.7 shows a featureless continuum (see Figure fig:bzb3 ).

gmw_00003443.name: WISE J090924.01+000211.1
gmw_00003443.overview: WISE J090924.01+000211.1 is an extremely luminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.871 [arXiv:2104.02462]. It hosts a luminous, unobscured type-1 active galactic nucleus that may be in a short-lived blowout phase [arXiv:2104.02462].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISE J090924.01+000211.1 as a z=1.871 extremely luminous infrared galaxy hosting a luminous unobscured type-1 AGN, with SED modeling confirming its ELIRG luminosity and short-lived blowout phase.

quotes.quote: In this study, we investigate the X-ray properties of WISE J090924.01+000211.1 (WISEJ0909+0002), an extremely luminous infrared (IR) galaxy (ELIRG) at z_ spec = 1.871 in the eROSITA final equatorial depth survey (eFEDS). WISEJ0909+0002 is a WISE 22 m source, located in the GAMA-09 field, detected by eROSITA during the performance and verification phase. The corresponding optical spectrum indicates that this object is a type-1 active galactic nucleus (AGN). Observations from eROSITA combined with Chandra and XMM-Newton archival data indicate a very luminous ( L (2--10 keV) = ( 2.1 0.2) 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 ) unobscured AGN with a power-law photon index of = 1.73 _ 0.15 ^ +0.16 , and an absorption hydrogen column density of (N_ H / cm ^ 2 ) < 21.0 .

The IR luminosity was estimated to be L_ IR = (1.79 0.09) 10^ 14 L_ from spectral energy distribution modeling based on 22 photometric data (X-ray to far-IR) with X-CIGALE , which confirmed that WISEJ0909+0002 is an ELIRG. A remarkably high L_ IR despite very low N_ H would indicate that we are witnessing a short-lived phase in which hydrogen gas along the line of sight is blown outwards, whereas warm and hot dust heated by AGNs still exist.

gmw_00003444.name: PGC 2402248
gmw_00003444.overview: PGC 2402248 is an extreme blazar [arXiv:2201.02454] and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2007.12609] observed in a low activity state [arXiv:2007.12609, arXiv:2212.03064]. Its host galaxy is dominated by a classical bulge [arXiv:2004.11359] with an old, metal-rich stellar population and no ongoing star formation [arXiv:2201.02454].

sources.summary: The paper classifies Swift J0733.9+5156 as a low-state BL Lac, supported by its optical break, blue continuum contribution, and multiwavelength blazar evidence.

quotes.quote: One further object presents an optical spectrum similar to an elliptical galaxy, i.e. Swift J0733.9+5156, at redshift z=0.064 0.002 . As we obtained a contrast break value of Br_ 4000 =0.23 0.03 from it, we classified it as a BL Lac object observed in a low activity state. The value for this source means that there is evidence of a blue contribution to its continuum that cannot be attributed to a regular elliptical galaxy nature. This indicates that, even though its spectrum is similar to an elliptical galaxy, we classify it as a BL Lac object which was observed in a low activity state. During such phases, the jet of a BL Lac object does not hide the host galaxy contribution but manifests itself as a blue excess Laurent98,Raiteri16 .

Indeed, this object has been classified as a `beamed AGN' in the Swift105 catalogue, which according to the definition reported in Swift105 means that it is an AGN but not a Seyfert `based on the presence and shape of optical emission lines in the literature'' Oh18 . Moreover, the positions of its X-ray, optical and radio counterparts are consistent with one another and all lie within the positional uncertainty of the ray source 3FGL J0733.5+5153 Acero15 , which was classified as a BCU (Blazar Candidate of Uncertain type). This means that it shows typical general characteristics of a blazar in the GeV energy band.

sources.summary: The paper models PGC 2402248, also 2WHSP J073326.7+515354, as a stationary EHBL/blazar whose MAGIC TeV spectrum is fit by a photohadronic model in a low-emission state.

quotes.quote: The MAGIC telescopes detected multi-TeV gamma-rays on April 19, 2018 for the first time from the EHBL PGC 2402248 which was simultaneously observed in multiwavelength by several other instruments. The broad band spectral energy distribution of the source is conventionally modelled using the leptonic and the hadronic models. Due to the success of the photohadronic model in interpreting the enigmatic very high-energy (VHE) flaring events from many high-energy blazars, we extend this model to explain the VHE events from PGC 2402248 observed by MAGIC telescopes and compare our results with other models. We conclude that the photohadronic fits are comparable and even fare better than most other models. Furthermore, we show that the spectrum is not hard and is in a low emission state.

In this program it selected the object PGC 2402248 (also known as 2WHSP J073326.7+515354) from the 2WHSP catalogue  Chang:2017 on the basis of its high synchrotron peak frequency ^ peak _ syn =10^ 17.9 Hz . The MAGIC telescopes observed the source from January 23 to April 19, 2018 (MJD 58141-58227) for 25 nights for a total of 23.4 h.
