On 19th April, for the first time, it detected TeV rays from the blazar PGC 2402248.

sources.summary: The paper applies a gamma-ray absorption method to the extreme BL Lac PGC 2402248, interpreting a 250 GeV spectral dip as interaction with narrow-line-region photons along the jet.

quotes.quote: We have first applied this method to a BL Lac object recently discovered at TeV gamma-ray energies. This source, named PGC 2402248 (or 2WHSP J073326.7+515354, z = 0.065 ), has been published by the MAGIC Collaboration in 2019 MAGIC_paper_pgc . In fig:pgc_gammaray_data we show the available dataset of the intrinsic spectrum at gamma-ray energies extending up to 5 TeV (already de-absorbed by the interaction with the extragalactic background light, EBL, see e.g. Hauser:2001, Franceschini17 ), which shows a continuously increasing flux up to about 100 GeV. At that energy, Fermi LAT and MAGIC data are well compatible, but at slightly higher energies of 250 GeV the flux decreases to Flux _ 250 GeV 1 2 Flux _ 100 GeV . Then, the flux grows up again gradually to rejoin the expected emission at about a few TeV.

Assuming that the absorption feature in the spectrum of the source is due to interaction as described in Section sec:gammagammainteraction , we can estimate the physical parameters of the large-scale interacting region. We note that the maximum of the interaction happens at about 250 GeV, and then we assume that the target photon field has energy in the optical-UV of about 4 eV. This value is compatible with the strongest emission lines of the spectrum of well-studied narrow-line radio galaxies e.g. osterbrock and also with the faint emission lines emerging in the optical spectrum of our source PGC 2402248 in pgc_optical_spectrum .

sources.summary: The paper measures the EHBL host galaxy redshift, stellar population, bulge-dominated morphology, and black hole mass from optical spectroscopy and imaging.

quotes.quote: In this work we report on the properties of 2WHSP J073326.7+515354 host galaxy in the optical band, which is one of the few EHBLs detected at TeV energies. We present the first measurement of the distance of the source, z =0.06504 0.00002 (velocity dispersion =237 9 km s^ 1 ). We also perform a detailed study of the stellar population of its host galaxy. We find that the mass-weighted mean stellar age is 11.72 0.06 Gyr and the mean metallicity [ M/H ] =0.159 0.016 .

In addition, a morphological study of the host galaxy is also carried out. The surface brightness distribution is modelled by a composition of a dominant classical bulge ( R_e=3.77 1 or equivalently 4.74 kpc) plus an unresolved source which corresponds to the active nucleus. The black hole mass is estimated using both the mass relation with the velocity dispersion and the absolute magnitude from the bulge yielding comparable results: (4.8 0.9) 10^8 M_ and (3.7 1.0) 10^8 M_ , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper discusses PGC 2402248 as an extreme TeV blazar/EHBL at z=0.065 with a gamma-ray absorption feature, old metal-rich host population, and LINER-like narrow lines.

quotes.quote: We discuss recent observations of an extreme blazar named 2WHSP J073326.7+515354 (or PGC 2402248), which shows evidence of such an absorption feature in its gamma-ray spectrum and narrow emission lines in the optical spectrum, suggesting the presence of narrow-line regions in its large-scale environment.

An interesting publication by the MAGIC Collaboration hereafter PaperI MAGIC_paper_pgc has announced the detection of a new TeV gamma-ray source named PGC 2402248 (also named 2WHSP J073326.7+515354). The campaign of simultaneous multi-wavelength (MWL) observations provided by PaperI confirmed that this source is a new EHBL with synchrotron peak located at _ peak ^ sync 10^ 17.8 0.3 Hz and gamma-ray spectrum extending up to 5 TeV. The redshift of z = 0.065 was reported for the first time by pgc_optical_spectrum hereafter PaperII - with a specific optical campaign.

Specifically, the galaxy is characterized by old stellar populations showing high metallicity and no evidence of ongoing star formation, with the latest star formation episodes happening at least 6 Gyr ago. After removing the emission of the host galaxy, the optical spectrum shows several surviving emission lines. The diagnostic diagrams of Kewley2006 applied to these lines support the classification of this source as a LINER (galaxy with low-ionization nuclear emission-line region) of the AGN type.

gmw_00003445.name: SR01
gmw_00003445.overview: SR01 is an early-type galaxy characterized by an extended star-forming region [arXiv:1206.5805]. It features an irregular ultraviolet ring within an extreme ultraviolet disk spanning approximately 70 kiloparsecs, with a ruffled morphology that suggests past galaxy interactions [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR01 is an extended-star-formation ETG with an irregular UV ring, an extreme roughly 70 kpc UV disk, oversized optical disk, and ruffled morphology suggestive of interactions.

quotes.quote: Another detection shown in Figure fig:ha is of SR01, classified as an irregular UV ring within extreme disk.

Irregular rings in extreme disks and Giant LSBs The two galaxies that we classify as irregular rings in extreme disks (SR01 and SR07) deserve special attention because they have whopping UV disks ( D 70 kpc) and their optical sizes by far exceed what is typical for their mass, which makes them potentially very rare objects. SR01 and SR07 are very similar to nearby giant low surface brightness galaxies (LSBs), such as Malin 1, Malin 2 and UGC6614 Giant LSBs are different from more common dwarf LSBs.
The ``ruffled'' UV appearance of SR01 and SR07 is strongly suggestive of interactions and less indicative of secular processes.

gmw_00003446.name: SR02
gmw_00003446.overview: SR02 is classified as a later-type, wide-ring galaxy [arXiv:1206.5805]. The galaxy features a flocculent ring structure that is restricted to the ring region and is unusually large for its mass [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR02 is described as a regular wide-ring, later-type galaxy whose flocculent ring is restricted to the ring region and is unusually large for its mass.

quotes.quote: For example, SR02 appears to have flocculent structure in the ring, but unlike typical flocculent spiral galaxies (e.g., NGC4414) this structure is restricted to the ring, and is not present in the inner region.
The only wide ring galaxy that was classified as a later type (SR02, SBa) does not have very prominent arms.
Figure fig:optsize_mass shows that most members of this subclass lie above the expected size for their mass, with the most extreme case by far being SR02, with its wide, flocculent ring.

gmw_00003447.name: SR03
gmw_00003447.overview: SR03 is an early-type galaxy characterized by a conspicuous optical ring that is detached from its central bulge [arXiv:1206.5805]. A stellar bar extends all the way to this ring, and the galaxy's overall optical size is significantly smaller than typical trends for its class [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR03 uniquely has a conspicuous detached optical ring, a bar reaching the ring, and an underestimated optical size that places it below the sample size trend.

quotes.quote: SR03 is the only galaxy in our sample that has a conspicuous optical ring that is detached from the bulge of the galaxy.

Namely, in SR03 the bar extends all the way to the ring, making it presumably a co-rotation ring, while in locally studied galaxies the co-rotation ring is basically an inner ring, imbedded in a disk or surrounded by a larger outer ring buta_combes,buta_morph .

Overall, ESF-ETGs tend to be larger than the corresponding red-sequence ETGs, though they range from significantly larger to similar in size, and even a few undersized SR03, the galaxy with the detached optical ring, is the only galaxy in the \ sample significantly below the trend.

gmw_00003448.name: SR05
gmw_00003448.overview: SR05 is a possible galaxy merger candidate that features an offset optical disk and a faint companion [arXiv:1206.5805]. The galaxy contains offset inner and outer rings, both of which display bright features [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR05 has offset inner and outer rings with bright features, plus an offset optical disk and faint companion, making it a possible merger candidate.

quotes.quote: In SR05 the outer ring is more complete but is somewhat offset from the inner ring, a possible aftermath of an interaction. Both the inner and the outer rings of SR05 have bright features in them.
SR05, with its offset outer ring, offset optical disk and a faint companion, is a possible merger candidate.

gmw_00003449.name: SR07
gmw_00003449.overview: SR07 is a rare galaxy classified as having an irregular ring in an extreme disk [arXiv:1206.5805]. Its ruffled ultraviolet appearance suggests that the galaxy has undergone interactions [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR07 shares the rare irregular-ring, extreme-disk class with SR01, resembles ESO 381--47 in far-UV morphology, and has ruffled UV structure suggestive of interactions.

quotes.quote: Irregular rings in extreme disks and Giant LSBs The two galaxies that we classify as irregular rings in extreme disks (SR01 and SR07) deserve special attention because they have whopping UV disks ( D 70 kpc) and their optical sizes by far exceed what is typical for their mass, which makes them potentially very rare objects. SR01 and SR07 are very similar to nearby giant low surface brightness galaxies (LSBs), such as Malin 1, Malin 2 and UGC6614 Giant LSBs are different from more common dwarf LSBs.
Interestingly, the far-UV morphology of ESO 381--47 observed by \ resembles that of SR07, with a main irregular ring surrounded by more diffuse and filamentary emission outside of it.
The ``ruffled'' UV appearance of SR01 and SR07 is strongly suggestive of interactions and less indicative of secular processes.

gmw_00003450.name: SR11
gmw_00003450.overview: SR11 is an early-type galaxy that exhibits a striking ultraviolet morphology [arXiv:1206.5805]. The galaxy features a strong bar, an inner ring, and arms that form an outer pseudoring structure driven by bar-induced dynamical processes [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR11 has striking UV morphology, arms forming a pseudoring, and a strong bar with inner-ring and outer-pseudoring structure explained by bar-induced dynamics.

quotes.quote: SR11 presents one of the most stunning UV morphologies in our sample, which is very different from what one expects to see in a red sequence early-type galaxy.
On the other hand, SR11, with arms forming a pseudoring, has a strong bar.

Altogether SR11, with its bar, inner ring, and outer pseudoring is very well explained by bar-induced dynamical processes, convincingly reproduced in numerical simulations schwarz_pasa,athanassoula To summarize, we find that among the ESF-ETGs the barred and non-barred galaxies appear quite similar in the UV.

gmw_00003451.name: SR13
gmw_00003451.overview: SR13 is an early-type galaxy that contains a highly asymmetric star-forming patch encompassing its optical center [arXiv:1206.5805]. This star-forming region is interpreted as a gas-rich dwarf galaxy in the final stages of coalescence and disruption [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR13 contains an asymmetric star-forming patch near the optical center; the paper interprets it as a later coalescence stage of a disrupted gas-rich dwarf.

quotes.quote: SR13 has a highly asymmetric star forming patch that encompasses the optical center of the galaxy but is brightest 5 kpc in projected separation from the center.
The star-forming patches in SR19, SR27 and SR30 could be gas-rich dwarfs being disrupted and absorbed by the ETG, but not yet having reached the center like SR13.
In SR13 the coalescence is almost complete.

gmw_00003452.name: SR14
gmw_00003452.overview: SR14 is an early-type galaxy classified as an SB0/a system [arXiv:1206.5805]. It features a ultraviolet disk with a central hole, a bar that may have cleared the center, and possible optical arms or a ring structure [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR14 has a UV disk with a central hole, possible optical arms or ring structure, and a bar that may be responsible for clearing the center.

quotes.quote: For SR14 (UV disk with a central hole) \ appears as a ring whose extent roughly follows the brighter, inner portion of the UV disk.
SR12 has well defined arms, while for SR14 (SB0/a) there is some ambiguity as to whether the arms are the brighter portions of the ring faintly visible in the optical.
In SR14 a bar is present and it may be responsible for the central clearing.

gmw_00003453.name: SR15
gmw_00003453.overview: SR15 is a lower-mass galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 9.5 solar masses in logarithmic scale [arXiv:1206.5805]. The object exhibits central ultraviolet emission and possibly contains several ultraviolet-bright knots [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR15 is a lower-mass small-scale-SF object with possible UV-bright knots, central UV emission, and a possible analogy to high-redshift blue spheroidals.

quotes.quote: Two exceptions are SR15 and SR25 with much lower masses of M_*=9.5 and M_*=9.6 respectively (Figure fig:mass_fuvr ).
Due to the high level of noise it is difficult to say more about the structure of these regions; SR15 possibly shows several UV bright knots.
Given their low mass, SR15 and SR25 could represent the local analogs of the high-redshift ``blue spheroidals'' of im_faber .

gmw_00003454.name: SR16
gmw_00003454.overview: SR16 is classified as a peculiar S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1206.5805]. The galaxy is dominated by a bright, unresolved central ultraviolet source, which may potentially be composed of old stars [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR16 is dominated by an unresolved central UV source; the paper classifies it as a peculiar S0 and says the UV source is unclear, possibly old stars.

quotes.quote: SR16 is dominated by a bright unresolved central source.

For the unresolved UV sources, one (SR16) is a peculiar S0 and the other (SR21) is an E. Given that the source of UV in SR16 is not clear (possibly old stars), we cannot place much weight on the fact that this galaxy, unlike most others in the sample, does show some evidence for disrupted morphology.

gmw_00003455.name: SR17
gmw_00003455.overview: SR17 is an early-type galaxy that lacks a ultraviolet ring [arXiv:1206.5805]. It exhibits asymmetric, tidally induced tails and has a nearby companion galaxy that shows optical disturbance, indicating a clear ongoing interaction [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR17 lacks a UV ring, shows tidal-looking tails and disturbed optical companions, and has the clearest UV and optical interaction signatures in the sample.

quotes.quote: SR17 is also the only ESF-ETG for which there is no evidence of a UV ring.
One galaxy (SR17) appears to have tidally induced tails, and there is a nearby companion that shows optical disturbance as well.
Of all the galaxies in our sample SR17 most clearly shows UV and optical signatures indicative of an interaction, with asymmetric arms that appear tidal in origin.

gmw_00003456.name: SR18
gmw_00003456.overview: SR18 is a galaxy featuring a compact optical source and an incomplete ultraviolet ring that may simply be fainter on one side [arXiv:1206.5805]. The galaxy also exhibits extended features aligned along the major axis of its optical disk [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR18 is a possible exception among regular rings, with an incomplete UV ring, compact optical source, and extended features along the optical disk major axis.

quotes.quote: A possible exception is the ring of SR18, which appears incomplete, but is perhaps just much fainter on one side.
Altogether, regular rings (with possible exception of SR18) suggest that the SF is not a result of violent processes such as galaxy interactions or mergers.
A possible exception is SR18, which shows what looks like an incomplete UV ring coincident with a compact optical source, and has some extended features along the major axis of the optical disk.

gmw_00003457.name: SR19
gmw_00003457.overview: SR19 is an early-type galaxy featuring an off-center star-forming patch with multiple bright ultraviolet clumps [arXiv:1206.5805]. This patch may be a gas-rich dwarf companion galaxy undergoing disruption and absorption [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR19 has an off-center star-forming patch with multiple UV clumps; after removing companion flux, its central UV emission resembles an old-star UV upturn.

quotes.quote: Next, in SR19, the star-forming patch, located some 9 kpc from the center in projection, again shows multiple bright UV clumps.

The star-forming patches in SR19, SR27 and SR30 could be gas-rich dwarfs being disrupted and absorbed by the ETG, but not yet having reached the center like SR13. After accounting for the UV flux in off-center companions, the central unresolved emission in SR19, SR27 and SR30 does not have a strong UV excess in FUV and is consistent with the old-star UV upturn.
The companion looks more distorted in the UV in the case of SR30, and even more so in SR19.

gmw_00003458.name: SR27
gmw_00003458.overview: SR27 is an early-type galaxy that features a narrow, extended ultraviolet structure [arXiv:1206.5805]. It has a relatively undisturbed companion galaxy, though this may represent a chance superposition with an unrelated ultraviolet-bright source [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR27 has a narrow extended UV feature and a relatively undisturbed possible companion, leaving open a chance superposition with an unrelated UV-bright galaxy.

quotes.quote: In SR27 the extended UV feature shows much less structure and is quite narrow.
In SR27 and SR30 the UV morphology of the companions is relatively undisturbed, so these are candidate interlopers.
Therefore, it is quite conceivable that either SR27 or SR30 is a chance superposition of an ETG and an unrelated UV-bright galaxy.

gmw_00003459.name: SR28
gmw_00003459.overview: SR28 is a highly inclined galaxy with an incomplete outer ring that may represent a former spiral arm [arXiv:1206.5805]. The galaxy also exhibits possible ultraviolet arms, which may indicate that it is currently fading [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR28 shows an incomplete outer ring or former spiral arm, uncertain UV structure because of high inclination, and possible UV arms consistent with fading.

quotes.quote: SR28 has an incomplete outer ring, which may also be a part of a (former) spiral arm.
UV structure in SR28 is difficult to determine due to the high inclination.
SR28 may show evidence of UV arms, so it could be another example of the fading category.

gmw_00003460.name: SR30
gmw_00003460.overview: SR30 features an off-center, elongated star-forming patch located at a projected distance of 9 kpc [arXiv:1206.5805]. This patch is responsible for 80 percent of the system's ultraviolet flux, while the undisturbed morphology of its companion suggests the system may be a chance superposition [arXiv:1206.5805].

sources.summary: SR30 has an off-center elongated star-forming patch contributing most UV flux, while the companion morphology leaves open a chance superposition interpretation.

quotes.quote: Finally, in SR30, the star-forming patch (at a projected distance of 9 kpc) shows somewhat non-uniform, elongated structure and accounts for 80 of the UV flux.
In SR27 and SR30 the UV morphology of the companions is relatively undisturbed, so these are candidate interlopers.
Therefore, it is quite conceivable that either SR27 or SR30 is a chance superposition of an ETG and an unrelated UV-bright galaxy.

gmw_00003461.name: W0010+3236
gmw_00003461.overview: W0010+3236 is an active galaxy that was identified based on its WISE colors [arXiv:2010.10641]. Near-infrared spectroscopy of the galaxy shows a featureless spectrum, leaving its redshift unconstrained [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W0010+3236 is included for WISE colors and similar spectra despite failing dropout criteria; NIRES shows no significant features, leaving redshift unconstrained.

quotes.quote: Note that W0010+3236, W0116-0505, and W0514-1217 do not satisfy the W1W2-dropout criteria, but are included due to the WISE colors and similar rest-frame optical spectra.
W0010+3236, W1905+5802, and W2235+1605 lack previously published redshifts. The NIRES spectra of W0010+3236 and W1905+5802 show no significant features, preventing a determination.
W0010+3236 W1905+5802, and W2016-0041 have featureless NIRES spectra.

gmw_00003462.name: W0116-0505
gmw_00003462.overview: W0116-0505 is a heavily obscured, hyper-luminous quasar at redshift 3.173 classified as a hot, dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:2206.04093]. It is a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1712.00031] that hosts a massive outflow [arXiv:2010.10641] and exhibits strongly polarized rest-frame ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2206.04093].

sources.summary: The paper classifies WISE J011601.41--050504.0 as a z=3.173 Hot DOG, measures strong rest-UV polarization, and argues its blue excess is scattered AGN light, possibly from dust or outflow.

quotes.quote: We report on VLT/FORS2 imaging polarimetry observations in the R_ special band of WISE J011601.41--050504.0 (W0116--0505), a heavily obscured hyper-luminous quasar at z=3.173 classified as a Hot, Dust-Obscured Galaxy (Hot DOG) based on its mid-IR colors. Recently, assef20 identified W0116--0505 as having excess rest-frame optical/UV emission, and concluded this excess emission is most likely scattered light from the heavily obscured AGN. We find that the broad-band rest-frame UV flux is strongly linearly polarized (10.8 1.9 , with a polarization angle of 74 9 deg), confirming this conclusion.

We analyze these observations in the context of a simple model based on scattering either by free electrons or by optically thin dust, assuming a classical dust torus with polar openings. Both can replicate the degree of polarization and the luminosity of the scattered component for a range of geometries and column densities, but we argue that optically thin dust in the ISM is the more likely scenario. We also explore the possibility that the scattering medium corresponds to an outflow recently identified for W0116--0505.

This is a feasible option if the outflow component is bi-conical with most of the scattering occurring at the base of the receding outflow. In this scenario the quasar would still be obscured even if viewed face on, but might appear as a reddened type 1 quasar once the outflow has expanded.

sources.summary: The paper detects W0116--0505 in X-rays, fits it with MYTorus, and identifies it as a Compton-thick QSO candidate with unusually high intrinsic X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: W0116--0505 According to Eq.  PB , W0116--0505 is detected in both the soft and hard bands with P_B 5 10^ 4 and 70 net counts in the 0.5-7 keV band (see Tab.  phot_tab ). We performed a basic spectral analysis using the MYTorus model in XSPEC and using the Cash statistics Cash79 .

In particular, the column density is constrained in the range (1.0-1.5) 10^ 24 , making W0116--0505 a Compton-thick QSO candidate. We computed the rest-frame iron K equivalent width ( EW ) following the procedure described in the MYTorus manual, where caveats are also discussed, and found EW=0.9 0.2 keV, consistent with the expectations for Compton-thick AGNs. Its intrinsic X-ray luminosity is the highest among Hot DOGs currently detected in X-ray observations.

sources.summary: The paper finds W0116--0505 has its targeted line redshifted out of the reference window, relies on additional CO observations, and derives a large positive far-infrared redshift offset.

quotes.quote: We know that for two sources, W0116--0505 and W0410--0913, the line is redshifted completely and partially, respectively, out of the frequency range covered by the reference SPW (and side-band) used to target . This is based on additional ALMA observations later obtained for these two sources, which targeted the mid-J CO(4 3) and CO(6 5) transitions GL2021 , respectively, as well as on the shallower observations of CO(4 3) analyzed in Fan2018 for W0410--0913. In addition, JVLA observations of the CO(1 0) line of both sources have also been recently presented in Penney2020 .

For both galaxies, the mid-J CO lines were clearly detected in the reddest SPW, which was originally dedicated to probe the dust continuum, allowing for a new redshift determination based on these additional far-IR data, z _ CO .
The velocity offsets of z _ CO with respect to z _ UV for W0116--0505 and W0410--0913 are v 1250 73 and 2487 66 , respectively.

sources.summary: W0116-0505 is included despite failing dropout criteria, is ambiguously AGN-dominated without H-alpha, and has the largest mass outflow rate in the observed sample.

quotes.quote: Note that W0010+3236, W0116-0505, and W0514-1217 do not satisfy the W1W2-dropout criteria, but are included due to the WISE colors and similar rest-frame optical spectra.
The remaining targets are placed in the AGN-dominated region, though W0410-0913 and W0116-0505 are ambiguous due to the lack of an \ detection.
The mass outflow rate for W0116-0505 is larger than that of any other observed Hot DOG, while the velocities of the outflows in W0410-0913 and W0220+0137 are the largest observed.

gmw_00003463.name: W0255+3345
gmw_00003463.overview: The galaxy W0255+3345 is an active galactic nucleus with a low Eddington accretion ratio [arXiv:2010.10641]. Despite this low accretion rate, the galaxy hosts active outflows and exhibits a luminosity dominated by its narrow emission-line component [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W0255+3345 has narrow-dominated luminosity, is excluded from Balmer SFR estimates by BPT placement, and shows outflows despite a low Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: However, in W0220+0137, W0255+3345, W0912+7741, and W2216+0723, the majority of the \ luminosity is in the narrow component, and W0338+1941 and W1724+3455 entirely lack a broad/blueshifted component, suggesting significant variation in the properties of the broad component between objects.
We exclude W0255+3345, W0514-1217, W0912+07741, W1801+1543, W2216+0713, and W2313-2417 from the SFR_ Balmer calculation based on the BPT placement in the left panel of Figure bpt .

While the radiation pressure from super-Eddington accretion provides a possible mechanism for driving the observed outflows, the lack of observed outflows in W1724+3455 despite f_ Edd 1 and the presence of such outflows in W0255+3345 and W2216+0723 despite a low value of f_ Edd suggests \ outflows may not be uniquely linked to the accretion rate.

gmw_00003464.name: W0338+1941
gmw_00003464.overview: W0338+1941 is a hot dust-obscured galaxy classified as star-formation-dominated [arXiv:2010.10641]. It displays a unique emission line profile and hosts a fast outflow detected in its H-alpha profile [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W0338+1941 has a unique line profile, star-formation-dominated classification, and the sample’s only clear fast H-alpha outflow, resembling other Hot DOG outflows differently.

quotes.quote: W0338+1941 displays a unique \ line profile among the observed targets.

W0338+1941 and W1724+3455 lack broad \ emission and are unambiguously in the star-formation dominated region, while W2238+2653 and W1835+4355 are near the starburst/AGN transition, with the classification depending on the inclusion of the broad/blueshifted \ emission in the / \ ratio.

W0338+1941 is the only Hot DOG in the sample to show clear evidence of a fast outflow in the \ profile. While the \ profile does not show any broad/blueshifted component, the width and blueshift of the broad \ component are 4300 \ and 1800 respectively, comparable to the broad \ kinematics seen in other Hot DOGs. The lack of an observed outflow in \ suggests the broad/blueshifted emission is due to \ rather than the \ doublet, which has a comparable critical density to the \ doublet. This suggests W0338+1941 hosts an outflow similar to those seen in other Hot DOGs, but at a higher density and/or lower ionization which results in the outflow being visible in the \ profile and not , in contrast with other Hot DOGs.

gmw_00003465.name: W0410-0913
gmw_00003465.overview: W0410-0913 is a hyper-luminous, heavily dust-obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1711.10615, arXiv:2303.16227] that exhibits disk-like rotation kinematics [arXiv:2303.16227]. The galaxy is surrounded by 24 Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies [arXiv:2303.16227] and hosts high-velocity molecular outflows [arXiv:1711.10615, arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: The paper identifies W0410 0913 as a hyper-luminous dust-obscured QSO with ALMA CO emission offset from its optical redshift and asymmetric velocity structure suggesting possible outflow.

quotes.quote: We select three hyper-luminous, heavily dust-obscured QSOs (W0149+2350, W0220+0137 and W0410 0913) from a new population recently discovered in Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer ( WISE , wright2010 ) all-sky survey, by using a so-called `` W1W2 dropout" method wu2012,eisenhardt2012 . The criterion is to select objects which are prominent in the WISE 12 m ( W3 ) or 22 m ( W4 ) bands, and faint or undetected in the 3.4 m ( W1 ) and 4.6 m ( W2 ) bands. This selection is very effective for finding high luminosity dust-obscured galaxies with dominant hot dust emission mostly at redshift z 1-4 assef2015a . Using X-ray observations stern2014,piconcelli2015,ricci2017 and the detailed spectral energy distribution (SED) analysis fan2016b , clear evidence has been found that the selected mid-IR luminous objects are actually highly dust-obscured, possibly Compton-thick AGN, with a high accretion rate close to the Eddington limit wu2018 .

The optical redshift of W0410 0913 presented in wu2012 was based on relatively faint C iv and He ii lines and no formal error bar was given. The CO line is detected, though very close to the low-frequency edge of the band, which corresponds to a shift of 2500 km s ^ 1 from the optical redshift. We fit the CO (4-3) line with a double Gaussian, which gives a better fit with the reduced ^2=1.15 than a single Gaussian does with reduced ^2=1.32 .
W0220+0137 and W0410 0913 have asymmetric velocity structures, suggesting possible molecular outflows, though a gas-rich merger scenario could also explain this.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA H2O and OH+ detections in W0410 0913, with broad luminous lines and ratios comparable to luminous AGN, suggesting AGN-dominated radiative output.

quotes.quote: Using ALMA Band-6 observations of two Hot DOGs, we have detected H _ 2 O( 2_ 02 1_ 11 ) in W0149 + 2350, and H _ 2 O( 3_ 12 3_ 03 ) and the multiplet OH ^+ ( 1_ 1 0_ 1 ) in W0410 0913. These detections were serendipitous, falling within the side-bands of Band-6 observations aimed to study CO(9 8) in these Hot DOGs. We find that both sources have luminous H _ 2 O emission with line luminosities of L_ H_2O(2_ 02 1_ 11 ) > 2.2 10^8 L_ and L_ H_2O(3_ 12 3_ 03 ) = 8.7 10^8 L_ for W0149 + 2350 and W0410 0913, respectively. The H _ 2 O line profiles are similar to those seen for the neighbouring CO(9 8) line, with linewidths of FWHM 800-1000 km s ^ 1 . However, the H _ 2 O emission seems to be more compact than the CO(9 8).

OH ^+ ( 1_ 1 0_ 1 ) is detected in emission for W0410 0913, with a FWHM =1000 km s ^ 1 and a line luminosity of L_ OH^+(1_ 1 0_ 1 ) = 6.92 10^8 L_ . The ratio of the observed H _ 2 O line luminosity over the IR luminosity, for both Hot DOGs, is consistent with previously observed star forming galaxies and AGN. The H _2 O/CO line ratio of both Hot DOGs and the OH ^+ /H _2 O line ratio of W0410 0913 are comparable to those of luminous AGN found in the literature.

sources.summary: ID28 is a hyper-luminous dust-obscured AGN with surrounding Ly-emitting galaxies; its disk-like kinematics include inner high-velocity gas likely from asymmetric rotation, bulge effects, or outflows.

quotes.quote: ID28 This is an hyper-luminous dust-obscured AGN that was identified by the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer WISE, Wright_2010 and extensively studied using ALMA observations e.g., Fan_2018, Diaz_2021, Ginolfi_2022 . Recently, Ginolfi_2022 report the presence of 24 Lyman- emitting galaxies on projected physical scales of 400 kpc around ID28. The kinematics of ID28 is peculiar as it shows the typical features of a rotating disk (e.g., S-shape along the major-axis PV, diamond shape along the minor axis PV) but also strong emission in the inner 1 kpc regions (see arrows in the PVDs, Fig. fig:pv7 ) that indicates the presence of gas moving at a line-of-sight velocity of 900 km s ^ 1 relative to the systemic velocity. This emission, not reproduced by the symmetric rotating disk model (see residuals in the channel maps, Fig. fig:channel5 ), can be explained in two ways. The first possibility is that the CO distribution is asymmetric between the approaching and the receding sides and there is an inner rise of the rotation curve caused by the presence of a compact bulge.

The second possibility is that this emission is due to non-circular motions driven by outflows. We note that strong outflow motions were already identified in ID28 from the analysis of rest-frame UV spectrum Ginolfi_2022 .

sources.summary: The paper finds W0410--0913 has its targeted line partially outside the reference window, uses additional CO data for redshift determination, and reports a large positive offset.

quotes.quote: We know that for two sources, W0116--0505 and W0410--0913, the line is redshifted completely and partially, respectively, out of the frequency range covered by the reference SPW (and side-band) used to target . This is based on additional ALMA observations later obtained for these two sources, which targeted the mid-J CO(4 3) and CO(6 5) transitions GL2021 , respectively, as well as on the shallower observations of CO(4 3) analyzed in Fan2018 for W0410--0913. In addition, JVLA observations of the CO(1 0) line of both sources have also been recently presented in Penney2020 .

For both galaxies, the mid-J CO lines were clearly detected in the reddest SPW, which was originally dedicated to probe the dust continuum, allowing for a new redshift determination based on these additional far-IR data, z _ CO .
The velocity offsets of z _ CO with respect to z _ UV for W0116--0505 and W0410--0913 are v 1250 73 and 2487 66 , respectively.

sources.summary: W0410-0913 suffered poorer sky subtraction, is ambiguously AGN-dominated because H-alpha is unavailable, and has among the largest observed outflow velocities.

quotes.quote: W2246-0526 and W0410-0913 in Figure halfdogs1 ), the long individual exposures resulted in relatively poor sky subtraction, particularly in the H band.
The remaining targets are placed in the AGN-dominated region, though W0410-0913 and W0116-0505 are ambiguous due to the lack of an \ detection.
The mass outflow rate for W0116-0505 is larger than that of any other observed Hot DOG, while the velocities of the outflows in W0410-0913 and W0220+0137 are the largest observed.

gmw_00003466.name: W0514-1217
gmw_00003466.overview: W0514-1217 is an active galaxy with a redshift of 2.235 [arXiv:2010.10641]. Although it is not classified as a Hot DOG due to its bright W1 flux, it shares a similar rest-frame optical spectrum with those objects [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W0514-1217 is not dropout-selected as a Hot DOG but is retained for spectral similarity, with a redshift differing from a previously reported spectroscopic value.

quotes.quote: W0514-1217 is not a Hot DOG according to the W1W2 dropout criterion due to its bright W1 flux, and was instead selected based on the [ W2 W3 ] color criteria of bridge_2013 .
We include W0514-1217 in subsequent analysis based on the similarity of the rest-frame optical spectrum to the targets which do satisfy the W1W2-dropout conditions.
The redshift of 2.235 listed for W0514-1217 in Table obsprops differs significantly from the spectroscopic value of 2.5 reported in Farrah_2017 .

gmw_00003467.name: W1322-0328
gmw_00003467.overview: W1322-0328 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2010.10641]. Based on its oxygen emission features, the galaxy has an adopted redshift of z = 3.043 [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W1322-0328 adopts an OII-based redshift and needs constrained fitting because the poor spectrum can otherwise fit continuum features or telluric residuals.

quotes.quote: Although the broad \ feature in W1322-0328 prefers a redshift z = 3.025 , the [ O 2 ] 3727 A feature indicates z = 3.043 , which is consistent with the published in Tsai_2015 .
We therefore adopt adopt z = 3.043 for W1322-0328, and constrain the \ fitting to this value.

The poor quality of the spectrum of W1322-0328 required significant additional constraints on the fitting to avoid fitting continuum features or telluric residuals, and the resulting \ measurement should be considered an upper limit.

gmw_00003468.name: W1724+3455
gmw_00003468.overview: W1724+3455 is an active galaxy that is unambiguously dominated by star formation [arXiv:2010.10641]. It lacks broad emission lines and statistically compelling evidence for blueshifted components or observed outflows, despite having an accretion rate near the Eddington limit [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W1724+3455 lacks statistically compelling blueshifted OIII, is star-formation dominated, and lacks observed outflows despite a near-Eddington accretion estimate.

quotes.quote: There is no statistically compelling evidence for a blueshifted \ component in W0338+1941 or W1724+3455, so a fit with only a single \ doublet and \ was used.

W0338+1941 and W1724+3455 lack broad \ emission and are unambiguously in the star-formation dominated region, while W2238+2653 and W1835+4355 are near the starburst/AGN transition, with the classification depending on the inclusion of the broad/blueshifted \ emission in the / \ ratio.

While the radiation pressure from super-Eddington accretion provides a possible mechanism for driving the observed outflows, the lack of observed outflows in W1724+3455 despite f_ Edd 1 and the presence of such outflows in W0255+3345 and W2216+0723 despite a low value of f_ Edd suggests \ outflows may not be uniquely linked to the accretion rate.

gmw_00003469.name: W1801+1543
gmw_00003469.overview: W1801+1543 is an active galaxy that exhibits a blended-line covariance [arXiv:2010.10641]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy have detected [O I] 6363 Å emission, and it is excluded from Balmer star formation rate calculations based on its BPT diagram placement [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W1801+1543 has notable blended-line covariance, detections of OI and SII-related features, and is excluded from Balmer SFR calculations based on BPT placement.

quotes.quote: The effect is most notable for W1835+4355, W2216+0723, and W2313-2417 and is present to a lesser extent in W1801+1543 and W2238+2653.

Other Lines In addition to , 5007 A, , and 6548 A, detections of [ O 2 ] 3727 A (13 targets), [ O 1 ] 6302 A (4 targets), [ O 1 ] 6363 A (W1801+1543), [ Mg 2 ] 2799 A (2 targets), H (W0514-1217), [ He 1 ] 3889 A (W0514-1217), and [ Ne 6 ] 3427 A (W0514-1217) are listed in the appendix, along with \ doublet detections (9 targets).
We exclude W0255+3345, W0514-1217, W0912+07741, W1801+1543, W2216+0713, and W2313-2417 from the SFR_ Balmer calculation based on the BPT placement in the left panel of Figure bpt .

gmw_00003470.name: W2216+0723
gmw_00003470.overview: W2216+0723 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy classified within the broad-H̑ Hot DOG subset [arXiv:1911.09828]. The galaxy exhibits observed outflows [arXiv:2010.10641] and a significantly broad [O II] line profile, which indicates a highly disturbed interstellar medium or strong active galactic nucleus outflows [arXiv:1911.09828].

sources.summary: W2216+0723 is in the broad-H Hot DOG subset, has a Balmer decrement comparable to the narrow-line value, and shows significantly broad [O II] consistent with outflow or disturbed ISM.

quotes.quote: The figure includes the 5/7 BPT non- H 2 Hot DOGs with significantly broad H ( _ H , broad/narrow >400 ) with S/N > 5 from Table 3 (W0114--0812, W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The normalized broad Balmer line components are weaker than the broad [ O 3 ] but are blueshifted for all five objects, with absolute values comparable to or smaller than the broad [ O 3 ] blueshift with a fraction of 0.53--1.23 times (median 0.65).

We check this by calculating the Balmer decrement of objects having S/N 5 in H and showing a broad component in both H /H (W1103--1826, W1136+4236, W2136--1631, W2216+0723). The values are 1.93 1.02, 4.28 0.87, 1.93 0.22, and 5.95 2.17 respectively, all comparable to or smaller than the narrow Balmer decrements in Table 8.

In addition to the likelihood of outflows in the narrow Balmer line region for some Hot DOGs, we plot in the remaining panels of Figure 8 the 3/6 BPT non- H 2 sources with a significantly broad [ O 2 ] ( _ O II , broad/narrow >400 , W1719+0446, W2026+0716, W2216+0723) with S/N > 5. Additionally, 1/3 of the remaining (3/6) sources (W0114--0812) shows _ O II , broad/narrow >300 . This can either be explained by strong AGN outflows broadening the [ O 2 ] line profile, or the kinematics of the ISM in the star-forming region being highly disturbed by AGN outflows and/or galaxy merging (e.g., Dia16,Dia18 ). As [ O 2 ] is a forbidden line, a fraction of them being broad adds support for the presence of outflows, and hints for kpc scale, narrow line region origin for at least some the broadened Balmer lines.

sources.summary: W2216+0723 has blended-line covariance, narrow-dominated luminosity, and observed outflows despite a low Eddington ratio estimate.

quotes.quote: The effect is most notable for W1835+4355, W2216+0723, and W2313-2417 and is present to a lesser extent in W1801+1543 and W2238+2653. The blending of the line profile results in strong covariances between the redshift and line parameters, as shown in the top of Figure corners for the case of W2216+0723.

However, in W0220+0137, W0255+3345, W0912+7741, and W2216+0723, the majority of the \ luminosity is in the narrow component, and W0338+1941 and W1724+3455 entirely lack a broad/blueshifted component, suggesting significant variation in the properties of the broad component between objects.

While the radiation pressure from super-Eddington accretion provides a possible mechanism for driving the observed outflows, the lack of observed outflows in W1724+3455 despite f_ Edd 1 and the presence of such outflows in W0255+3345 and W2216+0723 despite a low value of f_ Edd suggests \ outflows may not be uniquely linked to the accretion rate.

gmw_00003471.name: W2238+2653
gmw_00003471.overview: W2238+2653 is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits blended-line effects [arXiv:2010.10641]. The galaxy is positioned near the transition region between a starburst and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W2238+2653 has blended-line effects, lies near the starburst/AGN transition, and lacks an optical star formation rate because its NIRES spectrum is poorly flux-calibrated.

quotes.quote: The effect is most notable for W1835+4355, W2216+0723, and W2313-2417 and is present to a lesser extent in W1801+1543 and W2238+2653.

W0338+1941 and W1724+3455 lack broad \ emission and are unambiguously in the star-formation dominated region, while W2238+2653 and W1835+4355 are near the starburst/AGN transition, with the classification depending on the inclusion of the broad/blueshifted \ emission in the / \ ratio.
The NIRES spectrum for W2238+2653 is not well flux-calibrated (see Section sec:obs ), so there is no optical star formation rate estimate for this source.

gmw_00003472.name: W2305-0039
gmw_00003472.overview: W2305-0039 is a high-redshift galaxy that may be dominated by star formation based on its narrow line ratio [arXiv:2010.10641]. Although it hosts outflows, the galaxy lacks prior photometry and was omitted from outflow energetics due to flux calibration issues [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W2305-0039 lacked prior photometry for calibration, may be star-formation dominated by line ratio, and is omitted from outflow energetics despite significant outflows.

quotes.quote: No prior photometry or suitable 2MASS sources were available for W0010+3236, W0831+0140, W2016-0041, W2238+2653, and W2305-0039, all of which were observed under non-photometric conditions.

W2305-0039, and W0859+4823 may also be star-formation dominated based on the narrow/systemic / \ ratio, though the high redshifts placing \ beyond the reach of NIRES prevents a precise placement on the BPT.
W0831+0140, W2238+2653, and W2305-0039 are omitted due to issues with flux calibration, despite the presence of significant outflows.

gmw_00003473.name: W2313-2417
gmw_00003473.overview: W2313-2417 is an active galaxy [arXiv:2010.10641] that exhibits blended-line covariance [arXiv:2010.10641]. Unlike similar targets, it lacks a preferred blueshift for its broad oxygen emission [arXiv:2010.10641] and is excluded from Balmer star formation rate calculations due to its BPT diagram placement [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W2313-2417 has blended-line covariance, uniquely lacks a preferred blueshift for broad OIII emission, and is excluded from Balmer SFR calculation by BPT placement.

quotes.quote: The effect is most notable for W1835+4355, W2216+0723, and W2313-2417 and is present to a lesser extent in W1801+1543 and W2238+2653.
All targets except W2313-2417 prefer a significant blueshift to the broad \ emission.
We exclude W0255+3345, W0514-1217, W0912+07741, W1801+1543, W2216+0713, and W2313-2417 from the SFR_ Balmer calculation based on the BPT placement in the left panel of Figure bpt .

gmw_00003474.name: W2235+1605
gmw_00003474.overview: W2235+1605 is a high-redshift active galaxy [arXiv:2010.10641]. Its raw spectra lack apparent features, and telluric contamination prevents the clear identification and fitting of its emission lines [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W2235+1605 observations were shortened because raw spectra lacked features; telluric contamination prevents clear emission-line identification, and the source is dropped from later analysis.

quotes.quote: Observations of W0010+3236 and W2235+1605 were cut short when no features were apparent in the raw NIRES spectra in order to maximize time spent on other targets.
In W2235+1605, these telluric features prevent clear identification and fitting of emission features, and the quality of the \ fit in W0255+3345 is impacted by telluric features.
We therefore do not include W2235+1605 in subsequent analysis.

gmw_00003475.name: W2246-0526
gmw_00003475.overview: W2246-0526 is a highly luminous, hot dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:1712.00031, arXiv:2401.01871] at a redshift of approximately 4.6 [arXiv:2401.01871, arXiv:2010.10641] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2401.01871] and exhibits slow rotation [arXiv:2104.09495]. It features a turbulent interstellar medium [arXiv:2104.09495, arXiv:2010.10641] and dust streamers that connect it to three companion galaxies [arXiv:2104.09495].

sources.summary: The paper identifies W2246--0526 as one of the most luminous known galaxies, reports no significant X-ray detection, and derives upper limits on its counts, fluxes, and luminosity.

quotes.quote: W2246--0526 This Hot DOG is the most luminous galaxy in the sample, and one of the most-luminous known galaxies in the Universe. To our knowledge, this galaxy has the highest bolometric luminosity derived from SED fitting. Bolometric luminosities for other galaxies with similar or slightly higher power have been derived via scaling relations with optical emission-line luminosities.

The source is not significantly detected in either of the bands by any of its instruments, although a somewhat enhanced signal is perhaps visible in the PN camera in the full band ( 1-P_B 98.76 , corresponding to 2.5 in the Gaussian approximation). We also tried performing the standard detection procedure on the data from the combined cameras. Using EMLDETECT, https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/docs/xmm/sas/help/emldetect/node3.html W2246--0526 is not detected with a DET ML = 1.67, which is equivalent to a false-detection probability of 0.19. We considered only the PN dataset and derived upper limits at the 90 confidence level on the source counts, fluxes and luminosity, assuming the same spectral model used for W1838+3429.

sources.summary: The paper presents W2246--0526 as an extreme hot dust-obscured quasar galaxy whose dense, ionized ISM and XDR-like line ratios make it a benchmark high-redshift system.

quotes.quote: WISE J224607.6--052634.9 (W2246--0526) is a hot dust-obscured galaxy at z = 4.601, and the most luminous obscured quasar known to date. W2246--0526 harbors a heavily obscured supermassive black hole that is most likely accreting above the Eddington limit. We present observations with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) in seven bands, including band 10, of the brightest far-infrared (FIR) fine-structure emission lines of this galaxy: [ OI ] _ 63 m , [ OIII ] _ 88 m , [ NII ] _ 122 m , [ OI ] _ 145 m , [ CII ] _ 158 m , [ NII ] _ 205 m , [ CI ] _ 370 m , and [ CI ] _ 609 m . A comparison of the data to a large grid of Cloudy radiative transfer models reveals that a high hydrogen density ( n_ H 3 10^3 cm ^ 3 ) and extinction ( A_ V 300 mag), together with extreme ionization ( log(U)=-0.5 ) and a high X-ray to UV ratio ( _ ox 0.8 ) are required to reproduce the observed nuclear line ratios.

W2246--0526 hosts an AGN with a SMBH mass of M_ = 4.0 10^ 9 \ M_ , and it radiates well above the Eddington limit, with an Eddington ratio of _ Edd =2.8 assuming that the MgII 2799 \ Å emission line is being broadened by the SMBH gravity 2018ApJ...868...15T . W2246--0526 is a radio-quiet quasar according to the classification of 1989AJ.....98.1195K , but the central SMBH powers parsec-scale radio activity 2020ApJ...905L..32F . Given the extreme conditions, W2246--0526 provides a key laboratory to study the effect of the AGN on its surrounding ISM.

sources.summary: The paper detects a compact parsec-scale radio component in W2246-0526, interpreting it as SMBH-powered nonthermal activity amid extended radio emission from winds or jets.

quotes.quote: The origin of radio emission in W2246-0526 The EVN plus e-MERLIN observations of at 1.66 GHz detects an unresolved faint component with a size of 32 pc and a radio luminosity of L_ R = L_ (1+z)^ 1- = 4.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , assuming the spectral index =0 . Its position coincides with the peaks of the ALMA 212 m dust continuum, [ C II ] emission and the HST near-IR images Diaz-santos2016,Diaz-santos2018,Fan2016a . The observed high brightness temperature ( 8 10^ 6 K) cannot result from thermal emission, which generally has a T_ b <10^6 K. The VLBI component cannot be explained as a young radio supernova in the nucleus because its radio luminosity is two orders of magnitude higher than the known maximum luminosity ( L_ R 5 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 ) of young radio supernovae e.g. Weiler2002 . Instead, it may be naturally interpreted as the radio activity powered by the central accreting SMBH: a stationary jet base, a young out-moving jet, or a pc-scale radio-emitting outflow.

sources.summary: The paper treats W2246--0526 as the most distant and most luminous known Hot DOG, with turbulent ISM, companion-connected dust structures, robust line detection, and slow rotation.

quotes.quote: Further evidence comes from ALMA observations of the most luminous obscured quasar and Hot DOG known ( = 3.5 10 ^ 14 ; Tsai2015 ), WISE J224607.55--052634.9 (W2246--0526 hereafter; at z = 4.601), which revealed a highly turbulent ISM, with a FWHM _ CII 500 spread across the entire host galaxy, over 2.5 kpc DS2016 . Furthermore, recent deep ALMA imaging of the dust continuum at _ rest = 212 m of W2246--0526 shows streamers and bridges of dust extending up to 35 kpc in projected size, which seem to connect at least three companion galaxies with the central Hot DOG. These results suggest the most luminous obscured quasars may be interacting systems ---the result of ongoing merger-driven peaks of SMBH accretion and massive galaxy assembly in the early Universe DS2018 .
The line is robustly detected (SNR 20) in four out of the seven Hot DOGs in the sample: W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, the latter of which was already reported in DS2016 .

As can be seen, the kinematics are very diverse: W0134--2922 shows a very small velocity gradient; W0220+0137 displays a smoothly varying structure more consistent with a rotating disk; W0831+0140 shows a complex velocity distribution; and W2246-0526 shows relatively slow rotation, with v 150 .f., DS2016 .

sources.summary: W2246-0526 had poorer sky subtraction, a NIRES redshift consistent with prior detections, and previous spatially resolved evidence for turbulent mass ejection.

quotes.quote: W2246-0526 and W0410-0913 in Figure halfdogs1 ), the long individual exposures resulted in relatively poor sky subtraction, particularly in the H band.

The NIRES spectrum for W2246-0526 is consistent with the strong detection of [ OII ] 3727 A and weak detection of [ Mg 2 ] 2799 A at a redshift of 4.602, in good agreement with the values reported by Tsai_2018 from [ C 4 ] and [ Mg 2 ] detections and by diazsantos_2018 from Ly .

Extreme feedback is consistent with previous published observations of Hot DOGs. diazsantos_2016 obtained spatially-resolved observations of the [ C 2 ] line in W2246-0526 which indicated a turbulent ISM and significant isotropic mass ejection.

gmw_00003476.name: W1838+3429
gmw_00003476.overview: W1838+3429 is a high-redshift hot dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:1712.00031]. It is not detected in soft, hard, or full X-ray bands [arXiv:1712.00031], and near-infrared spectroscopy shows no clear emission-line detections [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes W1838+3429 as a long-exposure Hot DOG target, finding no significant X-ray detection and deriving upper limits using a heavily obscured MYTorus model.

quotes.quote: W1838+3429 This source is not detected in any of the soft ( 0.5-2 keV), hard ( 2-10 keV), or full ( 0.5-10 keV) bands by any of the individual instruments. For simplicity, we consider only the PN camera, which has the highest sensitivity. We computed upper-limits at the 90 confidence level on its photometry (Tab.  phot_tab ), assuming its spectrum is well described by the MYTorus model with N_H=10^ 24 , similarly to what we did in   det_snap . We note, however, that the particular choice of intrinsic N_H does not strongly affect the derived upper limit on luminosity because L_X is derived from the flux in the observed-frame hard band, which is marginally absorbed even by large column densities at high redshift.

sources.summary: W1838+3429 shows telluric-related issues and no clear NIRES emission-line detection despite a published redshift predicting OIII in the K band.

quotes.quote: This is most clearly seen in W2235+1605, and to a lesser extend in W1838+3429.

While W1838+3429 has a previously published redshift, the NIRES spectrum does not clearly detect any emission lines, despite the predicted presence of 5007 A at 2.1 m based on the redshift from Tsai_2015 .
In W1838+3429, no \ detection is made despite a redshift from Tsai_2015 which would place the \ feature in the K band.

gmw_00003477.name: W1905+5802
gmw_00003477.overview: W1905+5802 is an active galaxy that lacks a previously published redshift [arXiv:2010.10641]. Near-infrared spectroscopy of the source reveals a featureless spectrum with no detected emission lines, preventing a redshift determination [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W1905+5802 lacks a published redshift; NIRES finds no significant features, making the spectrum featureless and preventing a redshift determination.

quotes.quote: The detection of multiple strong lines enables robust constraints for all targets except W0010+3236, W1905+5802, W1838+3429, and W2016-0041, with typical errors of 10^ 3 in z .
W0010+3236, W1905+5802, and W2235+1605 lack previously published redshifts. The NIRES spectra of W0010+3236 and W1905+5802 show no significant features, preventing a determination.
W0010+3236 W1905+5802, and W2016-0041 have featureless NIRES spectra.

gmw_00003478.name: W2016-0041
gmw_00003478.overview: W2016-0041 is an active galaxy that lacks prior calibration photometry [arXiv:2010.10641]. Its near-infrared NIRES spectrum is featureless and cannot confirm its previously published redshift of 2.61 [arXiv:2010.10641].

sources.summary: W2016-0041 lacks prior calibration photometry, cannot confirm the published redshift from its NIRES spectrum, and is grouped among featureless spectra.

quotes.quote: No prior photometry or suitable 2MASS sources were available for W0010+3236, W0831+0140, W2016-0041, W2238+2653, and W2305-0039, all of which were observed under non-photometric conditions.
Similarly, the NIRES spectrum cannot confirm the published redshift of 2.61 for W2016-0041 from Jun_2020 , which would correspond to \ at 2.37 m and \ at 1.81 m.
W0010+3236 W1905+5802, and W2016-0041 have featureless NIRES spectra.

gmw_00003479.name: NGC 3972
gmw_00003479.overview: NGC 3972 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:2312.02282] that hosted the supernova SN 2011by [arXiv:2312.02282]. Its neutral hydrogen gas morphology matches its stellar disc, exhibiting solid-body rotation and asymmetric star formation [arXiv:1605.01638].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3972 was detected with NGC 3998, has HI matching its inclined stellar disc, asymmetric gas and star formation, and solid-body rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3972 NGC 3972 is an SAbc galaxy detected in the same field of view as NGC 3998 in new VLA HI observations. Although NGC 3972 lies beyond the primary beam half-power radius, the recovered integrated flux is comparable to that in . The HI morphology matches the stellar morphology and appears as a highly inclined disc (Fig.  fig:n3972sum ). The approaching (east) side has a higher column density of neutral gas as well as a higher density of star formation as seen in the narrowband H image. The straight isovelocity contours in the HI velocity field indicate solid body rotation, and the derived rotation curve in Fig.  fig:rc shows as much.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 3972 with JWST as a spiral SN Ia host, selecting JAGB stars and measuring a fiducial luminosity-function mode.

quotes.quote: NGC 3972 NGC 3972 (also known as UGC 6904) is a spiral galaxy that was observed with JWST in April 2023, and was host to SN 2011by. used 52 Cepheids to measure a distance modulus of _0=31.644 0.090 mag (or 21.3 0.9 Mpc).
For NGC 3972, JAGB stars were selected as having colors of 2.4<(F115W-F356W)<3.0 mag.
For NGC 3972, the fiducial mode was measured using JAGB stars with colors between 2.4<(F115W-F444W)<3.0 mag and an LF smoothed with a smoothing parameter of _s=0.25 mag.

gmw_00003480.name: MRC 0943-242
gmw_00003480.overview: MRC 0943-242 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.92 [arXiv:astro-ph/0502409]. Observations of its surrounding field identified a single nearby emitter, though the field's angular size was too small to confirm a galaxy overdensity [arXiv:astro-ph/0502409].

sources.summary: The paper uses MRC 0943-242 as a z=2.92 radio-galaxy field, finding one nearby emitter but judging the field too small to test an overdensity.

quotes.quote: The observations were centred on MRC 0943-242, a powerful radio galaxy at z=2.92 ( =9^h45^m32.7^s , =-24^ 28'49.7'' , J2000).

Clustering around the radio galaxy It is interesting to determine whether the fact that our observations were centred on radio galaxy MRC 0943-242 at z=2.92 has any influence on the number of emitters we find.

Kudritzki00 carried out a search with a similar flux limit to our average limit, targeted at a blank field at z=3.1 which is close to the redshift of our radio galaxy. They derive a number density of 14400 z ^ 1 deg ^ 2 . Based on this we would expect to find 1 emitter at redshift z 2.92 over our entire field, within a redshift interval of z 0.004 . Indeed we find one emitter with z_ rg z_ em = 0.002 (object 5644). It is evident that the angular size of our field is much too small to determine whether there is an overdensity around our target.

gmw_00003481.name: NGC 1411
gmw_00003481.overview: NGC 1411 is classified as a nonbarred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1304.3529]. It features three distinct lens structures, consisting of an outer lens, an inner lens, and a nuclear lens, which delineate zones with slight stellar population differences [arXiv:1304.3529].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1411 as a nonbarred S0 with three lens features, whose photometric and color profiles suggest slight stellar-population differences across lens edges.

quotes.quote: In both NGC 524 and NGC 1411, three distinct lens features are detectable, and each galaxy is classified as (L)SA(l,nl)0 ^ o , where (L) refers to an outer lens , (l) to an inner lens , and (nl) to a nuclear lens .

Figure ngc1411 shows a V band image, a B-V colour index map, and azimuthally averaged B , V , and B-V surface brightness profiles for NGC 1411. On the graph, the radial locations of the `edges' of the three lenses of the galaxy are indicated. The B-V colour index profile shows two zones of slightly bluer colours, one between the (nl) and (l), and one between the (l) and (L). The apparent sharp edges appear to delineate zones with slight stellar population differences.

gmw_00003482.name: ESO 565-11
gmw_00003482.overview: ESO 565-11 is a galaxy characterized by a misaligned bar-inner ring and a misaligned bar-oval system [arXiv:1304.3529]. Stellar outer arms emerge from the rim of its massive oval, which hosts a large, highly elongated nuclear ring [arXiv:1304.3529].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 565-11 as an extreme misaligned bar-ring and bar-oval system, suggesting a relatively new bar and an unusually young nuclear ring.

quotes.quote: ESO 565-11 is such an extreme case that it suggests a possible evolutionary effect. The galaxy is not only a misaligned bar-inner ring system, but also a misaligned bar-oval system, because the inner pseudoring lies around the rim of a massive oval, from which stellar outer arms emerge. Since long-term, stable misalignment of massive nonaxisymmetric components is unlikely, the suggestion is that the bar of ESO 565-11 is relatively new, and that it formed within a pre-existing oval that itself is a remnant of a past bar episode. Evidence in support of this idea is the presence of the large and very unusual nuclear ring (Buta et al. 1999). The extreme elongated shape and size of this feature (more than 3 kpc in radius) are likely indicators of its youth.

gmw_00003483.name: NGC 986
gmw_00003483.overview: NGC 986 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a strong spiral pattern curving off the ends of its central bar [arXiv:0812.2959, arXiv:1304.3529]. Because the bar and the spiral arms share the same pattern speed, it is considered a genuine example of a bar-driven spiral [arXiv:0812.2959, arXiv:1304.3529].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 986 as a barred spiral whose phase-shift plot has one main crossing, making it a likely bar-driven spiral with the bar ending inside corotation.

quotes.quote: The first example, NGC 986, also has a strong spiral breaking from the bar ends and shows only a single P/N crossing (arrow) in its phase-shift plot. It could be a genuine case of a ``bar-driven" spiral, where the two features have grown together and share the same pattern speed. Both NGC 175 and 986 satisfy the ``rule" that the bar extends no further than its CR (Contopoulos 1980).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 986 as a barred galaxy whose bar and spiral share a pattern speed, making it a genuine bar-driven spiral example.

quotes.quote: NGC 986, a barred galaxy with a strong spiral curving off the bar ends, shows a distribution with only a single major P/N crossing that places CR exactly around the bar ends. In this case, the bar and the spiral would have the same pattern speed, and BZ11 interpret NGC 986 as a genuine example of a `bar-driven spiral'. In contrast, NGC 175 is a barred spiral with two clear P/N crossings. The first CR circles the ends of the bar and the mode includes the inner part of a bright spiral pseudoring, which is interpreted as being bar-driven. The second CR refers to an independent outer spiral pattern.

gmw_00003484.name: NGC 175
gmw_00003484.overview: NGC 175 is a nearby Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2312.02389] classified as a nearly face-on barred spiral [arXiv:1807.01092] with a truncated disk [arXiv:1409.2876] and a distorted gas velocity field [arXiv:1405.5222]. The galaxy features a barlens structure [arXiv:1807.01092], a dust-obscured nuclear ring [arXiv:1807.01092], and an independent outer spiral pattern [arXiv:1304.3529].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 175 as a nearby Seyfert source hosting SN 2009hf, with no significant gamma-ray detection and an H.E.S.S.-based upper limit for UHECR luminosity.

quotes.quote: In this respect, for example, the sources NGC 1068, NGC 7755, NGC 175, and Arp 220 are nearby ( D_ s < 80 \ Mpc ) starburst and Seyferts Galaxies capable of producing substantial fluxes of multimessenger particles. Even though these sources are not high-energy neutrino generators , they can foster the emission of charged particles, contributing to the GeV-TeV extragalactic gamma-ray and neutrino emission.

For the Seyferts NGC 175 and NGC 7755 hosting supernova remnants (SN), significant gamma-ray emissions have not been observed for any of the objects. Therefore, upper limits on the > 1 TeV gamma-ray flux of the order of 10^ 13 \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 were obtained assuming the confidence level of 95 . Supernovae (SNe) emit TeV gamma rays at different stages of their evolution. In addition, core-collapse (cc-)SNe stemming from stellar progenitors can create a rich environment for UHECR acceleration .

The upper limit on the integral gamma-ray flux of NGC 175 hosting SN 2009hf obtained by H.E.S.S , at 95 CL, is I_ ^ UL (E_ ^ th > 1 TeV ) = 5.3 10^ 13 \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 and for NGC 7755 hosting SN 2004cx is I_ ^ UL (E_ ^ th > 1 TeV ) = 1.9 10^ 13 \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 . The plots in Fig. fig:upper (b) and () depict our upper limits for UHECR luminosity concerning the hosts of SNe SN 2009hf and SN 2004cx in Seyfert sources, utilizing values obtained from a more restrictive upper limit on the integral gamma-ray.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 0175 with a truncated disk, low bulge light fraction, possible elongated inner ring, central dispersion plateau, and plateau size slightly smaller than the bulge radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 0175: Also known as NGC 0171 in NED. A truncated disk is clearly visible in its SDSS surface-brightness profile. The bulge accounts for only 7 of the total galaxy light, in good agreement with the result obtained by buta09 . They used deep K_s- band imaging to study the bar properties and suggested that an elongated inner ring is present in the galaxy center.

The velocity dispersion profile shows a central plateau which does not correspond to any clear increasing rotation. ho07 reported a central velocity dispersion _0 =104 km s ^ 1 which agrees with our value ( _0 =112 km s ^ 1 ). The size of the plateau is barely smaller ( r_ drop =2.6 ) than the bulge effective radius ( r_ e =3.0 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0171 as a nearly face-on barlens galaxy with a bar-axis feature, dust-obscured nuclear ring, bar/barlens metal enrichment, and broad stellar mixing.

quotes.quote: NGC 0171 (T = 3) : This is a barlens galaxy seen nearly face-on. The unsharp mask image shows an elongated feature along the bar major axis at low surface brightness levels. A nuclear ring is manifested as an obscuration by dust.

The metal enrichment has occurred particularly at the edges of the bar and the barlens. However, contrary to the other barlens galaxies the fraction of the metal-poor stars (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = -0.70) starts to drop already at 24 arcsec, where the two-armed prominent spiral arms start. It seems that mixing of different stellar ages and metallicities appear in a large galaxy region, starting well outside the bar via the prominent spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 171 as a strongly barred late-type outlier whose gas velocity field is distorted, with an extended ring and unusually large kinematic misalignment.

quotes.quote: The only galaxy that seems to present large kinematic misalignment between both components is the late-type barred galaxy NGC 171. The large uncertainties in the ionized gas component as well the fact that the locations of maximum velocity present a large deviation from a straight line in the gas component suggest that the strong bar is the driver that distorted the ionised gas velocity field. From the SDSS band image, this object presents a bar as large as the FoV of the instrument with an extended ring. This is the only galaxy with these features in the sample presented here.

As we mention above, the barred galaxy NGC 171 is the object where is more difficult to trace a symmetric velocity field in its ionised gas component (see Appendix sec:maps ), the deviation of the maximmum-velocity positions from a straight line is of the order of PA _ kin 90 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 175 with NGC 986, describing two corotation crossings, a bar-driven inner pseudoring mode, and an independent outer spiral pattern.

quotes.quote: In contrast, NGC 175 is a barred spiral with two clear P/N crossings. The first CR circles the ends of the bar and the mode includes the inner part of a bright spiral pseudoring, which is interpreted as being bar-driven. The second CR refers to an independent outer spiral pattern.

gmw_00003485.name: NGC 1211
gmw_00003485.overview: NGC 1211 is a barred spiral or lenticular galaxy with rings [arXiv:1907.10188] that is considered extremely isolated [arXiv:1903.08168]. Its structure includes a bar [arXiv:0707.4599], a likely pseudobulge [arXiv:1907.10188], and a double outer ring showing a stellar population color dichotomy [arXiv:1304.3529].

sources.summary: The paper finds a second ellipticity plateau and inner ring or lens in NGC 1211, treats its oval disc and bar as nearly aligned, and handles its bar length with special care.

quotes.quote: For example, in NGC 1211 it is around 8 arcsec (1.7 Kpc).

Two of our galaxies, NGC 266 and NGC 7080 (and to a lesser extent also NGC 1211), have a second plateau at radii larger than the first one. It is interesting to note that these galaxies are those exhibiting the sharpest drops after the first plateau. A careful analysis of the images and the corresponding isophotal ellipse fits suggests that these plateaus are caused by a second component, also non-axisymmetric but not as much as the bar. We hesitate to call these components a lens, because their extent is longer than that of the bar, contrary to what was found by kor79 for lenses.

In NGC 1211, the oval disc has a position angle very similar to that of the bar, and in NGC 7280 the outer disc has a position angle similar to that of the bar. Hence, in these cases, the arbitrary 10 ^ threshold that defines L_ phase does not give a meaningful result, and one needs to search for a less conspicuous change in position angle that might indicate the transition between the components. A close inspection of the position angle radial profiles of NGC 1211 (Fig. res1211 ) reveals glitches at 27 arcsec and 37 arcsec in the direct and deprojected measurements, respectively, and we will hereafter use these values as L_ phase .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1211 as an extremely isolated galaxy west of S82-DG-1, with matching redshift-independent distances and a high peculiar velocity supporting their association.

quotes.quote: The result is the likely association with the NGC 1211 galaxy, located at only 6.4 arcmin of angular separation to the west of S82-DG-1. The NGC 1211 galaxy has three redshift-independent distances. , using the Tully-Fisher relation, obtained m-M = 32.27 0.69 mag equivalent to D = 28.4 _ 7.7 ^ +10.6 Mpc, , using the Fundamental Plane method, obtained m-M = 32.38 0.51 mag equivalent to D = 29.9 _ 6.2 ^ +7.9 Mpc and Cosmicflows-3 gives a value of m-M = 32.29 0.50 mag equivalent to D = 28.7 _ 5.9 ^ +7.4 Mpc. These values overlap with the distance obtained through the GCLF analysis, suggesting the association between S82-DG-1 and the NGC 1211 galaxy.

Further, the NGC 1211 galaxy exhibits very distinctive and interesting properties. According to the work by , NGC 1211 is considered an "extremely isolated galaxy". The authors define such a galaxy as " isolated from nearest neighbors more luminous than M _V = 16.5 mag by a minimum distance corresponding to 2.5 Mpc and 350 km s ^ 1 in redshift space ". Another interesting characteristic of NGC 1211 is its high peculiar velocity, which is perhaps related to its relative isolation.

sources.summary: The paper uses curvature profiles to identify NGC 1211 as a barred spiral/lenticular with rings, a likely pseudobulge, and inner nuclear or barlens structure.

quotes.quote: We begin by analysing \ and components from NGC 1211 (PGC 11670) galaxy of the EFIGI sample. It is classified as (R)SB()0/a RC3_1991 , i.e. barred spiral/lenticular with rings. fig:PGC0011670_r_kur shows the SDSS galaxy image (top), the residual from a single Sérsic fit by Morfometryka (middle) and the curvature (bottom). Changes in \ are related to the transition between regions dominated by different components.

This may indicate that the bulge of NGC 1211 is a pseudobulge. Note also that the innermost component also has a small . In they comment that NGC 1211 has an internal structure called ``barlenses'', which is a component different of a bulge contained inside the bar laurikainen2010 . also observed a nuclear structure in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 1211's double outer ring color dichotomy, interpreting the blue outer component as possible accretion or as R1R2 ring morphology.

quotes.quote: However, the double outer ring in NGC 1211 has a population dichotomy that is well shown in an SDSS colour image (Fig. ngc1211-Rcolor ). The outer outer ring is blue while the inner outer ring, the inner ring, the bar, and the bulge are defined by a much older and redder stellar population. The suggestion is that the outer outer ring is a recent acquisition, possibly an accretion feature where the material from a disrupted companion has settled into the disk plane of a host (R)SB(rl)0 ^+ galaxy. Alternatively, NGC 1211's outer structure may be related to what Buta (1995) referred to as an `R _1 R _2^ ' morphology, or double outer ring/pseudoring (Buta et al. 2007). These also can show a population dichotomy with the R _1 component being redder and made of older stars than the R _2^ component.

gmw_00003486.name: NGC 7702
gmw_00003486.overview: NGC 7702 is a double-ringed late S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1004.5063, arXiv:1304.3529]. It features a faint outer ring and a striking broadband inner ring [arXiv:1004.5063], which is oval in shape and resembles a system that has lost its primary bar [arXiv:1304.3529].

sources.summary: A ringed galaxy with a striking broadband inner ring but weak narrowband emission; H-alpha profile increases coincide with inner and outer rings.

quotes.quote: NGC 7702 (Figure img7 , top row). This galaxy is classified as an (R)S A B()0 ^ + . It has a faint outer ring, not displayed in the B band image, which was not visible in the H image. NGC 7702 also has an inner ring which is very striking in the broadband image, but mostly disappears in the narrowband image. The inner ring of NGC 7702 has a diameter of 13.7 kpc and emits 22 of the total H flux coming from this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7702 as a double-ringed late S0 resembling a formerly barred system, with an oval, bar-like inner ring but no clear primary bar.

quotes.quote: Other examples appear to be cases of former barred galaxies that in the course of secular evolution lost their primary bars. A good possible example of this is NGC 7702, a double-ringed late S0 that looks very much like an early-type barred galaxy with inner and outer rings and a nuclear bar (Buta 1991; Fig. non-barred-rings , bottom row). However, no clear primary bar crosses the bright inner ring, although this ring is undoubtedly oval in intrinsic shape and therefore bar-like.

gmw_00003487.name: NGC 4622
gmw_00003487.overview: NGC 4622 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy located in the Centaurus cluster [arXiv:1304.3529]. It is characterized by a unique counter-winding spiral structure, featuring two outer arms and a single inner arm that wind in opposite directions [arXiv:1304.3529, arXiv:1910.10926].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4622 as the best-studied counter-winding spiral, with non-overlapping arms, leading outer arms, and a possible minor-merger origin.

quotes.quote: The first counter-winding spiral identified, and the one that is best studied, is the nearly face-on SA()a spiral NGC 4622 located in the Centaurus cluster (Byrd et al. 1989). The galaxy has two high-contrast outer arms and a single lower-contrast inner arm, with no overlap between the patterns (Fig. ngc4622 , top row). Instead, the single inner arm and the two outer arms blend at the position of a conspicuous, offset inner ring.

A V - I HST colour index map showed thin curved dust lanes all lying on one side of the line of nodes, which can be identified as the near side. With the velocity field telling us that the north side is receding relative to the nucleus, this implied a clockwise rotation of the disk. This gave the surprising and unexpected result that the two strong outer arms, not the weaker inner arm, have the leading sense, a result that is difficult to accept.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC4622 as an unusual spiral with arms winding in opposite directions, reviewing leading-arm interpretations while preferring a west-near-side trailing-arm interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC4622 is well-known for its unique spiral structure winding in opposite directions. The two outer main spiral arms wind S-wise on the sky, but an additional Z-wise spiral arm is visible in the northeastern inner region. The south side of this galaxy is approaching ( Buta2003 ). Thomasson1989 and Byrd1989 made an N-body simulation of a retrograde encounter by a companion galaxy to reproduce the formation of a leading arm as seen in NGC4622. Buta1992 interpreted the inner arm of NGC4622 as a leading arm produced by such an encounter.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4622 as a prototypical unusual spiral whose wave pattern may arise from a retrograde encounter exciting persistent eccentric resonant orbits.

quotes.quote: A prototypical example is NGC 4622 discovered by by89 and modelled in some detail by Howard and Byrd (e.g., by93 ). They propose, on the basis of numerical models, that this unusual pattern is the result of a retrograde encounter with a companion galaxy, and that the inner arm at least could be long-lived.
