This is borne out by the subsquent observation at 8.4 GHz by Ojha et al. \ ojh04 in November 2002, which revealed two components separated by 3.3 mas. Acknowledging that a speed derived from only two observations should be treated with caution, at a redshift of 0.61, an apparent motion of 2.3 mas in 6.4 years corresponds to an apparent superluminal motion of 12.8 . Further observations are required to confirm this but, in any case, it is apparent that the source has a core-jet morphology.

gmw_00003520.name: PKS 0434 188
gmw_00003520.overview: PKS 0434 188 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1701.01172]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations resolve the source into two components of similar brightness, though further observations are required to distinguish between a compact-double or a core-jet morphology [arXiv:1701.01172].

sources.summary: PKS 0434 188 is described as a two-component VLBI/VSOP source; the authors say further observations are needed to distinguish compact-double from core-jet morphology.

quotes.quote: PKS 0434 188: Imaged as part of the VLBApls and resolved into two components of similar brightness (0.62 and 0.45 Jy) separated by 1.1 mas. The source was observed in February 2002 as part of the VSOP Survey Program with the resulting model containing two components (0.62 and 0.35 Jy) separated by 1.3 mas . (We note an error in the formatting of Table 3 of Dodson et al. \ which has resulted in a third component being erroneously listed for this source --- closer inspection indicates it is actually the second component of J0743 6726.) In the light of the previous example, further observations are required to better characterise the source evolution, both spectral and morphological, and distinguish between a compact double or core-jet morphology.

gmw_00003521.name: PKS 0642 349
gmw_00003521.overview: PKS 0642 349 is an active galactic nucleus with a core-jet morphology [arXiv:1701.01172]. Its structure is dominated by a compact VLBI core with secondary components located to the west, and it exhibits high fractional polarization and variability [arXiv:1701.01172].

sources.summary: PKS 0642 349 is dominated by a compact VLBI core with secondary western components, has core-jet characteristics, and shows the highest polarization and variability in the candidate sample.

quotes.quote: PKS 0642 349: The VLBApls image is dominated by a compact core of 0.69 Jy and a secondary component of 0.15 Jy located 3.4 mas to the west, with a possible third component a further 10 mas to west. This has the characteristics of a core-jet morphology.
We note that PKS 0642 349, which has a clear core-jet morphology, has the highest fractional polarisation (2 ) and highest variability index (0.09) of the nine candidates.

gmw_00003522.name: PKS 1619 680
gmw_00003522.overview: PKS 1619 680 is a radio-active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1701.01172]. High-resolution imaging reveals two components of similar flux density, though it remains unresolved whether the source has a compact-double or a core-jet morphology [arXiv:1701.01172].

sources.summary: PKS 1619 680 is resolved by LBA into two similar components; model fitting suggests one component may be elongated, leaving compact-double versus core-jet classification unresolved.

quotes.quote: PKS 1619 680; Our LBA image (Figure Fig2 ) reveals two components of similar flux density and with similar major axes separated by 7 mas. We note the modelfit to the data presented in Table tab2 has a linear (formally zero-width) second component: we do not interpret this literally but rather take it to suggest that an elogated Gaussian component would provide an acceptable fit, and that a higher resolution observation may resolve this component into a number of smaller components. Higher resolution observations are required to distinguish between a compact double morphology or a core-jet morphology.

gmw_00003523.name: MRC 1656 075
gmw_00003523.overview: MRC 1656 075 is a bright flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z = 3.742 [arXiv:2402.09908]. Very Long Baseline Interferometry observations reveal a core-jet morphology [arXiv:1701.01172] featuring a bent trajectory with mostly superluminal jet components [arXiv:2402.09908].

sources.summary: The paper targets NVSS J165844 073918 as a bright z = 3.742 FSRQ, gives its ICRF3 position, and finds a bent VLBI jet with mostly superluminal components.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: ICRF3 position: _ VLBI =16^ h 58^ m 44 061966 , _ VLBI =-07 39 17 6945

quotes.quote: To expand this limited sample in order to work towards improving the statistical constraints, we specifically targeted two FSRQs, NVSS J080518 + 614423 (J0805 + 6144 in short, z = 3.033 , 2005ApJ...626...95S ) and NVSS J165844 073918 (J1658 0739, z = 3.742 , 2008ApJS..175...97H ) because they are among the brightest high-redshift blazars accessible to VLBI imaging. Their flat radio spectra and compact mas-scale core--jet structures made them suitable for multi-epoch model-fitting analysis to measure jet kinematics. Both can be found among the targets of geodetic and astrometric VLBI observations 2017JGeod..91..711N , but they have not been the subjects of dedicated VLBI monitoring.
J1658 0739 (ICRF3 position: _ VLBI =16^ h 58^ m 44 061966 , _ VLBI =-07 39 17 6945 ) is also a regularly observed radio source.

In the case of J1658 0739 (Fig. fig:J1658-0739-pm ), the four jet components follow a bent trajectory, starting in the northeastern direction, and then turning northwards. The probably newly-emerging innermost components J1 and J2 were not detected at the first epoch in 2002 due to the limited angular resolution. The proper motions are apparently superluminal, except for J2 ( _ J2 ^ J1658 = 0.9 1.6 ) which is consistent with being stationary (Table tab:pm ).

sources.summary: MRC 1656 075 is dominated by a bright resolved component with a faint secondary; the flux-density disparity argues against compact-double classification and favors a core-jet morphology.

quotes.quote: MRC 1656 075: The VLBApls image is dominated by a bright, resolved 1.3 Jy component, with a 0.05 Jy secondary offset by 7.1 mas . The disparity in flux densities argues against classification as a compact double --- Peck Taylor pec00 , for example, adopted a requirement of a ratio less than 10:1 for a CSO --- and suggests a core-jet morphology. Multi-frequency data to examine the spectral characteristics of the components would be useful.

gmw_00003524.name: MRC 1722 644
gmw_00003524.overview: MRC 1722 644 is a compact double radio source [arXiv:1701.01172, arXiv:1707.01542] that lacks a known redshift or optical counterpart [arXiv:1707.01542]. It belongs to the gigahertz-peaked spectrum class of radio sources, featuring a very narrow radio spectrum that peaks near 1 GHz [arXiv:1707.01542].

sources.summary: MRC 1722 644 is resolved into widely separated components and classified as a compact double; its narrow spectral width supports the compact-double interpretation.

quotes.quote: MRC 1722 644: Our LBA observations resolve this source into two components separated by almost 40 mas (Figure Fig3 ). The brighter of these is more extended, and can be modelled by two overlapping components. This morphology is reminiscent of PKS 1934 638 , and we categorise it as a compact double. Dodson et al. \ dod08 note that in a VSOP Survey observation there were no detections on space baselines.
MRC 1722 644, which has the narrowest spectral width of the sample, has a compact double morphology, and while there are other examples of such a correlation it is not universal.

sources.summary: The paper identifies MRC 1722 644 as a mostly uncharacterized compact double radio source, lacking redshift and optical counterpart, with a GPS-class narrow spectrum peaking near 1 GHz.

quotes.quote: MRC 1722 644 First identified in MRC at 408 MHz as 1722 644, this is a radio source of mostly unknown multi-wavelength properties. It is a compact double at VLBI-resolution Edwards:2016jw , and has no known redshift or optical counterpart. This source is part of the GPS class of radio sources, due to its spectral peak at 1 GHz, and features a very narrow radio spectrum Edwards:2004ix .

gmw_00003525.name: PKS 1831 711
gmw_00003525.overview: PKS 1831 711 is an active galactic nucleus that is interpreted as having a core-jet morphology [arXiv:1701.01172]. The source exhibits long-term variability, with its VLBI flux density exceeding ATCA values during a temporary peaked-spectrum outburst [arXiv:1701.01172].

sources.summary: PKS 1831 711 is resolved into two components but favored as core-jet after considering variability; VLBI flux exceeds ATCA values, suggesting a temporary peaked-spectrum AGN outburst.

quotes.quote: PKS 1831 711: Our LBA observations resolve this source into two components separated by 13 mas (Figure Fig4 ). The brighter component is about twice as bright as the fainter, with the latter being more extended. The best modelfit to the more extended component is a single linear component which we consider in the same manner as for PKS 1619 680. Based on the image alone, we would provisionally classify this as a compact double, however as discussed in the next section, consideration of additional information leads us to favour a core-jet interpretation.

The biggest discrepancy is for PKS 1831 711, where the VLBI flux density signficantly exceeds the ATCA flux density. Edwards Tingay edw04 previously noted that the PKSCAT flux densities for this source, e.g. \ 1.15 Jy at 5.0 GHz ), differ significantly from more recent values, e.g., 2.29 Jy and 2.39 Jy , suggesting significant long term variability. The total flux density of the components in the LBA image, 3.4 Jy, is higher again, confirming this source is significantly more variable than the majority of GPS sources.

This is further evidence in favour of a core-jet interpretation of the morphology, and suggests that the source belongs to the class of AGN which only temporarily have a peaked spectrum during the spectral evolution of an outburst.

gmw_00003526.name: PKS 2146 783
gmw_00003526.overview: PKS 2146 783 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a strongly core-dominated radio source [arXiv:1701.01172]. Observations show that the source is unresolved and can be modeled by a single circular Gaussian component [arXiv:1701.01172].

sources.summary: PKS 2146 783 is described as a strongly core-dominated, unresolved LBA source, despite previous VSOP observations finding no detections on space baselines.

quotes.quote: PKS 2146 783: Our LBA observations yield a strongly core-dominated source, modelled by a single circular Gaussian component (Figure Fig5 ). Despite this, Dodson et al. \ dod08 found no detections on space baselines in a VSOP survey observation. Based on our observations, the source is unresolved.

gmw_00003527.name: DC8737
gmw_00003527.overview: DC8737 is a dispersion-dominated galaxy at a redshift of z = 4.5480 [arXiv:2311.11493] that is classified as a merger due to its multiple components [arXiv:2002.00967]. It features a compact, obscured starbursting core [arXiv:2311.08474] with spatially decoupled infrared and ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2402.07982].

sources.summary: DC8737 is the brightest ALPINE [CII] galaxy at z=4.5480; high-resolution data reveal a compact central region plus fainter extended emission in a more complex dispersion-dominated system.

quotes.quote: DC8737 DEIMOS COSMOS 873756 hereafter DC8737 is the brightest galaxy in the ALPINE survey and is at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 4.5480 . At the coarse 0.7 resolution of the original ALPINE data, it shows no signs of rotation but a high velocity dispersion. The [ CII ] emission is extended to the East. The galaxy was classified as dispersion dominated due to the presence of multiple components visible in data obtained with the Subaru telescope - as well as the aforementioned extended, fainter [ CII ] substructures .

DC8737 Low resolution observations (from the original ALPINE survey) of DC8737, the brightest galaxy in the ALPINE survey in [ CII ] , showed no signs of rotation, and suggested that this object is an extended dispersion dominated galaxy. However, the addition of high resolution observations that we present in this paper show that this object is definitely more complex than initially thought. In Section photometry we showed that approximately 50 of emission is traced by the HR+MR observations compared to HR+MR+LR. This indicates that there appears to be 2 components in [ CII ] : a compact and bright central region, and a fainter, more extended component.

sources.summary: The paper uses DC873756 as the main resolved example, finding high-S/N gas, an obscured core, different UV and IR star-formation morphologies, and a likely obscured starbursting core.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:maps_DC873756 we present the surface density maps of DC873756 as an example, while the other sources are shown and briefly discussed in Appendix sect:other_maps . We note that the gas map has a very high S/N, and only the galaxy core is detected in the obscured SFR map. We also note that the obscured and unobscured SFR surface density maps exhibit different morphologies with the UV star formation coming mainly from the diffuse gas extension in the northwest and not the IR-bright core.

We note that two sources have a significantly longer gas depletion timescale for large aperture radii (DC818760, DC873756), while the two others do not exhibit any significant trend (VC5101218326, VC5110377875). DC818760 is a multi-component system and the large radii include the two neighbors, while the small radii only include the central component. It is not surprising to find slightly different timescales in the various components. As discussed in Sect. sect:results , DC873756 probably hosts an obscured starbursting core with a short depletion timescale, while it is longer in the outskirts.

sources.summary: The paper describes CRISTAL-24 as below the main sequence with high infrared surface density, blue UV slope, inconsistent SED and UV+IR SFRs, and likely multi-phase/component structure.

quotes.quote: We also note that CRISTAL-24 is located below the main sequence due to small SFR _ SED .

Interestingly, though, CRISTAL-24 shows a very high I_ m of 5000 with a relatively blue UV slope =-1.55 . An inconsistent SFR _ SED and SFR _ UV+IR in CRISTAL-24 described in Section subsec:globsed may indicate multi-phase and component nature of the CRISTAL-24. concentration of the star-forming regions Since we do not find clear dependence on the potential interactions or UV-dust offsets, we will now compare f_ obs with the compactness of the star-forming regions.

CRISTAL-24 show 1.4 dex smaller SFR _ SED than SFR _ UV + SFR _ IR . This difference is due to the degeneracy of the dust extinction and stellar age and makes CRISTAL-24 distribute 1.2 dex lower than the main sequence.

sources.summary: The paper describes DEIMOS COSMOS 873756 as clumpy and dispersion-classified, with multiple optical offsets and evidence that its IR and UV emission are spatially decoupled.

quotes.quote: Some galaxies may also have non-uniform or clumpy star-formation e.g. Guo2012Multi-wavelength2, Hatsukade2015High-resolution3.042 in either UV or optical emission as can be seen for DEIMOS COSMOS 403030 and DEIMOS COSMOS 873756 in Appendix apx:significant_offsets Fig. fig:off4 and fig:off5 .
DEIMOS COSMOS 873756 (Appendix apx:significant_offsets Fig. fig:off5 ), classified as DIS, has multiple significant offsets, between optical and all other emissions.

The galaxy with the largest discrepancy in their analysis (DEIMOS COSMOS 873756; shown in Appendix apx:significant_offsets Fig. fig:off5 ) is one of the galaxies we find to have a significant spatial offset. Using higher resolution observations, Mitsuhashi2023TheZ=4-6 similarly find a significant difference between SED-derived SFR and total SFR of this galaxy (called CRISTAL-24 in their sample). Our results lend support to the idea put forth by these works of spatially decoupled IR and UV emission in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 873756 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 873756 : this object is classified as a merger because of multiple components in the ancillary optical Subaru images (and partly because of fainter [ CII ] structures around the main one). In particular, there is a bright UV-optical emitter southwest of the main [ CII ] emitter, just outside of the spectroscopic slit. Is important to stress that this companion is not responsible for the emission that is extracted from within the slit, right on top of the main [ CII ] peak. We re-extracted a [ CII ] spectrum from a smaller region around the optical rest-frame emission, and we find that the [ CII ] velocity peak is not affected.

gmw_00003528.name: VC8326
gmw_00003528.overview: VC8326 is an extended, dispersion-dominated galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.5678 [arXiv:2311.11493]. High-resolution observations reveal two distinct compact clumps [arXiv:2311.11493], making the system a solid merger candidate with differing obscured and unobscured star formation density maps [arXiv:2311.08474, arXiv:2311.11493].

sources.summary: The paper includes VC5101218326 in the ALPINE high-resolution sample, recovers large-scale [CII] emission, finds differing SFR maps, and identifies it as a solid merger candidate.

quotes.quote: For DC818760, DC873756, and VC5101218326 we also included the compact-configuration visibilities from the initial ALPINE observations to recover the large-scale components. This was not necessary for VC5110377875 since the full ALPINE integrated flux is already recovered by the new high-resolution observations alone. The data reduction is described in detail in a companion paper focusing on the morpho-kinematical analysis of these objects Devereaux2023 .

Similarly to DC873756, we note that DC818760 and VC5101218326 have very different obscured and unobscured SFR density maps, confirming the need to have access to both. For instance, both the central and western components of DC818760 host an important obscured star formation activity, but only the central one is detected in the unobscured SFR density map. The morphology and the kinematics of these two objects is discussed in a companion paper ( Devereaux2023 ; see also Jones2020 about the initial ALPINE data of DC818760). They are both identified as solid merger candidates.

sources.summary: VC8326 is a z=4.5678 extended dispersion-dominated galaxy whose high-resolution [CII] data reveal two real compact clumps, possibly merging galaxies rather than a single galaxy with in-situ clumps.

quotes.quote: VC8326 VUDS COSMOS 5101218326 (hereafter VC8326) has a redshift z=4.5678 . modelled its velocity map with 3D-Based Analysis of Rotating Object via Line Observations ( 3D-BAROLO ) , finding that this object is likely to be an extended dispersion-dominated galaxy.

Both concatenations show 2 clear compact clumps in [ CII ] which are clearly real emission. For VC8326, the low resolution observations from ALPINE was unable to resolve the two clumps and the [ CII ] emission appeared to come from a single component, which was dispersion dominated; the high resolution data is therefore crucial to reveal the real nature of this system. Understanding whether VC8326 is composed of 2 merging galaxies or rather a single galaxy with 2 in-situ clumps is not trivial. The separation of the two [ CII ] components, around 5 kpc, is much larger than typical galaxies at the same redshift ito2023 , this suggests that they could indeed be two individual merging galaxies.

gmw_00003529.name: DC8187
gmw_00003529.overview: DC8187 is a triple-galaxy system at a redshift of approximately 4.56 [arXiv:1908.07777, arXiv:2311.11493] that is identified as a merger candidate [arXiv:2311.08474, arXiv:2107.10856]. The system features a major merger between its central and eastern components [arXiv:2311.11493, arXiv:2401.03020], which host obscured star formation activity [arXiv:2311.08474].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source C as the central, dominant z=4.56038 component, with concentrated continuum emission, close association with source E, and separation from source W.

quotes.quote: Three sources are present, all roughly at the same declination. Using the CASA task imfit , we simultaneously fit three two-dimensional Gaussians to the moment zero map. The central source of this best fit (C) features an integrated flux density (4.9 0.3) Jy km s ^ 1 , a peak flux density (2.4 0.1) Jy beam ^ 1 km s ^ 1 , and a beam-deconvolved size (1.2 0.1)'' (0.8 0.1)'' at a position angle (111 11)^ , defined counterclockwise from north.

We find that sources E and W are both redshifted with respect to source C by 100 and 300 km s ^ 1 , respectively. The redshift of the dominant source C, which will be used as the redshift of this field, is 4.56038 0.00004 .

While the emission features three distinct peaks, the bulk of the continuum emission is concentrated in source C, with a strong extension to the east, and a 4 component coincident with source W. A single component, two-dimensional Gaussian fit to the combined emission of the source C and its eastern extension using CASA imfit yields a peak flux density of 0.44 0.05 mJy beam ^ 1 , an integrated flux density of 1.22 0.18 mJy, and a deconvolved size of (1.95 0.35)'' (0.71 0.18)'' at (90 7)^ .

sources.summary: The paper observes DC818760 in [CII], recovers large-scale components, finds different obscured and unobscured SFR maps, and identifies it as a solid merger candidate.

quotes.quote: For DC818760, DC873756, and VC5101218326 we also included the compact-configuration visibilities from the initial ALPINE observations to recover the large-scale components. This was not necessary for VC5110377875 since the full ALPINE integrated flux is already recovered by the new high-resolution observations alone. The data reduction is described in detail in a companion paper focusing on the morpho-kinematical analysis of these objects Devereaux2023 .

Similarly to DC873756, we note that DC818760 and VC5101218326 have very different obscured and unobscured SFR density maps, confirming the need to have access to both. For instance, both the central and western components of DC818760 host an important obscured star formation activity, but only the central one is detected in the unobscured SFR density map. The morphology and the kinematics of these two objects is discussed in a companion paper ( Devereaux2023 ; see also Jones2020 about the initial ALPINE data of DC818760). They are both identified as solid merger candidates.

sources.summary: DC8187 is a z~4.56 triple-galaxy system with interacting eastern and central galaxies plus a nearby third source; high-resolution data reveal [CII] clumps with velocity separation and enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: DC8187 DEIMOS COSMOS 818760 (hereafter DC 8187) is constituted of 3 galaxies at z = 4.56038 , z = 4.56628 and z =4.56229 , extensively studied in 2020MNRAS.491L..18J . The eastern and central sources are the 2 largest and brightest and are found to be close both spatially and in velocity, and therefore are considered to be undergoing a major merger. A third source (separate in velocity by 300 km s^ 1 and spatially by 18 kpc ) is detected nearby.

In particular, the central galaxy (that is currently interacting with the Eastern source) exhibits [ CII ] clumps surrounding the main component that were not identified with the original ALPINE data (top left panel of Figure FigureDC8187 ). These clumps exhibit a clear separation in velocity from the rest of the galaxy, as demonstrated by the moment 1 map Figure( FigureDC8187 ). These clumps are denser and thus more highly star forming compared to the underlying disk. They are also less bright and massive than the big clumps seen in VC8326, as they make up only a tiny fraction of the total [ CII ] emission.

sources.summary: The paper confirms DC 818760 as a likely triple merger with two optical components close in velocity and a third obscured source separated in space and velocity.

quotes.quote: DC 818760 : this source was studied in detail by and then further analyzed by . It is likely a triple merger, with the two main components visible in the optical maps and close in velocity (as shown by the PVD along the major axis), and another fainter source completely obscured in the optical and separated both in space and velocity from the other two objects. We thus confirm the previous merger classification by and . In this analysis, we use a pseudo-slit 7.5 arcsec wide to recover the full [ CII ] emission, and we consider only the two close components with the larger [ CII ] fluxes and optical emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DC 818760 as the only triple merger in the sample, with its major merger occurring between spatially and velocity-associated galaxies A and B while component C is a fainter upcoming minor merger.

quotes.quote: Looking at the spectra we note that the brightest emission is the one coming from the peak aperture (pink) i.e. the center of each galaxy, while 1" and optimal apertures in some cases have comparable emissions (see for example component A of both DC 818760 and vc 5101209780). The merging system DC 818760 is the only triple merger in our sample. The bottom left panel of Figure fig:example shows that the emission coming from component C is 4 and 5 times dimmer than the one coming from components A and B respectively. The major merger is indeed happening between galaxies A and B, which are closely associated both spatially and in velocity, while C is identified as an upcoming minor merger (see Jones+2020 for a detailed discussion about this system).

gmw_00003530.name: GRB 010222 host
gmw_00003530.overview: The GRB 010222 host is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0203154] located in the merger or peculiar region of the concentration-asymmetry diagram [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197]. It is characterized as a young starburst galaxy [arXiv:0708.4014] with blue optical colors [arXiv:0708.4014] and a high star formation rate [arXiv:0708.4014, arXiv:astro-ph/0508197].

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB010222 host/circumburst absorber using several spectra, deriving Si, Fe, Cr, Zn, and Mn columns plus a warm-halo depletion solution with 0.7 mag extinction.

quotes.quote: GRB010222 The optical transient of GRB010222 at z=1.475 was observed with the FLWO 1.5m telescope (Jha et al. 2001; FWHM =6 or 300-450 , S/N 10 ), with the 3.58m TNG (Masetti et al. 2001; FWHM =4.8 or 200-400 , S/N =10-20 ), with the 4.2m WHT (Salamanca et al. 2001; FWHM =3.3-5.8 or 300 , S/N 10 ), and with Keck/LRIS and ESI (Mirabal et al. 2002; FWHM =11-13 or 650 , and FWHM =0.4-0.8 or 30 ). Even though the different spectra span a time interval of 27 hours, starting 5 hours after the burst, the absorption line equivalent widths do not show significant time variability (Mirabal et al. 2002). Therefore, to obtain a better estimate of column densities, we combined the EWs of the same lines from different observations weighted according to the errors. We get N( Si 2 )=16.09 0.05 and N( Fe 2 )=15.32^ 0.15 _ 0.10 .

Using also the Zn 2 2026 detection, we obtain N( Zn 2 ) = 13.78 0.07 for b=70 . The Mn 2 column density is obtained from the Mn 2 2576,2594,2606 triplet. Best--fits for these two GRB--DLAs give a warm halo cloud depletion pattern (Figure f5 ), and dust extinctions of A_V 1.1 and 0.7 mags for GRB990123 and GRB010222, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 010222 host among four submillimetre/radio-bright GRB hosts, finding young starburst SEDs, blue optical colors, ages of 0.09-2.0 Gyr, and high star-formation rates.

quotes.quote: We present optical to radio spectral energy distribution fitting of the host galaxies of four long gamma-ray bursts: 980703, 000210, 000418 and 010222, which were detected at submillimetre and/or radio wavelengths. We find that only very young starburst galaxy models are consistent with the data having both blue optical colors and a pronounced submm emission. For each host we are able to construct a model consistent with the short- and long-wavelength parts of the spectra. We find galaxy ages ranging from 0.09 to 2.0 Gyrs and star formation rates ranging from 138 to 380 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper places the GRB 010222 host in the peculiar or merger area, but notes submillimeter emission and high inferred star formation consistent with an intense starburst.

quotes.quote: In total, we find that the hosts of GRB 980613, GRB 010222, GRB 020405 and GRB 020903 are all located in the peculiar section/merger area of the C-A diagram. For GRB 980613, GRB 020405 and GRB 020903 this is not unexpected, since these all appear very peculiar. For GRB 010222 this is less obvious, although for this galaxy a sub-mm flux is measured that is consistent with the host being an intense starburst galaxy (Frail et al. 2002).

Two of these, GRB 000418 and GRB 010222, have a very high star-formation rate inferred from sub-mm observations (Frail et al. 2002; Berger et al. 2003), which might seem contradictory to their elliptical appearances.
However, some GRB hosts, such as GRB 010222, are detected in the sub-mm, with a large inferred star formation rate and a large bolometric flux (Frail et al. 2002).

gmw_00003531.name: Superantennae
gmw_00003531.overview: The Superantennae is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy that hosts an optically identified Seyfert-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1803.02408]. It exhibits exceptionally large molecular line widths [arXiv:1803.02408], which indicate high turbulence within its dense molecular gas clouds [arXiv:1803.02408].

sources.summary: The paper presents Superantennae as a Seyfert-type ULIRG with high HCN molecular-line ratios, unusually broad line widths, and dense gas requirements from RADEX modeling.

quotes.quote: We see a trend that ULIRGs with optically identified Seyfert-type AGNs (e.g., G: Superantennae, I: PKS 1345 + 12) distribute at the right side with higher HCN-to-HNC flux ratios than those with optically elusive, but infrared-identified buried AGNs (e.g., C: IRAS 20551 4250, D: IRAS 08572 + 3915, H: IRAS 15250 + 3609; J: IRAS 06035 7102, L: IRAS 12127 1412) vei99 .

In Figure 25, the observed HCN-to-HCO ^ + and HCN-to-HNC flux ratios at J=3--2 and J=4--3 are high in Superantennae (object G), which shows by far the largest molecular line widths (Tables 4--7 and ima16c ). According to the original clumpy molecular gas model in our Milky Way galaxy sol87 , line opacity predominantly comes within individual clumps, with a minor contribution from foreground different clumps in a molecular cloud. However, the molecular volume number density of (U)LIRGs is expected to be higher than that in Galactic molecular clouds, in which case, obscuration by foreground clumps may not be negligible. If the high observed HCN-to-HCO ^ + and HCN-to-HNC flux ratios in Superantennae are partly due to reduced HCN line opacity by large line widths caused by high turbulence of clumps within molecular clouds, then it is indicated that obscuration by foreground clumps within a molecular cloud contributes to flux attenuation to some degree in ULIRGs other than Superantennae.

Figures 27d,e,f are RADEX calculations for the very large molecular line width of Superantennae ( v 1000 km s ^ 1 ). For a given kinetic temperature and volume number density, the flux ratios tend to be slightly lower than in Figures 27a,b,. Assuming the same molecular gas kinetic temperature range of T _ kin = 30--80 K, a volume number density of n _ H2 = 10 ^ 5 -10 ^ 6 cm ^ 3 is required to reproduce the J=4--3 to J=3--2 flux ratios of HCN, HCO ^ + , and HNC for Superantennae.

gmw_00003532.name: IRAS 13509 + 0442
gmw_00003532.overview: IRAS 13509 + 0442 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy dominated by starburst activity [arXiv:1803.02408, arXiv:2303.08178]. It lacks discernible active galactic nucleus signatures [arXiv:1803.02408, arXiv:2303.08178] and features relatively low-density, low-temperature nuclear molecular gas compared to other similar galaxies [arXiv:2303.08178].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 13509 + 0442 as an unusually extended ULIRG with roughly kiloparsec continuum emission, no clear buried AGN, and likely starburst dominance.

quotes.quote: For IRAS 13509 + 0442, the sizes of the continuum-emitting regions at 850 m in band 7 and 1.2 mm in band 6 are estimated to be 1 kpc. Although the synthesized beam sizes for the observations of this ULIRG are not very small, smaller apparent scales for the continuum emission are derived with comparable beam sizes for other ULIRGs (e.g., IRAS 15250 + 3609 and PKS 1345 + 12). The continuum emission of IRAS 13509 + 0442 is estimated to be spatially more extended than the majority of other ULIRGs. IRAS 13509 + 0442 does not exhibit obvious buried AGN signatures in the infrared ima07b,ima10,nar10 .

IRAS 13509 + 0442 does not display even the signature of the HCN v _ 2 =1f emission line at J=3--2 ima16c . No putative AGN in IRAS 13509 + 0442 is discernible, and this ULIRG may be dominated by 1 kpc-scale starburst activity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 13509 + 0442 as starburst-dominated, lacking luminous AGN signatures, and an exception among ULIRGs because its nuclear gas is relatively low density and temperature.

quotes.quote: Two sources (NGC 1614 and IRAS 13509 + 0442) and a secondary fainter nucleus of IRAS 12112 + 0305 (SW) are regarded as starburst-dominated, without discernible luminous AGN signatures, whereas the remaining ULIRGs' nuclei are diagnosed as containing luminous AGNs.

Furthermore, only NGC 1614 shows (1) a clear systematic decreasing trend in the HCN-to-HCO ^ + flux ratio from low-J to high-J transition in Figure fig:FluxRatio a and (2) significantly smaller high-J to low-J flux ratios for HCN than for HCO ^ + in Figure fig:FluxRatio b, while the remaining ten ULIRGs' nuclei do not except IRAS 13509 + 0442 which shows a weaker, but similar decreasing trend in the former. However, the remaining ULIRGs showing luminous AGN signatures (except IRAS 13509 + 0442) are better reproduced with enhanced HCN abundance relative to HCO ^ + .

(2) Nuclear molecular gas probed with HCN and HCO ^ + at J=2--1 to J=4--3 in all ULIRGs but IRAS 13509 + 0442 is significantly denser (10 ^ 4.5-5.5 cm ^ 3 ) and warmer ( 300 K or 10 ^ 2.5 K) than that in the LIRG NGC 1614. IRAS 13509 + 0442 has relatively low density ( 10 ^ 4.3 cm ^ 3 ) and temperature ( 130 K or 10 ^ 2.1 K) nuclear molecular gas compared to other ULIRGs. NGC 1614 and IRAS 13509 0442, together with IRAS 12112 + 0305 SW, are classified as starburst-dominated with no luminous AGN signature at any wavelength, whereas the remaining ULIRGs are regarded as containing luminous AGNs based on previous observations ( 2).

gmw_00003533.name: IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE
gmw_00003533.overview: IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE is a bright submillimeter continuum source located northeast of the IRAS 13509 + 0442 nucleus [arXiv:1803.02408]. It is optically faint, exhibits thermal dust emission, and is potentially a distant ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1803.02408].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE as a nearby continuum source with dust-like submillimeter emission, no line detections, optical faintness, and possible distant (U)LIRG nature.

quotes.quote: In the continuum images of IRAS 13509 + 0442 in Figure 1, a bright source was detected at 1 '' east and 8--9 '' north of the IRAS 13509 + 0442 nucleus. We denote this north-eastern source to as IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE. It was detected in the continuum emission map at 235 GHz with a flux of 1.9 mJy ima16c . The continuum fluxes at 310 GHz in band 7 (3.6--4.1 mJy; Table 3) are a factor of 2 higher than those at 235 GHz in band 6 (1.9--2.1 mJy; Table 3 and ima16c ), which suggests that emission in this frequency range comes from dust thermal radiation, rather than synchrotron or thermal free-free radiation. No clear emission lines were detected in the ALMA spectra of IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE (see Figure 11 and ima16c ).

However, we regard that this SDSS optical and UKIDSS near-infrared source is not directly related to IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE, because there is a 5 '' positional offset, while the peak positions of the SDSS optical, UKIDSS near-infrared, and ALMA (sub)millimeter data agree well for ULIRG IRAS 13509 + 0442 itself. Thus, IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE is a (sub)millimeter bright, but optically and near-infrared faint object. No source is catalogued at the position of IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE in the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-cm (FIRST) Survey data at radio 1.4 GHz (21 cm) whi97 . This kind of spectral energy distribution is often seen in distant dusty (U)LIRGs at z > 1 tam14 . Thus, IRAS 13509 + 0442 NE could be a distant (U)LIRG.

gmw_00003534.name: IRAS 20414 1651
gmw_00003534.overview: IRAS 20414 1651 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0906.2797] with a starburst-dominated infrared spectrum [arXiv:1803.02408] and a main nucleus displaying highly elongated isophotes [arXiv:0906.2797]. It exhibits double-peaked molecular emission line profiles [arXiv:1803.02408] and contains a central black hole estimated at 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:0906.2797].

sources.summary: The paper discusses IRAS 20414 1651 as a starburst-dominated ULIRG with possible vibrational HCN emission, elevated HCN/HCO+ ratios, and clear double-peaked molecular profiles.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20414 1651 also has a similar starburst-dominated infrared spectral shape and shows some possible signature of the HCN v _ 2 =1f J=3--2 emission line, although the detection significance is < 3 ima16c .

The three ULIRGs with no obvious infrared AGN signatures, IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE, IRAS 22491 1808, and IRAS 20414 1651, also show elevated HCN-to-HCO ^ + flux ratios at J=4--3, as previously recognized at J=3--2 ima16c . These three ULIRGs show signatures of vibrationally excited HCN v _ 2 =1f J=3--2 emission lines, although the detection significance is < 3 for IRAS 20414 1651 ima16c .

The other ULIRG, IRAS 20414 1651, also displays clear double-peaked emission line profiles for HCN J=4--3, HCO ^ + J=4--3, and HNC J=4--3 (Figures 12 and 17), as well as HCN J=3--2 and HCO ^ + J=3--2 ima16c . A similar double-peaked emission line profile is also seen in CS 7--6 (Figures 12 and 31). For the same reasons as applied to IRAS 12112 + 0305 NE, the double-peaked profiles of these molecular lines with various optical depths can be reasonably reproduced by rotation, if these lines trace similar dense molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 20414 1651 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20414 1651. This z=0.087 ULIRG was observed in CO by mirabel90 but not detected. It shows a main nucleus with highly elongated isophotes vks02 . murphy96 find a double core in the optical which appears to be one system in the infrared. farrah03 find its AGN contribution to the total IR luminosity of 13 , below the mean of their sample (23 ).
However, dasyra06b find a black hole mass of 10^8 M_ (larger than that of I05189 by a factor of 3.5 ) based on the bulge dispersion relation tremaine02 .

gmw_00003535.name: CSWA 20
gmw_00003535.overview: CSWA 20 is a strong gravitational lens system where a massive, foreground luminous red galaxy at redshift z = 0.741 magnifies a background star-forming galaxy at z = 1.433 [arXiv:0909.3301]. The lensed background source has a star-formation rate of approximately 25 solar masses per year and shows possible merger structure [arXiv:0909.3301].

sources.summary: The paper confirms CSWA 20 as a strong gravitational lens containing a massive luminous red lens and a magnified star-forming source with low reddening, subsolar metallicity, and possible merger structure.

quotes.quote: We have used spectra obtained with X-shooter, the triple arm optical-infrared spectrograph recently commissioned on the Very Large Telescope (VLT) of the European Southern Observatory (ESO), to confirm the gravitational lens nature of the CASSOWARY candidate CSWA 20. This system consists of a luminous red galaxy at redshift z_ abs = 0.741 , with a very high velocity dispersion, _ lens 500 , which lenses a blue star-forming galaxy at z_ em = 1.433 into four images with mean separation of 6^ . The source shares many of its properties with those of UV-selected galaxies at z = 2 -3: it is forming stars at a rate SFR 25 M_ yr ^ 1 , has a metallicity of 1/4 solar, and shows nebular emission from two components separated by 0.4^ (in the image plane), possibly indicating a merger. It appears that foreground interstellar material within the galaxy has been evacuated from the sight-line along which we observe the starburst, giving an unextinguished view of its stars and H ii regions.

This system, originally identified as a candidate from the CASSOWARY search of SDSS images, is found to consist of a luminous red galaxy at z_ abs = 0.741 which magnifies the light from a background star-forming galaxy at z _ em = 1.433 into four images of approximately equal brightness. With a velocity dispersion _ lens 500 km s ^ 1 , the lensing galaxy is among the most massive known; the mass M 4 10^ 12 M_ enclosed within the Einstein radius ( 21 kpc) is responsible for the wide separations ( 6^ ) between the four gravitationally lensed images of the source.

The source in CSWA 20 shares many of the properties of the much better characterised sample of `BX' galaxies at z 2 . Its star-formation rate, SFR 25 M_ yr ^ 1 , metallicity (O/H) 0.25 (O/H) _ , and velocity dispersion (equivalent to two components separated by v = 57 , with _1 = 85 \ and _2 = 26 ), are all within the range of values appropriate to star-forming galaxies at z = 2 -3.

sources.summary: The paper derives CSWA20's stellar mass from Sloan photometry using HyperZ SED fitting, BC2003 templates, fixed redshift, and a lensing magnification correction.

quotes.quote: The stellar mass of CSWA20 (ID 1) was not available in James2014 and we calculate it from the 5-filter (ugriz) Sloan Digital Sky Survey photometry of image 1 in Pettini2010 . We used the SED fitting code HyperZ Bolzonella2000 to determine the best fitting template spectra from a set of BC2003 models that use the Chabrier2003 initial mass function. We fixed the redshift to that of the source, and computed the scaling factor for the best fit model, from which we can determine the stellar mass. The procedure was the same as outlined in Christensen2012a . We use the James2014 magnification factor of =11.5 to scale the observed magnitudes to their intrinsic (i.e. lensing corrected) values and compute the total stellar mass of the galaxy to be log M _ = 10.3 0.3 M _ .

gmw_00003536.name: IRAS 04505-2958
gmw_00003536.overview: IRAS 04505-2958 is an interacting active galaxy system [arXiv:1003.3746] classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0511315, arXiv:1909.13242]. The system is a highly perturbed merger [arXiv:1003.3746] featuring multiple giant expanding shells [arXiv:0712.0288] and an ultra-luminous infrared companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511315].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 04505-2958 as a BAL QSO with giant explosive shells, an undetected host galaxy, and a possible galaxy-remnant/end phase caused by expelled ISM.

quotes.quote: We have performed a study of the kinematics, morphological, and physical conditions, in the BAL-QSO: IRAS 04505-2958. From this study, selected results are presented, mainly for the three more internal expanding shells. In particular, the GMOS data suggest that the outflow processes --in this QSO-- generated multiple expanding hypergiant shells (from 10, to 100 kpc), in several extreme explosive events. These new GMOS data are in good agreement with our evolutionary, explosive and composite model: where part of the ISM of the host galaxy is ejected in the form of multiple giant shells, mainly by HyN explosions. This process could generate satellite/companion galaxies, and even could expel a high fraction --or all-- the host galaxy.

A very interesting point about IRAS 04505-2958 is the fact that though shell S3 is clearly observed, at the same redshift the host galaxy of this QSO remains undetected, in spite of the very careful image analysis. Multiple explosive events expelling a high fraction of the host galaxy could be a possible explanation. Several explosive events can eject a high fraction of the ISM; this process would play a main role in the evolution of the star formation, finally defining the mass of the remnant of the original galaxy. We call a galaxy remnant this end product of the multiple explosive processes.

sources.summary: The paper argues HE0450-2958 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 quasar whose inferred host luminosity can match limits, while rejecting ejection from the companion ULIRG.

quotes.quote: Magain et al. (2005) argued that the host galaxy of the quasar in HE0450-2958 is substantially under-luminous given the likely mass of its nuclear black hole. Using kinematical information from the spectra of the quasar and the companion galaxy, an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy, we test the hypothesis that the black hole powering the quasar was ejected from the companion galaxy during a merger. We find that the ejection model can be securely ruled out, since the kick velocity required to remove the black hole from the galaxy is 500 km/s, inconsistent with the presence of narrow emission line gas at the same redshift as the quasar nucleus. We also show that the quasar in HE0450-2958 has the spectral characteristics of a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy and calculate a mass for its black hole that is roughly an order of magnitude smaller than estimated by Magain et al.

The predicted luminosity of the host galaxy is then consistent with the upper limits inferred by those authors. HE0450-2958 is a bright quasar at redshift z=0.285 . HST images revealed that the system is double, with an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) situated 1.5 arcsec from the quasar boyce-96,canalizo-01 .

sources.summary: HE0450-2958 is presented as an interacting QSO system with no centered host detected; the near-infrared data support a perturbed merger involving an off-centre stellar component.

quotes.quote: The QSO HE0450-2958 and the companion galaxy with which it is interacting, both ultra luminous in the infrared, have been the subject of much attention in recent years, as the quasar host galaxy remained undetected. This led to various interpretations on QSO and galaxy formation and co-evolution, such as black hole ejection, jet induced star formation, dust obscured galaxy, or normal host below the detection limit. We carried out deep observations in the near-IR in order to solve the puzzle concerning the existence of any host.

This is probably the case of HE0450-2958. Located at z=0.286, 04h 52m 30.10s 29d 53m 35.3s, this system was first detected as an IRAS source ( ), then identified as a QSO and studied as an interacting active galaxy ( , ). The secure detection of stars near the QSO, even if not centred on the nucleus, makes the HE0450-2958 system consistent with a highly perturbed merger, probably not requiring more exotic scenarios.

sources.summary: J0452 is classified as a simple NLS1; its XMM spectrum can be fit by either blackbody plus power law or blurred disk reflection with weak absorption and Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: J0452 (IRAS 04505-2958) We fitted the spectrum of J0452 between 2-12 keV with a simple power-law component reaching a ^2_ = 0.72, which reveals that J0452 is a S-NLS1. Its XMM-Newton EPIC (PN+MOS1+MOS2) spectra in 0.3-10.0 keV can be reproduced by a power-law plus blackbody model or a relativistically blurred photoionized disk reflection model, with additionally a weak intrinsic absorption, an O VII absorption edge, and a Fe K emission line. Both models give acceptable fits, as analyzed by Zhou2007 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses HE0450-2958 as an apparently host-poor quasar system where a kicked black-hole explanation was proposed, challenged, and made less certain by host-galaxy reanalysis.

quotes.quote: This interpretation has in fact been advanced for the quasar associated with the HE0450-2958 system, which appears to lack a luminous host galaxy (Magain et al. 2005; Haehnelt et al. 2005; Hoffman Loeb 2006). However a number of arguments suggest that the kick hypothesis is unlikely (Merritt et al. 2005); for instance, the quasar spectrum exhibits lines associated with the narrow emission line region at distances too great to have remain bound to an ejected BH. A recent re-analysis of the quasar image (Kim et al. 2006) also suggests that the presence of a host galaxy can not be ruled out.

gmw_00003537.name: IGR J16185-5928
gmw_00003537.overview: IGR J16185-5928 is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1805.11314, arXiv:1106.3023]. The galaxy exhibits a variable X-ray flux [arXiv:1106.3023] and displays a soft X-ray spectrum with a high reflection fraction [arXiv:1805.11314].

sources.summary: The paper models IGR J16185 5928 as a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 with soft X-ray spectrum, high reflection fraction, and lower limits on cutoff and coronal temperature.

quotes.quote: We study X-ray spectra of four Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxies (NLS1s), Mrk 110, SWIFT J2127.4+5654, IGR J16185 5928, and WKK 4438, using Suzaku and NuSTAR data. The spectra of the four sources are reproduced with a model consisting of a cutoff power law component, a reflection component, and a soft excess component. The photon indices of all the sources are found to be 2 . The cutoff energies are constrained to be 40 keV for Mrk 110, SWIFT J2127.4+5654, and WKK 4438, whereas a lower limit of 155 keV is obtained for IGR J16185 5928. We find that the NLS1s in our sample have systematically softer spectra and lower cutoff energies than Broad Line Seyfert 1 galaxies reported in the literature.
The fits give kT_ e 10 - 20 keV for Mrk 110 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654, and lower limits of kT_ e > 18 keV for IGR J16185 5928 and kT_ e > 5 keV for WKK 4438.

sources.summary: The paper classifies IGR J16185-5928 as an NLSy1 and links its variable flux and lack of soft-emission evidence to variability-dominated spectral modeling.

quotes.quote: This is the case of IGR J16185-5928 and IGR J19378-0617 with H _ > 2000 km s ^ 1 but classified as NLSy1 based on their [ OIII ] 5007/H _ and FeII/H _ ratios.
The only exceptions are IRAS 15091-2107 and IGR J16185-5928, where the 2-10 keV flux is more variable than the soft flux.

In the case of IGR J16185-5928, the XMM- Newton and INTEGRAL flux difference results in an extremely high reflection value R =14.9 ^ +9.3 _ 7.4 (with C _ pn/IBIS =1.4 ^ +0.6 _ 0.4 instead of 6.2 when modeling with a power-law); this is probably to be ascribed to variability as shown from the Suzaku longer observation, where a more moderate reflection fraction is measured (R = 0.9 0.2, Miniutti et al.

gmw_00003538.name: IGR J16426+6536
gmw_00003538.overview: IGR J16426+6536 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0903.1302] located at a redshift of z=0.323 [arXiv:0903.1302]. It is classified as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy [arXiv:0903.1302] and exhibits a steep hard X-ray photon index [arXiv:1106.3023].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J16426+6536 as a Seyfert 1/1.5 AGN from redshifted broad Balmer and narrow forbidden lines; it measures z=0.323 and concludes the observed AGN is likely the INTEGRAL counterpart.

quotes.quote: This spectrum has redshifted emission lines, including broad Balmer lines and narrow forbidden lines (Figure  fig:spectra a), indicating that this source is a Seyfert 1 AGN. This is congruous with its position out of the Galactic plane, with Galactic coordinates l=96.64^ , b=+37.65^ . To further classify the source, we use the scheme described in Winkler (1992), namely, the ratio of the flux in H   4861 to that in [ OIII ] 5007 , where these are the laboratory wavelengths. After dereddening the spectral lines using the Galactic absorption from along with the extinction law from , we find F(4861)/F(5007)=1.5 0.1 , which makes this a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy.

Calculating the redshift from both H and [ OIII ] 5007 and averaging, we find z=0.323 0.001 . Though we did not observe all possible sources within the INTEGRAL error circle, the identification of the one we did observe as an AGN allows us to conclude that this is very likely the counterpart of the INTEGRAL source.

sources.summary: The paper notes IGR J16426+6536 has only XRT data below 10 keV, a steep hard X-ray index, and a rapid flux increase on a sub-day timescale.

quotes.quote: In 5 other objects, XRT data are available below 10 keV, and, for IGR J16426+6536, only an XRT detection is available.
The classification as a NLSy1 for IGR J16426+6536 is given in Masetti et al.
Finally, IGR J16426+6536 shows a steep hard X-ray photon index, however the lack of data below 10 keV does not allow a constraint on the broad-band spectrum.

gmw_00003539.name: NGC 2655
gmw_00003539.overview: NGC 2655 is a giant disk galaxy [arXiv:2301.05326] classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:0705.4162] or Type-2 LINER active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.15376]. It is interacting with the companion galaxy NGC 2715 [arXiv:0705.4162] and displays complex structures, including a dusty polar ring [arXiv:0705.4162] and remnants of a recent minor merger [arXiv:2301.05326, arXiv:1907.07261].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2655 as a bright group member with Seyfert 2/LINER classifications, absorbed X-ray spectra, Fe K emission, and weak reflection fits.

quotes.quote: NGC 2655 NGC 2655 is the brightest member of a group NBGG 12-10 in the Nearby Galaxy Groups catalog of 1988tully . It is classified as Seyfert 2 in /BAT 70 month catalog 2013baumgartner and as a Type-2 LINER according to the optical classification done by 1997Ho,2010veroncetti . In the X-rays, 2009omaira found that the 0.2--10 keV spectrum is well modelled by a MEKAL plus 2 power-law components. 2016kawamuro fit 2 APEC models to the Suzaku spectrum of this galaxy and obtained a line of sight ( N_ H )=2.61^ +0.27 _ 0.17 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and =1.77^ +0.19 _ 0.07 , which are consistent with earlier fits to ASCA data 2002terashima . 2016kawamuro also detected a Fe K line at 6.4 keV, which was not detected in the previous ASCA and XMM-Newton observations 2002terashima2, 2009omaira .

We fitted the data with PEXMON placing an upper limit on R _ f <0.52. Modeling the reflector with a torus instead, using borus02 , we found a column density of the reflector of (N_ H,refl ) =23.24 _ 22.48 ^ 25.19 and CF>0.11. Modeling the reflection with an ionized disk instead of a torus, using XILLVER results in a highly ionized disk with a large reflection fraction <3.55 and R _ f =9.84 _ ** ^ 10 .

sources.summary: The paper presents new ionized-gas data for NGC 2655, finding regular outer gas, shocked polar structures, a recent satellite impact, and a multi-spin stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 2655 is a giant disk galaxy in the center of a group: at a currently accepted distance to the galaxy of 24.4 Mpc (the scale is 118 pc/arcsec), its absolute magnitude is M_K =-25 (LEDA and NED), and the mass of the stellar population is 2 10^ 11 solar masses (Bouquin et al., 2018). The group includes seven galaxies brighter than M_B=-15 , all of them are of the late type (Garcia 1993). With this configuration, one would expect that the whole gas content of NGC 2655 could result from accumulating the surrounding dwarfs by the central galaxy.

Measurements of the flux ratios of the emission lines in these four spectra showed that the characteristic flux ratio dominated by the highly-excited [ OIII ] 5007 line is observed throughout the disk of NGC 2655, not only in the ESE clump but also in the polar central loop (clumps N, W1, and S), and in the regular gaseous disk of NGC 2655 up to the distance of 8 kpc from the center.

But minor merging certainly took place in NGC 2655. It also brought along a noticeable amount of the gas with the spin strongly decoupled from the regular rotation of the outer disk, both stellar and gaseous. Apparently, a companion fell onto the galaxy almost vertically, and now, within two kiloparsecs from the center, we observe the remnants of the destroyed companion as a circumpolar loop -- the picture is very similar to Sagittarius dwarf torn apart by the Milky Way. But in the case of NGC 2655, there was much more gas in the merged companion.

sources.summary: The Suzaku spectrum of NGC 2655 requires extra thermal components, shows a heavy absorber, and newly detects a 6.4 keV iron line whose variability constrains the reflector size.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2655, the base-line model alone does not give an acceptable fit, leaving residuals in the soft X-ray band below 2 keV. Although one apec component is insufficient to improve the fit ( ^2/d.o.f = 226.4/169 ), adding a second apec model gives an acceptable fit ( ^2/d.o.f = 186.6/167 ). We obtain a hydrogen column density of N_ H = 2.61^ +0.27 _ 0.17 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and = 1.77^ +0.19 _ 0.07 , which are consistent with the ASCA result by .

We significantly detect an iron-K line at 6.4 keV, which was not detected in the previous ASCA and XMM-Newton observations ( Tera02 ; Gon09 ). The iron-K line fluxes observed with ASCA , XMM-Newton , and Suzaku were < 4.9 10^ 6 photons s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , < 3.3 10^ 6 photons s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , and 5.3 1.2 10^ 6 photons s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , respectively. Hence, the iron line flux slightly increased in the Suzaku observation (performed in 2014) compared with the XMM-Newton epoch (2005). This indicates that the spatial size of the reflector (torus) is less than a few pc.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2655 as an early-type Seyfert 2 spiral with off-plane gas, a dusty polar ring, merger remnants, and disturbed ionised-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 2655 is an early-type spiral galaxy, hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus. Optical and radio spectroscopic data exhibit off-plane gas in the central part 2655_1 , and a dusty polar ring seen in \ 2655_2 . This galaxy is interacting with its companion NGC 2715 and presents remnant signatures of a past merger 2655_interaction . The stellar continuum map (Fig.  pair1 ) shows an elongated feature, along the East-West direction, which corresponds well with the PA of the line of maximum stellar velocity.

The emission lines velocity fields show strong departures from axisymmetry, with an S-shaped zero velocity curve, the kinematic major-axis having a changing PA, from 90 in the inner 5 \ to near 180 in the outer parts. As hinted by 2655_4 , the ionised gas rotates together with the stars in the inner 5 , but follows the dust polar ring at radii >5 \ 6 , rotating perpendicularly to the galactic plane.

sources.summary: The paper follows long-running observations of NGC 2655, revising its gas-kinematic interpretation from active-nucleus effects to polar gas and then to a central velocity anomaly likely tied to minor merging.

quotes.quote: Many years ago we started its spectral study with three long-slit cross-sections sil2655 ; then we noted that the central part of NGC 2655, R< 5^ (0.8 ), had quite decoupled gaseous kinematics. Our first interpretation was that it might be an effect of an active nucleus. Later we had made panoramic spectroscopy with the IFU MPFS of the 6m telescope, and had changed our opinion: in the center of NGC 2655 we had detected an inner gaseous disk with polar rotation polars0 that had then been supported by a dust lane perpendicular to the isophote major axis polars0 .

But when we inspect the long-slit cross-sections made under good seeing conditions (Fig. fig_ls ), we understand that any rotation cannot be the reason of this anomaly: the line-of-sight velocity of the gas drops by some 400 to the north of the nucleus without any mirrored behavior in the south direction. Interestingly, just in the same place the stellar velocity dispersion rises enormously (Fig. fig_N2655sig ) betraying the presence of a secondary stellar component with strongly different velocity on our line of sight. Evidently, at R=8^ to the north of the nucleus and along the circumnuclear dust lane to the west of it we see the consequence of minor merging -- the remnant of a gas-rich dwarf satellite destroyed by gravitational tides and close in projection to the center of NGC 2655.

sources.summary: The paper produces only a summed NGC 2655 spectrum, finding a partial-covering plus hot-gas model with a hard photon index consistent with earlier work.

quotes.quote: NGC 2655 Insufficient Swift data was available to produce multiple flux-binned spectra for NGC 2655, so a single-summed spectrum was produced. We also find a partial covering model to be the best-fitting to the Swift data ( ^2_R = 1.15 ), when combined with an additional hot gas component. Previous work on NGC 2655 has also required a partial covering model, and a hot gas component in some cases e.g. Terashima2002,Gonzalez-Martin2009 . The best-fitting model has a very hard photon index ( = 1.35 ), but this is consistent with what others have found previously Terashima2002 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 225 as having a stable center inside 18 arcseconds, low A1, A3, and A4, and high A2 behavior similar to Arp 222.

quotes.quote: Arp 225: The coordinates of the centre are constant in the inner 18". This galaxy shows low values of A _1 , A _3 and A _4 but high A _2 values. The behaviour of A _2 is similar to that seen in Arp 222. A _2 maintains a value of 0.3 in the inner 45".

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2655 as a classical-bulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2655 .SXS0../Sa pec Classical Bulge. This bulge has moderately prominent dust-lanes, however it is not clear if the dust is spiral. The decomposition yields n_b=2.4 , and the bulges is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-0.6 mag.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2655 as a lenticular Seyfert in a loose group, with extended emission-line gas, merger-like optical and H I structure, and weak infrared lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 2655 (S2) NGC 2655 is a lenticular galaxy in a loose grouping of galaxies. Emission-line gas extends over several kpc Keel88 , and the galaxy's diffuse, extended optical structure and complex HI dynamics have been interpreted as indicating a succession of mergers Sparke08 . Optical line indices suggest recent star formation activity in this galaxy, and Silchenko06 find a luminosity-weighted mean age of 2 Gyr for the nucleus of NGC 2655.

gmw_00003540.name: NGC 266
gmw_00003540.overview: NGC 266 is a nearly face-on, strongly barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1509.02356, arXiv:1503.01836] that hosts a variable, low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1001.2924, arXiv:astro-ph/0508598]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed gas morphology likely caused by past interactions with its neighbor, UGC 499 [arXiv:2201.05080, arXiv:1503.01836].

sources.summary: Deep near-infrared imaging traces NGC 266's spiral arms, finds a large bar-related ellipticity plateau and steep drop, and separates bar, oval-disc or inner-arm, and outer-disc regions.

quotes.quote: The extent of this region is largest in NGC 266, where it is 23 arcsec (7.6 Kpc). After this plateau there is a steep drop, as one would expect from a very sharp transition between an elongated component (e.g. a bar) and a near-circular component (e.g. a ring or a disc). In some of our galaxies, like NGC 266 and NGC 7080, this drop is so steep that we have no isophotal fits in the corresponding very narrow radial extent.

NGC 266 shows an interesting behaviour that happens repeatedly in the ellipticity, b _4 and position angle profiles. One sees three distinct regions with the same boundaries in the three profiles. The first region clearly corresponds to the bar, the second to the oval disc/inner spiral arms just outside the bar (discussed above), and the third to the outer disc. This can also be assessed through optical images.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 266 as a nearly face-on early barred galaxy with anomalously red components, chemically enriched stellar populations, and likely ancient secondary star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 266. This classical, symmetrical, early-type barred galaxy is viewed nearly face-on. It is distinguished by its anomalously red colors. The U-B , B-V , V-R , and V-I values for the disk correspond to the integrated color indices of elliptical galaxies, and the color indices of the nucleus and bulge cannot be described using models with an exponential decay in the SFR and zero initial metallicity. All the components of NGC 266 -- the nucleus, bulge, disk, bar -- have the highest B-V values for these components among our sample galaxies (Figs. figure:fig8 a, figure:fig8 b).

The photometric characteristics of the components of NGC 266 are described well by models with a standard star-formation history in an initially chemically enriched stellar system (Fig. figure:fig2 ; the NCS for systems with Z(t = 0) = Z ). These colors can be explained by a secondary burst of star formation in the initial stage of the galaxy's evolution ( T_2 10 Gyr), for example, as a result of the merger of two stellar systems. An alternative explanation of the anomalous photometric parameters of NGC 266, namely, the presence of a relatively large amount of dust, seems less probable to us; an excess of dust in a galaxy can be explained only as the result of non-standard evolution of the stellar system.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 0266 as a barred galaxy with H-alpha emission along its spiral arms and center, plus disturbed gas morphology and additional major-axis gas reported in prior velocity maps.

quotes.quote: NGC 0266 (see Fig. Figure_AB1 ): The H emission of this barred galaxy was studied by Epinat2008 . These authors obtained the velocity maps with data from the GHASP survey, finding gas with disturbed morphology, that could be consistent with past interactions. Considering all the regions with H emission of NGC 0266 (out to 2 ), we only find emission along the spiral arms and extended in the centre (see upper panel of Fig. Figure_AB1 ). In the work by , they detected additional gas distributed along the major axis of the galaxy, following the spiral arms but also randomly distributed, with large voids of gas near the centre.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 266 hosts a variable weak AGN and adopts a single-epoch VLBA dataset to represent its radio spectral-energy distribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 266. -- The radio properties of the AGN in NGC 266 have been studied by doi05a using observations at multiple epochs. They found significant variability in both the radio luminosity and the shape of the radio spectrum. Here we adopt their data from VLBA observations taken on a single epoch, 2003 March 8.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 266 as a LINER in a strongly barred galaxy interacting with UGC 499, with broad optical H-alpha and little GNIRS line emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 266 (L1.9) This LINER is hosted in strongly barred galaxy Veron-Cetty06 which has undergone an interaction with neighboring UGC 499 Noordermeer05 . NGC 266 has been studied from X-rays Younes12 to radio frequencies Nagar05 . In its optical spectrum, a broad H component is obvious even before profile decomposition, with a FWHM of the broad component of 1350 km/s Ho97 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 266 as a distant LLAGN with a steep high-frequency spectrum and no detected jet, suggesting unresolved small-scale jets may dominate its high radio power.

quotes.quote: NGC 266 and NGC 3147 show steep spectra, but have no detectable jet in spite of their high core powers (Table table4 ). Since the two LLAGNs are the most two distant LLAGNs in our sample, it could be somewhat hard to reveal their putative small-scale jet structures. Their high radio powers might be also responsible for the dominance of such small-scale jets.

gmw_00003541.name: NGC 4450
gmw_00003541.overview: NGC 4450 is an early-type [arXiv:1106.2165], anemic [arXiv:0803.3638] barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2201.05080] in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0803.3638] that hosts a LINER nucleus [arXiv:2107.07635, arXiv:1503.01836]. The galaxy features a truncated star-forming disk [arXiv:2201.05080], a very uniform color distribution [arXiv:1106.2165], and little detected neutral hydrogen [arXiv:astro-ph/0610114].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4450 as an anemic Virgo galaxy whose model underpredicts mass, likely from non-axisymmetry or dark matter, while showing no evidence for recent ICM stripping.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

The exceptions are NGC 4450, and NGC 4569. In both cases, this implies that the galaxies are more massive than predicted by our models. In the case of NGC 4450, the MGE fit (Fig. fig4 ) exhibits a big discrepancy in P.A on one of the isophotes, indicating the presence of an important non-axisymmetric structure which might account for the missing mass. In NGC 4569, dust lanes are prominent until at least 5 kpc through the disk, which prevented us from including all the luminous mass.

NGC 4450 is an anemic galaxy with no evidence of recent ICM stripping. Its maximum circular velocity as determined by us is lower than the maximum H velocity, indicating that our model contains less mass than needed for reproducing the observed H velocities, probably due to either non-axisymmetric features which were not included or a significant dark matter halo. If we include a dark matter halo in order to match the H rotation curve (Fig figdm ), it is likely located inside the maximum rebound radius ( d_ bary = 3.3 Mpc).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4450 as an early-type representative, notes its uniform colour distribution, and finds weaker principal-component trends consistent with sSFR and age-gradient interpretations.

quotes.quote: In the rest of the paper we discuss the analysis and the results for the full sample, but we show specific figures and plots only for two galaxies which are representative of late star-forming spirals (NGC 4254) and early type galaxies (NGC 4450), with the remaining five being presented in online appendices.

By comparing Fig. fig_SEDcorr_N04254 and fig_SEDcorr_N04450 it is immediately clear that the late-type, star-forming galaxy NGC 4254 spans a much broader range of colours than the early type NGC 4450, which is overall very uniform. Apart from this, both galaxies display common features in the colour-colour distributions, such that one can divide up these figures into three distinct regions, corresponding to different spectral correlations.

Although not as clear as for NGC 4254, PC1 trends which can be interpreted as tracing sSFR are seen or hinted at also for earlier type galaxies, such as NGC 4450. Concerning PC2 it can be seen that three of the five ``regular'' galaxies (NGC 4254, 4321, 4450) display a trend of PC2 decreasing at increasing SB( H ), thus consistent with the hypothesis that PC2 reflects mean age gradients.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 4450 as a barred spiral with truncated star-forming disk, concentrated nuclear Core-halo H-alpha emission, sparse outer emission, and kinematic evidence for an additional rotating component.

quotes.quote: NGC 4450 (see Fig. Figure_AB6 ): This barred spiral galaxy was classified as hosting a truncated star forming disc at 60 from the nucleus, given the non-continuous H emission at large scales Koopmann2001, Chemin2006 . In the innermost parts of the galaxy (i.e. 5 from the centre) we find the H emission concentrated in a Core-halo morphology. This is in agreement with the image on , where the nuclear emission is not extended further out to the galaxy ( < 10 ), except for some sparse regions at larger scales ( 30 , i.e. 3 kpc). obtained the velocity field for this galaxy, finding two plateaus at a distance 5 from the centre, that suggested the presence of an additional rotating component. The [ O III ] emission has an offset of 25 with respect to the main stellar component, that was ascribed to the recent acquisition of gas into the galaxy Cortes2015 .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4450's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has a nucleus classified as LINER 1. The long FoV reaches HII regions 5 ^ from nucleus. The gas kinematics shows again a distorted rotation pattern whose KMA seems to approximately coincide with the PMA. A blueshifted knot of gas emission at 1 5 (100 pc) E of the nucleus coincides with an increase in the velocity dispersion that suggests it is gas in outflow , as previously discussed in brum2017 , but with below the 600 threshold.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4450 as a medium-inclination LINER with asymmetric narrow lines, double-peaked broad H-alpha, extended radio-core emission, and weak GNIRS emission lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 4450 (L1.9) Hosted in an SA(s)ab galaxy with medium inclination and soft spiral arms, this LINER has asymmetric narrow optical lines and a double-peaked, broad H line Ho00 . VLA images reveal extended emission around the core Anderson05 , whose radio spectrum is flat or mildly inverted Ho01c . The GNIRS spectrum (Fig.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4450 as an Sab galaxy with bar class 2, noting that both visual atlas material and near-infrared imaging reveal its bar.

quotes.quote: Likewise, NGC 4450 (Sab) is of bar class 2. NGC 4450 is illustrated in Panel 110 of Sandage Bedke 1994, and there is a distinct visual impression of a bar. This is clearly evident in the near-infrared (see Fig. 7).

sources.summary: VCC 1110 received extra 70 and 120 m ISOPHOT observations, letting the authors compare constrained SED-fit predictions with data not used in the fit.

quotes.quote: One galaxy, VCC 1110, was observed at two additional wavelengths, 70 and 120 m, in order to have a more detailed knowledge of the shape of the SED.
For one galaxy, VCC 1110, observations with the ISOPHOT C70 and C120 filters were made to obtain a more detailed knowledge on the shape of the SED.

For the case of VCC 1110, where observations in more than 3 filters were available, we have used the same constrained fit and compared the predictions of the model fit with the available data at 70 and 120 m, not used in the fit.

sources.summary: The atlas identifies NGC 4450 as a Virgo LINER galaxy with nuclear radio emission, a compact disk component, and little detected H i.

quotes.quote: Another member of the Virgo Cluster. Nuclear emission and a disk with small scale-length are detected in the radio continuum map of this LINER galaxy. It exhibits little H i .

gmw_00003542.name: NGC 4750
gmw_00003542.overview: NGC 4750 is a spiral galaxy classified as an active galactic nucleus host with a bright nucleus, a nuclear bar, and a detached ring [arXiv:1503.01836]. Its H-alpha gas morphology displays a bubble structure correlated with soft X-ray emission, with gas outflowing from the nucleus rather than following its spiral arms [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 4750 as Bubble, with H-alpha gas correlated with soft X-rays, dust and spiral features, and gas apparently outflowing from the nucleus rather than following spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 4750 (see Fig. Figure_AB7 ): The morphology of the H gas of this galaxy, Bubble , is correlated with the soft X-ray emission (see Sect. SubSec_DiscXray ). reported the presence of spiral features emerging from the nucleus, stronger in the north-east direction, using NICMOS/HST images. They found evidence of strong dust features and star formation. In our data, specially in the sharp-divided image, we also detect the spiral arms and the presence of dust. However, the gas seems to be outflowing from the nucleus rather than being distributed along the spiral arms, and we do not detect star-forming regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4750 as an unusual AGN host with a bright nucleus, nuclear bar, detached ring, faint broad H-alpha wings, and infrared emission lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 4750 (L1.9) The host of this AGN has an unusual morphology and is classified as a (R)SA(rs)ab galaxy. It has a very small, very bright nucleus with a nuclear bar. Additionally, images of the galaxy show a ring completely detached from the nucleus and probably consisting of two overlapping spiral arms Erwin04 .

gmw_00003543.name: NGC 1961
gmw_00003543.overview: NGC 1961 is a warped SABc spiral galaxy characterized by dust lanes [arXiv:1503.01836] and a hot gaseous halo [arXiv:1403.0584]. This starburst galaxy likely collided with a companion in the past [arXiv:1503.01836], and its extended halo contains low-metallicity gas likely accreted from the intergalactic medium [arXiv:1508.01514].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 1961, finding an extended low-metallicity hot halo detected to 80 kpc, with temperature and density profiles, massive baryonic content, and likely accreted gas.

quotes.quote: We examine 11 XMM-Newton observations of the giant spiral galaxy NGC 1961, allowing us to study the hot gaseous halo of a spiral galaxy in unprecedented detail. We perform a spatial and a spectral analysis; with the former, the hot halo is detected to at least 80 kpc and with the latter its properties can be measured in detail up to 42 kpc. We find evidence for a negative gradient in the temperature profile as is common for elliptical galaxies. We measure a rough metallicity profile, which is consistent with being flat at Z 0.2 Z_ .

Converting to this metallicity, the deprojected density profile is consistent with previous parametric fits, with no evidence for a break within 42 kpc ( 0.1R _ vir ). Extrapolating to the virial radius, we infer a hot halo mass comparable to the stellar mass of the galaxy, and a baryon fraction from the stars and hot gas of around 30 . The cooling time of the hot gas is orders of magnitude longer than the dynamical time, making the hot halo stable against cooling instabilities, and we argue that an extended stream of neutral Hydrogen seen to the NW of this galaxy is instead likely due to accretion from the intergalactic medium. The low metallicity of the hot halo suggests it too was likely accreted.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1961 as an external X-ray constraint, citing diffuse emission and revised luminosities while treating the galaxy as too massive to be more than an upper limit for the model.

quotes.quote: Anderson11 and Bogdan13 detect diffuse X-ray emission around NGC 1961 and NGC 6753. For NGC 1961, Anderson11 find a luminosity of 4 10^ 40 between 0.6 and 2.0 keV within 50 kpc of the galaxy. This was revised by Anderson15 to 9 10^ 40 .
