Bogdan13 estimate a bolometric luminosity of 6 10^ 40 inside an annulus of 23-70 kpc for NGC 1961 and NGC 6753. However, since these galaxies are much more massive, with stellar masses an order of magnitude higher than the MW, they serve as upper limits for our current work.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1961 as a massive spiral with a deeply imaged X-ray halo whose emission and central hot-CGM metallicity are below the simulations' predictions.

quotes.quote: Existing deep imaging of a handful of individual X-ray halos is capable of probing the S_ X values of our stacks, as in the cases of massive spirals (NGC 1961, anderson16 ; NGC 6753, bogdan17 and the sample of li17 ). The emission at 50 kpc from these halos is several times less than that of the high-mass high-sSFR stacks from both simulations. These galaxies were targeted based on being X-ray-bright and massive late-types. If they are representative of the galaxies in our eROSITA simulated stacks, the observations suggest that both EAGLE and TNG over-predict extended emission from high-mass star-forming galaxies in general.
We also find that the metallicity of the central hot CGM of EAGLE galaxies is generally higher than the 0.1 derived for NGC 1961 and NGC 6753.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1961 as an XMM-Newton example, describing it as a giant spiral with a hot gaseous halo visible to about three arcminutes.

quotes.quote: As an illustration we examine a recent 20 ks XMM-Newton EPIC observation of NGC 1961 (obs id 0723180101), a giant spiral galaxy surrounded by a hot gaseous halo ( Anderson2011 ; Bogdan2013 ). The galaxy is placed at the aimpoint of the telescope for this observation. We reduce the data according to the procedure of Snowden and Kuntz (2013) to produce a PN image in the 0.4-1.25 keV energy band (a band which avoids the instrumental line at 1.5 keV and the uptick in the instrumental background below 0.4 keV). The X-ray emission from the galaxy and its corona is visible to 3 arcminutes, at which point it becomes indistinguishable from the background.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1961 as a warped, dusty SABc galaxy likely affected by a past companion collision, classified as a starburst and host to recent supernovae.

quotes.quote: Studies of the CO emission in NGC 1961 (UGC 3334) suggest that the galaxy once collided with a companion, and that its disk has since become strongly warped Combes09 . Consideration of the radio and infrared properties of this galaxy led to its classification as a starburst by Condon91 , and it has been host to several recent supernovae. Dust lanes are visible in the optical image of this inclined, SABc galaxy Sandage94 , and nondetection of a nuclear UV source also suggests that it may be fairly dusty Barth98 .

gmw_00003544.name: NGC 2832
gmw_00003544.overview: NGC 2832 is a cD elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148, arXiv:1503.01836] and the brightest member of the Abell 779 cluster [arXiv:1503.01836]. It features boxy isophotes [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148], a central kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0906.0287, arXiv:0808.1521], and is tidally interacting with its companion galaxy, NGC 2831 [arXiv:0808.1521, arXiv:1503.01836].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2832 as the Abell 779 cD galaxy, with generally boxy isophotes, little dust, and nuclear ionized gas clumps.

quotes.quote: NGC 2832 --- Fig. 1(d) : NGC 2832 is the cD galaxy of the Abell 779 cluster. Its B4 parameter indicates that the isophotes are generally boxy. There is little evidence of dust in the galaxy except perhaps for a hint of a narrow, twisting lane meandering through the core along a north-south axis. In an F814W WFPC2 image, Seppo et al. (2003) find no signature of dust absorption in the central 4 4 . The ionized gas consists of a clump centered on the nucleus with a possible second and fainter clump immediately NE of it.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC2832's published profiles and central kinematically decoupled core with SSP parameters that agree well with earlier measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC2832 The surface brightness profiles were published by Schombert (1986) and Jord an et al. \ (2004), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile shows the presence of a KDC in the centre of this galaxy (Paper 1). The SSP equivalent parameters derived for the central 1 8 R _ e in this study ( (age) = 0.929 0.024; [ E/Fe ] = 0.380 0.043; [ Z/H ] = 0.480 0.062) are in very good agreement with that derived by other authors. Proctor Sansom (2002) derived (age) = 0.875 0.069; [ E/Fe ] = 0.300 0.025; [ Z/H ] = 0.457 0.074 for the central 3.6 1.25 arcsec ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC2832 as tidally interacting with companion NGC2831, cites surface-brightness and HST imaging, and finds a central kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: NGC2832: This galaxy is thought to be tidally interacting with its companion, NGC2831. The surface brightness profile were published by Schombert (1986) and Jord an et al. \ (2004), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile shows the presence of a KDC in the centre of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2832 as Abell 779 brightest elliptical, possibly interacting with NGC 2831, with weak optical emission, little AGN evidence, and an old central population.

quotes.quote: NGC 2832 (L2::) NGC 2832 is an elliptical galaxy and the brightest galaxy of the Abell 779 cluster. It may be interacting with a satellite galaxy, NGC 2831 Lauer88 . The object has some weak optical line emission, leading to an uncertain LINER 2 classification Ho97 , but evidence of AGN activity has not been forthcoming for instance, the nucleus has not been detected in VLBA and VLA observations; Liuzzo10,Nagar05 .

gmw_00003545.name: ESO 501-22
gmw_00003545.overview: ESO 501-22 is an edge-on galaxy undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2312.09220]. Residual maps indicate that the stripping occurs face-on, with the stripped material escaping northward perpendicular to the galactic disk [arXiv:2312.09220].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 501-22 as an edge-on ram-pressure-stripping candidate whose residual map indicates face-on stripping with material escaping northward perpendicular to the disk.

quotes.quote: For instance, for ESO 501-22, which is an edge-on galaxy, the residual map shows that it is undergoing face-on stripping. The stripped material is projected in the North direction and is escaping perpendicularly from the disc.

gmw_00003546.name: ESO 501-65
gmw_00003546.overview: ESO 501-65 is a face-on spiral galaxy undergoing edge-on ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2312.09220]. This process has created jellyfish tentacles in the northeast, compressed the galaxy's southwest leading side, and caused its outer arms to unwind [arXiv:2312.09220].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 501-65 as a face-on spiral undergoing edge-on stripping, with northeastern jellyfish tentacles, southwest compression, and unwinding outer arms.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, ESO 501-65 is a face-on spiral galaxy that is suffering edge-on stripping. The jellyfish "tentacles" are in the Northeast direction, and in this case, the leading side of the spiral (Southwest direction) is being compressed by the ram pressure. The stripping arms are also "unwinding" at larger radii, which will be analyzed in more detail in the next section.

gmw_00003547.name: IC 4248
gmw_00003547.overview: IC 4248 is a face-on spiral galaxy that is falling edge-on into the intergalactic medium wind [arXiv:2312.09220]. This interaction has compressed the leading western side of its disc and created trailing tentacles in the east that open at large radii [arXiv:2312.09220].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 4248 as a face-on spiral falling edge-on into the IGM wind, with eastern trailing tentacles, opened outer radii, and west-side compression.

quotes.quote: Similarly, IC 4248 is another face-on spiral galaxy falling edge-on into the intergalactic medium (IGM) wind. The "tentacles" are trailing in the East direction and are opening at large radii as well. In the West direction, the leading side of the disc is also being compressed.

gmw_00003548.name: NGC 5635
gmw_00003548.overview: NGC 5635 is an edge-on galaxy undergoing face-on ram pressure stripping, with stripped material escaping perpendicularly from the disc toward the northwest [arXiv:2312.09220]. It features an asymmetric rotation curve, lagging extra-planar gas, and diffuse stellar tails that may indicate a recent accretion event [arXiv:2207.02906].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC5635 as a problematic massive disc with asymmetric rotation, lagging extra-planar gas, diffuse stellar tails, and no robust dark-halo decomposition.

quotes.quote: Notable exceptions are NGC5635 and UGC12591. The former has an asymmetric rotation curve and, on the approaching side, shows a significant amount of lagging extra-planar gas, which might indicate a recent accretion event Saglia+1988 , also supported by diffuse stellar tails in the optical images. The latter is an extremely massive, lenticular galaxy with relatively little gas, and it is barely detected in our new VLA \ data ( SNR 3 in most channels). Although we decided to model these two galaxies and we keep them in our sample, we note that their kinematics, and thus the following dynamical analysis, may be very uncertain, in particular for UGC12591.
Again, the most problematic galaxies are NGC5635 and UGC12591, for which the kinematic and mass modelling did not allow us to unequivocally decompose the contribution of the visible and dark matter.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5635 among four RPS candidates and describes it as an edge-on galaxy undergoing face-on stripping with material removed toward the northwest.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig_model_jelly shows the RGB images and residual maps of four RPS candidates, namely ESO 501-22, ESO 501-65, IC 4248, and NGC 5635. In each example, the residual map in the g-band clearly displays RPS features. For instance, for ESO 501-22, which is an edge-on galaxy, the residual map shows that it is undergoing face-on stripping. The stripped material is projected in the North direction and is escaping perpendicularly from the disc. On the other hand, ESO 501-65 is a face-on spiral galaxy that is suffering edge-on stripping. The jellyfish "tentacles" are in the Northeast direction, and in this case, the leading side of the spiral (Southwest direction) is being compressed by the ram pressure. The stripping arms are also "unwinding" at larger radii, which will be analyzed in more detail in the next section. Similarly, IC 4248 is another face-on spiral galaxy falling edge-on into the intergalactic medium (IGM) wind. The "tentacles" are trailing in the East direction and are opening at large radii as well. In the West direction, the leading side of the disc is also being compressed. Finally, NGC 5635 is an edge-on galaxy that is undergoing face-on stripping. The stripping material is heading in a North-West direction and is being removed perpendicularly from the disc.

gmw_00003549.name: IC 4275
gmw_00003549.overview: IC 4275 features an asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution that is aligned with an unwinding spiral arm [arXiv:2312.09220]. This specific morphology is consistent with the effects of ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2312.09220].

sources.summary: The paper says IC 4275 has an asymmetric H I distribution aligned with an unwinding arm, giving a morphology consistent with ram pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: As for IC 4275, the H i contour map (Fig. 9 in their work) reveals an asymmetric H i distribution aligned with the detected unwinding arm (see Fig. B1 in the supplementary material), indicating a morphology consistent with RPS. However, they did not provide a specific scenario for this galaxy.

gmw_00003550.name: NGC 1376
gmw_00003550.overview: NGC 1376 is a spiral galaxy characterized by unwinding arms [arXiv:2312.09220]. It exhibits an asymmetric distribution of neutral hydrogen that aligns with these arms, likely caused by ram pressure stripping rather than gravitational interaction with its dwarf companion [arXiv:2312.09220].

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 1376 has H I asymmetry matching its unwinding arms and that ram pressure is more likely than a companion-driven gravitational perturbation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1376 was included in the study conducted by , where a H i asymmetric distribution was detected for this galaxy. Interestingly, this asymmetric distribution is in the same direction as the unwinding arms detected in our work (see Figs. 3 and 8 of ). They attributed the morphological perturbation to its compact dwarf companion, SBS 0335-052. However, considering the projected distance between them (approximately 140 kpc), the RPS scenario is more likely for NGC 1376 than a gravitational scenario.

gmw_00003551.name: NGC 3314A
gmw_00003551.overview: NGC 3314A is a spiral galaxy in the Hydra I cluster that overlaps NGC 3314B along the line of sight without being physically related [arXiv:2107.04486, arXiv:2312.09220]. It is a jellyfish galaxy undergoing active ram pressure stripping, featuring a lopsided disk and star-forming gas tails [arXiv:2209.05605, arXiv:2107.04486].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3314a as a jellyfish galaxy undergoing ram pressure stripping, with star-forming gas tails, substantial stripped gas, and a 33-53 Myr stripping timescale.

quotes.quote: The \ and optical imaging show some of our key results: (1) NGC 3314a is a classic ``jellyfish'' galaxy with bright star forming regions coincident with an extended \ tail; (2) NGC 3312 has a sharply swept-back disk with the \ disk edges now trailing behind the galaxy as it moves through the cluster; (3) the ring galaxy, LEDA 753342, is not obviously experiencing ram pressure; and (4) NGC 3314b has already lost a significant amount of its \ gas through its interaction with the ICM. As the deepest \ observations to date, with excellent spatial resolution, combined with deep optical imaging, we report the conclusive observation of active ram pressure stripping in NGC 3312, NGC 3314a, and NGC 3314b. The first two galaxies show not only gas loss through truncated \ disks and extended \ tails (Figure fig:fdp24 ), but active star formation in H streams and clumps in and around the stripped gas.
For NGC 3314a, we estimate 1.1 10^9 \ or 38 4 of the measured gas mass. The southernmost part of the \ tail extends to about 40 kpc from the optical galaxy center.

For NGC 3314a, we find similar extents, with detections out to distances of 37 kpc and maybe as far 59 kpc. Note that these distances, derived from optical data, extend beyond the outermost contours in the \ data where the low column densities are likely due to gas being dispersed and/or being used up in star formation. With these distances and relative velocities we derive a conservative estimate to the duration of the stripping event of 32-59 Myr for NGC 3312 and 33-53 Myr for NGC 3314a.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3314A as the foreground spiral in NGC 3314AB, associated with an extended southwest filament network, lopsided disk structures, and possible combined tidal and ram-pressure histories.

quotes.quote: NGC 3314AB is a system of two spiral galaxies, member of the Hydra I cluster (see top-left panel of Fig. fig:mosaic ), seen in projection on top of each other along the line-of-sight. NGC 3314A is the foreground galaxy, with a heliocentric velocity of cz=2795 km s ^ 1 Christlein2003 , while NGC 3314B has cz=4665 km s ^ 1 Keel2001,McMahon1992 .

The highest signal-to-noise stacked ACS/HST mosaic (see Fig. fig:UV_HST ) extends out to 1.4 arcmin ( 20 kpc) on the SW side. Here, the disk of NGC 3314A has a lop-sided morphology, and several filamentary structures, containing bright knots, are also detected. Since the ACS/HST mosaic covers the 60 of the VST images around NGC 3314, the morphological assessment of the outermost regions could not be done. Based on the new VST images, we concluded that the features detected from HST data on the SW side of NGC 3314A are part of the more complex and extended structure of stellar filaments, which we estimated to be 20 kpc wide and 50 kpc long (see top-right panel of Fig. fig:mosaic ), becoming fainter ( _g 26-27 mag/arcsec ^2 ) at larger radii. The GALEX data show that they have UV emission out to 3.7 arcmin ( 54 kpc) from the centre of NGC 3314A (see Fig. fig:UV_HST ).
The structure of NGC 3314A could be even more complex, where tidal forces might have act in the past, while RPS is still ongoing.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3314A among Hydra I RPS candidates, notes it overlaps NGC 3314B without being physically related, and cites H I evidence for stripping.

quotes.quote: conducted a multi-wavelength analysis of four galaxies (NGC 3312, NGC 3314A, NGC 3314B and LEDA 753354) located in the center of the Hydra I galaxy cluster, three of which are also included in our sample of RPS candidates. It should be noted that NGC 3314A and NGC 3314B are two overlapping spiral galaxies with a velocity difference of about 1850 km/s, indicating that they are not physically related. In our study, it was only possible to estimate the non-parametric parameter for NGC 3314A. These authors found evidence for RPS in NGC 3314A, and NGC 3314B through the signature of H i contours.

gmw_00003552.name: NGC 3314B
gmw_00003552.overview: NGC 3314B is a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2209.05605] that is projected behind NGC 3314A and is not physically related to it [arXiv:2312.09220]. It is in an advanced stage of ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2209.05605, arXiv:2312.09220], having lost a large portion of its gas with no remaining star-forming tail [arXiv:2209.05605].

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 3314b as a background edge-on late-type galaxy in an advanced ram pressure state, with significant gas loss and no H I or star-forming tail.

quotes.quote: In addition, we also detect NGC 3314b in \ (Figure fig:fdp4b ), the close to edge-on spiral galaxy seen in nearly perfect projection behind NGC 3314a.

The \ and optical imaging show some of our key results: (1) NGC 3314a is a classic ``jellyfish'' galaxy with bright star forming regions coincident with an extended \ tail; (2) NGC 3312 has a sharply swept-back disk with the \ disk edges now trailing behind the galaxy as it moves through the cluster; (3) the ring galaxy, LEDA 753342, is not obviously experiencing ram pressure; and (4) NGC 3314b has already lost a significant amount of its \ gas through its interaction with the ICM. As the deepest \ observations to date, with excellent spatial resolution, combined with deep optical imaging, we report the conclusive observation of active ram pressure stripping in NGC 3312, NGC 3314a, and NGC 3314b. The first two galaxies show not only gas loss through truncated \ disks and extended \ tails (Figure fig:fdp24 ), but active star formation in H streams and clumps in and around the stripped gas. NGC 3314b reveals a late-type galaxy at a more advanced stage of RPS.
We also resolve NGC 3314b for the first time and show that it is in an advanced state of RPS, with no evidence for an \ (or star forming) tail.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3314B as an overlapping spiral not physically related to NGC 3314A, with previous H I contours providing evidence for ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: It should be noted that NGC 3314A and NGC 3314B are two overlapping spiral galaxies with a velocity difference of about 1850 km/s, indicating that they are not physically related. In our study, it was only possible to estimate the non-parametric parameter for NGC 3314A. These authors found evidence for RPS in NGC 3314A, and NGC 3314B through the signature of H i contours.

gmw_00003553.name: LEDA 753354
gmw_00003553.overview: LEDA 753354 is a galaxy that may be in the early stages of experiencing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2312.09220]. Its neutral hydrogen contours reveal a faint trailing tail extending toward the northeast, while a star-forming arc is visible on its leading compressed side [arXiv:2312.09220].

sources.summary: The paper says LEDA 753354 may be in an early ram-pressure stage, with H I contours showing a faint northeast trailing tail and optical residuals showing a leading star-forming arc.

quotes.quote: Regarding LEDA 753354, proposed that this particular galaxy may be at an early stage of experiencing ram pressure. The H i contours (Fig. 4 in their study), revealed the presence of a faint tail extending in the trailing side and directed towards the North-East. Interestingly, in the opposite direction of this tail, in what is the leading compressed side, there is a star-forming arc, which is observed in the residual maps shown in Fig. B1 in the supplementary material . Then, the optical morphology reinforces the RPS scenario for LEDA 753354.

gmw_00003554.name: FS90-98
gmw_00003554.overview: FS90-98 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy and a member of the Antlia cluster [arXiv:1403.3534]. It exhibits an iE morphology characterized by two non-central star-forming knots superposed on a larger, low surface brightness elliptical component [arXiv:1403.3534].

sources.summary: FS90-98 is confirmed as an Antlia blue compact dwarf with H emission, cluster-member velocity, iE morphology, two star-forming knots, and BCD-consistent near-infrared structure.

quotes.quote: FS90-98, FS90-106, and FS90-147 are confirmed as BCDs and cluster members, based on their morphology, K_S surface photometry, oxygen abundance, and velocity redshift.

Morphological analysis FS90-98 resembles to an iE galaxy, defined by Loose Thuan ( ) as a compact galaxy showing several knots closed to centre superposed on a larger low surface brightness non-star-forming elliptical component. It shows two non-central star-forming knots (noted 1 and 2) separated by 4.4^ in the net H right-hand panel image of Figure  fig1 . The colour profile appears slightly bluer towards the outskirts, with total colour of R - K_S = 2.02 mag. The K_S profile could be fitted with a sech plus a Gaussian component, consistent with the BCD classification of this galaxy.

gmw_00003555.name: FS90-106
gmw_00003555.overview: FS90-106 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Antlia cluster [arXiv:1403.3534]. It exhibits a very blue color, a velocity of 2580 km/s, and at least two non-central, hydrogen-emitting knots [arXiv:1403.3534].

sources.summary: FS90-106 is confirmed as an Antlia blue compact dwarf; it has H-emitting knots, a very blue color, member velocity, and BCD-consistent morphology despite shallow K_S data.

quotes.quote: FS90-98, FS90-106, and FS90-147 are confirmed as BCDs and cluster members, based on their morphology, K_S surface photometry, oxygen abundance, and velocity redshift.

FS90-106 shows a similar iE appearance in both R and H images with at least two non-central knots visible in the H image separated by 2.5^ . The total color R - K_S = 1.12 is very blue, consistent with a BCD classification. Nevertheless, the K_S image is the faintest in the whole sample, so the K_S apparent magnitude and colour profile should be regarded with caution outside 3^ radius.

Averaging our measurements obtained from the two bright lines, 5007  [ OIII ] and 6563 H , we obtain the following redshift and velocities for the three galaxies observed in spectroscopy mode: for FS90-98 z = 0.0095, v = 2850 km/s, for FS90-106 z = 0.0086, v = 2580 km/s, and for FS90-147 z = 0.0062, v = 1860 km/s.

gmw_00003556.name: FS90-147
gmw_00003556.overview: FS90-147 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy and a confirmed member of the Antlia cluster [arXiv:1403.3534]. It features several non-central knots visible in hydrogen-alpha imaging, alongside diffuse near-infrared emission that becomes redder toward its outskirts [arXiv:1403.3534].

sources.summary: FS90-147 is confirmed as an Antlia blue compact dwarf with H emission, member velocity, several non-central knots, diffuse near-infrared emission, and probable BCD structure.

quotes.quote: FS90-98, FS90-106, and FS90-147 are confirmed as BCDs and cluster members, based on their morphology, K_S surface photometry, oxygen abundance, and velocity redshift.

FS90-147 clearly shows a few non-central knots in the Gemini R and H images, labelled along the two slits A and B in Figure  fig1 . The most visible are the four central bright knots in the H image. The brighter pair (along slit A) is separated by 2.9 , while the fainter pair along slit B is separated by 4.2 (nodes marked as B1 and B2).

Other diffuse regions are visible to the north and south in the H image, from which node B3 was also placed along slit B. The K_S image is quite shallow and shows extended diffuse NIR emission, while the R - K_S profile seems to become redder towards the outskirts sampled areas, and total colour is R - K_S = 2.25 mag. All these findings, together with the surface profile in K_S make the BCD classification probable for this galaxy.

gmw_00003557.name: FS90-155
gmw_00003557.overview: FS90-155 is a galaxy that features a nucleus located close to its center [arXiv:1403.3534]. It exhibits a flocculent structure resembling spiral arms winding in a counter-clockwise direction, but it lacks detected H-alpha emission [arXiv:1403.3534].

sources.summary: FS90-155 lacks detected H emission and remains unconfirmed as a star-forming Antlia member, but the paper describes its nucleus, flocculent spiral-like structure, color, and matching K_S measurement.

quotes.quote: FS90-155 and FS90-319 did not show any H emission, and they could not be confirmed as dwarf cluster star-forming galaxies.

FS90-155 clearly shows a nucleus close to centre in the R , H and K_S images, having no H emission. Its margins appear well defined towards the north-east, while towards the south this galaxy shows some flocculences (more visible visible in the original FITS image), which resembles more of a spiral galaxy with counter-clockwise winding arms. The colour profile varies between 1.5 < R - K_S < 3.0 with total colour R - K_S = 2.43 mag.
FS90-155 shows some flocculent structure in both R and K_S images resembling spiral arm structures and showing no H emission.

gmw_00003558.name: FS90-319
gmw_00003558.overview: FS90-319 is an extremely faint galaxy that lacks detectable H-alpha emission [arXiv:1403.3534]. It features elliptical diffuse emission with two brighter knots superposed in the north, and it exhibits a flat color profile [arXiv:1403.3534].

sources.summary: FS90-319 lacks H emission and has uncertain membership; the paper describes it as extremely faint, with two northern knots, elliptical diffuse emission, flat color, and BCD-candidate context.

quotes.quote: FS90-155 and FS90-319 did not show any H emission, and they could not be confirmed as dwarf cluster star-forming galaxies.

Two objects were detected by 2MASS, namely FS90-155 whose 2MASS K_S = 13.26 magnitude coincides with our VISTA measurement, and the extremely faint galaxy FS90-319 whose shallower 2MASS measurement ( K_S = 13.76 mag) comes quite close to our VISTA measurements ( R = 13.16 mag).

FS90-319 shows two brighter knots superposed in the North of an elliptical diffuse emission in all R , H and K_S images, although no H emission could be detected. Due to the brighter knot in the North-East, both R and K_S profiles show a peak around 6^ radius, and the colour profile appears flat at R - K_S 2.4 mag.

gmw_00003559.name: SDSS J101129.49 + 014323.3
gmw_00003559.overview: SDSS J101129.49 + 014323.3 is a strong gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens at a redshift of 0.331 [arXiv:1602.02927, arXiv:1604.08339]. This lens consists of an interacting, merging early-type galaxy pair [arXiv:1602.02927, arXiv:1604.08339] that magnifies a background star-forming Lyman-alpha emitter at a redshift of 2.701 into an Einstein cross [arXiv:1604.08339, arXiv:astro-ph/0606210].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J1011 + 0143 as an interacting early-type galaxy-pair lens with kiloparsec mass-light offsets and a magnified background Ly emitter.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of significant mass/light offsets in the strong gravitational lensing system SDSS J1011 + 0143. We use the high-resolution Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) F555W- and F814W-band imaging and Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectroscopy of this system, which consists of a close galaxy pair with a projected separation of 4.2 kpc at z_ lens 0.331 lensing a Ly emitter (LAE) at z_ source = 2.701 . Comparisons between the mass peaks inferred from lens models and light peaks from HST imaging data reveal significant spatial mass/light offsets as large as 1.72 0.24 0.34 kpc in both filter bands. Such large mass/light offsets, not seen in isolated field lens galaxies and relaxed galaxy groups, may be related to the interactions between the two lens galaxies.

The foreground-light distribution is modeled as four distinct S e rsic components to substantially capture the distorted morphological shapes due to galaxy interactions. To model the total-mass distribution, we adopt a two-SIE clump model. With three S e rsic source components, we obtain a satisfactory fit to the observations. The V- and I- band AB apparent magnitudes of the primary lens are 19.54 0.11 and 17.69 0.11 , and 22.14 0.11 and 20.38 0.11 for the secondary lens.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1011+0143 as an Einstein-cross system with a bright elliptical lens, a star-forming lensed source, and HST evidence that the lens is a merging galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: Einstein cross lens system SDSS J1011+0143 was discovered by bol06 , who found the lens galaxy is a bright elliptical at z_ lens =0.331 , while the lensed source is a Ly bright, star-forming galaxy at redshift z_ source =2.701 from Keck B band image. The four lensed images form a perfect Einstein cross, and the cross relation inferred from Keck B band image has a relatively lower value. Later, based on higher resolution observations through the HST F555W ( V ) and F814W ( I ) filters of the Wide Field Channel (WFC), shu16a found the lens in fact comprises a merging galaxy pair with a small projected separation of 4.2 kpc. As shown in Table 1, the cross relations derived from the HST V and I band data are significantly larger than that derived from Keck B band data.

The deviations stem from the differences in the relative brightness of image C. Normalized by the other three images, image C appears much more bright in the HST data than that in the Keck data. The reasons for such differences are still uncertain. We suspect that they can be caused by the different foreground-light subtraction approaches.

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J101129.49 + 014323.3 as a wide-separation Einstein-cross lens whose bright elliptical lens magnifies a faint z=2.701 star-forming Ly-alpha galaxy.

quotes.quote: This Letter reports the discovery of a new spectroscopically-selected strong gravitational lens with a emitting galaxy as its source, SDSS J101129.49 + 014323.3 (hereafter ). The lensed galaxy forms a highly symmetric Einstein cross which, by virtue of its large physical scale, provides leverage for determining the dark-matter halo mass of the lensing elliptical. The system also provides a highly magnified view of an intrinsically faint, compact, and high-redshift star-forming galaxy.

The total mass of the lensing galaxy within the 8.8 0.1 kpc enclosed by the lensed images is (5.2 0.1) 10^ 11 M_ . The lensed galaxy is a low mass galaxy (0.2 L_* ) with a high equivalent-width Ly line ( EW_ Ly ^ rest = 46 5 ).

gmw_00003560.name: SDSS J1707+6443
gmw_00003560.overview: SDSS J1707+6443 is a nitrogen-loud quasar located at a redshift of approximately 3.2 [arXiv:1205.5092]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with a relatively high Eddington ratio and features dense gas in its narrow-line region [arXiv:1205.5092].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J1707+6443 as a z=3.2 nitrogen-loud quasar whose NLR is not extremely metal-rich, has dense kpc-scale gas, and a moderately high Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: We present near-infrared spectroscopy of the z 3.2 quasar SDSS J1707+6443, obtained with MOIRCS on the Subaru Telescope. This quasar is classified as a ``nitrogen-loud'' quasar because of the fairly strong N iii ] and N iv ] semi-forbidden emission lines from the broad-line region (BLR) observed in its rest-frame UV spectrum. However, our rest-frame optical spectrum from MOIRCS shows strong [ O iii ] emission from the narrow-line region (NLR) suggesting that, at variance with the BLR, NLR gas is not metal-rich. In order to reconcile these contradictory results, there may be two alternative possibilities; (1) the strong nitrogen lines from the BLR are simply due to a very high relative abundance of nitrogen rather than to a very high BLR metallicity, or (2) the BLR metallicity is not representative of the metallicity of the host galaxy, better traced by the NLR. In either case, the strong broad nitrogen lines in the UV spectrum are not indication of a chemically enriched host galaxy.

We estimated the black hole mass and Eddington ratio of this quasar from the velocity width of both C iv and H , that results in log( M_ BH / M_ ) = 9.50 and log( L_ bol / L_ Edd ) = -0.34. The relatively high Eddington ratio is consistent with our earlier result that strong nitrogen emission from BLRs is associated with high Eddington ratios. Finally, we detected significant [ Ne iii ] emission from the NLR, implying a quite high gas density of n_ e 10^6 cm ^ 3 and suggesting a strong coupling between quasar activity and dense interstellar clouds in the host galaxy.

gmw_00003561.name: NGC 1270
gmw_00003561.overview: NGC 1270 is an elliptical or compact elliptical galaxy located in the core of the Perseus cluster [arXiv:1906.11867, arXiv:1907.10188]. It hosts a central supermassive black hole, an old stellar population, and a cool X-ray emitting minicorona [arXiv:1308.2682, arXiv:1906.11867].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1270 as a compact high-dispersion outlier: it has a 2 kpc half-light radius, very high velocity dispersion, old stellar population, and little radial age variation.

quotes.quote: In the meantime, we can look at one spectacular outlier in our sample: NGC1270. This galaxy has a half-light radius of 2 kpc, and a central stellar velocity dispersion of 370 .

van den Bosch and collaborators have found a handful of galaxies like NGC 1277, including NGC 1270 for low-mass versions see trujilloetal2009,jiangetal2012, ferremateuetal2012 . They have disky light profiles and are rotationally dominated at large radius. NGC 1270 appears to be an extreme example of a compact galaxy that formed at high redshift, and then grew no more. With a central age of 11 Gyr, it is one of the oldest galaxies in the sample (Paper I), has a very high central \ EW, and shows no gradients as a function of radius ( log Age/ log R_e =−0.20 0.13 ). As far as we can tell, NGC 1270 follows the \ relation (Paper I) but the statistics are very limited for velocity dispersions so high.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1270 as an elliptical or compact elliptical whose curvature and residual image suggest an inner spheroidal component and possible small disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1270 is classified as an elliptical galaxy RC3_1991 and further as a compact elliptical galaxy yildrim2017 . The curvature (R) is displayed in fig:cut_NGC1270_f160w_kur_new , its shape is not continuous (as expected) and having small oscillations of low scale. However, the curvature has a smooth overall shape, is high in inner regions (around R/R_p 0.2 ) and drops to near zero at large radius. As can be seen in the residual image, the central region ( R/R_p 0.20 ) indicates an inner spheroidal component with different properties compared to the main body of the galaxy (missing light regarding the model for the main body). There is a sign of a small disk within R/R_p 0.1 aligned with the apparent main axis of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1270 with Chandra, describing it as a Perseus elliptical with a supermassive black hole and a cool X-ray minicorona that survives in the intracluster medium.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The coordinates of NGC 1270 and NGC 1272 are RA (J2000): 49.7419 ; DEC (J2000): +41.4702 and RA (J2000): 49.8385 ; DEC (J2000): +41.4908 , respectively.

quotes.quote: We study the X-ray properties of two elliptical galaxies, NGC 1270 and NGC 1272, in the core of the Perseus cluster with deep Chandra observations. Both galaxies have central supermassive black holes, the mass of which is 6.0 10^ 9 \ M _ and 2.0 10^ 9 \ M _ respectively. Our aim is to examine relatively cool soft X-ray emitting gas within the central region of these massive early-type galaxies.

With thermal emission from the minicorona dominating over any power-law radiation components, we find that both NGC 1270 and NGC 1272 encompass minicoronae, the temperature and radius of which are 0.99 keV and 0.63 keV; 1.4 kpc and 1.2 kpc respectively.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1270 as an example galaxy for globular-cluster analysis, showing its radial surface-density fit, luminosity function, and I-H color subpopulations.

quotes.quote: As an example, in Figure fig:NGC1270_GCspatialDist we show the GC surface number density for the galaxy NGC 1270, and its best \ fit.

In Figure fig:GCLF_NGC1270 we show the luminosity functions for the inner and outer subpopulations (top panel), as well as the GCLF (bottom panel) in the I band. We also show the best Gaussian*Pritchet function (dotted curve), and the corresponding single Gaussian (solid curve) for the galaxy NGC 1270.
In Figure fig:colorHist_NGC1270 we show the color I-H distribution for galaxy NGC 1270, plotting the inner , outer and from 0 to 2 \ subpopulations.

gmw_00003562.name: E 0015+162
gmw_00003562.overview: E 0015+162 is a relatively bright radio-quiet companion quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0301123]. Its X-ray spectrum fits an absorbed power law with a steep photon index and shows evidence of an iron fluorescence line [arXiv:astro-ph/0301123].

sources.summary: The paper measures E 0015+162 as a relatively bright radio-quiet companion quasar, finding an absorbed power-law X-ray spectrum, a steep photon index, and evidence for Fe fluorescence.

quotes.quote: The nearest companion cluster and relatively bright companion quasar E 0015+162 (which is of an X-ray luminosity more typical of a local Seyfert galaxy) are both well measured in the \ EPIC data. In previous \ data the emission from the quasar is confused with that from and so values reported for the quasar based on that observation are unreliable. We find that the spectrum of E 0015+162 fits well a power law absorbed only by the column density of gas through our Galaxy, with a relatively steep power-law photon index of = 2.38 0.06 . There is evidence for the detection of an Fe fluorescence line.

gmw_00003563.name: EGS 31125
gmw_00003563.overview: EGS 31125 is a high-redshift disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.06 [arXiv:2309.10038]. While it appears unbarred in Hubble Space Telescope F160W imaging, observations with the James Webb Space Telescope reveal that it is a strongly barred galaxy [arXiv:2309.10038].

sources.summary: The paper uses EGS 31125 to show JWST's effect on bar classification: it is strongly barred in F444W, unbarred in HST F160W, and at z 2.06.

quotes.quote: The galaxy EGS 31125 is shown in Figure Fig: filter_comp in the three different filters employed: HST WFC3 F160W and JWST NIRCam F356W and F444W. EGS 31125 is classified in the F444W filter as strongly barred and unbarred in the F160W filter. This figure clearly shows the impact of improved sensitivity and longer wavelength with JWST on the galaxy at redshift z 2.06 and how distinctive the disc structures become see also Guo_2023 .

gmw_00003564.name: DESJ 0405-3308
gmw_00003564.overview: DESJ 0405-3308 is a quadruply imaged lensed quasar with a spectroscopic redshift of 1.713 [arXiv:1805.12151]. The lensing deflector is modeled as a singular isothermal ellipsoid [arXiv:1805.12151] and is estimated to be an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1908.06983].

sources.summary: The paper confirms DESJ 0405-3308 as a quadruply imaged lensed quasar, presents imaging and spectra, models the lens with an SIE profile, and notes flux-ratio anomalies.

quotes.quote: DESJ 0405-3308 follow up imaging was obtained with Magellan IMACS in bands g, and i on 2016 November 29 and with SOAR SAMI in bands R,V and z on 2016 December 3. The z band image was used for PSF fitting astrometry measurements. The photometry for the lensed images and galaxy (see Fig. 0405im for naming scheme) is shown in Table 0405phot . A single slit was used on Magellan IMACS through images B and C. Fig. 0405spec shows the extracted spectra and the identified lines at a redshift of z=1.713 0.001.

In making the lens model, we have selected a Singular Isothermal Ellipsoid (SIE) for the mass profile and have obtained a good fit within the astrometric uncertainties. The constraints used in the lens model are only the relative position of the quasar images and the lens galaxy. The best fit SIE parameters obtained were an Einstein radius R_E =0 69 and an ellipticity e =0.17 at a position angle e_ PA =29 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper uses WGD J0405 photometry and satellite-galaxy information to estimate a photometric deflector redshift, while adopting a population-mean halo-mass prior.

quotes.quote: We use this procedure to construct a prior for the halo mass of each quad, with the exceptions of B1422, PS J1606, and WGD J0405. The stellar velocity dispersions implied by the Einstein radii of these systems is significantly lower than the stellar velocity dispersion in the sample of quads used to calibrate the halo-to-stellar mass ratio in Lagattuta++10 , and as such the estimate of the halo mass using the above procedure may not be accurate for these systems. For B1422, PS J1606, and WGD J0405, we therefore assume the population mean of 10^ 13.3 0.3 inferred by Lagattuta++10 .

The quads PS J1606 and WGD J0405 do not have measured spectroscopic redshifts, so we use photometry from Shajib++18 to obtain photometric redshift estimates. The photometry from Shajib++18 comes in three bands: F160W, F814W, and F475X with magnitude uncertainties of 0.1-0.3 dex. We use the software package eazy Brammer++08 , and restrict the templates to only consider the SEDs for early-type galaxies, which are 90 of galaxies acting as strong lenses. We verify this procedure is accurate by applying it to other deflectors in sample analyzed by Shajib++18 that have measured spectroscopic redshifts, and then proceed to derive PDFs for deflector redshifts in the systems PS J1606 and WGD J0405. The results are shown in Figure fig:photozs . The top row shows four quads from the sample analyzed in Shajib++18 with measured spectroscopic redshifts, and the bottom row shows the pdfs output by eazy for the systems PS J1606 and WGD J0405.

gmw_00003565.name: DESJ 0407-5006
gmw_00003565.overview: DESJ 0407-5006 is a double lensed quasar system with a redshift of z=1.515 [arXiv:1805.12151]. The system features a nearly circular lensing galaxy and has an Einstein radius of 0.87 arcseconds [arXiv:1805.12151].

sources.summary: The paper confirms DESJ 0407-5006 as a double lensed quasar, reports imaging, spectra, a nearly circular lens galaxy, and a lens model with small external shear.

quotes.quote: Follow up imaging for DESJ 0407-5006 was obtained with Magellan IMACS in the i-band on 2016 December 1. The photometry for the lensed images and galaxy is shown in Table 0407phot . Follow-up spectra were obtained with NTT-EFOSC2, as described above, with an exposure time of 600s. Fig. 0407spec shows the extracted spectra and the identified emission lines at a redshift of z=1.515 0.001.

The light profile fit shows a nearly circular shape for the lensing galaxy. Given the reduced number of positional constraints of this double system, we have selected a singular isothermal sphere plus external shear as a mass model and we have added the spectroscopic flux ratio measured on top of the CIII ] and MgII emission lines as a constraint (we avoid using the CIV since it is very close to the edge of the CCD) to the relative positions of the quasar images and the lens galaxy. We conservatively measure a flux ratio of A/B=3.0 0.2, which is consistent with the observed i band flux ratio. The best fit requires Einstein radius of R_E =0 87, together with a small external shear ( <0.06 at 90 confidence) at 130 degrees measured north to east (or south to west).

gmw_00003566.name: UDF-21003
gmw_00003566.overview: UDF-21003 is a robustly detected high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 9.8 [arXiv:2212.10126, arXiv:2306.02468]. It exhibits a very blue ultraviolet slope, which indicates the presence of a young, low-metallicity stellar population [arXiv:2212.10126].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDF-21003 as a newly detected, robust z~9.8 candidate with a very blue UV slope consistent with a young, low-metallicity stellar population.

quotes.quote: UDF-21003, with a photo-z of z=9.8^ +0.2 _ 0.1 , is a newly-detected source which is in fact the most robustly detected candidate in the sample. It was previously undetected likely due to the close positioning of a low-redshift nearby source.

However, we measure a robust UV slope for UDF-21003, finding =-2.5 0.2 . This represents a very blue UV continuum indicating the presence of a young, low-metallicity stellar population. However, we note that cullen2022 found a median UV slope of = -2.29 0.09 at 8<z<16 , indicating that UDF-21003 is fully consistent with the average UV slope found at these redshifts.

sources.summary: The paper uses JADES-GS-53.17551-27.78064 as an example GOODS-S EAZY fit, identifying it as an F115W dropout with evidence for z greater than 9.

quotes.quote: In Figure example_SED_fit we show the EAZY fit to an object in GOODS-S, JADES-GS-53.17551-27.78064, along with the P(z) surface, and the JADES NIRCam thumbnails. The source is an F115W dropout, with no visible flux at shorter wavelengths. The fit constrained at z < 7 produces significantly more F115W flux than is observed, lending evidence of this galaxy being at z > 9 .

gmw_00003567.name: Arp 147
gmw_00003567.overview: Arp 147 is a collisional galaxy system comprising an early-type intruder galaxy and a star-forming ring galaxy [arXiv:1105.4423, arXiv:2401.02025]. This classical empty ring galaxy features uniformly distributed compact emission knots [arXiv:0807.1477] and a collision-triggered starburst [arXiv:1105.4423].

sources.summary: The paper presents Arp 147 as a collisional ring-galaxy system: IFS kinematics constrain the collision velocity, age, and impact angle, while the ring shows triggered star formation and spatial metallicity variation.

quotes.quote: We present observations of Arp 147, a galaxy system comprising a collisionally-created ring galaxy and an early-type galaxy, using the Oxford SWIFT integral field spectrograph (IFS) at the 200-inch Hale telescope. We derive spatially resolved kinematics from the IFS data and use these to study the interaction between the two galaxies. We find the edge-to-edge expansion velocity of the ring is 225 8 km s^ 1 , implying an upper limit on the timescale for the collision of 50 Myrs. We also calculate that the angle of impact for the collision is between 33^ 54^ , where 0 ^ would imply a perpendicular collision. The ring galaxy is strongly star-forming with the star formation likely to have been triggered by the collision between the two galaxies.

The eastern half of the ring exhibits a metallicity a factor of 2 higher than the western half. The ionisation parameter, measured across the ring, roughly follows the previously observed trend with metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp147 as a classical empty ring galaxy with uniformly distributed compact emission knots, diffuse ring emission, and a red nucleus shaping the K-band morphology.

quotes.quote: Arp147: This classical empty ring galaxy is the brightest \ emitter in our sample (leaving out NGC985, where the Seyfert nucleus is responsible for the emission), with the emitting compact knots more or less uniformly distributed in the ring. The space between the knots in the ring is also filled with diffuse \ emission. Note that there is no compact \ knot associated with the nucleus, which is extremely red and gives a crescent shape to the ring in the K band. Several filamentary structures on the northeastern segment can be noticed in the \ image. The observed crescent structure and the displaced nucleus have been reproduced by ger92 , using stellar and gas dynamical models, with G1 as the intruder.

sources.summary: The paper uses Arp 147 as a ring-galaxy modeling example, describing two ring galaxies and a best-fit decomposition with ring, bulge, and fine-structure components.

quotes.quote: The HST /F814W image of the field Arp 147 contains two ring galaxies (Figure arp147 , Table arp147-table ), one of which has a bulge-like component with a tidally disturbed outer region (Galaxy 1), and the other is a pure ring (Galaxy 2). The best-fitting model for Galaxy 2 is a single-component ring, modified by Fourier modes, as seen in Figure arp147 b , whereas Galaxy 1 requires two ring components, a bulge, and an inner fine-structure component (Figures arp147 e-h ). The fine-structure component of Galaxy 1 can be easily seen in the surface brightness profile as an upturn within =0 2 of Figure arp147 i (left).

sources.summary: The MOND simulations treat Arp 147 as a massive collisional ring galaxy whose observed stellar masses and intruder-to-target mass ratio can be explained without dark matter halos.

quotes.quote: The observed massive ring galaxies, for instance, Arp 147 with a stellar mass of 2.1 10^ 10 Romano+2008 for the disc galaxy and a massive intruder galaxy with an ITMR of 1.75 , later the disc galaxy mass is suggested 6.1-10.1 10^ 10 by Fogarty+2011 and the corresponding ITMR is between 0.6 and 0.4 , reside in the mild-MOND regions. In the existing Newtonian modellings Mapelli_Mayer2012 , f=0.5 is used by assuming the dynamical mass follows the stellar mass. Such a ring galaxy can be well explained by our collisional scenario in MOND, without the presence of dark matter halos for both galaxies.

gmw_00003568.name: NGC 2681
gmw_00003568.overview: NGC 2681 is a nearly face-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271, arXiv:astro-ph/9906262] classified as a LINER [arXiv:1104.4891, arXiv:astro-ph/0604487] that hosts three nested barlike structures [arXiv:astro-ph/9906262]. Its central tens of parsecs contain a dynamically decoupled stellar-gaseous disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0311271] and a point-like ultraviolet spike [arXiv:astro-ph/0001311] that shows no short-term X-ray variability [arXiv:1407.0663].

sources.summary: The paper fits two NGC 2681 X-ray observations with a thermal component and absorbed power law, finding parameters and fluxes consistent with earlier analyses.

quotes.quote: NGC 2681. One of the two observations performed with \ has been already reported in satyapalapj05:sfVSliner . The authors fit the 0.5-8 keV spectrum with a combination of a thermal component with kT 0.7 keV and a power-law with a photon index 1.6 . No intrinsic absorption was required. The same observation was treated in gonzalezmartin09aa and same results were derived after fitting the spectrum with a mekal and a power-law.

gonzalezmartin09aa derived a 2-10 keV flux of 2 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . We fit the spectrum of the two \ observations of NGC 2681 simultaneously with a combination of a thermal component and an absorbed power-law. We found similar results to that derived in the previous works ( 1.5 and kT 0.6 keV) with a 2-10 keV flux of 3 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 2681 has a dynamically decoupled central region and an inclined or polar stellar-gaseous disk in its central tens of parsecs.

quotes.quote: The panoramic spectroscopy data (Fig. 2681m ) show that stars at >3-4'' exhibit a circular rotation where the value of matches the disk's line of nodes. But at <2'' we observe strong changes of the dynamical axis of the stars by 30^ 90^ . The variations in the velocity field of the ionized gas are smaller, but differ systematically from that of the stars by 10-15^ at >3'' . Also the symmetry center of the gas velocity field is shifted by 1.5 '' to the east from the continuum center, similarly to NGC 470 . In addition, a sharp turn of the isovelocities is seen at <2'' (80 pc). So this region is dynamically decoupled concerning both the stars and the gas.
Thus an inclined (or polar) stellar-gaseous disk exists in the central few tens parsecs of NGC 2681 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2681 as a nearly face-on early-type galaxy with three barlike structures, arguing that the outer bar morphology and NICMOS imaging support a triple-bar interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2681, Figure 1: This galaxy is close to face-on; outer disk isophotes have ellipticity < 0.07 . Thus, the three barlike structures cannot be projection effects. The outermost bar is relatively large, though not outside the range for early-type galaxies found by . Although this structure is relatively faint, the PA is nearly stationary at 30 for a 45 - 60 (1c). At these radii, the isophotes have the squared-off ends (1a, d) characteristic of bars in early-type galaxies ( ).
The NICMOS image confirms the existence of a third bar --- tentatively identified by and as the galaxy's secondary bar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2681 as an S0/a galaxy with a central point-like spike whose FOC/FOS properties resemble NGC 4552 and may trace accretion-disk emission.

quotes.quote: To investigate the UV upturn of ellipticals, in 1993 HST/FOC images in several UV bands were obtained for the central regions of the elliptical galaxies NGC 1399 and NGC 4552 and of the bulge of the S0/a galaxy NGC 2681. A point-like source -- or spike - was evident at the center of both NGC 4552 and NGC 2681, with their photometric profile being indistinguishable from the PSF of the pre-COSTAR HST (Paper I [ 1 ] ).

In summary, there are quite many analogies between the central spikes in NGC 4552 and NGC 2681. The possibility that the line emission in the NGC 2681 spike originates in an accretion disk seen more face on than in NGC 4552 cannot be ruled out.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2681 as a LINER with a central bright source, diffuse emission, radio loudness, broad H emission, and central X-rays dominated mostly by stellar processes.

quotes.quote: NGC 2681 (L1, SB0/a) has three point sources within its central kiloparsec. The brightest point source is at the exact center and is detected in both the soft and hard bands. This is reminiscent of NGC 1553, except that NGC 2681 has more diffuse emission than NGC 1553. The spectrum of the central source is best fitted by a two-temperature plasma model or a power-law plus a single-temperature plasma model (see Table 3).

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 2681 is fit without parameter variability across Chandra observations, shows no four-month variation, and has no detected short-term variation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2681 : The SMF0 results for Chandra data with no improvement when vary the parameters. Thus, the object do not vary in a period of four months. Short term variations are not detected.

gmw_00003569.name: red tip
gmw_00003569.overview: The red tip is a resolved, very red object with a disk-like surface brightness profile [arXiv:0906.1069]. It is physically associated with nearby extended radio emission, suggesting that the object may be a background, faint head-tail radio galaxy [arXiv:0906.1069].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the red tip as a resolved, very red object near PKS 0521--365, with disk-like profile and nearby radio emission suggesting a background faint radio galaxy.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: optical position (RA=05 22 57.3 ; Dec= -36 27 25.5) of the red tip

quotes.quote: The near-IR image obtained by MAD also clearly detects and resolves the diffuse emission at about 9 arcsec NW from the nucleus (see Table 3 for details). The observed apparent mag in K = 17.9. The object is very red: R- K = 3.4 (using R mag = 21.3 derived from HST image). This feature is well resolved in the near-IR and has an average surface brightness that is well described by a linear (disk like) profile.

We performed a detailed analysis of the radio emission in the region around the red tip . This revealed extended radio emission at about (RA=05 22 57.38 ; Dec= -36 27 23.8 ) that is not exactly coincident with the optical position (RA=05 22 57.3 ; Dec= -36 27 25.5) of the red tip but sufficiently close to be considered physically associated. The shape of this radio structure suggests that it has the characteristic morphology of a head-tail radio galaxy. Based on these coincidences we argue that the red tip could be a background, faint radio--galaxy.

gmw_00003570.name: J07173724+3744224
gmw_00003570.overview: J07173724+3744224 is a foreground elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.1546 [arXiv:1711.09500]. It is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy and hosts a globular cluster population with a bimodal color distribution [arXiv:1711.09500].

sources.summary: The paper measures J07173724+3744224's globular clusters, identifying it as a foreground elliptical and FR-I radio galaxy with redshift 0.1546 and a bimodal color distribution.

quotes.quote: To increase the diversity of environments in which the GC system of elliptical galaxies have been measured, we studied the GC population of the elliptical galaxy J07173724+3744224 (hereafter J07173724). This galaxy is in the foreground of the galaxy cluster MACS J07175+3745 ( z=0.5458 ), which is part of the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) Frontier Fields observation campaign Koekemoer14,Lotz17 . The redshift of J07173724 ( z=0.1546 0.0001 ) was measured by using the LRIS instrument on Keck-I, but was not included in their published catalogue (H. Ebeling, private communications).

J07173724 ( _ J2000 =07^ h 17^ m 37 2 , _ J2000 =37 44 23 0 ) is one of the most distant galaxies to have its GC population measured, third behind the brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 2744 z=0.308 ; Lee16 and Abell 1689 z=0.18 ; Alamo13 . We also note that J07173724 is catalogued as a FR-I type Fanaroff74 radio galaxy Bonafede09,vanWeeren16 with a linear-like feature having a total projected length of 240 kpc, as measured from a combined 2-4 GHz JVLA image see fig. 1 from vanWeeren17 .

We present the first measurement of the globular cluster population surrounding the elliptical galaxy J07173724. This galaxy is located in the foreground of the HST ACS observations of the galaxy cluster MACS J0717.5+3745. Based on F435W and F814W images, J07173724 is found to have a bimodal colour distribution with red GCs 1-3 more abundant than blue GCs.

gmw_00003571.name: MCG-01-35-011
gmw_00003571.overview: MCG-01-35-011 is a spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 103.3 megaparsecs [arXiv:2211.04423]. It is known to be the host galaxy of the supernova SN 2018evt, which occurred at a projected offset of 6.5 kiloparsecs from the galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.04423].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG-01-35-011 as the spiral host of SN 2018evt, giving its nucleus coordinates, redshift, radial velocity, distance, and the supernova's projected offset.

quotes.quote: It was discovered at V 16.5 mag in Aug. 2018 Nicholls_etal_2018 , in the outskirts of the sprial galaxy MCG-01-35-011 (redshift z = 0.025352 0.000133 ; daCosta_etal_1998 ).
The coordinates of the nucleus of the host spiral galaxy MCG-01-35-011 are ( , )_ Host = (13^ h 46^ m 39 779 0 004 0 164 , \ 09^ 38'45 641 0 070 0 125 ) ; see Fig. Fig_image .

This value is consistent with the reported redshift of the host galaxy; it is used throughout the paper. We interpret the radial velocity as exclusively due to redshift and adopt a Hubble constant of H _0 = 73.24 1.74 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 Riess_etal_2016 . We derive a distance for the host of 103.3 2.5 Mpc. We measure the angular separation between SN 2018evt and the nucleus of its host as 13 0 , corresponding to a sky-projected separation of 6.5 0.2 kpc.

gmw_00003572.name: EN1 202260
gmw_00003572.overview: EN1 202260 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=1.545 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214]. It is detected in the X-rays and exhibits broad type-1 emission lines, with its infrared emission dominated by hot dust [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EN1 202260 as a z=1.545 4.5 micron-peaker with broad type-1 AGN lines, X-ray detection, and hot-dust-dominated IRAC emission.

quotes.quote: A fourth 4.5 m-peak object lies at z=1.545 (target EN1 202260) and is characterized by broad emission lines, which testify to the presence of a type-1 AGN component contributing to the UV-optical emission. This object is detected also in the X-rays. The IRAC spectral energy distribution shows a red 8.0-5.8 m observed color (see Fig. fig:ib_seds1 ), the 8.0 m observed flux being likely dominated by hot dust heated by the AGN component. Photometric and spectroscopic redshift estimates are in agreement, the former having been obtained with a type-1 AGN template (Polletta et al., in prep.).

gmw_00003573.name: LH 572243
gmw_00003573.overview: LH 572243 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.820 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214]. Its narrow-line spectrum and X-ray data indicate the presence of a type-2 active galactic nucleus, though its spectral energy distribution can be modeled using a simple starburst template [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214].

sources.summary: The paper describes LH 572243 as a z=1.820 narrow-line source whose C IV emission and X-ray data indicate type-2 AGN activity, while its SED can be fit without an AGN component.

quotes.quote: Finally, source LH 572243 lies at z=1.820 , having narrow lines detected in its spectrum. A bright, narrow Civ ( =1549 ) emission line, without any P-Cyg profile, is detected, testifying to the presence of a type-2 AGN nucleus e.g. farrah2005 , which is also confirmed by X-ray data.

The LRIS spectrum of source LH 572243 shows a weak, but clear, Civ emission, with no P-Cyg profile, which we interpreted as due to AGN activity. The stellar synthesis fit, however, reproduces the observed SED without the need of any type-2 AGN component.
In two cases (EN1 340451 and LH 572243) the whole SED, up to the mid-IR is reproduced with a simple starburst model (see above).

gmw_00003574.name: EN2 172324
gmw_00003574.overview: EN2 172324 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a redshift between 1.7 and 2.0 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214]. It hosts a type-2 active galactic nucleus and contains a reddened torus as well as a warm dust component [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214].

sources.summary: The paper treats EN2 172324 as a z~1.7-2.0 high-redshift starburst with type-2 AGN signatures, a reddened torus, and a difficult-to-fit IRAC peak requiring warm dust.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies have a confirmed redshift between 1.7 and 2.0, narrow Ly and other emission lines and SED typical of high-redshift starbursts, i.e. EN1 342445, EN2 11091 and EN2 172324. The latter shows a bright Civ narrow line, with no P-Cyg profile, that we interpret as produced by a type-2 AGN. A reddened torus contributes also to the IRAC SED, shifting the peak to the 5.8 m band (see Section sect:IB_SED ).

Only in one case (EN2 172324) is a warm dust component needed in order to explain the near-IR fluxes. Nevertheless, in this latter case, our code cannot fully reproduce the observed shape of the IR-peak, even including the type-2 AGN. Other effects might explain the observed SED of EN2 172324. Dust in AGB stars can significantly modify the shape of the IR-peak, changing its colors.

gmw_00003575.name: EN1 340451
gmw_00003575.overview: EN1 340451 is a faint optical galaxy at a high redshift of z=2.866 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214]. It hosts a type-2 active galactic nucleus and a moderate starburst within an extremely massive host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0703214].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EN1 340451 as the highest-redshift IR-peaker detected, a faint z=2.866 source with narrow lines suggesting type-2 AGN activity and a massive starburst host.

quotes.quote: The highest redshift IR-peaker detected is a a z=2.866 source, EN1 340451, lying at the upper redshift limit of the 5.8 m-peak selection. This is a very faint optical source, with bright IRAC emission and a weak 24 m flux. We detect Ly- , He ii ( =1640 ) and Ciii ] ( =1909 ) narrow lines. The wavelength coverage of the data has a gap in the range =5500-6150 , where Civ ( =1549 ) would fall, but the presence of He ii (with a ionization energy of 54.4 eV, four times that of Hi ) suggests the presence of a type-2 AGN component.

Similarly, we classified source EN1 340451 as a type-2 AGN, on the basis of the He ii narrow line detected with Keck, but its broad-band SED is well fitted by a stellar model, with no need for additional components. Thus this source is best fitted by a moderate starburst (SFR =56.1 M _ /yr) hosted in an extremely massive galaxy with M =6.69 10^ 11 M _ .

gmw_00003576.name: J021830.5 053124
gmw_00003576.overview: J021830.5 053124 is a lensed source with a small Einstein radius [arXiv:1309.0836]. Its 880-micron emission has been resolved using very extended Submillimeter Array data, which reveals a two-image configuration [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper presents new very extended SMA data for J021830.5 053124, resolves its 880 micron emission, and models a small-Einstein-radius lens with a two-image configuration.

quotes.quote: J021830.5 053124: SMA data from the compact and extended array configurations were originally presented in Ikarashi:2011qy and Wardlow:2013lr . This paper presents new data obtained the in the very extended array, permitting the first resolved measurement of the 880 m emission from this object. The best-fit lens model finds a position for the lens that is offset relative to that indicated in the HST image by 0 26 in RA and 0 11 in Dec. These values are near the expected level of 0 2-0 3 absolute astrometric uncertainty between the SMA and HST reference frames. The lensed emission is barely resolved by the SMA due to the small Einstein radius of the lens.

gmw_00003577.name: J022016.5 060143
gmw_00003577.overview: J022016.5 060143 is an astronomical object that has been the subject of detailed gravitational lensing studies [arXiv:1309.0836]. Its observed 880-micron emission is modeled using a two-component lens and a three-component source [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper tests Fu:2013lr with visibility-plane modeling, using two lens components and three source components, and finds a modest total 880 micron magnification factor.

quotes.quote: J022016.5 060143: This object is the subject of a detailed study by Fu:2013lr . We present it here mostly for completeness, but also to test the validity of the model in Fu:2013lr using the visibility-plane lens modeling technique described in this paper. We use a two-component lens model and a three-component source model to reproduce the observed 880 m emission. We enforce a minimum Einstein radius of _ E > 0 2 for both lenses (corresponding to a minimum mass of M_ lens > 1.6 10^ 10 M _ ). This is intended to reproduce the prior on the lens masses used by Fu:2013lr based on the lens stellar masses and an assumed relation between the stellar mass and dark matter halo mass.

gmw_00003578.name: J083051.0 + 013224
gmw_00003578.overview: J083051.0 + 013224 is a strong gravitational lens system with a complex lensed emission configuration [arXiv:1309.0836]. This configuration is caused by a secondary foreground lensing galaxy located less than one arcsecond northwest of the primary lens [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper describes J083051.0 + 013224 as an unusual lens configuration with a secondary foreground galaxy, spectroscopic lens redshifts, nearly equal Einstein radii, and inferred lens masses.

quotes.quote: J083051.0 + 013224: The lensed emission has an unusual configuration that reflects the complexity of the foreground mass distribution due to the presence of a secondary lensing galaxy <1 northwest of the primary lensing galaxy. WHT/ACAM spectroscopy shows that the primary lens is located at z_ lens = 0.626 based on G-band and Mg absorption features, while the secondary lens has very faint continuum and an emission line at observed frame 7462 . If this feature is [ O 2 ] 3727, then its redshift is z_ lens2 = 1.002 . The best-fit lens model for this system assigns each of the foreground galaxies approximately equal Einstein radii ( 0 4 ). Given the stated lens and source redshifts, this implies lens masses of 0.7 10^ 11 M _ and 1.6 10^ 11 M _ , respectively.

gmw_00003579.name: J084933.4 + 021443
gmw_00003579.overview: J084933.4 + 021443 is a strong gravitationally lensed source [arXiv:1309.0836]. The system includes a lensed source designated as T, which has been analyzed using visibility-plane lens modeling [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper revisits J084933.4 + 021443 with visibility-plane modeling, isolates the lensed source called T, and finds a larger magnification factor than an earlier analysis.

quotes.quote: J084933.4 + 021443: This object is the subject of a detailed study by Ivison:2013fk . It is presented here for completeness and to ensure that the use of a visibility-plane lens model provides the same results as given in Ivison:2013fk . Note that the center and extent of the image cutout shown in Figure fig:modeling has been adjusted from the SMA emission centroid used in Figure fig:imaging to include only the lens and the lensed source dubbed ``T'' in Ivison:2013fk . This is done simply to facilitate the comparison of model and data. An additional two sources used in the fitting process are not shown in this diagram.

gmw_00003580.name: J090302.9 014127
gmw_00003580.overview: J090302.9 014127 is a galaxy that exhibits strong gravitational lensing with very small image separations [arXiv:1309.0836]. Tentative CO emission from the lens was previously reported but could not be confirmed by subsequent, more sensitive observations [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper reports very small image separations for J090302.9 014127, notes unconfirmed tentative CO emission from the lens, and treats the lens as an insignificant submillimeter emitter.

quotes.quote: J090302.9 014127: The image separations are close to the smallest values found in the SMA subsample ( _ E = 0.35 0.02 ). There has been tentative evidence ( 3 ) of CO( J=5-4 ) emission from the lens at z_ lens = 0.942 0.004 Lupu:2012ly , but this has not been confirmed with subsequent, more sensitive observations that rule out lens redshifts from 0.922 to 0.944 Omont:2011fk . In the lens model presented here, the lens is assumed to be an insignificant submm emitter.

gmw_00003581.name: J091043.1 000321
gmw_00003581.overview: The galaxy J091043.1 000321 exhibits residual surface-brightness emission that may indicate a complex source structure [arXiv:1309.0836]. Alternatively, this emission could be caused by external shear from an edge-on galaxy located 4.4 arcseconds to the northwest [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper finds residual surface-brightness emission around J091043.1 000321 and suggests either external shear from a nearby edge-on galaxy or source complexity beyond a single Sersic profile.

quotes.quote: J091043.1 000321: This is one of a handful of objects with emission at the > 2 level that can be seen in the surface brightness map made from the residual visibilities. An edge-on galaxy located 4 4 to the northwest with a position angle of 135 ^ east of north could be responsible for an external shear that has not been included in the lens model. Alternatively, the residual flux density may reflect a more complicated source structure than can be represented by our choice of a single S ersic profile.

gmw_00003582.name: J091840.8 + 023047
gmw_00003582.overview: J091840.8 + 023047 is a submillimeter galaxy that lacks an obvious morphological signature of gravitational lensing [arXiv:1309.0836]. It is unique among similar objects for having no counterpart within 1 arcsecond of its 880-micrometer centroid in Hubble Space Telescope imaging [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper describes J091840.8 + 023047 as lacking an HST counterpart and obvious SMA lensing morphology, making it a possible rare unlensed submillimeter galaxy needing follow-up.

quotes.quote: J091840.8 + 023047: This object is unique in having no counterpart within 1 of the 880 m centroid in the HST /F110W Snapshot imaging. There is no obvious morphological signature of lensing based on the SMA data, so this may be a rare unlensed SMG. It is not included in Figure fig:modeling since no lens model is available. Further investigation is needed to determine the nature of this object.

gmw_00003583.name: J103826.6 + 581542
gmw_00003583.overview: J103826.6 + 581542 is a background astronomical source that is subject to strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1309.0836]. The lensed source has an estimated size of 0.45 arcseconds and is magnified by a factor of 7.1 [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper checks J103826.6 + 581542 against Wardlow:2013lr, finding consistent source size and magnification, but notes that no source redshift is available for deeper analysis.

quotes.quote: J103826.6 + 581542: This object was originally presented by Wardlow:2013lr . We present it here for completeness and to test the validity of the model in Wardlow:2013lr using the visibility-plane lens modeling technique described in this paper. Our results for the size ( 0.45 0.18 vs. < 0.5 ) and magnification factor of the background source ( 7.1 1.5 vs. 5.32^ +1.28 _ 1.06 ) are consistent with those reported in Wardlow:2013lr . Since a redshift measurement for the background source remains unavailable, no further analysis is possible for this object.

gmw_00003584.name: J105712.2 + 565457
gmw_00003584.overview: J105712.2 + 565457 is a lensed source galaxy [arXiv:1309.0836]. High-resolution imaging of the object is still insufficient to distinguish cleanly separated lensed images, and no lens model is currently available [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper re-reduces J105712.2 + 565457, gaining slightly better spatial resolution, but still cannot distinguish cleanly separated lensed images or provide a lens model.

quotes.quote: J105712.2 + 565457: This object was originally presented by Wardlow:2013lr . We present a slightly modified reduction of this object here. Instead of MIRIAD's mossdi task, which was used in Wardlow:2013lr to image this object, here we have shifted all of the visibility datasets to have the same phase center and then used MIRIAD's clean task. This resulted in slightly improved spatial resolution ( 0 68 0 57 vs. 1 18 0 97 ), that is still not sufficient to distinguish cleanly separated images from a single lensed source. It is not included in Figure fig:modeling since no lens model is available.

gmw_00003585.name: J105750.9 + 573026
aliases_external: gravitationally lensed SMG in HerMES field
gmw_00003585.overview: J105750.9 + 573026 is a strongly lensed, high-redshift submillimeter galaxy at z = 2.958 [arXiv:1104.4115, arXiv:1802.00242]. It is a merger system consisting of a dusty starburst galaxy and an optically bright Lyman break-like galaxy [arXiv:1802.00242], which are magnified by a foreground group of galaxies [arXiv:1104.4115, arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper measures HLSW-01 as a strongly lensed, ultra-luminous submillimeter galaxy at z=2.958 and models its molecular gas from multiple CO lines.

quotes.quote: We report on the redshift measurement and CO line excitation of HERMES J105751.1+573027 (HLSW-01), a strongly lensed submillimeter galaxy discovered in Herschel /SPIRE observations as part of the Herschel Multi-tiered Extragalactic Survey (HerMES). HLSW-01 is an ultra-luminous galaxy with an intrinsic far-infrared luminosity of L_ FIR = 1.4 10^ 13 L_ , and is lensed by a massive group of galaxies into at least four images with a total magnification of = 10.9 0.7 . With the 100 GHz instantaneous bandwidth of the Z-Spec instrument on the Caltech Submillimeter Observatory, we robustly identify a redshift of z=2.958 0.007 for this source, using the simultaneous detection of four CO emission lines ( J = 7 6 , J = 8 7 , J = 9 8 , and J = 10 9 ). Combining the measured line fluxes for these high- J transitions with the J = 1 0 , J = 3 2 and J = 5 4 line fluxes measured with the Green Bank Telescope, the Combined Array for Research in Millimeter Astronomy, and the Plateau de Bure Interferometer, respectively, we model the physical properties of the molecular gas in this galaxy.

High resolution imaging at 880 m with the Submillimeter Array (SMA) reveals at least four images separated by 9 , and higher resolution K_p band imaging shows strong lensing arcs. The lensing model (Gavazzi et al. 2011; hereafter G11) suggests that HLSW-01 is lensed by a massive group of galaxies, with a total gravitational magnification of = 10.9 0.7 . When corrected for the magnification, this galaxy is found to be a very bright ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) with an FIR luminosity of L_ FIR = (1.43 0.09) 10^ 13 L_ and an implied SFR of 2460 160 M_ yr ^ 1 (Conley et al. 2011; hereafter C11).

sources.summary: The paper presents HLock01 as a bright lensed high-redshift SMG merger, combining a dusty massive SMG and an optically bright LBG separated by 3.3 kpc.

quotes.quote: The submillimeter galaxy (SMG) HERMES J105751.1+573027 (hereafter HLock01) at z = 2.9574 0.0001 is one of the brightest gravitationally lensed sources discovered in the Herschel Multi-tiered Extragalactic Survey. Apart from the high flux densities in the far-infrared, it is also extremely bright in the rest-frame ultraviolet (UV), with a total apparent magnitude m_ UV 19.7 mag. We report here deep spectroscopic observations with the Gran Telescopio Canarias of the optically bright lensed images of HLock01. Our results suggest that HLock01 is a merger system composed of the Herschel selected SMG and an optically bright Lyman break-like galaxy (LBG), separated by only 3.3 kpc in projection.

We have presented a detailed study of HLock01, one of the first gravitational lensed sources discovered in the HerMES survey. Unlike other SMGs, HLock01 is apparently very bright in all observed spectral bands, even in the optical. It is magnified by a factor of around 9 by a galaxy group-scale dark matter halo at z = 0.645 and comprises four images in the observed plane.

sources.summary: The paper models a gravitationally lensed high-redshift SMG at the stated coordinates, recovering its stellar and gas extents and a magnification of about 10.9.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the modeling of the gravitationally lensed SMG , also referred to as , discovered in Herschel /SPIRE observations during the Science Demonstration Phase by the HerMES program.
