Its J2000 coordinates are RA=10:57:51.0, Dec=+57:30:25.8 with a lens photometric redshift of =0.60 0.04 oyaizu08 and a source redshift of =2.9575 0.0001 (Riechers et al. \ 2011; Scott et al. \ 2011; hereafter R11 and S11). The goal of this lens modeling work is two-fold.

The high resolution imaging available in optical and Near-IR channels, along with CO emission obtained with the Plateau de Bure Interferometer, allow us to precisely estimate the intrinsic source extension and hence estimate the total lensing magnification to be =10.9 0.7 . We measure the half-light radius R_ eff of the source in the rest-frame Near-UV and V bands that characterize the unobscured light coming from stars and find R_ eff,* = [ 2.0 0.1 ] , in good agreement with recent studies on the Sub-Millimeter Galaxies population. This lens model is also used to estimate the size of the gas distribution ( R_ eff,gas = [ 1.1 0.5 ] ) by mapping back in the source plane the CO ( J=5 4 ) transition line emission.

sources.summary: The paper adds very extended SMA data for J105750.9 + 573026, compares SMA and Keck lens models, and finds similar magnification but positional/modeling tensions.

quotes.quote: J105750.9 + 573026: This object was originally analyzed in a series of papers reporting its discovery, ISM properties, gas dynamics, and (on the basis of K_ p band imaging) lensing geometry Conley:2011lr, 2011ApJ...733...29S, Riechers:2011uq, Gavazzi:2011lr . We present new very extended array data here and compare the SMA and Keck lens models. The very extended array data are not as sensitive as the compact array data, so natural weighting provides a beamsize of 0 99 0 94 . This is insufficient to resolve the individual images of the lensed source seen in the image from the compact array only data and is further indication that the lens model is primarily constrained by the compact array data. Because the S/N and spatial resolution in the Keck image are superior to those of the SMA image, we fix the parameters of the lens to match those of the model found by Gavazzi:2011lr .

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ105751 + 573026 _ 1,2,3,4,5,6 (HLock01; S 34) : This type 2 foreground lens is formed by a group of six galaxies (labelled from `1' to `6' in Fig. fig:models_full ) at z_ l ^ phot =0.60 Gavazzi2011 . We simultaneously model them with GALFIT using respectively two Sérsic components, one de Vaucouleurs and one Sérsic component, one Sérsic component, one de Vaucouleurs component, one Sérsic component, and a PSF. With respect to Gavazzi2011 we divide galaxy 5 into two separate components. The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =2.96 Gavazzi2011 forms a bright arc on the NE side of galaxy 1 that splits into two images in the proximity of galaxy 6, two additional bright compact images on the S and NW of galaxy 1, and, a faint counter image near the NE side galaxy 1 (which was predicted but not observed by Gavazzi2011 .

gmw_00003586.name: J113526.3 014605
gmw_00003586.overview: J113526.3 014605 is a galaxy with clearly resolved submillimeter emission detected at 880 micrometers [arXiv:1309.0836]. However, it lacks a detected counterpart in the K-band, and further observations are required to determine if the system is undergoing strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper finds no K-band counterpart for J113526.3 014605; its resolved SMA emission lacks separated lensed images, so stronger submillimeter and optical/near-infrared follow-up is required.

quotes.quote: J113526.3 014605: There is no counterpart detected in a 15-minute K_ s integration with the WHT. The SMA 880 m image is clearly resolved, but does not show individual, well-separated images of a lensed SMG. It is not included in Figure fig:modeling since no lens model is available. Further investigation is required to determine whether strong lensing is occuring in this object. The main avenues for progress are higher-spatial resolution submm imaging (e.g., SMA very extended array) and deeper observations in the optical or near-IR to determine whether or not there is a lensing galaxy.

gmw_00003587.name: J114637.9 001132
gmw_00003587.overview: J114637.9 001132 is a submillimeter galaxy that is subject to strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1309.0836]. High-resolution observations resolve the lensed emission of the background source into five distinct images [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper presents extended SMA data resolving J114637.9 001132 into five lensed SMG images, with source-plane morphology consistent with earlier work and residuals indicating model limitations.

quotes.quote: J114637.9 001132: This object was originally presented by Fu:2012uq . Here, we present new SMA extended array data that resolve the lensed emission into five striking images of the SMG. The position and morphology of the lensed SMG in the source plane reported here are statistically consistent with those found by Fu:2012uq . The presence of flux density at the 3 level in the map of the residual visibilities (see Figure fig:modeling ) indicates our simple assumptions about the lens mass model (singular isothermal ellipsoids at the locations of galaxies identified in the Keck-II/NIRC2-LGSAO and HST /F110W imaging) and/or the background source S ersic profile may be breaking down.

gmw_00003588.name: J125135.4 + 261457
gmw_00003588.overview: J125135.4 + 261457 is a high-redshift galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1801.01831]. Observations of the reconstructed source reveal an elongated morphology, suggesting it may comprise two partially blended components [arXiv:1801.01831].

sources.summary: The paper finds HATLASJ125135.4+261457 has a slightly larger Einstein radius than B13 and an elongated reconstructed source, suggesting two partially blended components that bias simple Sersic modelling.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ125135.4+261457 : The estimated Einstein radius is slightly higher than reported by B13 ( _ E =1.10 0.02^ versus _ E =1.02 0.03^ ) while the rotation angle of the lens is smaller ( _ L =28 2.5^ versus _ L =38 1^ ). The reconstructed source is quite elongated, extending in the SW to NE direction, with a shape that deviates from a perfect ellipse. This might suggest that, at the scale probed by the SMA observations, the source comprises two partially blended components. This morphology is not accounted for by a single elliptical S e rsic profile, which may explain the observed discrepancies with the results of B13.

sources.summary: The paper notes a larger-than-expected declination offset for J125135.4 + 261457, but the lens model independently predicts the lens potential shape, supporting a robust model.

quotes.quote: J125135.4 + 261457: The offset between the position of the lens from the lens model and from the WHT astrometry is 0 01 in RA and 0 38 in Dec compared to the best-fit parameters from the lens model. The offset in Dec is larger than expected given the astrometric uncertainty in aligning the SMA and WHT reference frames. On the other hand, the lens model correctly predicts the ellipticity and position angle of the lens potential (no priors were assumed for the shape of the lens potential), a strong indication that the lens model is robust.

gmw_00003589.name: J132427.0 + 284452
gmw_00003589.overview: J132427.0 + 284452 is a gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.68 [arXiv:1512.03059, arXiv:1308.4403]. It is a disk-like starburst system with a high star formation rate [arXiv:1512.03059, arXiv:1308.4403] and a compact active galactic nucleus contribution to its radio flux [arXiv:1308.4403].

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs HATLAS J132427.0+284452 as a z=1.68 lensed dusty star-forming galaxy, finding differential magnification between stars and dust, high star formation, and large stellar mass.

quotes.quote: 2, 85748 Garching, Germany ^ 18 Instituto de Astrof sica de Canarias (IAC), E-38200 La Laguna, Tenerife, Spain ^ 19 Departimento de Astrof sica, Universidad de La Laguna, E-38206, La Laguna, Tenerife, Spain ^ 20 Department of Physics Astronomy, University of British Columbia, 6224 Agricultural Road, Vancouver, BC V6T 1Z1, Canada We present a source-plane reconstruction of a Herschel and Planck detected gravitationally-lensed dusty star-forming galaxy (DSFG) at z=1.68 using Hubble , Sub-millimeter Array (SMA), and Keck observations. The background sub-millimeter galaxy (SMG) is strongly lensed by a foreground galaxy cluster at z=0.997 and appears as an arc of length 15^ in the optical images. The continuum dust emission, as seen by SMA, is limited to a single knot within this arc. We present a lens model with source plane reconstructions at several wavelengths to show the difference in magnification between the stars and dust, and highlight the importance of a multi-wavelength lens models for studies involving lensed DSFGs. We estimate the physical properties of the galaxy by fitting the flux densities to model SEDs leading to a magnification-corrected star formation rate of 390 60 M _ yr ^ 1 and a stellar mass of 1.1 0.4 10^ 11 M _ .

HATLAS J132427.0+284452 is a Herschel Astrophysical Terahertz Large Area Survey (H-ATLAS) selected strongly lensed arc of length 15'' at z = 1.68 . HATLAS J132427 is also Planck detected at 1.30 0.15 Jy in the 350 m band and is one of a few high- z Planck detections in H-ATLAS. A lens model with source plane reconstructions at several wavelengths allows the estimation of magnification factors for the stars _ stars 16 and the dust _ dust 5 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a blind Herschel redshift for the lensed starburst HATLAS J132427, confirms it with CO, and characterizes its dust, radio, star formation, lensing, and AGN-related emission.

quotes.quote: We report the redshift of HATLAS J132427.0+284452 (hereafter HATLAS J132427), a gravitationally lensed starburst galaxy, the first determined `blind' by the Herschel Space Observatory . This is achieved via the detection of consistent with z=1.68 in a far-infrared spectrum taken with the SPIRE Fourier Transform Spectrometer. We demonstrate that the redshift is secure via detections of CO 2 1 and 3 2 using the Combined Array for Research in Millimeter-wave Astronomy and the Institut de Radioastronomie Millim e trique's Plateau de Bure Interferometer.

The SPIRE FTS spectrum of HATLAS J132427, shown in Fig. fig:fts_spec , displays a 7.5 marginally resolved (1.2 GHz spectral resolution) emission line at (709.9 0.4) GHz . Attributing this to indicates a redshift, z=1.677 0.001 . No other lines were detected, with 3 < 200 , 230, 150, 600 Jy for [ O iii ] 88 , [ N ii ] 122 , [ O i ] 145 , and [ N ii ] 205 respectively, meaning that this redshift remained tentative at this point.

This source appears to be a disk-like system, with a best-fit of 2.91 10^ 13 ^ 1 , dust temperature of = 33 , and SFR of 4 , 300 ^ 1 yr ^ 1 , and a compact AGN-related contribution to the radio flux density.

sources.summary: The paper models J132427.0 + 284452 with two lens masses tied to Keck detections, while noting nearby clusters and weak constraints because the SMA source is not multiply imaged.

quotes.quote: J132427.0 + 284452: This object is the subject of detailed studies by George:2013qy and Fu et al. (in prep.) to explore its dust, gas, and stellar properties. In this paper, we use the Keck-II/NIRC2-LGSAO image to constrain the positions of the lenses and apply our visibility-plane lens modeling technique to the SMA data. We find that a two-lens mass model is needed to reproduce the observed data. These correspond directly to two galaxies detected at high significance in the Keck imaging. However, it should be noted that there are two nearby clusters detected in the Red-Sequence Cluster (RSC) survey RCS J132427 + 2845.2 at z=0.997 0.017 and RCS J132419 + 2844.7 at z=0.802 0.018 Gladders:2005qy .

gmw_00003590.name: J132859.3 + 292317
gmw_00003590.overview: J132859.3 + 292317 is a submillimeter galaxy that is well-detected and spatially resolved at 880 micrometers [arXiv:1309.0836]. It is optically undetected in SDSS imaging, which may indicate it is either lensed by a faint foreground galaxy or is an intrinsically bright, unlensed source [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper finds J132859.3 + 292317 spatially resolved but optically undetected, leaving it unclear whether it is lensed by a faint foreground galaxy or is an unusually bright unlensed SMG.

quotes.quote: J132859.3 + 292317: The nature of this object is unclear based on existing data. It is well-detected and clearly spatially resolved by the SMA at 880 m. The problem is the lack of optical or near-IR imaging at a depth beyond what is achieved in SDSS, where the object is undetected in all bands. One plausible interpretation is that the object is lensed by a relatively low-mass foreground galaxy at intermediate redshift so that it is undetected in the SDSS images. Alternatively, it is conceivable that the object is not lensed and has an intrinsic submm flux density of S_ 880 = 51.8 2.0 mJy.

gmw_00003591.name: J133649.9 + 291801
gmw_00003591.overview: J133649.9 + 291801 is an astronomical object that lacks any detection in the SDSS optical bands [arXiv:1309.0836]. Its Submillimeter Array morphology provides evidence typical of strong lensing, and the object is assumed to be strongly lensed [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper compares J133649.9 + 291801 to another optically undetected source, notes that its SMA morphology resembles strong lensing, and assumes the object is strongly lensed.

quotes.quote: J133649.9 + 291801: This object is similar to J132859.3 + 292317 in that there is no significant detection in any of the SDSS optical bands, nor is deeper optical or near-IR imaging available. However, the SMA morphology provides evidence typical of strong lensing. Here, we assume that the object is strongly lensed.

gmw_00003592.name: J134429.4 + 303036
gmw_00003592.overview: J134429.4 + 303036 is a distant source that exhibits strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1309.0836]. Its lensed images are well-separated and well-detected in Submillimeter Array data, which has enabled researchers to model its intrinsic source size and luminosity [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper describes well-separated lensed images for J134429.4 + 303036, high-S/N residuals from simplified assumptions, and a lens model that captures most SMA emission.

quotes.quote: J134429.4 + 303036: The lensed images are well-separated and well-detected in the SMA data. In fact, the S/N is so high that the map of the residual visibilities reveals emission at the 3 level, likely indicating that some of our model assumptions are over-simplifications. Nevertheless, the model captures the vast majority of the SMA emission and therefore provides a fair representation of intrinsic source size and luminosity. This object is similar to J125135.4 + 261457 in that the lens model successfully predicts the ellipticity and position angle of the lens potential without any non-standard priors placed on these parameters.

gmw_00003593.name: J141351.9 000026
gmw_00003593.overview: J141351.9 000026 is a galaxy that is not strongly lensed [arXiv:1309.0836]. Lens modeling indicates a modest magnification for the object, with the lens located northeast of a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper finds no SMA counterimage for J141351.9 000026, concludes it is not strongly lensed, and models a modest magnification with the lens near a BCG.

quotes.quote: J141351.9 000026: No counter image of this target is detected in the SMA data, an immediate indication that this object is not strongly lensed. The possibility of counter images falling outside of the SMA primary beam (FWHM of 36 at 880 m) is low due to the lack of such a counterpart in the SPIRE maps. The lens modeling confirms this, with _ 880 = 1.8 0.3 . The lens is located 7 7 northeast of a brightest cluster galaxy (BCG). The lens redshift used here is from the BCG, and in order to derive the mass of the lens from its _ E value, we have assumed that it is located at the same redshift as the BCG.

gmw_00003594.name: J142823.9 + 352619
gmw_00003594.overview: J142823.9 + 352619 is a starburst-dominated, ultraluminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.3 [arXiv:1005.1511, arXiv:2311.01158]. The background source is gravitationally lensed, forming a close arc and a diffuse secondary image around a foreground lens [arXiv:2311.01158].

sources.summary: The paper presents Herschel PACS line observations of this lensed starburst ULIRG, detecting far-infrared lines and finding PDR and star-formation properties unlike local ULIRGs.

quotes.quote: Naval Research Laboratory, Remote Sensing Division, 4555 Overlook Ave SW, Washington, DC 20375, USA Universidad de Alcal a de Henares, E-28871 Alcal a de Henares, Madrid, Spain Tel Aviv University, Sackler School of Physics Astronomy, Ramat Aviv 69978, Israel Received 30 March 2010; accepted 28 April 2010 We present Herschel PACS observations of rest-frame mid-infrared and far-infrared spectral line emissions from two lensed, ultra-luminous infrared galaxies at high redshift: MIPS J142824.0+352619 (MIPS J1428), a starburst-dominated system at z = 1.3, and IRAS F10214+4724 (F10214), a source at z = 2.3 hosting both star-formation and a luminous AGN. We have detected [ O I ] 63 m and [ O III ] 52 m in MIPS J1428, and tentatively [ O III ] 52 m in F10214. Together with the recent ZEUS-CSO [ C II ] 158 m detection in MIPS J1428 we can for the first time combine [ O I ] , [ C II ] and far-IR (FIR) continuum measurements for photo-dissociation (PDR) modeling of an ultra-luminous (L _ IR 10 ^ 12 L _ ) star forming galaxy at the peak epoch of cosmic star formation. We find that MIPS J1428, contrary to average local ULIRGs, does not show a deficit in [ O I ] relative to FIR.

MIPS J142824.0+352619 (MIPS J1428 hereafter) was discovered in Spitzer MIPS images of the NOAO Deep Wide Field Survey Bootes field. Subsequent Spitzer IRS low resolution spectra (Desai et al. 2006), sub-mm (Borys et al.

sources.summary: The paper presents new SMA data for J142823.9 + 352619, finds the source unresolved and likely not strongly lensed, and says only its small size is robust.

quotes.quote: J142823.9 + 352619: This object was originally discovered in Spitzer mid-IR imaging of the Bo otes Field Borys:2006lr and has since been the subject of a great many follow-up observations, a thorough summary of which may be found in Wardlow:2013lr . We present new SMA extended array data which do not resolve the source, further corroborating the idea that this object is very small and is not strongly lensed (i.e., < 2 ). Since we know that there is an intervening galaxy along the line of sight to the Herschel source, we impose a minimum Einstein radius for the lens of _ E > 0 1 (corresponding to a minimum lens mass of M_ lens > 10^ 10 M _ ). The constraints on the lens model are weak. One of the few robust claims we can make regarding this source is that it is very small ( a_ s < 0 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper models this strong-lensing system, describing the foreground lens components and the background source morphology, such as arcs, rings, knots, multiple images, or shear-driven structure.

quotes.quote: HERMESJ142824 + 352620 (HBootes03; S 38) : We model this type 1 foreground lens at z_ s ^ spec =1.03 Borys2006 with two Sérsic components using GASP2D . The background lensed source at z_ s ^ spec =1.33 Borys2006 forms a closer arc on the NE of the lens and a diffuse secondary image on the SW side of the lens. It was observed with SMA 340 GHz, Bussmann2013 , and ALMA Band 6, Amvrosiadis2018 in the sub-mm/mm wavelength range. The sub-mm/mm follow-up observations show no significant difference in morphology with respect to the near-IR observations.

gmw_00003595.name: J142825.5 + 345547
gmw_00003595.overview: J142825.5 + 345547 is a lensing galaxy classified as a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1309.0836]. The galaxy features a dust lane and possesses very little bulge, which makes estimating its peak position difficult [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper reports a lens-position offset for J142825.5 + 345547, attributes difficulty to an edge-on dusty lens, yet regards the model as robust from resolved lensed images.

quotes.quote: J142825.5 + 345547: This object is the subject of a detailed study by Wardlow et al. (in prep.). The lens model suggests the lens is located 0 20 west of the SMA emission centroid, but the peak of the edge-on spiral seen in the astrometrically-aligned HST image occurs 0 25 east of the SMA emission centroid. This is a difference of 0 45 in RA (the difference in Dec is insignificant) and is larger than the expected 1 astrometric uncertainty of 0 2 . However, estimating the peak position of the lensing galaxy is difficult because it is nearly exactly edge-on with a prominent dust lane and very little bulge. Moreover, the lensed images are well-resolved and well-detected in the SMA data, and the lens model correctly predicts the position angle and approximate ellipticity of the lens without any priors on these parameters.

gmw_00003596.name: J143330.8 + 345439
gmw_00003596.overview: J143330.8 + 345439 is a background source that is subject to strong gravitational lensing [arXiv:1309.0836]. Although its spatial resolution is insufficient to resolve clearly separated images, the system has been modeled using known spectroscopic redshifts for both the lens and the background source [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper re-reduces J143330.8 + 345439, still cannot identify separated images directly, but uses lens and source redshifts to infer strong lensing and model the system.

quotes.quote: J143330.8 + 345439: This object was originally presented in Wardlow:2013lr . We present a slightly modified reduction of this object here in which we shift all of the visibility datasets to have the same phase center and then use MIRIAD's clean task. This provides slightly improved spatial resolution that is still insufficient to identify clearly separated images of a single lensed source. Nevertheless, we use the knowledge that spectroscopic redshifts are available for both the lens and background source to infer that strong lensing is occuring. We then model this object using the same set of model parameters as applied to the other objects in the SMA subsample.

gmw_00003597.name: J144556.1 004853
gmw_00003597.overview: J144556.1 004853 is a submillimeter galaxy with no available lens or source redshift [arXiv:1309.0836]. Submillimeter Array data resolve its 880-micrometer emission at a relatively low signal-to-noise ratio, while near-infrared imaging detects a counterpart that may be either a lens or an intrinsic component of the galaxy [arXiv:1309.0836].

sources.summary: The paper says J144556.1 004853 lacks redshifts, has low-S/N resolved SMA emission, and has a Keck counterpart that could be either the lens or intrinsic SMG structure.

quotes.quote: J144556.1 004853: No lens or source redshift is available for this object. The SMA data resolve the 880 m emission, but detect the source at relatively low S/N (S/N < 6 ). Keck-II/NIRC2-LGSAO K_ s band imaging detects a counterpart that is located between the resolved components identified in the SMA data. This could be a detection of the lens, or it could simply be an intrinsic component of an unlensed SMG. Unlike J132859.3 + 292317, the total 880 m flux density ( S_ 880 = 9.0 2.1 mJy) is not unprecedented for unlensed SMGs.

gmw_00003598.name: NGC1047
gmw_00003598.overview: NGC 1047 is a tidally disturbed galaxy that is physically interacting with NGC 1052 [arXiv:1903.07285, arXiv:1907.03761]. This ongoing interaction has produced a clear tidal extension to the north-west, possible bridge features, and boxy outer isophotes [arXiv:1903.07285].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1047 as tidally disturbed and physically interacting with NGC 1052, showing extensions, possible bridge features, and boxy outer isophotes.

quotes.quote: The tidal tail as seen in \ around NGC 1052 extends towards NGC 1047 (see Figure lsbs ). NGC 1047 shows a clear tidal distortion/extension to the north-west, which is on the opposing side in respect to NGC 1052. NGC 1047 also shows tidal features towards NGC 1052, which could be a bridge . The outer isophotes of NGC 1047 appear to be boxy.

The LSB features between NGC 1052 and NGC 1047 and the lopsided LSB feature on the counterside of NGC 1047 undoubtedly show that NGC 1052 and NGC 1047 have an ongoing interaction and are therefore at the same distance. The boxy appearance of the outer isophotes of NGC 1047 further indicates tidal disturbance.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC1047 as interacting with NGC1052, argues a 4.6 Mpc literature distance conflicts with that interaction, and places it in the 19 Mpc projected group.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, NGC1052 is interacting with another galaxy in the field-of-view: NGC1047. This is clearly shown in the Dragonfly deep image of the group shown in Fig. 4 of 2019ApJ...874L...5V and the deep image by the Heron telescope provided by Fig. 1 of 2019A&A...624L...6M . This interaction is also very likely responsible for the perturbed distribution of HI around NGC1052 found in 1986AJ.....91..791V . In fact, this idea fits very well with the tail of HI gas of NGC1052 directly pointing to NGC1047 see Fig. 5 of 1986AJ.....91..791V . It is worth noting that distance quoted in the literature for NGC1047 of 4.6 Mpc 1984A&AS...56..381B is clearly incompatible with the ongoing interaction between this object and NGC1052. .

gmw_00003599.overview: NGC 4178 is a bulgeless, barred spiral galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2211.17271, arXiv:astro-ph/0505407]. The galaxy is highly inclined [arXiv:1304.1279] and may host a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.17271, arXiv:1705.09667], though recent NuSTAR observations did not detect it [arXiv:2211.17271].

sources.summary: The paper examines 4178 as a bulgeless spiral with debated obscured AGN evidence, reporting NuSTAR non-detection and lower limits or alternatives for its proposed nuclear source.

quotes.quote: 4178 is a bulgeless spiral galaxy residing at a distance of 16.2 Mpc away kent2008 in the Virgo Cluster. satyapal2009 first reported on the presence of an AGN in 4178 based on the detection of a high-ionization [ Ne V ] 14.3 m emission line -- a reliable tracer of AGN activity abel2008 in the mid-infrared (mid-IR) -- using the Spitzer Space Telescope . Given that the optical emission shows no sign of an AGN and instead is consistent with an HII star forming region e.g., secrest2012 , the detection of the [ Ne V ] emission line suggested that 4178 in fact hosted an optically elusive AGN. Follow-up observations presented in secrest2012 revealed a weak ( 5.3 ) and predominantly soft X-ray point source coincident with the nucleus of the galaxy. secrest2012 concluded that the X-ray source properties were consistent with a heavily obscured AGN with an absorbing column density of N_ H =5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , covering factor of C=0.99, and a photon index of =2.3^ +0.6 _ 0.5 .

Neither of the reported AGNs in 4178 and J0851+3926 are detected in any energy band, so we instead calculated the upper limits on the counts and fluxes for each AGN candidate (as outlined in Section sec:analysis ).

If NGC 4178 hosts a heavily obscured AGN, as posited by secrest2012 , it would need to be obscured by log( N_ H / cm ^2)>24.2 (assuming =2.3 and C=0.99) to remain undetected by in the 10-24 keV band. If 4178 instead hosts an unobscured AGN =2.6 , secrest2012 , its intrinsic flux is below the detection limit of the observation.

sources.summary: The paper detects faint central CO in NGC 4178, notes its typical CO line ratio, and cautions that its edge-on orientation may mix nuclear and outer-disk gas.

quotes.quote: We have studied the molecular gas in the vicinity of low-luminosity active galactic nuclei (AGNs) in three bulge-less spiral galaxies: NGC 1042, NGC 4178, and NGC 4395. The (1-0) and (2-1) transitions of gaseous carbon monoxide (CO) are clearly detected within the central kpc of all three galaxies. In general, the CO emission is faint, as may be expected from their less-than-spectacular star formation activity.

As can be seen in the right panel, the value of r_ 21 appears unusually high in two of the AGN (NGC 1042 and NGC 4395), while it is rather typical in NGC 4178. In this context, it is important to note that both NGC 1042 and NGC 4395 are oriented nearly face on (as is the entire comparison sample), while NGC 4178 is an edge-on spiral (see Fig. fig:mosaic ). Therefore, it is possible (and in fact rather likely) that the line-of-sight towards the nucleus of NGC 4178 contains gas located in its outer disk, and that our measurements for this galaxy are not really representative of the nuclear gas properties.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4178 as highly inclined, with asymmetric radio and H-alpha emission, low radio surface brightness, no detected polarization, and a shallow spectral index in star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4178 This highly inclined galaxy has an asymmetric radio continuum emission distribution at 6 and 20 cm, the southwestern part being brighter than the northeastern part of the galactic disk. This asymmetry along the galaxy's major axis is also observed in H . The overall radio continuum surface brightness is low ( 200 Jy/beam at 6 cm). No polarized emission is detected neither at 20 cm nor at 6 cm. The average degree of polarization is very low ( < 4 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4178 as a barred spiral whose inner gaseous disk is nearly face-on, producing zero velocity gradients along both major and minor axes.

quotes.quote: This is also the case of NGC 4178 where the innermost portion of the gaseous disk is nearly face on.
A zero velocity gradient is observed for the ionized-gas component along the minor axis of NGC 4178.

NGC 4064, NGC 4178, and NGC 4189 host a large-scale bar (RC3, but see also CAG). Qualitatively, the minor-axis gas kinematics can be explained as due to non-circular (e.g., Athanassoula 1992) or off-plane (e.g., Friedli Benz 1993) motions induced on the gaseous component by the tumbling triaxial potential of the bar. The innermost portion of the gaseous disk of NGC 4178 is nearly face on since a zero velocity gradient is observed along both major and minor axis.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4178 as a late-type bulgeless disk with [NeV] and Chandra nuclear X-ray evidence for a heavily absorbed AGN and a 10^4-10^5 solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 4178 is another late-type bulgeless disk galaxy that has a prominent [ NeV ] emission line suggesting the presence of an AGN ( 2009ApJ...704..439S ). Chandra observations of NGC 4178 reveal the presence of unresolved nuclear X-ray emission spatially coincident with the dynamical center of the galaxy and suggest that the AGN is heavily absorbed and accreting at high rates ( 2012ApJ...753...38S, 2013ApJ...777..139S ). Using the fundamental plane of accreting BHs, the correlation between nuclear stellar cluster mass and BH mass, and the bolometric luminosity, the authors estimate a range of BH masses for this source of 10^ 4 10^ 5 M _ .

gmw_00003600.name: J0851+3926
gmw_00003600.overview: J0851+3926 is a bulgeless spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1296 [arXiv:2211.17271]. Mid-infrared colors and near-infrared broad Pa-beta emission line detections suggest the galaxy hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.17271].

sources.summary: The paper studies J0851+3926 as a bulgeless spiral with a likely heavily obscured AGN, using NuSTAR non-detection and infrared diagnostics to constrain its obscuring column.

quotes.quote: J0851+3926 is a bulgeless spiral galaxy at z=0.1296 originally selected by satyapal2014 based on its Wide-Field Infrared Survey Explorer ( WISE ) mid-IR colors; J0851+3926 satisfied the stringent 3-band mid-IR AGN color cut defined by jarrett2011 , suggestive of a powerful, dust obscured AGN. J0851+3926 was selected again in bohn2020 in an effort to measure black hole masses in optically elusive AGNs in bulgeless galaxies. While the Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich BPT, baldwin1981 optical spectroscopic line ratios are consistent with a composite galaxy bohn2020 - which are commonly assumed to arise from a mixture of AGN and star formation driven emission, based on the criteria laid out in kewley2001,kewley2006,kauffmann2003 - shocks can also give rise to similar emission line ratios, leading to an ambiguous optical classification . Coupled with the lack of Balmer emission lines in the optical band, there is no definitive evidence in the optical for an AGN satyapal2014,bohn2020 . As a part of their elusive AGN campaign, bohn2020 published near-infrared spectra obtained from the Near-Infrared Echellette Spectrometer (NIRES) and Near-Infrared Spectrometer (NIRSPEC) on Keck for J0851+3926 and reported the detection of a broad ( 1489 184 km s ^ 1 in NIRSPEC, 1363 31 km s ^ 1 in NIRES) Pa emission line in both observations.
For J0851+3926, the non-detection suggests there is no X-ray emitting AGN with an observed flux in excess of 9.40 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ( =1.8 ) in the 10-24 keV band.

In order to remain undetected by , the central source in J0851+3926 would need to be obscured by log( N_ H / cm ^2)>24.1 , assuming a covering factor of C=0.5, or log( N_ H / cm ^2)>24.3 assuming C=0.99.

gmw_00003601.name: ceers-2112
gmw_00003601.overview: ceers-2112 is a barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3 [arXiv:2311.04283]. With a stellar mass of 3.9 billion solar masses, it is considered a progenitor of the Milky Way [arXiv:2311.04283].

sources.summary: The paper presents ceers-2112 as a z~3 barred spiral, deriving its stellar mass, Milky Way-progenitor status, coordinates, bar length, and structural evidence from JWST imaging.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension = 214.97993 degrees; declination = 52.991946 degrees; J2000.0

quotes.quote: Here we report observations of ceers-2112, a barred spiral galaxy at redshift z_ phot 3 , which was already mature when the Universe was only 2 Gyr old. The stellar mass ( M_ = 3.9 10^9 M_ ) and barred morphology mean that ceers-2112 can be considered a progenitor of the Milky Way Papovich2015, SotilloRamos2022, Xiang2022 , in terms of both structure and mass-assembly history in the first 2 Gyr of the Universe, and was the closest in mass in the first 4 Gyr. We infer that baryons in galaxies could have already dominated over dark matter at z 3 , that high-redshift bars could form in approximately 400 Myr and that dynamically cold stellar disks could have been in place by redshift z = 4–5 (more than 12 Gyrs ago) Rizzo2020, Lelli2021 . The barred nature of ceers-2112 (right ascension = 214.97993 degrees; declination = 52.991946 degrees; J2000.0) is identified through the multiwavelength analysis of the James Webb Space Telescope Near Infrared Camera (JWST/NIRCam) images taken during the first epoch (21–22 June 2022) of the Cosmic Evolution Early Release Science CEERS; Finkelstein2017 campaign.

Thus, we performed a multicomponent two-dimensional (2D) photometric decomposition of ceers-2112, assuming that its surface-brightness distribution is the sum of a double-exponential disk and a Ferrers bar (Fig. 1c) and found that the galaxy has a stellar bar with length r_ Ferrers = 0.42 0.03 arcsec (3.3 kpc). The decomposition of the azimuthal luminosity surface-density distribution into the Fourier m components using the composite ceers-2112 image provided the third piece of evidence that the galaxy has a prominent bar (maximum relative amplitude of the m = 2 to m = 0 component I_2/I_0 > 0.4 ) with strength S_ bar = 0.23 0.01 Aguerri2000, Athanassoula2002 .

gmw_00003602.name: 2MASX J07001137-6602251
gmw_00003602.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J07001137-6602251 is classified as a Seyfert or non-star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1904.09293]. It has a stellar mass of approximately $1.1 \times 10^{10} M_{\odot}$ [arXiv:1904.09293] and exhibits radio emission consistent with ongoing star formation [arXiv:2404.12431].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the ASASSN-19bt host as a possible Seyfert or non-star-forming galaxy with SPOG-like features, then fits its SED to estimate stellar, bulge, and black-hole masses.

quotes.quote: As can be seen in the left panel of Figure fig:host_lines , 2MASX J07001137-6602251 is similar to the "shocked post-starburst" (SPOG) galaxies identified by alatalo16 . SPOGs combine strong Balmer absorption with emission line ratios consistent with shocks and inconsistent with star-formation. This selection differs from an E+A or K+A selection by allowing for H emission, even if much of the emission is unlikely to be from star formation. Most SPOGs have star formation histories similar to traditionally-selected post-starburst galaxies, yet are on average younger and thus may have higher dust obscuration french18 .

The host of ASASSN-19bt lies in the Seyfert or non-star-forming portion of the diagram, along with several other TDE hosts, but it lies outside the region enclosing galaxies with line ratios consistent with shocks. Thus, while it is similar in many ways to SPOGS, it is likely not a member of this population. We fit the spectral energy distribution (SED) of the host galaxy using the Fitting and Assessment of Synthetic Templates fast ; kriek09 code. We used the UVOT UV and U , APASS BVgri , 2MASS JHK_S , and WISE W1 and W2 magnitudes given in Table tab:host_mags to constrain the model.

The fast fit indicates that has a stellar mass of M_ =1.1^ +1.3 _ 0.1 10^ 10 M _ , an age of 3.2^ +5.8 _ 0.1 Gyr, and a star-formation rate of SFR =1.7^ +0.6 _ 0.1 10^ 1 M _ yr ^ 1 . Using the average stellar-mass-to-bulge-mass ratio from the hosts of ASASSN-14ae, ASASSN-14li, and ASASSN-15oi holoien14b,holoien16a,holoien16b to scale the stellar mass of the host, as we have done with previous TDEs e.g., holoien18b , results in an estimated bulge mass of M_B 10^ 9.4 . This corresponds to an estimated black hole mass of M_ BH =10^ 6.8 mcconnell13 , comparable to the BH mass estimates for other TDE hosts e.g., holoien14b,holoien16a,holoien16b,brown17a,wevers17,mockler18 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J07001137-6602251 as ASASSN-19bt's host galaxy and models pre-disruption radio emission as a constant host component consistent with ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: ASASSN-19bt ( z = 0.0262 ) was discovered by the All-Sky Automated Survey for SuperNovae ASAS-SN; Shappee, Kochanek_2017 on 2019 January 29 in the galaxy 2MASX J07001137-6602251 holoien_2019 .

A pre-disruption radio detection implies that some of the radio emission we observe is due to background emission from the host galaxy and not associated with the TDE. If the host galaxy emission component is constant in time, then the archival radio observations and the non-detection from ALMA in 2019 require that the host spectral energy distribution (SED) at radio frequencies follows a power law F_ ^ 0.7 or steeper. For our modeling, we follow the same procedure outlined in alexander_2016 , and assume that the host component of the radio emission follows a single power law with F_ (439 \ Jy ) ( /2.1 GHz)^ 1 , where 439 Jy is the weighted average of the two archival detections at 2.1 GHz. We find that the archival detection is consistent with radio emission from ongoing star formation in the host galaxy.

This is consistent with holoien_2019 , who reported that an archival spectrum of 2MASX J07001137-6602251 was well fit by a star-formation rate (SFR) of 0.17^ +0.06 _ 0.01 M_ \ yr^ 1 . We note that holoien_2019 did not include a possible AGN component when modelling the archival host galaxy spectrum, therefore the inferred SFR should only be considered an upper limit.

gmw_00003603.name: AGC 232041
gmw_00003603.overview: AGC 232041 is a disk galaxy [arXiv:1410.7464]. Its stellar mass was re-estimated to be approximately 10 to the power of 11.05 solar masses after its initial Sloan Digital Sky Survey stellar mass was flagged as unreasonably large [arXiv:1410.7464].

sources.summary: The survey flags AGC 232041 as having an implausible SDSS stellar mass, recomputes it from luminosity and color, and marks the galaxy specially in comparison plots.

quotes.quote: All our targets have SDSS MPA/JHU stellar masses in the interval log / = [ 10.3, 11.4 ] except for AGC 232041, which has an unreasonably large value of log / =12.37 . Hence we computed new stellar mass estimates from the \ luminosity and the \ colour following zibetti09 , and applying k corrections based on analytical approximations by chilingarian10 . For the other galaxies in our sample there is an excellent correlation between these estimates and the MPA/JHU values, with an offset of 0.21 dex (Zibetti's values are systematically larger). Hence we replaced the stellar mass of AGC 232041 with the new estimate, log / =11.05 (after subtracting the systematic offset), which falls well within the stellar mass interval of the other galaxies in our sample. This galaxy is identified by a different symbol in our plots.

gmw_00003604.name: DRC-2
gmw_00003604.overview: DRC-2 is a gas-rich, star-forming protocluster galaxy [arXiv:2008.10613] that hosts a luminous, deeply buried Compton-thick quasar [arXiv:2008.10613]. The system is resolved into multiple rest-frame ultraviolet components [arXiv:2008.10613], suggesting an ongoing major merger [arXiv:2003.13694] and galaxy interaction [arXiv:2008.10613].

sources.summary: The paper presents DRC-2 as a gas-rich, star-forming, interacting protocluster galaxy hosting one of the most luminous and distant Compton-thick QSO candidates.

quotes.quote: The X-ray luminosity of DRC-2 is typical of luminous optically selected Type 1 QSOs. However, DRC-2 is obscured by a large column density, similar to some luminous hot dust-obscured galaxies (Hot DOGs) and X-ray selected AGN with moderate luminosity. DRC-2 is therefore one of the most luminous and distant QSOs deeply buried in Compton-thick material e.g., Vito18b , and the first one to be found in a protocluster environment e.g., Lehmer09a,Digby-North10 . DRC-2 is the second most gas-rich ( M_ H_2 3 10^ 11 M_ ) and star-forming SFR 1000 M_ yr^ 1 ; Oteo18,Long20 member of the protocluster after DRC-1. Moreover, like DRC-1, it is resolved into multiple rest-frame UV components, suggesting ongoing galaxy interaction.

sources.summary: The paper treats DRC-2 as one of the most luminous DRC components, models its broad undetected [C I] line from CO width, and places it beside an extended Ly blob.

quotes.quote: The total obscured SFR of DRC -- which, recall, is only the core of our larger over-density of DSFGs -- is as extreme as SFR 6,500 M _ yr^ 1 , and about 75 of that is taking place in three DRC components: DRC-1, DRC-2 and DRC-3, those with L_ IR 5 10^ 12 L_ .

For the DRC components whose [ C i ] (1--0) line is not detected (see for example DRC-2, whose [ C i ] (1--0) is not clearly detected in the spectrum -- the line is expected to be extremely broad, judging by the ^ 12 CO(6--5) profile), we have assumed that the [ C i ] (1--0) and ^ 12 CO(6--5) transitions have the same width.

The Ly image reveals the presence of Ly blob next to DRC-2 and extended over 60 kpc , similar to what is found around others high-redshift DSFGs Ivison1998MNRAS.298..583I,Chapman2001ApJ...548L..17C,Umehata2015ApJ...815L...8U,Geach2016ApJ...832...37G and radio galaxies Swinbank2015MNRAS.449.1298S .

sources.summary: The paper treats DRC-2 as a blended, likely merging DSFG whose elevated 3.6 micron emission drives a poorer SED fit and may trace recent extreme star formation.

quotes.quote: So, where necessary, we repeat this exact method for multiple objects detected within the same radius in the K _s band image, although this only applies to two sources: DRC-2 and DRC-7.

For the remaining objects: all of the best-fit model SEDs have reduced ^ 2 < 2 , except for DRC-2 with ^ 2 _ red 3 . For this object, the higher ^ 2 is likely due to the excess emission measured at observed-frame 3.6 m; DRC-2 is likely an ongoing major merger (see e.g. Figure fig:insetcontours ), and the excess 3.6 m emission may be driven by increased (and redshifted) H flux from a recent extreme star-formation event, captured in the wide-banded IRAC Channel 1 smit16 .

gmw_00003605.name: DRC-8
gmw_00003605.overview: DRC-8 is a massive, young, gas-rich, and dusty starbursting galaxy located at redshift z ~ 4 [arXiv:2206.04082]. It is a multi-component system that likely represents an ongoing merger event [arXiv:2003.13694], exhibiting elevated star-formation efficiency driven by a relatively small gas reservoir [arXiv:2003.13694].

sources.summary: The paper treats DRC-8 as a multi-component, likely merging DRC member, includes an extra UV counterpart, and finds elevated star-formation efficiency near the starburst regime.

quotes.quote: The only exception to this case is DRC-8 in which we choose to include the additional rest-frame UV object 2^ to the southwest of the brightest part of the ALMA centroid as the ALMA observations appear to also detect this additional object.

However, for DRC objects 1,2,7, and 8, which show multiple possibly interacting components, the fwhm of the CO( 6-5 ) emission used to originally spectroscopically confirm cluster membership is extremely broad ( >1000 km s ^ 1 , see Figure 2 in oteo18 ). We interpret this as evidence that these four objects are likely ongoing merger events, and therefore the 2-3 mm continuum measurements represent star-formation triggered within the global system.

DRC-8 and 9 have elevated SFRs near the starburst regime (above the expected z 4 main-sequence star-formation law at a given molecular gas mass scoville ). These objects also lie within the M _* SFR main-sequence which may suggest that their high star-forming efficiencies (=SFR/M _ H_2 ) are driven by relatively small gas reservoirs rather than extreme rates of star-formation.

sources.summary: The paper includes DRC-8 as a massive young starbursting, gas-rich dusty galaxy at z 4 and estimates its gas and ion densities for the model.

quotes.quote: The five galaxies we have chosen are the Milky Way (a large, older, spiral galaxy), M82 (a starburst galaxy), the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC) (a dwarf galaxy), NGC 4449 (a dwarf starburst galaxy), and DRC-8 (a massive, young, starbursting galaxy).

Our models are very general, and so for concreteness, we picked five different galaxies that we believe are representative of the many types of star forming galaxies in our universe: a large spiral (the Milky Way), a gas rich dwarf (the LMC), a large starburst (M82), a dwarf starburst (NGC 4449) and a large, gas rich, dusty galaxy viewed at z 4 (DRC-8). Their parameters are given in Table 1.

For DRC-8, we estimated based on discussions with Arianna Long that n_ gas = 10^3 cm^ 3 and that n_ ion = 10^ 1.5 cm^ 3 . We then obtained the mass densities using the conversions from eqn. ( eq:massconvert ).

gmw_00003606.name: UGC 9133
gmw_00003606.overview: UGC 9133, also known as NGC 5533, is an early-type disk galaxy located approximately 51.22 Mpc away [arXiv:astro-ph/0310868, arXiv:1603.02685]. It features a large disk scale length of 9.3 kpc and exhibits a strongly declining rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0310868, arXiv:2011.02878].

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 9133 to illustrate mass modeling of early-type disks, describing its strongly declining rotation curve, large disk scale length, and models requiring near-maximum stellar mass-to-light ratios.

quotes.quote: UGC 9133 for example, having one of the most strongly declining rotation curves in the sample, has a disk scale length of 9.3 kpc.

To illustrate the method, we show here the analysis for UGC 9133. Some basic parameters of this galaxy are given in table 1, and an R-band image from the JKT is shown in fig. 2. The H i observations are shown in fig. 3.

The maximum M/L-ratios are reached in models where the bulge and disk account fully for the observed rotation velocities in the inner regions, with the dark halo only contributing at larger radii ('maximum bulge+disk', upper panels in fig. 4). In this case however, the steep rise of the rotation curve in the central regions and the decline at larger radii also allow us to put tight lower limits to the M/L-ratios for bulge and disk ('minimum bulge+disk', lower panels in fig. 4), thereby fixing the stellar M/L-ratios to within a factor of 2.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5533 as a nearby declining-rotation-curve galaxy, deriving its f(R) rotation and lensing behavior, closest approach scale, and characteristic halo-length parameter.

quotes.quote: For instance, we work with the two nearby controversial galaxies NGC 5533 and NGC 4138 and explore their galactic features by analysing the lensing angle profiles in f(R) background. We study the two controversial galaxies i.e., NGC 5533 and NGC 4138 via the observations of rotational velocity profile beyond the optical disk size and lensing angle profile in f(R) background. In Section rgb 1.00,0.00,0.00 (III) , We explore the galactic dynamics via the f(R) rotational velocity profiles for the nearby galaxies (under controversy due to their declining rotation curve) NGC 5533 and NGC 4138 and obtain their respective galactic length scale free parameter r_0 with O(10^ 6 ) .

The magnitude of effective f(R) lensing angle is _ Net 4.2 10^ 6 and _ Net 1.5 10^ 6 for NGC 5533 and NGC 4138, respectively, with the corresponding approximate closest approach at about 22 kpc and 6 kpc in f(R) background. For the two galaxies (NGC 5533 and NGC 4138), we have the value of such approximate f(R) characteristic length as 446.600 kpc and 177.828 kpc which might represent their halo size.

sources.summary: The paper gives NGC 5533 distance and coordinates, then finds the EFE improves the Milgromian fit and identifies SDSS J141606.29+352022.5 as a possible source.

quotes.quote: (16) NGC 5533 : This galaxy with a distance of 51.22^ +5.98 _ 11.02 Mpc n3521-lu, n2998-md, n3521-md from us is located at the celestial coordinates of ( 14^h16^m07.7^s, 35^d20^m38^s ). Applying the EFE, our results show a good improvement in Milgromian dynamics fits such that the reduced ^2 decreases from about 2.5 to 1.1. The best fit is obtained with an external acceleration of a_e=0.20^ +0.05 _ 0.06 . SDSS J141606.29+352022.5 1-n5533 is a galaxy that can induce the necessary external field.

gmw_00003607.name: Leo A
gmw_00003607.overview: Leo A is a dwarf galaxy located within the Local Group [arXiv:2012.01586]. Based on its proper motion, it is unlikely to have experienced past interactions with either the Milky Way or M31 [arXiv:2012.01586].

sources.summary: The paper derives Leo A's proper motion but finds only tiny parameter-space regions for past Milky Way or M31 interaction, leaving current proper motions insufficient to decide backsplash status.

quotes.quote: We find that NGC 6822, Leo A, IC 1613 and WLM have sufficient supergiants with reliable astrometry to derive proper motions. An additional three galaxies (Leo T, Eridanus 2 and Phoenix) are close enough that their proper motions have already been derived using red giant branch stars. Systematic errors in Gaia DR2 are significant for NGC 6822, IC 1613 and WLM. We explore the orbits for these galaxies, and conclude that Phoenix, Leo A and WLM are unlikely to have interacted with the Milky Way or M31, unless these large galaxies are very massive ( 1.6 10^ 12 M_ ). We rule out a past interaction of NGC 6822 with M31 at 99.99 confidence, and find there is a <10 chance that NGC 6822 has had an interaction with the Milky Way. We examine the likely origins of NGC 6822 in the periphery of the young Local Group, and note that a future interaction of NGC 6822 with the Milky Way or M31 in the next 4 Gyrs is essentially ruled out.

It is interesting to note that NGC 6822, Leo T and Leo A could have interacted with either the Milky Way or M31, depending on their tangential velocities. For the latter two, M31 interactions can only have occured in a relatively small region of parameter space for the setup with the most massive M31 ( 2.6 10^ 12 M_ ).

Leo A: There is only a tiny regime of parameter space in which Leo A could have interacted with either the Milky Way or M31. For the latter, M31 must be massive (as in LG2; around twice the most likely mass for the Milky Way). For the former, the Milky Way must also be relatively massive (as in LG3; at the upper end of the current preferred mass range) and the relative tangential velocity of the two must be less than around 40 km s ^ 1 . Like Leo T, the current estimate for the proper motion of Leo A is completely useless for distinguishing these scenarios, but the relatively tiny regions of parameter space that need to be excluded means that future improvements in the size of the uncertainities associated with the proper motion may put meaningful constraints on the likelihood or otherwise of a backspash origin;

gmw_00003608.name: WLM
gmw_00003608.overview: WLM is a dwarf galaxy located in the periphery of the Local Group [arXiv:2012.01586]. Orbital analyses indicate that it is unlikely to have interacted with the Milky Way or M31, unless M31 is relatively massive [arXiv:2012.01586].

sources.summary: The paper derives WLM's proper motion; it finds no Milky Way interaction solutions and allows an M31 interaction only if M31 is relatively massive and WLM is near apocenter.

quotes.quote: We find that NGC 6822, Leo A, IC 1613 and WLM have sufficient supergiants with reliable astrometry to derive proper motions. An additional three galaxies (Leo T, Eridanus 2 and Phoenix) are close enough that their proper motions have already been derived using red giant branch stars. Systematic errors in Gaia DR2 are significant for NGC 6822, IC 1613 and WLM. We explore the orbits for these galaxies, and conclude that Phoenix, Leo A and WLM are unlikely to have interacted with the Milky Way or M31, unless these large galaxies are very massive ( 1.6 10^ 12 M_ ). We rule out a past interaction of NGC 6822 with M31 at 99.99 confidence, and find there is a <10 chance that NGC 6822 has had an interaction with the Milky Way. We examine the likely origins of NGC 6822 in the periphery of the young Local Group, and note that a future interaction of NGC 6822 with the Milky Way or M31 in the next 4 Gyrs is essentially ruled out.

WLM: Within the setups explored, this dwarf can only have interacted with M31, and can only have done so if M31 is relatively massive (as in LG2 and LG3). Those orbits which lead to an interaction require WLM to be close to apocenter, and with relative velocities between the two less than 60 km s ^ 1 . This region of parameter space overlaps with our current estimate of its proper motion.

gmw_00003609.name: C0
gmw_00003609.overview: C0 is a strongly lensed, multiply-imaged system at a redshift of z=1.60 [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705]. Discovered during a survey for Extremely Red Objects, the system consists of three components and exhibits a faint radial arc [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705].

sources.summary: The paper describes C0 as a strongly lensed, three-component ERO system at z=1.60, using its symmetric knots and faint radial arc to constrain the Abell 68 mass model.

quotes.quote: C0 source This source is a strongly-lensed ( 3.2 mag.) multiply-imaged system with three components, discovered during a survey for Extremely Red Objects (EROs) in the fields of 10 massive galaxy cluster lenses at z 0.2 Smith02a . A redshift measurement on the brightest image, C0a, was presented in Smith02b . Based on the 4000 break identification at = 1.04 0.01 m, the redshift is z=1.60 0.03 . This image was also included in two LRIS masks, but we failed to detect strong spectral features in the wavelength range 5500-9200 \ . Because of the clear symmetry of images C0a and C0b with respect to the critical line, it is possible to isolate 4 bright knots in each image a , b and and use the corresponding 12 images to constrain the mass model.

gmw_00003610.name: C4
gmw_00003610.overview: C4 is a highly magnified, gravitationally lensed Lyman-alpha blob located at a redshift of z=2.63 [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705]. Reconstructed source-plane morphology reveals that the galaxy has a bright, elongated shape with several knots spanning a scale length of 3 kpc along its major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705].

sources.summary: C4 is presented as a highly magnified z=2.63 lensed Lyman-alpha blob whose reconstructed source-plane morphology shows a bright elongated knotty galaxy resolved to 30 pc scales.

quotes.quote: We now turn to a specific issue relating to the origin of the so-called Lyman- blobs, of which C4 at z=2.63 is the first lensed example. We have used the updated mass model to reconstruct the source-plane morphology of C4 . The high magnification ( 4 mags.) enables us to resolve the source morphology at 30 pc scales. The galaxy displays a bright component with an intrinsic elongated shape and several knots, at a scale length of 3 kpc along the major axis.

gmw_00003611.name: C15
gmw_00003611.overview: C15 is a high-redshift galaxy at z=5.421 [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705]. It is a triply imaged R-band dropout that exhibits a faint continuum and Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705].

sources.summary: C15 is a triply imaged R-band dropout at z=5.421, with faint continuum, Lyman-alpha emission, small physical size, and luminosity below typical narrow-band z~5.7 LAEs.

quotes.quote: The three images identified as C15, C16 and C17 have been selected as R band dropouts on the basis of color-color diagrams combining R , I and z band filters. Indeed, they are very faint in the HST image ( R_ 702 25.7 ), undetected at shorter wavelengths in the B or R band with CFH12k with a combination of red (R-I)_ AB 1.7 and blue (I-z)_ AB 0.5 colors, as measured with aperture photometry on the seeing-matched images. Such a spectral energy distribution (SED) is characteristic of galaxies in the redshift range 5 z 6 , where the Lyman- break in the spectral continuum occurs between the R and I bands.

C15 source A strong asymmetric emission line is clearly seen on the LRIS spectrum at the position of C15a and C15b (Figure C1a , top-right), with a central peak at 7808 . In both cases, a faint continuum is detected on the red side of the emission line, with a flux 4.4 1.5 10^ 19 ergs s ^ 1 \ cm ^ 2 \ ^ 1 . We interpret the emission line as Lyman- at z=5.421 .

gmw_00003612.name: C20
gmw_00003612.overview: C20 is a high-redshift arclet galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.689 [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705]. It exhibits extended Lyman-alpha emission, and gravitational lensing mass models predict the system has two counter-images with similar optical colors [arXiv:astro-ph/0702705].

sources.summary: C20 is an arclet with extended Lyman-alpha emission at z=2.689; the mass model predicts two counter-images with similar optical colors for the system.

quotes.quote: The arclet identified as C20 was serendipitously covered during the same LRIS long-slit observation. We interpret the extended emission seen in the spectrum (Sect. redshifts ) as Lyman- at z=2.689 . This is also supported by the mass model, which predicts 2 detected counter-images with similar optical colors. We refer to this system as C20a,b,.

gmw_00003613.name: MORA-0
gmw_00003613.overview: MORA-0 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:1007.0200] with a photometric redshift estimated between z=3 and z=5 [arXiv:1007.0200, arXiv:2110.06930]. It is part of a coherent megaparsec-scale structure at z=4.6 [arXiv:2112.07430] and is gravitationally lensed by massive foreground galaxies [arXiv:2112.07430].

sources.summary: The paper reports MM1 as an unresolved, significantly detected submillimeter galaxy candidate with precise SMA astrometry and redshift estimates placing it at roughly z=3-5, consistent with a high-redshift SMG.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The measured SMA position for MM1 is ( J2000 )=10^ h 00^ m 15.612^ s , ( J2000 )=+02 15 49.00

quotes.quote: SMA detections Fitting two-dimensional Gaussians to the SMA images, leads to flux densities of 16.8 1.5 mJy and 8.5 2.0 mJy for MM1 and MM14, respectively. The MM14 detection is tentative since, as we shall see below, it could not be reliably identified with any significant counterpart at other wavelengths. The Gaussian fit indicates that both sources are unresolved with a maximum deconvolved FWHM size of 1.8 1.0 and 2.0 0.6 , respectively, consistent with the sizes found for high-redshift SMGs, which are typically unresolved at 2 resolution Iono2006,Younger2007, Wang2007,Younger2009 .

The measured SMA position for MM1 is ( J2000 )=10^ h 00^ m 15.612^ s , ( J2000 )=+02 15 49.00 , with a positional error in the Gaussian fit of 0.09 , while for MM14 it is ( J2000 )=10^ h 00^ m 47.329^ s , ( J2000 )=+02 10 21.44 , with a positional error in the fit of 0.15 .

For MM1, we also computed redshifts by fitting templates to the UV-to-mm photometry. We used the same SWIRE templates, redshift and extinction ranges. The best fit is produced by an Arp220 model, with A_ V =0.3 and z=4.5 . The value of ^2 is dominated by the ratio between the submm to optical light, indicating a possible range of redshift from 3.0 to 5.0. Our redshift estimates are in good agreement with the redshift implied by the submm to radio flux ratio and supports a higher redshift than that obtained purely from the optical photometry.

sources.summary: MORA-0 is the highest-SNR MORA source, previously detected as MAMBO-1/AzTECC7/850.04, with photometric redshift evidence and multi-band ALMA/radio detections.

quotes.quote: MORA-0, a.k.a. 850.04 or MAMBO-1 The highest signal-to-noise source in the MORA maps is detected at nearly 8 , and has been previously identified as a DSFG through detection at 1.2 mm MAMBO-1 in bertoldi07a , 1.1 mm AzTECC7 in aretxaga11a , and at 850 \ 850.04 in casey13a . The source is only marginally detected with Herschel SPIRE and SCUBA-2 at 450 \ oliver12a,casey13a . To date, it does not have a reliable spectroscopic confirmation despite being targeted repeatedly in near-infared campaigns; the H-band MOSFIRE spectrum presented in casey17a is spatially offset from the ALMA source by 1 '' . The closer (and much fainter) OIR counterpart, only 0 3 offset from the ALMA centroid, has an OIR-based photometric redshift from the laigle16a COSMOS2015 catalog of z_ phot =3.31^ +0.76 _ 0.81 ; the source is missing from the COSMOS2020 photometric catalogs for no obvious reason other than its marginal detection near the detection limit of the near-infrared catalogs. MORA-0 has a FIR photometric redshift from brisbin17a of z_ FIR =4.4 1.0 .

sources.summary: The paper places AS2COS0002.1 in a z=4.6 structure and notes its lensing magnification depends on nearby massive foreground galaxies in a group environment.

quotes.quote: Three target SMGs, AS2COS0001.1, AS2COS0002.1, and AS2COS0006.1, have been shown to be part of a z=4.6 coherent Mpc scale structure Mitsuhashi:2021aa . Notably, we find that if we only look at the mass distributions of a much closer surroundings, the magnifications of a few SMGs, such as AS2COS0002.1 and AS2COS0014.1, would be significantly underestimated. This is because in these cases there are a few very massive (up to 10^ 11.5 M_ ) foreground galaxies, and in the case of AS2COS0002.1 a number of galaxies including the very massive ones are located at z 1 , suggesting a group environment.

gmw_00003614.name: MORA-1
gmw_00003614.overview: The submillimeter galaxy MORA-1, also known as AzTEC-5, is classified as a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1606.05350] at a redshift of approximately 3.8 to 3.97 [arXiv:1312.1173, arXiv:2110.06930]. It is an ongoing galaxy merger containing multiple dust, ultraviolet, and infrared components [arXiv:1702.07527, arXiv:1802.07751] encompassed by extended radio emission [arXiv:1509.07147].

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC/C42 as a candidate Taffy-like SMG merger resolved into two dust components encompassed by 3 GHz radio emission.

quotes.quote: Among our target SMGs, AzTEC/C22 and AzTEC/C42 are good candidates for Taffy-like systems, as illustrated in Fig. figure:taffy. The SMG AzTEC/C42 is unresolved in our ALMA 1.3 mm image, but was resolved into two components with ALMA at about three times higher resolution at _ obs =994 m (Cycle 1 ALMA project 2012.1.00978.S; PI: A. Karim). The detected 3 GHz radio emission encompasses both the dust-emitting components, which are separated by 5.3 kpc.

A well-separated binary nature of AzTEC/C22 and C42 suggests an incomplete, or an early-stage SMG-SMG merger, and part of the radio emission from these systems is likely to arise from a magnetised medium between them. Among the aforementioned systems, we derived a flat rest-frame spectral index of _ 3.6 GHz ^ 7.8 GHz =-0.37 0.27 for AzTEC/C22a (the northern component), and a fairly flat value of _ 6.5 GHz ^ 13.9 GHz =-0.50 0.24 for AzTEC/C42. Hence, our candidate Taffy systems, AzTEC/C22 and C42, could potentially be high-redshift manifestations of the trend found by Murphy (2013).

sources.summary: AzTEC5 has four UV components at a plausible shared photometric redshift; irregular UV emission, three FIR peaks, and aligned IRAC peaks suggest an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: AzTEC5 : For this system, three main UV components are detected in all three HST bands (AzTEC5-2, AzTEC5-3 and AzTEC5-4) and a fourth component is detected only in F814W (AzTEC5-1). AzTEC5 is the only source in our sample that lacks spectroscopic confirmation, but photometric redshift estimation indicates a plausible solution for all four components at the same redshift (see Section subsec:mstar_cav ). The irregular rest-frame UV morphology of AzTEC5-2 and AzTEC5-4, with emission connecting both in F160W , is suggestive of an ongoing merger.

The rest-frame FIR has three emission peaks. Two bright peaks associated with AzTEC5-1 and AzTEC5-2 respectively, and a fainter peak in between them, which is not detected in any HST bands. Besides, the FIR peaks related with AzTEC5-1 and AzTEC5-2 are aligned with the position of two peaks in the IRAC images, suggesting that the bulk of the stellar mass is associated with these two components which are probably merging.

sources.summary: The paper resolves AzTEC5 along its radio major axis, links the radio emission to two ALMA components, and finds the 3 GHz size much larger than the UV/optical diameter.

quotes.quote: AzTEC5 . The 3 GHz FWHM size we have determined for this source is 0 95^ +0.07 _ 0.07 < 0 38 , i.e. the major axis is resolved while the minor axis is unresolved. Miettinen et al. (in prep.) used ALMA (PI: A. Karim) to observe AzTEC5 at _ obs =994 m ( _ rest 245 m) continuum and an angular resolution of 0 52 0 30 . The source was resolved into two components with a projected separation of 0 75 ( 5.86 kpc at the source redshift). The northern ALMA component is perfectly coincident with our 3 GHz source ( 0 03 offset), but we note that the 3 GHz emission extends towards the southern ALMA FIR component, and hence the detected 3 GHz emission encompasses the two ALMA-detected components.
The major axis FWHM of AzTEC5 at 3 GHz is 7.9 6.6 times larger than its UV/optical diameter.

sources.summary: The paper includes C1-21316/AzTEC 5 as a z=3.971 submillimeter galaxy, detected in SPIRE and radio, with MIR AGN contribution and radio-loud AGN classification.

quotes.quote: Table tab-obs also lists C1-21316 from the sample of marchesini10 but not targeted by our spectroscopic follow-up program, since it has a previously confirmed spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =3.971 ( capak10, smolcic12 ; but see also miettinen15 ). C1-21316 is also a sub-millimeter galaxy (SMG) listed as AzTEC 5 in the AzTEC millimeter survey catalog in COSMOS scott08 .
Only C1-21316, which is a sub-millimeter galaxy and a radio-loud AGN, is detected in SPIRE and at longer wavelengths; C1-2127 is detected in the blue and green SPIRE bands, but not at 500 m.

From the sample of galaxies presented here, only C1-21316 (AzTEC-5) is detected at 1.4GHz schinnerer10 , with a flux of 0.126 0.015 mJy. We calculated the rest-frame flux densities assuming the canonical value of the radio spectral index of =0.8 condon92 . The calculated rest-frame flux densities is L_ 1.4GHz = 10.6 1.3 10^ 27 W/Hz, above the threshold to be classified as radio-loud AGN (log( L_ 1.4GHz >25), schinnerer07 ).

sources.summary: MORA-1 is identified with AzTEC-5/AzTEC5-1, detected in multiple far-infrared and ALMA bands, with uncertain but adopted photometric redshift around z~3.78.

quotes.quote: MORA-1, a.k.a. AzTEC-5 The second most significant source, MORA-1, has been studied under many names, the most widely-used of which is AzTEC-5 for its initial detection in scott08a at 1.1 mm. magnelli19a includes a nice discussion of its known characteristics and redshift constraints, which we summarize here for completeness. AzTEC-5 is Herschel SPIRE, SCUBA-2 (450 \ and 850 ) and ALMA detected, with continuum measurements in bands 6 and 7. The literature alludes to a spectroscopic identification for AzTEC-5 of z=3.791 based on Ly emission in a DEIMOS spectrum capak10a,smolcic12a ; however, that solution was revealed to be inaccurate by subsequent ALMA follow-up that failed to detect emission lines to corroborate the redshift. gomez-guijarro18a identify four components of AzTEC-5 in deep near-infrared imaging, which they dubbed AzTEC5-1, 5-2, 5-3, and 5-4.

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC5 as a robust SMA-position counterpart with a photometric redshift matching z = 3.97 and secure recovery by all four original-position association methods.

quotes.quote: AzTEC5. A robust single identification 0.38 arcsec from the SMA position. z_ phot is in excellent agreement with the spectroscopic redshift z = 3.97 , while z_ LW is somewhat under-estimated. This source would have been securely identified using all four types of statistical association on the basis of the original AzTEC position.

gmw_00003615.name: MORA-2
gmw_00003615.overview: MORA-2 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy detected in COSMOS submillimeter surveys [arXiv:2110.06930]. It has an estimated optical-infrared photometric redshift of approximately 3.36 [arXiv:2110.06930], while alternative analyses suggest a redshift of about 4 [arXiv:1312.1173].

sources.summary: MORA-2 is a 7.6 sigma 2 mm source, detected in wider-field COSMOS submillimeter surveys and ALMA bands, with OIR and millimeter photometric redshifts in agreement.

quotes.quote: MORA-2 a.k.a. COS850.0020 The third source in the MORA sample, MORA-2, is detected at a signal-to-noise of 7.6 at 2 mm. It has no match in the earliest SCUBA-2 surveys roseboom12a,casey13a but is detected in the wider-field surveys of COSMOS from geach17b and simpson19a . Both ALMA band 6 and 7 data exist for MORA-2, which was selected both as a submillimeter source and dropout source in the ZFOURGE survey spitler12a . Its OIR photometry give a photometric redshift from COSMOS2020 of z_ phot =3.36^ +0.60 _ 0.28 . There are no spectroscopic constraints for MORA-2; our millimeter-derived photometric redshift for the source is z_ mm =2.8^ +0.6 _ 0.6 which is consistent with the OIR photometric redshift.

sources.summary: The paper describes COSLA-23N as a robust PdBI-position counterpart at about z = 4, recoverable from the original LABOCA position by all but the 24 micron method.

quotes.quote: COSLA-23N. A robust single identification 0.11 arcsec from the PdBI position. z_ phot and z_ LW are in good agreement that the source has a redshift z 4 . This object would have been correctly identified using all but the 24 m identification technique on the basis of the original LABOCA position.

gmw_00003616.name: MORA-3
gmw_00003616.overview: MORA-3 is a bright, high-redshift dusty starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.6 [arXiv:2003.05484, arXiv:2110.06930]. It consists of two physically associated components that are likely undergoing a rapid, prograde merger within a single dark matter halo [arXiv:2003.05484, arXiv:2102.06250].

sources.summary: AS2COS 0001.1 is a bright z=4.624 SMG with strong 337 GHz line emission; together with 0001.2 it likely occupies one halo in a prograde merger.

quotes.quote: We note that these sources have also been discussed in a very recent paper by , where they are named AzTEC2-A and AzTEC2-B respectively, and we compare our results to those from their analysis in the following.

AS2COS 0001.1 0001.2 were identified in an archival ALMA observation of S2COSMOS 0001, the highest significance source in the S2COSMOS survey (SNR = 28, S_ 850 m =, 16.8 mJy). The ALMA-detected SMGs have 870- m flux densities of 13.5 0.3 and 3.6 0.2 mJy -- where these fluxes are based on the line-free continuum in the ALMA cubes. As discussed by and , the ALMA observations of both these sources show strong lines at 337 GHz with apparently high equivalent widths (see Figure fig:cii ). The ^2 P _ 3 / 2 ^2 P _ 1 / 2 fine structure line of atomic carbon (C ^+ ) at 157.7 m, hereafter [ C ii ] , is typically the strongest far-infrared emission line in the spectra of star-forming galaxies (e.g. \ Brauher08,Santos13 ). The [ C ii ] emission can comprise 2 per cent of the total bolometric luminosity of a source, and is typically an order of magnitude brighter than other atomic or molecular emission (e.g. \ [ N ii ] 122 m, [ O i ] 145 m, [ N ii ] 205 m, or mid- J ^ 12 CO). At the depth of our observations, [ C ii ] is the most likely identification for the line emission from AS2COS 0001.1 0001.2, placing these sources at a redshift of z = 4.624 0.001 and 4.635 0.001, respectively.

Hence the observed spatial and velocity offsets between AS2COS 0001.1 0001.2 are consistent with them occupying a dark matter halo which a mass of 10 ^ 13 , suggesting that it is likely these SMGs are physically-associated within a single dark matter halo (see also the discussion of S2COSMOS 0003 in Wang16 ). Based on the orientation of their velocity fields, these two galaxies appear to be co-rotating in a prograde orbit, with a velocity offset comparable to their internal rotation velocities, suggesting the possibility we are witnessing a rapid and highly efficient merger.

sources.summary: MORA-3/AzTEC-2 is a two-component high-redshift DSFG with confirmed z~4.63 spectroscopy, ALMA/radio detections, foreground lensing, and membership in a z=4.6 overdensity.

quotes.quote: MORA-3, a.k.a. AzTEC-2 or 450.03 The fourth source, MORA-3, is more widely known as AzTEC-2 younger07a detected with the SMA, and at 450 \ and 850 \ by SCUBA-2 where it is known as 450.03; casey13a . It was also previously detected at 2 mm as GISMO-C2 magnelli19a . It is detected by Herschel SPIRE and has ALMA continuum data in band 6 and 7. The interferometric data reveal two millimeter sources, the primary (coincident with the 2 mm emission) is AzTEC2-A and the secondary fainter source (detected at 2.6 significance in the 2 mm map) is AzTEC2-B. While a spectroscopic identification based on a possible OIR counterpart existed at z=1.123 smolcic12a,casey17a 1 '' to the south of the primary source A, that redshift has since been shown to be associated with a foreground galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats AS2COS0001.1 as a bright SMG in a close pair, part of a z=4.6 structure, with prior CO and CII detections favoring double-peaked profiles.

quotes.quote: For AS2COS0001.1, AS2COS0008.1, and AS2COS0028.1, where the nearby SMGs are also detected in a few cases and close enough to potentially affect the spectra, we re-image the cubes using the Briggs baseline weighting with a robust parameter of -1.5 We have also tried uniform weighting but some spectra become too noisy. Although marginal ( 2 over the FWHM) in our data, the same line from AS2COS0001.2 (and AS2COS0001.1) was detected in high significance ( >5 ) by Jimenez-Andrade:2020aa with much deeper data from NOEMA, and Simpson:2020aa and Mitsuhashi:2021aa both reported detection of [ CII ] line from AS2COS0001.2. Three target SMGs, AS2COS0001.1, AS2COS0002.1, and AS2COS0006.1, have been shown to be part of a z=4.6 coherent Mpc scale structure Mitsuhashi:2021aa .

As a check, the lensing magnification of AS2COS0001.1 was also estimated by Jimenez-Andrade:2020aa with a different assumption of the density profile, and they found a factor of 1.5, consistent with our results. Jimenez-Andrade:2020aa reported CO(5-4) and [ CII ] detection on AS2COS0001.1, and with better signal-to-noise measurements they favored double-peak line profiles for both lines.

sources.summary: The paper identifies AzTEC2 through radio and 24 micron emission, rejects a nearby low-redshift alternative, and says the radio position and mm/radio redshift support the true counterpart.

quotes.quote: AzTEC2. A secure radio and 24 m identification without a visible optical or K band counterpart (and hence no stellar mass estimate in Table 5). An alternative object 1.4 arcsec away from the SMA position was selected by Smolcic et al. (2012) and found to have a spectroscopic redshift z = 1.125 . However, since the radio position is only 0.39 arcsec from the SMA position and the mm/radio flux ratio yields a long-wavelength redshift estimate of z_ LW = 3.60 , this low-redshift object cannot be the correct identification (its mm/radio flux-density ratio is 150 , inconsistent with such a low redshift; see Fig. 4). The correct radio identification would still have been secured without the improved positional accuracy provided by the SMA interferometry.

sources.summary: The paper identifies an additional 3 GHz source southwest of AzTEC2 that may be a radio lobe or merger component and notes a weak ALMA feature nearby.

quotes.quote: AzTEC2 exhibits an additional 3 GHz source to the SW, which might be an indication of a merging pair (or a radio jet). The additional source has a major axis FWHM of 0 72^ +0.31 _ 0.44 , which is also larger than the median _ maj of 0 54 0 11 .

The additional 3 GHz feature towards AzTEC2 could, in principle, represent a radio-emitting lobe of a jet interacting with the surrounding medium or a merger component (projected separation is 12.7 proper kpc at the redshift of AzTEC2). We note that the 1.3 mm emission detected towards AzTEC2 with ALMA also shows an additional weak ( 2.9 ) feature at 2 14 to the SW of AzTEC2 (Cycle 2 ALMA project 2013.1.00118.S; M. Aravena et al., in prep.), but its peak position lies 0 60 away to the SW of the 3 GHz feature; this offset is within the large statistical positional uncertainty of 1 of the weak 1.3 mm feature [ _ stat ( S/N )^ 1 ] .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 450.03/AzTEC2 as a high-redshift DSFG at z=4.61, composed of two same-redshift sources, with blended foregrounds preventing reliable stellar-mass measurements.
